title: Chief Commercial Officer (International Education)
description: About the Role This is a newly created Executive Leadership position with a strong regional growth mandate, with particular emphasis on the China and ASEAN markets. As SagePaths Global Education continues its expansion across Asia, the Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will play a pivotal role in shaping the Group’s commercial strategy, driving sustainable revenue growth, and strengthening its international education ecosystem. The CCO will be responsible for building a scalable, data-driven, and high-performance commercial framework that integrates student recruitment, admissions, marketing, partnerships, and digital systems across all business units. This role is instrumental in translating strategic vision into commercial success and long-term market leadership. About SagePaths Global Education SagePaths Global Education is a leading regional education group headquartered in Singapore, operating a diversified portfolio of education and education-related businesses. Our brands include SagePaths Global Education, Singapore Education Development Centre, SagePartners Business Consultancy, SOVO Student Apartment, Pickering Institute, Pickering Global Campus, as well as a growing network of Overseas Teaching Centres across China. We specialise in building a fully integrated education ecosystem that supports international students across their entire academic journey, including: International student placement and recruitment Cross-business services centred around international students Student accommodation development and operations Academic pathways and university articulation programmes Foundation and Diploma programmes Online and blended education delivery Transnational education and cross-border programme delivery Our vision is to build a globally connected education ecosystem that empowers students through high-quality, accessible, and future-oriented learning pathways. We work closely with universities, private education institutions, and overseas partners to deliver well-recognised academic programmes and meaningful international collaboration opportunities. As we enter our next phase of strategic development, we are strengthening our Executive Leadership Team to support rapid regional expansion and organisational scaling. The Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will be a core member of this leadership group, working directly with the Managing Director and the Board to lead the Group’s commercial transformation, revenue growth strategy, and international market expansion. This is a rare opportunity to shape the commercial architecture of a fast-growing international education group and to make a lasting impact on its regional and global footprint. Key Responsibilities Reporting directly to the Managing Director, the Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will lead the Group’s end-to-end commercial strategy, with full responsibility across: Marketing, branding, and corporate communications for all SagePaths and Pickering brands Student recruitment strategy, sales performance management, admissions operations, and conversion optimisation Agent network development, channel management, and strategic partnerships (both domestic and international) Commercial systems architecture, including CRM, websites, digital marketing platforms, and data analytics infrastructure Pricing strategy, revenue diversification initiatives, and market expansion planning The role carries executive accountability to: Drive sustainable enrolment growth and strong revenue performance across both onshore and offshore markets Build a predictable, data-driven recruitment pipeline supported by disciplined sales governance and reporting structures Optimise admissions workflows, conversion rates, and overall student acquisition efficiency Lead CRM and digital platform integration to deliver a seamless, consistent, and high-quality student recruitment experience Strengthen commercial compliance with Singapore PEI regulations (SSG, EduTrust), overseas regulatory frameworks, and partner contractual obligations Champion an exceptional student experience from first engagement through to enrolment, consistently applied across all business units Develop and execute a customer-centric, brand-driven marketing strategy aligned with SagePaths’ premium and professional positioning Build, manage, and scale strategic partnerships with universities, private education institutions, overseas schools, and pathway providers Establish scalable commercial processes, structures, and performance frameworks that support rapid regional and international growth The Successful Candidate As a key member of the Executive Leadership Team, the CCO will work closely with the Managing Director, Executive Dean, and Operations to translate strategic direction into measurable and sustainable commercial performance. We are seeking a commercially astute, internationally minded leader with: A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Commerce, or a related discipline; postgraduate qualifications are strongly preferred 10 years of senior commercial leadership experience, MUST within international education, transnational education, or higher education services Bilingual proficiency in English and Mandarin Chinese is essential , with the ability to conduct professional-level communication, negotiations, and relationship management with business partners, universities, and partner institutions across Chin Proven experience leading large-scale student recruitment, admissions, and marketing operations across multiple markets Deep understanding of international education ecosystems, student mobility trends, agent networks, and education partnerships Strong familiarity with compliance frameworks, including Singapore PEI / EduTrust requirements and international regulatory standards A strong track record in building, scaling, and optimising CRM systems, digital marketing platforms, and performance dashboards Experience integrating CRM with LMS, finance, admissions, and student management systems A strategic mindset combined with strong execution capability and operational discipline Exceptional leadership skills with the ability to build high-performing, values-driven teams across marketing, recruitment, and admissions Comfort and effectiveness operating within a multi-country, multi-brand, and fast-growth environment Regular travel across Singapore, China, and Southeast Asia will be required to support regional operations and partnerships.

title: Middle and High School Social Studies Teacher
description: REPORTS TO: Middle & High School Principal JOB GOAL: The successful Social Studies teacher at ICS must have a strong understanding of standards based assessment as well as concept based teaching and learning.  Experience closing the achievement gap in a variety of contexts would be considered an asset as well. They must also develop engaging and innovative lessons that allow our students to grow academically, socially and emotionally. The teacher should be confident in incorporating technology (including emerging technologies such as GenAI) and hands-on learning experiences into the classroom with enthusiasm and also believe in collaboration with other colleagues, promoting understanding and communication in a diverse learning and cultural environment.  While we are a US curriculum school, the teacher should be able to deliver the curriculum through a wider lens of perspectives and cultures. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Plan, prepare, and execute appropriate lessons, utilizing appropriate resource materials and activities. Follow the prescribed scope and sequence of curriculum and assessment as scheduled. Employ a variety of instructional aides, methods, and materials that will promote best practices in order to reach the whole child as stated in the ICS mission statement. Assess student learning against the appropriate standards on a regular basis and provide progress reports as required. Identify the needs of students through diagnostic procedures and align instructional objectives to these needs. Effect student learning through mastery of the subject material by utilizing valid teaching techniques to achieve curriculum goals within the framework of the school’s philosophy. Eager to explore and learn about emerging technologies, such as Generative AI, with a commitment to integrating them effectively into the classroom to foster critical thinking and guide students in developing ethical and responsible use. Ensure Biblical Worldwide Integration (BWI) is mapped throughout the curriculum. Complete lesson/unit plans, Atlas, ESO documentation, and PLC expectations within specified time frames. Maintain student records as required by the school. Communicate with parents, students, and other professional staff regarding student progress (behavioral and academic). Follow school policies, procedures, rules, regulations, and guidelines and the provisions of the contract. Maintain proper discipline in the classroom and on the school premises utilizing teacher created discipline plans in conjunction with a school-wide discipline plan. Inform the administration if/when unable to fulfill any duty assigned. Prepare adequate information and materials for a substitute teacher. Plan and lead a student Chapel when called upon. Supervise extracurricular activities, organizations, and outings as assigned. Complete the Christian Philosophy of Education in-service program within the first three years of teaching at ICS. Attend and participate in devotions, teachers’ meetings, evening programs (if involving his or her students or as requested by the administration), and other meetings as assigned. Carry out supervisory duty as assigned (before school, break, lunch, after school). Maintain a classroom in a clean and educationally stimulating environment. Maintain regular and accurate attendance and grade reports that provide comprehensive information for each student’s learning progress. Keep students, parents, and the administration informed of students’ progress or deficiencies, and provide sufficient and timely notice to parents when students fail to meet learning standards. Submit all appropriate reports to the administration, guidance office, and business office as required by school regulations or as requested. Work with the administration in implementing all policies, procedures, and directives governing the operation of the school. Complete professional development programs as required by the administration and/or ICS Board. Maintain teaching credentials as required through sponsoring agencies (state/provincial education department, ACSI, etc.). Acquire and maintain teaching credentials as required by the laws of Singapore, NICS, and/or ICS. Demonstrate the character qualities of enthusiasm, courtesy, flexibility, integrity, kindness, self-control, perseverance, and punctuality. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity, and optimism. Develop and maintain rapport with students, parents, and staff by treating others with friendliness, open-mindedness, dignity, and consideration. Respectfully submit to and demonstrate loyalty to constituted authority. Maintain a personal appearance that models cleanliness, modesty, good taste, and is in agreement with school policy. QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s Degree Teaching credential from a US state, Canadian province or from ACSI (exceptions will be approved by the Academic Board) Expertise in working with students and teaching Advanced Placement (AP) courses Knowledge of specific subject(s)

title: Teacher of Psychology (and English)
description: About the School - Brighton College (Singapore): Brighton College (Singapore) is an exciting place to work, and it is our staff who make our community a vibrant and stimulating environment. The academic success of the Brighton College Family of Schools is due to excellent, innovative and well-planned teaching. Above all, therefore, we are looking for individuals who foster a passion for learning and who demonstrate an ability and genuine desire to develop pupils and staff to their fullest potential. The school now has an exciting opportunity for an experienced Teacher of Psychology (and English) to join our fantastic team. About Cognita: Cognita is a global leader in independent education.  Founded in 2004, we are a growing community of 100+ schools in 21 countries - in Europe, North America, Latin America, Asia and the Middle East - serving more than 100,000 students.  Each of our schools are proudly unique, however our collective purpose is to create an environment where everyone can, "Thrive in a rapidly evolving world". At Cognita, we know every student is unique.  We nurture their distinct personalities and strengths. We support them academically, socially, and emotionally, wherever they are in the world, wherever they’re starting from.  And each and every one of our schools is unique too. We protect what’s special about them, while offering them rich knowledge, opportunities, and best practices. And they get to be part of something bigger.  We open their classrooms to the collective wisdom of over 100 schools in 21 countries, ensuring every child gets an education that’s, quite literally, world class. It’s an individual approach that empowers every school, teacher and student to focus on what they do best and thrive. About the Role: Brighton College (Singapore), is seeking an inspirational Teacher of Psychology (and English) to join our expanding Senior School community from August 2026. This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to the development of the school’s academic provision as Brighton College (Singapore) grows from a first-class Prep School into one of the region’s leading all-through schools, offering education from Pre-Nursery to Year 13. What you will be doing: Deliver high-quality Psychology and English lessons across the Senior School, ensuring all pupils are engaged, challenged and supported to make excellent progress. Take responsibility for the teaching, progress and attainment of timetabled pupils in Years 7 to 12 from August 2026, extending this responsibility to Year 13 as the school grows. Teach and support pupils preparing for A-level Psychology (starting with the new Year 12 cohort in August 2026). Teach and support pupils in Key Stage 3 and 4 English. Provide academic guidance for Sixth Form pupils interested in pursuing Psychology, or related degrees in the humanities and social sciences, at leading universities. Contribute to a rich and varied co-curricular programme, offering opportunities for pupils to extend their learning through activities such as psychology clubs, essay and writing competitions, debating and enrichment projects. Support and help organise academic events, workshops and trips related to Psychology and wider humanities disciplines. Contribute to the assessment, tracking and reporting of pupil progress in both subjects, ensuring accurate, constructive and timely feedback is provided. Demonstrate the experience and ability to teach Psychology effectively at Key Stage 5, and English across Key Stages 3–4, adapting lessons to meet the needs of pupils of all abilities. Act as an outstanding classroom practitioner, modelling excellent teaching, intellectual curiosity, empathy and high expectations in both subjects. Promote excellence, critical thinking and a love of language, communication and human understanding in all areas of the Psychology and English curriculum. Work closely with the Head of Humanities, Head of English, and the Heads of EAL and AEN, to develop tailored pathways for pupils requiring additional support. Contribute to the school’s pastoral system and act as a tutor for a Senior School year group. Uphold the highest standards of safeguarding and child protection. Undertake any other reasonable management requests in support of the school’s mission, vision and values. Who we are looking for: Bachelor’s degree in Psychology, with PGCE or equivalent. Expert knowledge or experience of successfully delivering Psychology A-level and English at Key Stage 3 (essential). Expert knowledge or experience of successfully delivering English at Key Stage 4 (preferred). International school experience (preferred). Exemplifies the Brighton College (Singapore) values of Curiosity, Confidence and Kindness. An international outlook and understanding of working in diverse environments. title: Principal Manager, Communications and Digital Marketing
description: As Communications team lead, you will shape and drive Lifelong Learning Singapore (LLSG) strategic narrative to position the organisation as a national catalyst for lifelong learning. You will lead the development of integrated marketing and communications strategies and deepen engagement across individuals, enterprises, and partners. This is a leadership role that demands both strategic foresight and operational excellence. You will steward LLSG’s brand, voice and outreach efforts across digital, media, and stakeholder channels, ensuring that LLSG’s programmes, advisory services, and initiatives are not only visible but valued. Your primary responsibilities include the following: · Craft and evolve LLSG’s strategic messaging to reinforce its role as a trusted enabler of lifelong learning and skills development in Singapore. · Lead the design and delivery of integrated, multi-channel marketing and outreach strategies that align with organisational goals and audience needs. · Drive brand visibility and develop messaging frameworks, ensuring consistent narratives across digital, social, EDMs, media, and events. · Drive audience-targeted campaigns (individuals, enterprises, partners) using Search Engine Optimisation (SEO) / Search Engine Marketing (SEM), social media, and paid/organic outreach. · Establish KPIs and measurement frameworks to track and analyse campaign performance, using insights to improve engagement, visibility, and conversions. · Partner with internal teams and external agencies to amplify LLSG’s programmes and ensure effective execution. · Lead, coach, and develop a team, ensuring accountability and high-quality delivery across branding, digital, and content. · Monitor emerging trends in communications, digital engagement, and public sentiment to proactively shape LLSG’s outreach strategies and future-proof its brand positioning. · Experiment with new formats (e.g., podcasts, webinars, interactive content, community activations) to deepen engagement and expand reach across diverse audience segments. · Strengthen internal communications to build organisational culture, align teams around strategic priorities, and foster employee advocacy. · Any other responsibilities deemed important to support the overall efforts of LLSG. Requirements · Has strong skills/competencies in communications, marketing and business. This should be demonstrated through candidates’ portfolio. · At least 8–10 years of relevant work experience in strategic communications, marketing, or brand management, ideally in public sector or complex stakeholder environments. · Strong command of spoken and written English, with proven ability to craft persuasive messages for diverse audiences. · Experience in digital marketing, content strategy, branding, and campaign analytics. · Data-driven, with strong analytical and problem-solving skills to evaluate campaign effectiveness. · Strategic thinker able to balance big-picture goals with hands-on execution. · Collaborative, adaptable, and resilient to change, with strong stakeholder management skills. · Proven leadership skills, able to inspire, guide, and grow a communications team. Candidates who do not have the required qualification or relevant experience are welcome to apply. Successful candidates will be offered a 2-year contract in the first instance and may be considered for an extension or be placed on a permanent tenure. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Risk Executive
description: Investment Company of the People’s Republic of China (Singapore) Pte Ltd is a fully owned subsidiary of the People’s Bank of China. The company manages a part of foreign exchange reserve within the scope authorized by the People’s Bank of China, undertakes and transacts various kinds of investment businesses. Job Description: The successful candidate will perform the following key functions: Provide analytical oversight of geopolitical and international developments by identifying, assessing, and measuring political, economic, and security-related risks with potential global and regional impact. Monitor geopolitical risk exposures across countries, regions, and thematic risk categories against defined risk appetites, thresholds, and early-warning indicators, escalating material developments as required. Analyze geopolitical risk reports and events by attributing outcomes to underlying risk drivers such as policy decisions, leadership changes, electoral cycles, alliance dynamics, and macroeconomic or security shocks. Track and assess global geopolitical developments, emerging risk trends, and structural shifts. Conduct independent, in-depth risk research on selected countries, regions, or themes, and produce regular risk assessments, briefs, and early-warning reports. Requirements: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Political Economy, International Political Economy, International Relations, Political Science, Economics, or related discipline, with a strong focus on geopolitics and global risk analysis Demonstrated experience in geopolitical risk analysis, country risk, sovereign risk, political risk advisory or related research roles within financial institutions, corporates, consultancies or government bodies Strong understanding of geopolitical risk drivers, including political systems, conflict dynamics, sanctions, international trade regimes, and global governance structures. Excellent research, analytical, and critical thinking skills, with the ability to conduct independent, in-depth analysis Strong written communication skills, with a track record of producing clear, concise, and decision-oriented risk reports, briefs, and presentations. Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with cross-functional teams, including risk management, compliance, and senior management. Bilingual with professional proficiency in Mandarin Chinese and English, including strong Mandarin communication skills and excellent Chinese-language report writing capability Interested applicants may apply by sending in your updated resume to hr@icprc.com

title: Manager, Treasury & Governance, Office of Finance
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Singapore Management University is a place where high-level professionalism blends together with a healthy informality. The 'family-like' atmosphere among the SMU community fosters a culture where employees work, plan, organise and play together - building a strong collegiality and morale within the university. Our commitment to attract and retain talent is ongoing. We offer attractive benefits and welfare, competitive compensation packages, and generous professional development opportunities - all to meet the work-life needs of our staff. No wonder, then, that SMU continues to be given numerous awards and recognition for its human resource excellence. RESPONSIBILITIES Responsible for financial books of the University's subsidiaries, ensuring compliance with Singapore FRS and any local statutory requirements. Lead financial audits and statutory filings of overseas operations, as required by local regulations. Oversee monthly and annual close process and full financial reporting (all functions) of overseas operations. Keep abreast of new tax rules and guidance, in Singapore and locations where the University operates and assess impact to the University. Perform assessments of complex accounting issues and address queries on accounting and other financial matters. Engage in business partnership with other University departments, provide financial support and business insights. Develop and implement accounting policies, procedures and controls, governance framework for all overseas entities. Provide recommendations for continuous improvement on policies and processes. Design financial analysis to extract key insights from financial data. Prepare key reports, papers and presentations to senior management, the Board, and other stakeholders. Coordinate interactions both within Finance and with other internal and external stakeholders, eg represent SMU in independently liaising with external stakeholders (e.g. auditors, lawyers, bankers) and internal stakeholders (e.g. senior management). Manage/support systems implementation and related IT projects e.g. HRFS project. Lead/support finance related and cross department projects. Oversee the work of the direct reports, including the outsourced vendor and all offshore accounting staff. Other ad-hoc duties and responsibilities. QUALIFICATIONS Degree or professional qualification in Accounting or Finance with at least 5 years of relevant financial accounting experience. Prior experience in external audit or exposure to international operations, tax, or financial governance would be advantageous. Candidates with Chartered Accountant (CA) / Certified Public Accountant (CPA) would have an advantage. Prior experience in the government or education sector and/or management of overseas entities would be preferred. Strong technical knowledge in accounting, well versed in FRS and its application. Experience in process flow, governance and compliance matters. Highly proficient in Microsoft Office applications. Strong written and verbal communication and presentation skills. Knowledge and hands-on experience in SAP will be advantageous. Highly motivated individual, resourceful and able to multi-task and work independently. Good analytical and organization skills, able to work flexibly to lead projects and demonstrate critical thinking to problem solve. Effective team lead in a hands-on management capacity. Proactive and mature team player with good interpersonal skills who can communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders. OTHER INFORMATION #LI-JN2 Candidates who do not possess the stipulated qualifications but have relevant work experience may still apply. Remuneration and appointment terms shall commensurate with qualifications and experience. SMU reserves the right to modify the appointment terms where necessary. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - Singapore Management University

title: Compliance, GIR Compliance, Analyst, Singapore
description: GLOBAL COMPLIANCE Our division prevents, detects and mitigates compliance, regulatory and reputational risk across the firm and helps to strengthen the firm’s culture of compliance. Compliance accomplishes these through the firm’s enterprise-wide compliance risk management program. As an independent control function and part of the firm’s second line of defence, Compliance assesses the firm’s compliance, regulatory and reputational risk; monitors for compliance with new or amended laws, rules and regulations; designs and implements controls, policies, procedures and training; conducts independent testing; investigates, surveils and monitors for compliance risks and breaches; and leads the firm’s responses to regulatory examinations, audits and inquiries. You'll be part of a team with members from a wide range of academic and professional backgrounds, such as law, accounting, sales, and trading. We look for those who possess sound judgment, curiosity, and are able to adapt to a changing regulatory landscape. RESPONSIBILITIES AND QUALIFICATIONS Business Supported: Global Investment Research Division (GIR). From macroeconomic forecasts to individual stock analysis, GIR develops tools and insights to help shape investment strategies for clients and the firm. GIR analysts/associates work on client-focused research in the equity, fixed-income, currency, and commodities markets, mining big data that enters markets around the world each day to identify game-changing insights. HOW YOU WILL FULFILL YOUR POTENTIAL Initiative, creativity and drive, together with the ability to work closely and comfortably with research analysts, are essential attributes of all group members. Because the role requires substantive interaction with research analysts and advising on rules and laws impacting research across jurisdictions in Asia Pacific (including Greater China), candidates with legal, regulatory, compliance or other investigatory experience, who can function in a compliance or legal arena, are being sought. Work with global team members located in New York, Singapore, Shanghai, Tokyo, Sydney, Bengaluru, Dallas and London. Advisory and Compliance Oversight. Providing advice to the GIR business, including senior management, on regulatory and reputational related matters, and acting as a resource on a daily basis for compliance and policy issues. Policies and Procedures. Ensuring that GIR has appropriate policies and procedures in place. Assessing, designing and driving completion of necessary upgrades, updates and new policies. Working closely with Legal and GIR to fulfill requirements. Regulatory Matters. Helping to manage GIR’s responses to requests for information from regulators, keeping apprised of latest regulatory developments as it relates to GIR. Training. Designing, executing and delivering training on policies and procedures. Controls/Surveillance/Testing. Creating controls, rolling out surveillance and monitoring of policies and procedures. SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE WE ARE LOOKING FOR PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Legal/Regulatory background Fluency in English and Chinese (reading, speaking and writing) as the role involves dealing with jurisdictions across Asia Pacific, including Greater China, products and regulations. Strong oral and written communication skills; ability to communicate confidently and effectively with a diverse audience Quick learner who can adapt well to a fluid regulatory and business environment Ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously, and to work effectively in a team Organized, detail-oriented, enthusiastic and professional ABOUT GOLDMAN SACHS At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world. We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers . We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Learn more: https://www.goldmansachs.com/careers/footer/disability-statement.html © The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., 2026. All rights reserved. Goldman Sachs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, veterans status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

title: Senior Manager, Learning & Development
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION MINDS has been caring for Persons with Intellectual Disabilities (PWIDs) and their families since 1962. Grounded in the belief that all PWIDs have innate abilities and talents, MINDS is committed to empowering them and enhancing their quality of life, while advocating for greater inclusivity for PWIDs as a nation. MINDS is today one of the largest charities in Singapore employing more than 1000 staff. With the key focus to expand community-based services, improving the employability of PWIDS, strengthening education, and engendering inclusive community living, MINDS offers a holistic range of services and programmes across the PWIDs' lifespan. These include schools, employment and training development centres, home-based care services and community-based services to cater to the physical, psychological, environmental and social needs of PWIDs. For more information, please visit www.minds.org.sg. RESPONSIBILITIES The Senior Manager, Learning & Development is responsible for establishing the organisation's learning and development strategy and recommending organisational design and/or change interventions to deliver business strategies and plans effectively. He/she collate and monitor the budgets for learning and development programmes to build capabilities in the organisation. He/she influences stakeholders to redesign the organisational model and spearheads organisational change interventions to drive the integration of Competencies / Skills Frameworks across the organisation's learning programmes. He/she develops and oversees talent development programme to support succession planning, career progression, and employee engagement. He/she builds leadership capability across all levels through Integrating leadership frameworks (e.g. Clifton Strengths, Serving Leader) into development pathways. Job Responsibilities Conduct learning needs analysis to assess their current capabilities and areas of development required to deliver against the organisation's strategy, business restructuring and future plans. Develop customised learning and development strategies, plans and curricula to improve overall workforce capabilities for current and future roles, and to promote lifelong learning. Engage with senior management / HODs to seek clarity on business strategy and develop organisational learning and development strategies based on organisation's current and future business and skill requirements. Compile and monitor budget for learning and development and prioritise spending to invest on strategic organisational capabilities to enable current and future business strategy and objectives. Monitor and assess the effectiveness and business impact of learning and development strategy and programmes to meet current and future business needs. Apply data governance concepts and principles to identify, collect and prepare data for analytics and HR metrics benchmarking. Look across a series of data and anticipate implications of business activity on HR practices. Review and develop HR policies, practices or strategies that competitively position organisation as an employer of choice. Develop communication material/ information kits for the HR programmes/ activities to convey desired learning and development messages clearly and effectively. Create high impact, consistent and accurate messaging using appropriate tools to drive staff learning and development, aligned with the organisation-wide communications strategy. Understand organisational and individual challenges, help the senior leaders to define the problem and identify root cause of the development related issues Leverage Skills Maps of the Skills Frameworks to identify learning gaps and develop competency framework. Create and align learning roadmaps and programmes with reference to MINDS Career Maps and MINDS Competency Framework. Establish Learning & Development strategy in accordance with MINDS Competency Framework components. QUALIFICATIONS Degree in Human Resource Management or Business Administration or equivalent HR Professional Certification OTHER INFORMATION At least 10 years of relevant working experience, with at least 5 years in a middle to senior L&D role. Strong leadership and supervisory skills, with exemplary personal conduct and professionalism. Excellent communication, negotiation and interpersonal skills both written and verbal. Coaching, System thinking, Leadership Development background Should preferably have experience in HR transformation projects leading to excellence in organisation culture or learning & development or HR practices. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - MINDSG LTD

title: Examination Preparation Coordinator (Primary School)
description: Job Description – Examination Preparation Coordinators for English, Mathematics andScience Singapore International School Dili (SIS) Timor Leste Position Overview Singapore International School Dili (SIS) is an international primary school utilising the Singapore curriculum and located in the capital city of Timor-Leste. Having successfully completed our first year of operations, SIS has achieved its initial student enrolment goals and is now entering the next phase of growth and academic strengthening. As the school prepares its students for the International Primary School Leaving Examination (iPSLE) in Grade 6, SIS is establishing a dedicated Examination Preparation Department to systematically raise academic readiness and ensure students are well prepared for a range of examinations (not limited to iPSLE alone) that may be required by parents in the future depending on each child’s pathway and needs. We are therefore seeking experienced and enthusiastic educators from Singapore with a strong tuition and examination-focused background, who are deeply familiar with the Singapore curriculum and assessment standards, to join this pioneering department. This role is ideal for educators who have at least three years of tuition experience, are highly familiar with the Singapore syllabus, and are passionate about helping students achieve examination confidence and mastery. SIS is moving beyond conventional hiring based solely on formal teaching qualifications and is instead looking for educators with strong practical experience in preparing students for academic assessments. The Examination Preparation Department will work closely with the Academic Planning Team, classroom teachers, and the principal to realise the SIS educational philosophy: “Allowing your child to study at SIS will offer parents who are undecided today the luxury of choice to make the best decision for that child in the future.” Being part of this department involves: Designing structured exam-focused lesson plans aligned to the Singapore syllabus and focused on the three core principles of focus, content readiness and exam familiarisation. Working alongside subject teachers to strengthen students’ foundational understanding. Developing revision frameworks, practice systems, and assessment strategies. Establish and manage the school’s examination invigilation premises in full compliance with the guidelines and requirements set by the respective examination boards. Tracking student progress closely and providing targeted academic interventions. Preparing students not only for iPSLE, but for other examinations as required by parents’     future plans for their children. Cultural sensitivity, adaptability, patience, and creativity remain important, given the international context and diverse student backgrounds. Above all, we are looking for educators who are passionate about academic excellence and who believe in giving students strong foundations that keep future options open. DO NOTE THAT THIS IS A OVERSEAS ASSIGNMENT AND THE CANDIDATE IS REQUIRED TO RELOCATE TO TIMOR LESTE. Having said that, this is a rare opportunity to be part of a growing Singapore-standard school overseas, and to directly shape how students in Timor-Leste are prepared for international academic pathways. Reporting Accountability The Examination Preparation Coordinator reports directly to the principal of SIS Dili. Terms of Employment Fixed Term (number of days per year) Salary & Benefits An expatriate package which includes flight, accommodation and other allowances to be provided on top of a base salary. Qualifications and Experience Possess at least three years of teaching experience in a tuition center that delivers the Singapore primary school curriculum. Hold a minimum of a Diploma or Bachelor’s degree from a recognised institution. Teaching or curriculum development experience in English, Math and Science. Good interpersonal skills. Able to interact and work with diversity. Strong command of English. Below is the YouTube link to our school's interview by MediaCorp Channel 8: https://youtu.be/2kC4ivv7x8k?si=ygJbTayqiNeflzwc (The interview is in mandarin)

Beyond fostering social, emotional, and intellectual growth through structured activities like arts, STEM, life skills, and wellness initiatives, Glyph Community now also focuses on uplifting the living standards of the families we serve. Role Overview We are hiring a Digital Fundraising Executive (Contract) to grow our online fundraising and donor engagement efforts starting January 2026. You will plan and manage digital campaigns, apply for fundraising grants, and support the charity in meeting its fundraising goals. Roles and Responsibilities Plan and run digital fundraising campaigns on social media, crowdfunding sites, and email independently Write and design fundraising content to attract and retain donors Apply for relevant fundraising grants and assist in donor reporting Track and report campaign and donor data using Excel or other tools Support other fundraising or administrative tasks as assigned Key Performance Indicators Growth in total online donations and number of active donors Number of campaigns and grant applications completed on time Accuracy and timeliness of reporting and documentation Exact metrics to be aligned on if shortlisted for interview Requirements Diploma or Degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field Non-degree holders with relevant fundraising experience and demonstrated proficiencies will be considered 1 to 3 years of experience in digital marketing, fundraising and/or content creation Strong  writing and communication skills Proficient in design tools (Adobe PS / AI, Canva, Davinci Resolve, Capcut), social media tools (meta business suite), Microsoft Office (Words, Excel, PowerPoint) and CRM (HubSpot). title: Assistant Professor/Associate Professor/Full Professor of Philosophy
description: The School of Humanities invites outstanding academics to apply for faculty positions in philosophy at the rank of Assistant Professor, Associate Professor, or Full Professor. Promoting the strategic development of NTU Philosophy, we are hiring for multiple positions. The rank and areas of specialization are open for all positions. NTU’s philosophy programme offers a Major and Minor in Philosophy. It is a postgraduate degree programme, with both MA and PhD students, and our degree programmes have a strong record of job placement. NTU philosophy faculty have particular strengths in ethics, Chinese philosophy, and philosophy of science. We welcome applicants who are working in the programme’s strengths, and we aim to develop new research strengths. We also welcome applicants who are working in other areas such as metaphysics, epistemology, philosophy of mind, philosophy of language, history of philosophy, political philosophy, philosophy of technology, and digital humanities. More information can be found in https://www.ntu.edu.sg/soh/about-us/philosophy/faculty-members . The successful applicants are expected to engage actively in research, teaching, and service in the School of Humanities. A relevant PhD A record of research excellence A demonstrated commitment to teaching excellence at undergraduate and postgraduate levels Prior teaching experience is preferred, with evidence of effective pedagogy and student mentorship Young and research intensive, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore) is ranked among the world’s top universities. The School of Humanities (SoH) at NTU Singapore is committed to promoting high quality research and teaching. Students are given ample opportunities to develop strong critical thinking, research, and communication skills that enable them to make meaningful contributions to Singapore and beyond. The School is committed to both disciplinary excellence and interdisciplinary innovation and has particular research and teaching interests in Digital Humanities, Medical Humanities, Environmental Humanities and Global Asian studies. The closing date for application is 30 September 2025. Applicants are invited to submit their applications (cover letter, CV, research and teaching statements, and Google Scholar citation report if available) through the NTU career portal: https://ntu.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/Careers/job/NTU-Main-Campus-Singapore/Assistant-Professor-Associate-Professor-Full-Professor-of-Philosophy_R00021345 Only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Enquiries about the position may be addressed to: Chair, Philosophy Search Committee Email: philosophy-search@ntu.edu.sg

title: Trainer / Lecturer - IT & Digital Skills (Institute Higher Learning)
description: Stag Match® Institute is a forward-thinking educational provider in Singapore, committed to equipping adult learners with in-demand skills through high-quality, industry-relevant programs. We deliver a wide range of courses aligned with the needs of today's workforce and the evolving digital landscape. Position Overview: We are seeking dedicated and experienced Trainers/Lecturers (Full-Time or Adjunct) to join our team and deliver training within our Institute of Higher Learning (IHL)-level programs. You will facilitate engaging, practice-oriented sessions in a variety of IT domains for adult learners, supporting their upskilling, reskilling, and career advancement goals. Key Responsibilities: Design and deliver structured training sessions in IT disciplines such as: UX/UI Design Product Management Full-Stack Development Data Analytics Cloud Computing New Media & Digital Marketing AI Agentic Ai Generalist Develop and maintain course materials, including lesson plans, slide decks, assessments, and practical projects. Conduct learner assessments, provide constructive feedback, and support learning outcomes. Facilitate an interactive and inclusive adult learning environment, both in theory and application. Stay updated on current industry practices, tools, and technologies to ensure training content remains relevant and industry-aligned. Collaborate with program coordinators and internal teams to ensure training delivery excellence and learner satisfaction. Engage with stakeholders to align course content with industry and workforce development needs. Participate in course evaluation and continuous improvement processes. Requirements : Bachelor’s Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Digital Marketing, or a related field. Minimum 2 years of experience in adult training, education, or professional development, preferably within IHL or CET contexts. Solid industry knowledge and hands-on experience in one or more IT subject areas listed above. Strong facilitation, presentation, and classroom management skills. Familiarity with adult learning principles, online/blended learning tools, and outcome-based education methodologies. Ability to work independently and collaboratively with a cross-functional team. Willingness to commit a minimum of 10 working days over the course duration. Job Types: Full-time, Part-time, Permanent, Contract, Freelance

title: Lecturer in Linguistics and Multilingual Studies
description: Young and research intensive, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore) is ranked among the world’s top universities. The School of Humanities (SoH) at NTU Singapore is committed to promoting high quality research and teaching. Students are given ample opportunities to develop strong critical thinking, research, and communication skills that enable them to make meaningful contributions to Singapore and beyond. The School is committed to both disciplinary excellence and interdisciplinary innovation and has particular research and teaching interests in Digital Humanities, Medical Humanities, Environmental Humanities and Global Asian studies. Lecturer in Linguistics and Multilingual Studies (LMS) The School of Humanities invites outstanding academics to apply for faculty positions at the rank of Lecturer. The Division of Linguistics and Multilingual Studies invites applications for a full-time lecturer position. The appointment will be on an initial 3-year contract, with possibility of renewal and promotion. Candidates must have a Ph.D. in Linguistics, and possess relevant teaching experience at both the undergraduate and postgraduate level. Candidates should specialise in one or more core areas of linguistics including, but not limited to phonetics/phonology, morphology/syntax, semantics/pragmatics, sociolinguistics, and psycholinguistics. The teaching load for a lectureship is 3-4 courses per semester. The appointed candidate is expected to coordinate and teach a range of core and elective courses in the LMS undergraduate curriculum, as well as the Masters of Arts in Linguistics (MALing) Program. The candidate will also be expected to supervise final year undergraduate theses. While this is a teaching position, a demonstrated commitment and track record of research excellence will be an advantage. Application Procedure Applicants are invited to submit their applications (cover letter, CV, research and teaching statements, and Google Scholar citation report if available) through the NTU career portal: https://ntu.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/Careers/job/NTU-Main-Campus-Singapore/Lecturer-in-Linguistics-and-Multilingual-Studies_R00021403. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Enquiries about the position may be addressed to: LMS Search Committee Chair Email: lms-search@ntu.edu.sg

title: Youth Worker (TOUCH Youth Enablement @ ITE Colleges) - 2 Years Contract
description: Job Summary: The Youth Worker is responsible for planning, designing, conducting and evaluating impactful programs to enhance youths’ social-emotional competencies and prepare them for future education and careers opportunities. This role includes mentoring youth, fostering community partnerships, curriculum and programme development, and overseeing day to day operations to create a supportive environment for youth development. Principal Responsibilities and Duties: 1. Youth Engagement Support and manage centre operations and activities that will appeal to, and enhance participation of youths. Implement outreach efforts to promote availability and utilization of services. Develop positive and trustworthy relationships with the youths. 2. Programme Development, Management and Implementation Develop, design, lead and conduct programmes in Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) and/or school programmes using evidence-informed frameworks and content that promote holistic youth development. Deliver programmes independently and motivate youths in the programme. Assist in the evaluation and measurement of programme effectiveness and impact and identify areas of improvement. Conduct ongoing evaluation to assess and enhance programme effectiveness. Provide cross-service training and facilitator support whenever necessary. 3. Youth Mentoring Identification of developmental needs, mentoring goals of youth. Use of appropriate mentoring and counselling approaches (in adherence to the Code of Professional Ethics) to guide and support youths, including those with special needs. Equip youths with social-emotional competencies and work readiness skills (e.g., resume writing, interview skills, stress management) to thrive in their internship. Work closely with social work team to provide integrative social work support to youths. 4. Volunteer Management / Community and Industry Partnerships Collaborate with community partners in schools, social service agencies and caregivers to assess the needs of youths and provide a strong social safety net. Manage and build relationship with stakeholders such as industry partners, employers and schools to achieve holistic support for the youths. Recruit, train, deploy and manage volunteers for programmes effectively matching roles with programme objectives, providing feedback for improvements, and ensuring alignment with programme goals. Working Conditions: 5 day work week: Mon-Fri Expected to work some Saturdays should programme fall on weekends Location: ITE Colleges Essential Skills and Qualifications: Degree in any discipline Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience in youth work, counselling, psychology or social work, with some background in programme design and programme delivery. Experience working with youths, especially those with special needs, is an advantage. Strong ability to demonstrate empathy and care for others, communicate effectively to build a strong rapport. Ability to collaborate effectively with team members, sharing information and contributing to collective goals. Demonstrates good organisational and administrative abilities. Demonstrates resourcefulness and proactive approach to problem-solving. Flexible and adaptable to varying situations. Exhibits personal effectiveness, independence and good time management skills. Proficient in participating actively in group discussions and contributing to effective programme outcomes.

title: Full Professor (Tenured) in Environment and Society
description: Asian School of the Environment Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Full Professor in Environment and Society The Asian School of the Environment (ASE) ( https://www.ntu.edu.sg/ase ) is an interdisciplinary school at Nanyang Technological University (NTU) that uses Earth and environmental science, engineering, technology, and the social sciences to address key environmental challenges. Many ASE researchers work closely with the Earth Observatory of Singapore ( https://earthobservatory.sg/ ), and/or Singapore Centre for Environmental Life Sciences Engineering ( https://scelse.sg/ ), and/or Centre for Climate Change and Environmental Health, CCEH ( https://www.ntu.edu.sg/cceh ). The Asian School of the Environment (ASE), College of Science (CoS) at the Nanyang Technological University (NTU) in Singapore seeks to appoint an accomplished and visionary scientist as Full Professor in Environment and Society. We welcome applicants with research interests including but not limited to environmental governance and decision-making, political ecology, environmental or ecological economics, risk and resilience in socio-environmental systems, and coupled human–environment systems. Candidates in relevant fields may also be considered for additional appointment at the Earth Observatory of Singapore (EOS). Qualities and Leadership Candidates should have a strong track record of impactful, interdisciplinary research, demonstrated leadership in securing major grants and building collaborative research networks, supervising graduate students, experience mentoring junior faculty, and contributions to institutional or programmatic development, especially within interdisciplinary environments. The successful candidate will have opportunities and will be expected to convene and inspire diverse teams, fostering collaborations across disciplines and institutions to develop, pursue, and lead large-scale, high-impact research initiatives that address pressing environmental and societal challenges. Applicants must have a PhD in a relevant field and would normally already hold a tenured Professorial position in a university (or equivalent institution). The successful candidate will: • Build and lead a pioneering research programme in environment and society • Convene and lead large collaborative projects and initiatives at regional and global scales • Teach at undergraduate and postgraduate levels in ASE • Contribute to mentoring and academic leadership within NTU • Develop collaborations with researchers across NTU, Singapore, and internationally ASE and CoS seeks a diverse and inclusive workforce and is committed to equality of opportunity. We welcome applications from all and recruit based on merit, regardless of age, race, gender, religion, marital status and family responsibilities, or disability. Emoluments The commencing salary is competitive and will be commensurate with the candidate's qualifications and experience. The University offers a comprehensive fringe benefit package. Application Procedure Interested applicants are invited to submit through the NTU career portal a completed application package which include: 1. Cover letter describing experience and interests in research, teaching, and leadership. 2. A full Curriculum vitae 3. Publication list 4. A summary of the future research, teaching and service plans, and 5. The names/emails of four individuals willing to provide letters of reference. References will only be contacted if you are shortlisted for the position. All applications and materials submitted will be held in strict confidence. The Search Committee will begin reviewing applications on 3 November 2025. For further enquiries, please contact Search Committee Chair, Professor Steve Yim ( steve.yim@ntu.edu.sg ) Please click here to apply.

title: Senior Research Fellow (Climate Finance)
description: Nanyang Business School (NBS) invites applications for the position of Senior Research Fellow (Post-doc) with Prof. Xin Chang’s research group. This position is supported by a Tier III grant to establish a Climate Transformation Programme (CTP) at NTU. As part of this programme, the Climate Finance Cluster will assess the multifaceted business risks posed by the physical impacts of climate change, evaluate the implications of carbon pricing and public procurement, and facilitate the transition to a low-carbon economy. The Senior Research Fellow will be responsible for developing integrated approaches to assess company-level climate risks, assembling datasets on mandatory carbon pricing and government procurement, and incorporating these resources into empirical analyses of financial markets and economic outcomes. The researcher will contribute to advancing how climate risks are measured, priced, and managed by companies and investors, and will see this work through to publication in major research journals. Duties and Responsibilities: Lead the development of company-level climate risk measures and related analytical frameworks, including the construction of databases, regional climate-risk summaries, and empirical models that incorporate climate risk into asset-pricing applications. Quantify the economic and financial impacts of mandatory carbon pricing and government procurement policies on firms and markets. Perform data management, large-scale statistical analyses, and ensure high standards of documentation and reproducibility. Work effectively with colleagues across the CTP and with local and international collaborators. Mentor junior colleagues and students. Write, present, and publish research findings in peer-reviewed journals. Knowledge and Experience Requirements: PhD degree in statistics, computer sciences, economics, or a closely related field. Excellent knowledge of computational linguistic tools and machine learning techniques. Experience with data analysis with Python and STATA. Prior experience in publishing in peer-reviewed business, economic, or scientific journals Excellent oral and written communication skills in English. Key Competency Requirements: Ability to work independently to develop research in challenging environments. Collaborative, with an interest in working with other researchers and scientists (this is a large team project) to produce high-quality and impactful research. Interest in climate, green, or sustainable finance. Excellent oral and written communication skills. Interested applicants are invited to submit a cover letter, full CV and names and contact details of three referees in one document. Enquiries about this position can be made to Nur Masturah (nurmasturah.am@ntu.edu.sg). We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Manager/Assistant Senior Manager (Experiential Learning), Undergraduate Studies Office
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description: About NUS Business School Founded in the same year that Singapore gained independence, NUS Business School stands today among the world’s leading business schools. It is distinctive for offering the best of global business knowledge with deep Asian insights, preparing students to lead Asian businesses to international success and to help global businesses succeed in Asia. For more than 50 years, NUS Business School has offered a rigorous, relevant and rewarding business education to outstanding students from across the world. That would not have been possible without a group of dedicated professionals who are passionate about building a positive environment to grow and groom future business leaders. And we are looking to add to our BIZ family! To learn more about the NUS Business School, please visit https://bschool.nus.edu.sg/ Job Description As part of the Student Life and Experiential Learning team, the Manager/Assistant Senior Manager will manage the Experiential Learning components of the NUS BBA, with primary responsibility for the Field Service Projects (FSP), a credit-bearing graduation requirement that integrates academic learning with real-world business problem-solving. This role provides end-to-end leadership of the FSP ecosystem, working closely with faculty, industry partners, students, and internal stakeholders to ensure academic rigor, operational excellence, and a high-quality student experience. At the same time, this role will also work closely with the Team Lead to oversee that Business Internships (BI), a key element of the Work Experience Milestone for NUS BBA students. Programme Ownership and Strategy Provide overall stewardship of Field Service Projects (FSP), including special series FSP with distinct objectives, as a core graduation requirement of the NUS BBA. Review and enhance FSP policies, frameworks, and processes to ensure alignment with programme learning outcomes and evolving industry expectations. Contribute to broader work experience and experiential learning strategy within the undergraduate curriculum. Industry and Faculty Engagement Develop, manage, and sustain relationships with industry partners to source meaningful, high-impact FSP projects. Work closely with faculty advisors to support project scoping, academic supervision, assessment alignment, and quality assurance. Serve as the key liaison between external partners, faculty, and the School. Student Experience and Delivery Oversee student lifecycle for FSPs, including project matching, briefings, timelines, issue management, and completion. Ensure consistent and transparent communication to students on expectations, requirements, and milestones. Manage student-related escalations and provide sound judgment on complex or exceptional cases. Operations and Governance Lead and execute end-to-end operational planning and execution of FSP cycles, including schedules, project approvals, grading liaison, and graduation clearance. Ensure compliance with University and School policies, assessment standards, and audit requirements. Analyse programme data and feedback to inform continuous improvement. Stakeholder and Team Collaboration Work closely with colleagues across the Undergraduate Studies Office, Career Services (BIZCareers), faculty teams, and central units. Where applicable, supervise or guide junior staff supporting experiential learning administration and delivery. Prepare management updates, reports, and recommendations for senior leadership. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree required; postgraduate qualification is an advantage. At least 5–8 years of relevant experience in higher education administration, programme management, or experiential learning initiatives. Experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder programmes is highly desirable. Strong programme management and organisational skills, with attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple timelines. Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, including confidence working with faculty and industry partners. Sound judgment, maturity, and discretion in handling student and academic matters. Comfortable working in a structured yet evolving academic environment. Data-informed, improvement-oriented mindset. (Appointment job grade will commensurate with the selected candidate's experience)

title: Job Coach
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION MINDS has been caring for Persons with Intellectual Disabilities (PWIDs) and their families since 1962. Grounded in the belief that all PWIDs have innate abilities and talents, MINDS is committed to empowering them and enhancing their quality of life, while advocating for greater inclusivity for PWIDs as a nation. MINDS is today one of the largest charities in Singapore employing more than 850 staff. With the key focus to expand community-based services, improving the employability of PWIDS, strengthening education, and engendering inclusive community living, MINDS offers a holistic range of services and programmes across the PWIDs' lifespan. These include schools, employment and training development centres, home-based care services and community-based services to cater to the physical, psychological, environmental and social needs of PWIDs. For more information, please visit www.minds.org.sg. RESPONSIBILITIES Work closely with the Teachers and AHPs to prepare and assess student's readiness for job attachments and job placements. Identify and provide support to develop the students' confidence and interest in the job before job attachments. Source and arrange for job attachment opportunities for students. (School based, community based and industrial based). Provide job matching for students. Analyse and evaluate the suitability of the job site. Prepare the students for job attachments. Negotiate a set of employment terms with the employer after the attachment if applicable. Provide continued support and guidance to the students on the job. Work closely with families, teachers, community partners and employers in supporting students during the job attachment period and to be work ready. Work closely with other school staff to support and improve vocational curriculum and resources. Work with teachers and staff to contribute and support students IEP/ITP. • Conduct training/sharing with school staff on trends used to support students at the worksite. To develop training plans and materials to conduct in house training for students to prepare them for work, with accommodations and adaptations where necessary. (eg: Travel training, interview skills, grooming skills etc in collaboration with other school staff) To conduct pre- and post-vocational assessments of the students. Submit, upkeep reports and records related to attachment and job interviews. • Plan, carry out and monitor attachment in school, when attachment out of school is not available. Plan and co-ordinate work exposure activities such as learning journeys and Job shadowing for students. Work with TPC and post school agency/employer to ensure smooth transition into School-to-Work transition programme and post school services, including EDC and the MATM process. Support school programme to enhance students' holistic learning experience. Educate, communicate & work collaboratively with students' caregivers/case workers & employers to jointly support & sustain students' work attachment and experience. Collaborate with other departments (such as the Employment Development Centres, MINDS Institute, Caregiver Support Services, Me-Too Club, Strategic Development) to provide holistic & other positive student outcomes such as caregiver support, self-advocacy, peer support group & socio-recreational opportunities & student success stories to empower & engage student QUALIFICATIONS Diploma or Degree in any discipline with at least 2 years working experience. OTHER INFORMATION A strong team-player with excellent people skills. A self-motivated and independent individual. Willing to think out of the box when working with people with intellectual disabilities. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - MINDSG LTD

title: Programmes Executive
description: The Programmes Executive plays a crucial role in supporting Habitat for Humanity Singapore’s  programmes, both locally and overseas.  This individual should be passionate about working with vulnerable individuals and families, as well as relating to volunteers. As an entry-level executive, you will be instrumental in the successful management and execution of local projects, including assessing homeowner needs, evaluating living spaces and rehabilitation works required. From there, you will take charge of coordinating and facilitating a range of rehabilitation works from engaging professional services such as pest control and furniture, to working with volunteers on the ground with painting, intensive cleaning and home organisation. You won’t be alone in this effort; You’ll collaborate closely with a dedicated team of staff and volunteers to ensure smooth and efficient service delivery. Beyond local initiatives, a portion of your role will also involve contributing to managing overseas projects for the Global Village programme. Job Scope: Local Programmes Project Management · Plan and coordinate projects, ensuring they align with established timelines and budgets. · Managing project logistics and operations for all local projects such as Project HomeWorks, UnLitter Red Dor and other future local projects/events · Liaising and communicating with referring partners and social workers from various agencies (Family Service Centres, Active Ageing Centres, Hospitals, etc), to assist the beneficiaries and making sure that there is a long-term plan in place for a sustainable change and providing safe and sanitary home environment. · Documenting project details and expenses in a timely and accurate manner. · Work closely with programme teams and volunteers to ensure successful project implementation. This may also involve volunteer recruitment, briefing and training. · Prepare and manage logistics required for the project For Project HomeWorks specifically: · Evaluate the conditions of the homes (clutter, pest infestation, cleanliness, and willingness of the homeowner) and rehabilitation needs through joint home visits with referring partner. · Work with referring partners and social workers to create solutions through discussion and agreements on works to be done to achieve safe and sanitary living conditions. · Work with referring partners and social workers to evaluate the progress and the project completion. Overseas Programmes Project Management · Serve as primary liaison with Habitat partners, managing communications regarding proposals, volunteer itineraries and budgets, and all necessary documentation in accordance with standard operating procedures. · Oversee timely submission of reports to Habitat regional and international counterparts. · Travel overseas as needed to provide on-site oversight and audit project progress. Volunteer / Homepartner Management · Maintain accurate and timely records in our Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and other internal systems, ensuring data is readily available for reporting and analysis. · Manage and maintain organised documentation digitally. · Schedule and coordinate volunteer programmes, leading team briefings and presentations as needed. · Gather and analyse feedback to continuously improve internal processes · Effectively manage emergency situations should they arise. A successful candidate would be · Possesses a diploma from an accredited institution · Possesses strong organisational skills with meticulous attention to detail · Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to effectively engage beneficiaries, volunteers and partners · A self-motivated and mature individual who thrives in a team environment and demonstrates strong initiative · Maintains a reasonable level of physical fitness as the role may involve some physically demanding tasks (Eg. packing and moving logistics) · Proficient in Microsoft Office applications and email communication · Fluent in written and spoken English · The job involves interaction with beneficiaries who may only speak Mandarin, Malay, Tamil or dialects. Knowledge of any of such basic conversational language is preferred. · Possesses a valid Class 3/3A driving license · Available to work on some weekends (1-2 Saturdays per month) · A passion for our charity’s mission and a genuine desire to make a positive impact. · Comfortable to uphold, communicate and affirm Habitat for Humanity’s principles (vision and mission): pls refer to https://www.habitat.org.sg/about-us for details. If you are interested to join us and be part of our exciting and meaningful ministry, please email us your complete curriculum vitae. Do include a cover letter with your salary expectations and email it to hr@habitat.org.sg . Only short-listed candidates will be contacted for an interview. Habitat's Expectations Surrounding Safeguarding: Habitat for Humanity requires that all employees take seriously their ethical responsibilities to safeguarding our intended beneficiaries, their communities, and all those with whom we work. Managers at all levels have particular responsibilities to support and develop systems that create and maintain an environment that prevents harassment, sexual exploitation and abuse; safeguards the rights of beneficiaries and community members (especially children); and promotes the implementation of Habitat for Humanity's code of conduct.

title: Pass-It-On Executive (Part-Time, Contract)
description: Pass-It-On (PIO) is a project initiated by Central Singapore CDC and managed by The Helping Hand. The project provides a meaningful avenue for the redistribution of unwanted yet usable items to individuals and families in need—giving items a second life. PIO operates via a web-based platform that connects members of the public who wish to donate items with social workers from Social Service Agencies (SSAs) supporting beneficiaries. The PIO Executive plays a role in ensuring the effective operation and impact of the PIO platform. Responsibilities: Oversee the day-to-day operations of the PIO platform, ensuring smooth functionality and positive user experience. Drive increased usage of the PIO platform and improve match rates between donors and social workers. Respond to and manage enquiries from the public and social workers registered on the platform. Support in monitoring project KPIs and prepare reports required by CDC. Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders to support project objectives and continuous improvement. Skills: Good written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage diverse stakeholders effectively. Proactive, organized, and collaborative team player who is also able to work independently. Familiarity with Microsoft Office applications. Prior experience working in the social service sector will be an advantage.

title: Customer Service Officer (West)
description: At Mavis Tuition Centre, we are committed to providing top-quality educational services to help students excel academically. We offer a comprehensive range of subjects, ensuring a well-rounded and thorough education.
