Join us at Mavis Tuition Centre, where our exceptional services turn the journey of learning into an exciting and rewarding experience! Job Highlights 5-day work week Leave benefits Medical benefits and insurance Variable bonuses Performance incentives Responsibilities Engage customers in a friendly and approachable manner Assist customers with their queries and concerns with a service-oriented attitude Actively promote the organisation’s programmes to prospective clients in a professional manner Perform administrative duties pertaining to student and course registration matters Requirements Minimum ‘N’ Level Able to commit to weekends Travelling required Strong interpersonal and communication skills Positive work attitude, team player with strong service mindset Work in a fast-paced environment, able to multitask Why choose us? Supportive Peers Recognition & Rewards Room for Growth & Career Advancement Opportunities Comprehensive Training Program tailored to enhance skill set

title: Events Specialist (Members Engagement), Member & Corporate Relations
description: The Singapore National Employers Federation (SNEF) is the national trade union of employers, representing more than 3,900 employers and the interests of all sectors of the economy. In the tripartite system, SNEF is the body representing employers and it aims to be the trusted partner of the National Trades Union Congress representing the labour movement, and the Ministry of Manpower representing the Government. SNEF’s vision is to help organisations achieve sustainable growth that is supported by responsible employment practices. Therefore, the federation aims to strengthen industrial and labour relations through advancing tripartism and responsible employment practices. Industrial peace will, in turn, help organisations achieve business sustainability. The Event Specialist, Members Engagement will play a pivotal role in engaging SNEF’s membership base and partners. This function is responsible for organising events, meetings and conferences, to attract new members, driving engagement with key decision-makers, partners and organisations. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced B2B environment, enjoys interacting with senior management, and demonstrates strong ownership of projects and budgets. This is a 2-year contract role with provision of extension yearly based on performance. Responsibilities: Plan, coordinate, and execute events end-to-end, including concept development, scheduling, budgeting, logistics, and post-event evaluation. Brainstorm and initiate event ideas to increase outreach to members, partners and organisations. Coordinate with internal teams, vendors, and external partners to ensure smooth event delivery. Track event outcomes and prepare post-event summaries and reports. Be open to working weekends on event days as required (off-in-lieu will be provided) Maintain and administer the CRM system to ensure accurate data entry, data integrity, and regular data cleansing. Manage and build strong relationships with members Take on projects and tasks as assigned by the reporting officer. Requirements: Relevant experience in planning meetings, incentives, conventions, conferences and/or special events. Experience in managing outside vendors, sourcing and negotiating contract services. Highly motivated, results-driven, and resilient with the ability to operate independently while collaborating effectively within a team structure. Ability to communicate effectively both verbally and in writing. Ability to work well under pressure and adapt to ever-changing scenarios Capability of problem solving – Anticipating, initiating and resolving issues Strong relationship-building and communication capabilities, with the confidence to engage and influence external stakeholders at senior levels.

title: Social Worker (Family Service)
description: REACH Family Service is a community based social service agency commissioned and funded by the Ministry of Social and Family Development. We serve vulnerable and low-income families and individuals residing in the Bishan, Sin Ming, Shunfu and Thomson vicinity. We collaborate with clients to harness their strengths and resources as well as to provide the support they need to face challenges and strengthen family bonds. JOIN US if you are a passionate individual, service oriented and capable of working effectively in group settings and committed to exemplify the values of the profession and the agency. The role of the Social Worker is to provide support for low-income and/or vulnerable individuals and families with social and emotional issues, and work towards stability, self-reliance and social mobility. He / She is responsible to develop and implement intervention plans according to professional judgement and knowledge, conduct casework and group work as well as implement and evaluate community development activities and programmes to address service gaps in the community. Key Responsibilities: · Provide casework intervention, counselling and information & referral to families in line with REACH FSC Guidelines and MSF’S Services and Practice Standards for FSC. · Attend to callers/walk-ins and refer clients to appropriate agencies. · Maintain timely and detailed case recordings. · Ensure KPIs and standards of C&C are met. · Participate in supervision sessions and maintain proper recording of supervision, especially for risk cases. · Undertake supervision of interns, if assigned. · Develop, plan, lead, and evaluate evidence-based programmes/ group work with research components. · Network and collaborate with other organization/community stakeholders in planning and implementation of programme/group work. · Undertake additional responsibilities such as committee work for agency initiatives. Requirements · Possess a Degree in Social Work or Graduate Diploma in Social Work · Minimum 2 years of relevant experience · Registered with Social Work Accreditation and Advisory Board · Proficient in English and at least one other language · Strong communication and interpersonal skills · A good team player, dynamic and self-motivated · Deep passion in reaching out and making a meaningful impact in the lives of those in need · Only Singaporeans

title: Marketing Manager
description:
Nanyang Institute of Social Sciences (NISS) is an institute of higher education registered with SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) under the Ministry of Education of Singapore, aiming to create a lifelong learning platform that incorporates quality higher education resources from the East and the West. We are now inviting applications for the position Marketing Manager , responsible for the overall marketing strategy and execution of the School’s academic programmes, leading the marketing team to build and scale the function, and overseeing overall marketing performance. Job Responsibilities: Develop and execute marketing strategies across all social media and online channels. Manage social media and online platforms, optimize advertising campaigns, and improve ROI. Oversee content direction and design style, driving continuous updates and iterations. Explore offline channels to achieve omnichannel coverage. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree or above; majors in Marketing, Advertising, Communications, Business, or related fields preferred. Experience in marketing team management, project management, or the higher education sector is a plus. Strong understanding of social media platforms and digital channels, including
advertising operations. Skilled in a variety of marketing tools and techniques, including graphic design and video editing. Excellent writing and content creation skills, with a keen eye for detail and quality.

title: Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design
description: School of Art, Design and Media College of Computing and Data Science Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design The School of Art, Design and Media (ADM) at Nanyang Technological University Singapore (NTU) invites applications for a one-year Visiting Professor position in Games and/or Animation. ADM is part of the College of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (COHASS). This position is intended for eminent overseas academics such as international prize recipients or distinguished scholars whose presence will enhance the academic standing of the faculty. The primary responsibility of this role is teaching. Therefore, candidates should be able to demonstrate a strong teaching track record in a current or previous Associate Professor academic level position. We seek candidates with expertise in one or more areas of Games and/or Animation, including fields such as game design, interactive media, concept development, animation, and creative technologies. The appointed faculty will play a key role in supporting ADM’s position as a leader in contemporary digital media and design education in the region. Located in multi-cultural Singapore, the School of Art Design Media (ADM) in Nanyang Technological University (NTU) offers a studio-based education in a comprehensive university setting, providing students with the knowledge and skills needed to develop and produce groundbreaking creative works and scholarly activity. ADM also promotes Singapore’s creative culture and identity and is positioned to play a central role in the transformation of Singapore into a regional center of media and creativity. Designed and taught by international faculty drawn from nearly 20 countries, the school offer 4-year BFA programs in Design Art and Media Art, with an adaptive and evolving curriculum building upon excellence in our core disciplines of Animation, Film, Game, Photography, Interaction, Product Design, and Visual Communication, The programs are designed to meet the highest professional and academic standards worldwide and are responsive to student interests and evolving global needs. Post-graduate programs combine theory and practice, offering MA and PhD by research and a MA in Museum and Curatorial Practice. In 2026 ADM will start an innovative MSc Game Design Art & Technology, taught jointly with the College of Computer and Data Sciences (CCDS). ADM ranks amongst the top 5 art and design schools in Asia and top 20 worldwide (QS World University Rankings by subject 2025). NTU is a research-intensive university, home to 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in Engineering, Science, Humanities, Social Science, Business, Education and Medicine. Ranked amongst the world’s top 30 universities by QS, NTU has also been named amongst the world’s top young universities for the past 7 years. Further information about the University and the School can be viewed at the following websites: NTU : www.ntu.edu.sg ADM : www.ntu.edu.sg/adm Applicants must be confident navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of digital media and technology and demonstrate a strong commitment to teaching innovation and student mentorship. The role is teaching-focused; applicants should bring an established record of tertiary-level teaching experience and curricular engagement. As a creative practitioner and educator with expertise in animation, games, or interactive media, candidate will demonstrate: A strong record in tertiary education (A terminal MFA (or its equivalent) or PhD degree in a relevant field is required), with the ability to guide student-led projects from concept to completion across games, animation, visual development, or interactive media. · A recognised practice in animation, games, or related fields, with an impactful portfolio showcasing innovation, storytelling, and design across emerging technologies such as AR/VR, motion graphics, generative AI, or installations. · Evidence of scholarly output in contemporary forums related to games, animation, or creative computing, and a commitment to advancing the discipline through research or creative practice. · A willingness to engage with cultural, ethical, and philosophical dimensions of media practice, and the ability to connect with fields like digital humanities, creative coding, or social platforms. · A passion for education and the ability to foster a supportive, inclusive, and diverse learning environment, with strong interpersonal and organizational skills. · National or international recognition by academic or industry, with a track record of leadership and contribution to the evolution of games, animation, or interactive media. Applicants are invited to submit their applications through the NTU career portal by clicking on the “Apply” button above. Please provide the following in your application: 1. Cover letter; 2. Curriculum vitae (including records of competitive grants and graduate supervision, significant creative works and scholarly publications; if applicable); 3. Teaching statement; 4. Research statement; The closing date for applications is 26 September 2025. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified via email.

title: Research Engineer/Fellow (UX) - JL
description: As a University of Applied Learning, the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) works closely with industry in its research pursuits. This position is situated within the Centre for Immersification, dedicated to applied research in immersive technologies and bridging the gap between academia and industry. Research staff at the Centre have the opportunity to be equipped with applied research skill sets relevant to industry demands while working on innovative projects within the university. The research team is seeking a Research Fellow or Research Engineer with strong expertise in Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) and social science methodologies to lead the user research and policy development, as part of an interdisciplinary team investigating harmful user-generated content in virtual worlds as part of a national research initiative on Online Trust and Safety. The primary responsibility of this role is to investigate users’ experiences with harmful content in these virtual environments, review existing reporting guidelines on these platforms, and evaluate users’ responses to alternative mechanisms. The successful candidate will work with a diverse team of faculty members, engineers, and industry partners, including labs at the College of Computing and Data Science (CCDS) at NTU. Conduct a comprehensive review of literature, existing safety policies, community standards, and moderation tools currently used in online social gaming platforms Design and execute surveys and interviews to collect data (including participant recruitment) to address the relevant research questions. Perform statistical analysis on survey/experimental data, and Conduct thematic analysis on interview transcripts and open-ended survey responses. To lead and report research findings in a form of high-quality publications and present in international peer-reviewed conferences and journals. Develop policy recommendations based on the research findings. Carry out Risk Assessment, and ensure compliance with Work, Safety and Health Regulations. title: (NEW! ) 2-Hours Career Clarity: A Real-World Exploration Workshop
description: Location @ Manulife Tower (Nearest MRT: Telok Ayer Station or Raffles Place) 11 February 2026, Wednesday 7pm to 9pm (2 hours) Refreshments Provided Feeling uncertain about your career direction? Whether you’re stepping into your first job or rethinking your next move, most people face the same question: “How do I choose a career I won’t regret later?” This workshop will be a guided, real-world exploration designed to help you gain clarity before making long-term career decisions. Why Join? Instead of sitting and listening, you’ll take part in guided activities that help you reflect, discuss, and discover: ✅ Explore Career Trade-Offs – Understand the real fears and realities of employed roles vs self-employed careers and which risks you’re personally more comfortable managing. ✅ Real-Life Decision Simulation – Step into real-world scenarios people face and experience how career outcomes (including income and responsibility) are created. ✅ Self-Fit & Lifestyle Reflection – Clarify what kind of work, structure, and lifestyle fits you best at this stage of life. ✅ Behind-the-Scenes Industry Insights – Get an honest look at how modern financial advisory careers actually work — beyond stereotypes. ✅ Enjoy a Refreshing Experience – Engage in meaningful discussions while enjoying complimentary refreshments! Who Should Attend? ✅ Fresh graduates exploring career directions ✅ Working professionals considering a career switch ✅ Individuals who want clarity before committing years to a path Important Information: Registration starts 20 minutes before the event! Please arrive on time. Each participant will receive a $40 cash transport token at the end of the workshop. This is a physical event. No zoom session will be conducted. Participants may register for only ONE session. If you have attended this workshop before, we kindly ask you to allow new participants to join. Thank you for your understanding. Requirements: ✅ 21 years old and above ✅ Fresh Graduates, Part-Time Students, Homemakers looking for job flexibility or Mid-Career Switch ✅ Minimum A Levels, Diploma or Degree Holders Seats are limited! title: Teacher of Mathematics - Senior School
description: Tanglin Mission Tanglin Trust School Singapore has a long tradition of providing British-based learning with an international perspective. At Tanglin we strive to make every individual feel valued, happy and successful. Responsibility, enthusiasm and participation are actively encouraged and integrity is prized. Working together in a safe, caring yet stimulating environment, we set high expectations whilst offering strong support, resulting in a community of lifelong learners who can contribute with confidence to our world. Our Vision We aspire to be the best school in the world with a dynamic learning community which nurtures and inspires every individual to be the best they can be. Our core values of Respect, Responsibility and Purpose are at the heart of everything we do across the school. The Role We require a full-time teacher to work in the Mathematics Faculty who could potentially teach across the full 11 – 18 age range.  In the Sixth Form, the school offers A Level Mathematics, A Level Further Mathematics, and IB Mathematics (Analysis and Approaches and Applications and Interpretations at Higher and Standard levels). Whilst it is not a pre-requisite, experience in either post-16 pathway would be an added advantage. The role reports to Head of Faculty – Mathematics. Key Responsibilities 1. Teaching & Learning Deliver the curriculum to assigned classes in accordance with the School’s timetable. Teach responsively according to the abilities and learning needs of individual students. Support nurturing the attributes of the Tanglin Learner Profile with its emphasis on ‘risk-taking’, ‘independence’ and ‘resilience’ as key features of student development. Promote independent learning and study skills by adopting teaching and learning strategies which develop the competence and confidence of every student. Deliver lessons that inspire and enthuse students to love and enjoy Mathematics. 2. Planning & Development Work with the members of the department to review and develop schemes of work for Years 7 to 13 as appropriate. Plan, jointly with other teachers where appropriate, the long, medium and short term curriculum for each class taught according to those schemes of work. Contribute to the planning and implementation of curricular, cross-curricular and other initiatives according to the school’s calendar. Support a school culture of innovation that includes integration of technology and the on-going development of the ‘digital citizen.’ Keep abreast of current developments in Mathematics education and to assist the Head of Faculty (HOF) and/or Assistant Head of Faculty (AHOF) in adapting curriculum content, methods of teaching and examining as appropriate. Assist the HOF in identifying areas for professional development within the Mathematics department. 3. Management of Resources and the Teaching Environment Assist the HOF/AHOF in identifying appropriate resources within the limits of the departmental budget. Contribute to the resources of the department through writing of assignments, tests, worksheets and other resources as appropriate and making them available to all colleagues. Assist the HOF/AHOF in ensuring that departmental rooms present stimulating environments, which help to influence students’ attitudes positively towards Mathematics. Work on tasks as reasonably directed by the Head of Faculty for the fair distribution of work amongst the department. 4. Assessment, Recording & Reporting Ensure students’ work is regularly assessed according to Maths department policy. Set homework in line with school policy. Report to parents in accordance with the school’s reporting policy as laid down in the staff handbook. Use the information gained from assessment formatively, to inform planning and generate further improvement in pupil achievement. Assist the HOF/AHOF in the setting and marking of school examinations and to supervise entries for external examinations. 5. Pastoral Care and Co-Curricular Responsibilities Perform the role of form tutor as required and fulfil the responsibilities of that position. Contribute to co-curricular activities that broaden pupils’ experiences and contribute to the general life of the school. Follow the school’s policies and procedures with regard to discipline and behaviour, daily routines, duties, homework, leave of absence, and visits, as laid down in the staff handbook. Take part enthusiastically in whole school and senior school themed days. Be responsible for reporting any concerns about a child to the Designated Safeguarding Lead. 6. Communication and Liaison Promote the study of Mathematics amongst students and parents. Attend and contribute to meetings of a professional nature with HOF, Head of Year (HOY), Senior School Leadership Team (SSLT) and in other circumstances when requested. Liaise with the AHOF and HOF in respect of pupil records, rewards and sanctions. Maintain appropriate links with teaching colleagues in all sections of the school and foster cross-curricular links where appropriate. Liaise with members of the learning support team with regard to individual students who may have particular needs, to ensure that those needs are understood, planned for and met. Liaise with pastoral staff, including form tutors, HOYs, the assistant heads of school and the head of careers on matters pertaining to the study of Mathematics. Contributie to the strengthening of the parent-teacher partnership in individual and whole school initiatives as and when required. Personal Specification Proven experience of being able to enthuse and inspire students of all abilities to love and enjoy maths. The ability to teach using a range of teaching and learning styles that capture and hold the attention of inquisitive learners. A passion for getting students to see beyond the artificial barriers that can limit their learning. Excellent communication and team-working skills that allow you to work productively with a department of dedicated and enthusiastic Maths teachers with a range of experiences and backgrounds. The drive to develop creative new approaches to student learning within a collaborative environment. Experience of teaching the British National Curriculum for Maths, I/GCSE Maths and have probably taught either A-level Maths or Maths as part of the International Baccalaureate diploma programme. (Applications from teachers who only wish to teach Key Stage 3 or Key Stage 3 & 4 are welcomed). Experience of integrating technology such as iPads or TI-inspire calculators into their lessons. Proven experience of supportive, caring and informed pastoral guidance of students. Can contribute to the school’s broad range of co-curricular activities.

title: Sales Manager (1 Year Contract)
description: About Singapore Red Cross Academy (SRCA) The Singapore Red Cross Academy is the training arm of the Singapore Red Cross, dedicated to building community resilience by equipping individuals and organisations with essential humanitarian knowledge and skills. We are the region's go-to institute for humanitarian studies, offering a comprehensive suite of courses in First Aid & Resuscitation, Psychosocial Support, and other humanitarian and caregiving programmes. We strive to empower individuals and communities to help save lives. Job Purpose The job incumbent will be responsible for driving the sales performance of the Singapore Red Cross Academy's range of courses and training programmes to corporate clients, government agencies, educational institutions, and the general public. This role requires a dynamic and results-driven individual with a passion for humanitarian work and a proven track record in achieving ambitious sales targets. Remarks: Competitive basic pay plus commission/ incentive based on performance. Key Responsibilities 1. Sales Strategy and Execution: Develop and implement strategic sales plans to achieve and exceed course enrolment and revenue targets for SRCA. Identify new business opportunities and target markets for SRCA's training offerings, particularly within the corporate and institutional sectors. Conduct market research to stay abreast of industry trends, competitor activities, and pricing strategies. Prepare and deliver compelling sales presentations, proposals, and tender submissions to potential clients. 2. Client Relationship Management (CRM): Cultivate and maintain strong, long-term relationships with key corporate accounts and institutional partners. Act as the primary point of contact for large-scale training enquiries and customised corporate training solutions. Ensure high levels of client satisfaction and secure repeat business and referrals. 3. Sales Operations and Reporting: Manage the end-to-end sales cycle, from lead generation and qualification to negotiation and closing. Maintain accurate records of sales activities, pipelines, and forecasts using the CRM system. Prepare regular sales reports and dashboards for management, detailing performance against targets and key metrics. Work closely with the Operations and Marketing teams to ensure seamless course delivery and effective promotional campaigns. 4. Product Knowledge and Advocacy: Maintain a deep understanding of all SRCA courses, accreditations (e.g., SRFAC, MOM), and subsidy schemes (e.g., SkillsFuture). Act as an ambassador for the Singapore Red Cross humanitarian mission and the value of its training programmes. 5. Team Collaboration (if applicable to team structure): Mentor and provide guidance to junior sales or account management staff, fostering a high-performance sales culture. Collaborate with the Curriculum Development team to communicate market needs and client feedback for continuous course improvement. Job Requirements Qualifications and Experience: Minimum Diploma or Degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field. Minimum 3 years of relevant sales experience. Experience in the training and education sector is a plus. Proven track record of consistently meeting or exceeding sales quotas. Skills and Knowledge: Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills. Strong business acumen and ability to understand client training needs. Proficiency in using CRM software and Microsoft Office Suite. Knowledge of Singapore's training landscape, including government grants and accreditation bodies, is highly advantageous. Demonstrates a high level of initiative, self-motivation, and integrity. Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team.

title: Student Recruitment Manager (China Market)
description: Role Overview The Student Recruitment Manager (China Market) is responsible for leading and driving student recruitment strategies across Mainland China for SagePaths and its partner institutions. This role oversees market development, recruitment operations, and team performance to ensure sustainable growth in student enrolment for higher education pathways, undergraduate, and postgraduate programmes. You will design and execute market-specific recruitment strategies, manage a high-performing recruitment team, strengthen institutional partnerships, and position SagePaths as a trusted and leading international education brand in China. The role requires strong market insight, strategic thinking, leadership capability, and deep understanding of Chinese student recruitment ecosystems. Key Responsibilities 1. Recruitment Strategy & Market Development Develop and implement annual student recruitment strategies for the China market aligned with company growth targets. Identify new market opportunities, cities, schools, and channels to expand recruitment coverage. Monitor recruitment performance, forecast enrolment trends, and optimise conversion pipelines. 2. Team Leadership & Management Lead, coach, and manage the China recruitment team to achieve individual and team KPIs. Set clear targets, performance benchmarks, and professional development plans for team members. Foster a results-driven, ethical, and student-centred recruitment culture. 3. Brand Building & Marketing Collaboration Work closely with marketing and communications teams to localise campaigns, messaging, and promotional materials for the China market. Ensure consistency of brand positioning and messaging across online and offline platforms. Drive high-impact recruitment campaigns through digital marketing, exhibitions, school visits, webinars, and roadshows. 4. Partnerships & Stakeholder Engagement Build and maintain strong relationships with: Chinese high schools and international departments Education agents and sub-agents Overseas partner institutions Alumni, parents, and key opinion leaders (KOLs) Represent SagePaths at education fairs, partner meetings, and industry events. 5. Market Intelligence & Data Analysis Conduct regular competitor analysis and market research on student behaviour, pricing trends, and channel effectiveness. Analyse recruitment data to identify gaps, opportunities, and strategic adjustments. Provide management with market insights, forecasts, and performance reports. 6. Process Optimisation & Compliance Ensure recruitment processes comply with partner institution requirements and regulatory standards. Optimise CRM usage, admissions workflows, and data reporting systems. Uphold ethical recruitment practices and protect institutional reputation. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in any discipline; Master’s degree preferred. Minimum 6 years of relevant experience, with at least 3 years in international student recruitment, higher education management, or education consultancy. Proven track record in managing recruitment teams and achieving enrolment targets in the China market. Strong understanding of: China education landscape and student decision-making behaviour Study abroad pathways and university admissions processes Digital recruitment channels (XHS, WeChat, Douyin, Bilibili, etc.) Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Highly analytical, data-driven, and commercially aware. Strong presentation and negotiation skills with schools, partners, and parents. Able to work under pressure, manage multiple projects, and meet tight timelines. Fluent in Mandarin and English (spoken and written). Preferred Attributes Experience working with international universities or private education institutions. Strong network within China’s international education sector. Strategic mindset with hands-on execution ability. High level of professionalism, integrity, and accountability.

title: Senior/Lead Manager, PW Mark Administration Office
description: The Progressive Wage Mark (PW Mark) is an accreditation scheme under the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) that recognises eligible firms that pay progressive wages to lower-wage workers. The PW Mark Admin Office (PWMAO), housed within SBF’s Workforce Development Division (WDD), serves as the public-facing body for PW Mark administration, certification, renewal and outreach to businesses. This role leads the strategic development and review of policies impacting PW Mark (such as the PW Model), oversees programme planning and evaluation, and drives data-informed decision-making to enhance impact and operational effectiveness. The incumbent will also spearhead stakeholder engagement and cross-functional initiatives to strengthen SBF’s role in the PW Mark and the larger Human Capital ecosystem. The role is critical to repositioning PWMAO and its strategies from a regulatory outfit for compliance requirements into a strategic enabler for enterprise transformation, offering employers skills, capability pathways, and ecosystem support. This is a strong value proposition aligned with One SBF and One WDD’s enterprise and workforce transformation priorities to build progressive and inclusive practices across PWM sectors and roles. Job Responsibilities: 1. Policy Development & Capability Development Conceptualise and lead initiatives that integrate the Progressive Wage Model (PWM) into broader enterprise and workforce transformation strategies, beginning with the Retail and Food Services sectors. Regularly conduct research work and translate emerging workplace policies and ecosystem developments into actionable and business-centric programmes that build enterprise capabilities. Co-design and deliver sector-specific PWM and capability bundles with SBF Business Institute (SBI), sequencing interventions with diagnostics and bundling leadership, human capital, digital and sustainability tracks. Integrate Career Conversion Programmes (CCPs), such as CCP for SME Executives, into PWM adoption journeys to enable job redesign and mid-career entry, aligning employer onboarding and funding navigation across SBI and other key ecosystem initiatives, e.g. Enterprise and Workforce Transformation Package (EWTP) and the Jobs-Skills Integrator (JSIT) initiative. Establish impact metrics covering engaged and benefitted firms, capability uptake and wage or skills progression proxies, and use analytics dashboards such as Oracle NetSuite or Power BI to inform programme pivots. 2. Industry Development and Stakeholder Engagement Cultivate and deepen strategic partnerships with government agencies, industry bodies and ecosystem partners to advance PWM adoption. Support the Director in leading and overseeing the team’s industry development and Know-Your-Customer (KYC) efforts across all PWM sectors. Design and execute outreach strategies that drive awareness and recognition of PW Mark, especially for sectors hiring PWM Retail and Food Services job roles across retail, restaurants, catering, attractions and hotels. Embed One WDD orchestration by partnering EWTP, JSIT and Mid-Career Pathways Programme (MCPP) teams to progress firms from PW Mark awareness to capability uplift with SBI and CCPs, leveraging TACs as multipliers. Drive TAC-led outreach and ecosystem partnerships to scale PWM and capability programme adoption, leveraging TAC networks for sectoral transformation. Convene sector relevant roadmaps and practitioner playbooks, for example PWM and tech-enabled productivity, omnichannel and service excellence for the retail sector, in partnership with TACs, IHLs and solution partners. 3. Operations Excellence Identify systemic challenges and implement solutions that enhance operational performance and stakeholder experience. Handle the end-to-end process of PW Mark application, renewal, approval or rejection, appeal and assist with escalated cases where necessary. Oversee the planning and execution of outreach sessions to create awareness, understanding and recognition for PW Mark among businesses. Drive operational technology improvements including PWMAO CRM optimisation and an AI chatbot for enquiries to reduce low-value administrative work and free up officer bandwidth for industry development and capability building. Institutionalise audit-ready processes and LOA compliance for MOM-funded workstreams and standardise impact reporting and knowledge management across PWMAO and SBI. 4. Mentorship & Capability Building Mentor and develop junior officers, fostering a high-performance culture within the team. Lead cross-functional projects that deliver holistic business solutions and accelerate PWM and/or PW Mark adoption. Coach officers to deliver cross-functional PWM projects paired with EWTP, JSIT, MCPP and CCPs, ensuring sector-tailored capability pathways and sustained employer outcomes. Job Requirements: 6 to 8 years of experience in policy development, programme management, public sector strategy, systems-thinking and/or strategic planning. Excellent conceptual thinking skills, able to structure analysis for complex issues, using qualitative and quantitative data to distil insights and propose direction and actionable solutions. Familiar with data analytics tools and methodologies (e.g., Power BI, Python). Experience translating PWM policy into sectoral capability solutions, with facility in enterprise dashboards or CRM systems for impact tracking and decision-making. Good understanding of enterprise dynamics and industry-specific challenges across sectors hiring PWM workers (e.g., Retail, Food Services, Hospitality). Ability to design programmes that balance regulatory compliance with business needs. Familiarity with CCP frameworks including CCP for SME Executives and the ability to orchestrate CCP deployment alongside PWM adoption and SBI capability programmes. Strong relationship-building and stakeholder-influencing capabilities to deepen partnerships across public and private ecosystems and drive win-win alignment on strategic initiatives. Ability to orchestrate TAC engagement and ecosystem partnerships to scale programme uptake and accelerate industry-wide transformation. Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to craft compelling narratives and clearly present complex concepts to diverse audiences across B2B, B2G and G2B contexts. Proven track record in delivering data-driven outreach campaigns, articulating employer success stories, and using insights (e.g. A/B testing for EDMs and social content) to convert interest into meaningful engagement and capability uptake. Proven strategic and cross-functional leadership capability in leading teams and driving end-to-end initiatives from policy to programme to outcomes with strong governance, with professional certifications (e.g. PMP, Agile, Lean Six Sigma) as an added advantage.

title: GRIT: Group Legal and Compliance, Transformation
description: Why Join Be at the heart of change within OCBC’s Group Legal & Compliance division. As a Transformation Programme Associate, you’ll help build a more efficient and forward-thinking legal and compliance function. You'll gain hands-on experience with cutting-edge technologies and contribute directly to impactful initiatives, shaping the future of risk management at Singapore’s longest-established bank. How you succeed You’ll thrive by proactively seeking opportunities to improve processes. Embrace a fast-paced environment and quickly learn new technologies. Success lies in your ability to collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders, delivering on projects with meticulous attention to detail. A curious mindset and a willingness to experiment will be key in driving innovation. What you do You’ll be involved in a wide range of activities, including: Process Mapping & Analysis: Document and analyse legal and compliance workflows, identifying areas for improvement. Project Support: Track action items, maintain project documentation, and coordinate with cross-functional teams. Tech Exploration: Build prototypes using tools like Power Automate, Power Apps, and Gen AI to demonstrate potential solutions. Meeting & Communication: Schedule meetings, prepare materials, and assist with stakeholder communications. Stakeholder Engagement: Support change management activities and facilitate buy-in for new processes. Continuous Learning: Stay updated on industry trends and participate in relevant training programs. Who you work with Group Legal & Compliance takes care of integrity and ensures compliance with all rules and regulations, developing the policies & procedures that manage and mitigate risk. In an evolving world where we’re actively driving digital transformation, it’s no easy task. But we thrive on the challenge because we’re future-ready and focused on driving change. Who you are A recent graduate from a Polytechnic or University with a diploma or degree in the relevant field. Possess a Business, Information Systems, or a related discipline. Demonstrate a proactive approach and a strong work ethic. Exhibit a keen interest in emerging technologies and process improvement. Showcase strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Possess excellent communication and interpersonal abilities. Learning Objectives This role will provide you with: Hands-on experience with process mapping, analysis, and improvement techniques. Practical skills in project management and execution. Exposure to cutting-edge technologies like Power Automate, Power Apps, and Gen AI. Opportunities to collaborate with stakeholders at all levels. A deeper understanding of legal and compliance operations within a leading financial institution. Who we are As Singapore’s longest established bank, we have been dedicated to enabling individuals and businesses to achieve their aspirations since 1932. How? By taking the time to truly understand people. From there, we provide support, services, solutions, and career paths that meet their individual needs and desires. Today, we’re on a journey of transformation. Leveraging technology and creativity to become a future-ready learning organisation. But for all that change, our strategic ambition is consistently clear and bold, which is to be Asia’s leading financial services partner for a sustainable future. We invite you to build the bank of the future. Innovate the way we deliver financial services. Work in friendly, supportive teams. Build lasting value in your community. Help people grow their assets, business, and investments. Take your learning as far as you can. Or simply enjoy a vibrant, future-ready career. Your Opportunity Starts Here. title: Learning Support Officer (1-year contract)
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION MINDS has been caring for Persons with Intellectual Disabilities (PWIDs) and their families since 1962. Grounded in the belief that all PWIDs have innate abilities and talents, MINDS is committed to empowering them and enhancing their quality of life, while advocating for greater inclusivity for PWIDs as a nation. MINDS is today one of the largest charities in Singapore employing more than 850 staff. With the key focus to expand community-based services, improving the employability of PWIDS, strengthening education, and engendering inclusive community living, MINDS offers a holistic range of services and programmes across the PWIDs' lifespan. These include schools, employment and training development centres, home-based care services and community-based services to cater to the physical, psychological, environmental and social needs of PWIDs. For more information, please visit www.minds.org.sg. RESPONSIBILITIES The role of the Learning Support Officer (LSO) is to support Teachers in the conduct of lessons and school programmes, to maximise student engagement in learning. The LSO will work in support of, and in collaboration with teachers to provide for safe, conducive and engaging curricular and co-curricular programmes for students. The LSO will also collaborate with other staff, such as HODs, allied professionals, teacher assistants and admin staff as necessary, in the efforts to support effective teaching and learning. The LSO may be assigned in specific areas of need, such as (and not limited to):• IT-enriched or technology-assisted learning• Domestic Science / Food & Beverage learning• Vocational training which includes transition planning• ASD-friendly structures and strategies• Learning in authentic / outdoor /community-based settings. The LSO will manage resources and data related to the day-to-day supporting role in the specific area(s) assigned. The LSO may be assigned to participate in school projects or committee roles. Supporting the curriculum Assist in creating clean, safe and conducive learning environment which promotes student leaning and development. Assist teachers in sourcing for, preparing and producing learning materials. Assist and guide students in carrying out instructions and activities during and after lessons/programmes. Assist departments to source for relevant programmes / activities. Holistic student development Build relationships with students. Support teachers with input on students' individualised learning needs and goals. Assist teachers with data collection and management related to specific areas of support to enhance student learning and experience. Collaborate with allied professionals and other relevant staff to support students' development and well-being. Professional development Proactively engage in professional development activities for knowledge and skills development to enhance service delivery. Differentiated Role Profile The LSO's role may be differentiated and focused on specific areas of need, such as (and not limited to): Support class lessons in the following areas: IT-enriched or Technology-assisted programmes, which includes training and guidance in the use of devices and application. Domestic Science programme, which includes setting up the room, preparation of materials and equipment for use, and cleaning up after lesson. Vocational programme which includes  WSQ courses Supporting transition planning, to coordinate with different staff (teachers, AHPs, JCs) and caregivers, to facilitate: IEP/ITP meetings Family Envision Meetings ASD-friendly structures and strategies, which include Maintain ASD-friendly structures (e.g. visual schedules, work systems, sensory corner) Develop appropriate ASD-friendly learning resources Implement classroom routines Set up appropriate environment for out-of-classroom setting Learning in authentic/ outdoor/ community-based settings, which include Source for alternative learning spaces that enrich student learning Conduct recce and risk-assessment of outdoor settings Liaise with community/ external partners Co-teach/ coach students in PE/CCA and out-of-class lessons QUALIFICATIONS GCE ‘A' Levels, Higher NITEC or Diploma At least 2 years of experience related to education/ special needs. Experience in the school's specific areas of need is an advantage OTHER INFORMATION Ability to work independently as well as part of a team Good interpersonal and communication skills Strong organisation, time management and mulit-tasking skills Great attention to details with positive attitude Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - MINDSG LTD

title: Change Management Specialist #ESY
description: 1. Change Strategy & Operating Model •	Support implementation of change strategies to support the rollout of advanced AI solutions across the bank. •	Partner with HR to embed change implications into people and change strategy, workforce planning, and job architecture. 2. Change Impact & Workforce Transition •	Support change impact assessments across Business Units, identifying impacts to roles, skills, workflows, governance, and controls. •	Support the execution of workforce transition plans, including role evolution, redeployment pathways, and reskilling needs. 3. Adoption, Engagement & Enablement •	Support the design and deployment of change management artefacts (playbooks, toolkits, leader talking points, adoption dashboards). •	Support upskilling and retraining initiatives in collaboration with HR, Learning & Development, and external vendors. 4. Stakeholder Management & Governance •	Support the coordination with senior management, BU leaders, external consultants (e.g. Accenture) to ensure coherence between strategy, delivery, and change. 5. Communications & Measurement •	Support the shaping of clear, credible, and consistent change narratives. •	Support the definition and tracking of change effectiveness and adoption metrics (e.g. usage, behaviour change, capability readiness). •	Continuously refine change approaches based on feedback and data. Interested candidates, please email your resume to annasee@recruitexpress.com.sg Anna See Sing Yee CEI Reg R25157535 Recruit Express Pte Ltd EA License No: 99C4599 We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted

title: Mathematics Teacher
description: About Us: Kangaroo Learning Centre Pte Ltd (“KLC”) was established in Singapore in May 2022 as part of the Fang Tian Education Group, a leading education institution founded in 2014 in China, which serves over 100,000 students annually. We are one of Singapore’s fastest-growing math tuition centres . Out of 4000 students who participated in the Singapore Mathematical Olympiad (SMO), 118 students were invited for Rounds 2 and 51 are from our learning center. We are also the only centre in Singapore that offers parent sit-in during classes, a flexible refund policy, and a dual-teacher classroom model. Our unique approach and proven results have earned us a strong reputation among parents and students alike. The centre offers a range of academic and enrichment programs for students of different ages, including primary and secondary school students, as well as those preparing for the PSLE, Math Olympiad, GCE ‘O’-Level and ‘A’-Level examinations. At KLC, there's no office politics — we value direct, honest communication and a results-driven mindset. We’re not just looking for employees, but co-partners who are eager to grow with us , take initiative, and play an active role in building something meaningful together. Job Overview: At Kangaroo Learning Centre (KLC), our mission is to develop the best teachers in Singapore. We provide comprehensive training and a one-on-one mentorship program led by experienced senior teachers and management. Whether you're just starting out or looking to grow further, you'll receive the guidance, support, and structured development needed to thrive. Join us and build a rewarding teaching career with a clear path toward excellence, leadership, and long-term growth. Job Responsibilities: Create an interactive, inquiry-based, and student-centred classroom environment that guides and motivates students to enjoy learning mathematics. Communicate closely with students, provide timely feedback to parents, and proactively support students in their learning. Participate in center branding activities such as YouTube content creation, article writing and sharing, etc. Familiarity with the question-setting patterns and trends of PSLE, O-Level, A-Level, and Math Olympiad competitions (e.g., RMO, APMOPS, NMOS, SMO) is an added advantage. Support and participate in any teaching-related tasks and team activities assigned by the centre. Job Requirements: A bachelor’s degree or above from an accredited university (e.g., NUS, NTU, SMU, or equivalent). Candidates without experience are welcome to apply. Open to all nationalities (Sponsored for work passes) No prior teaching experience required — comprehensive training and mentorship will be provided. Responsible, adaptable, and willing to continuously learning. Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills, with a positive, collaborative attitude and a strong passion for working closely with students, parents, and colleagues. Able to commit weekdays (inc. evenings) & weekends (Note: Most student classes are held on weekends) What We Offer Performance-based salary reviews — high-performing teachers may receive salary of up to S$11,960 per month. Additional S$1,000 monthly Guaranteed Teaching Allowance will be paid during the first 3 months probationary period. The first year S$50,000 Guaranteed Annual Salary . We top up the balance if you didn’t get S$50,000 annual salary in the first year. Structured one-on-one mentorship and coaching. Comprehensive training — perfect for those starting their teaching career. Dual career progression paths provided — grow into either a senior teaching expert or move towards leadership and management roles. Opportunities to be involved in content creation, curriculum development, conducting activities with students and parents, and team leadership.

title: Manager, Adjuncts and Events Management, SCIS
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Singapore Management University is a place where high-level professionalism blends together with a healthy informality. The 'family-like' atmosphere among the SMU community fosters a culture where employees work, plan, organise and play together - building a strong collegiality and morale within the university. Our commitment to attract and retain talent is ongoing. We offer attractive benefits and welfare, competitive compensation packages, and generous professional development opportunities - all to meet the work-life needs of our staff. No wonder, then, that SMU continues to be given numerous awards and recognition for its human resource excellence. RESPONSIBILITIES This position is for School of Computing and Information Systems (SCIS). Manage the entire function of adjuncts' recruiting, hiring, onboarding and offboarding: Liaise with the Undergraduate team to determine the number of headcounts required for each term. Prepare requisitions for adjunct headcounts, including prepare and submit Job-bank postings. Prepare summary of applicants for Associate Dean's (Undergraduate Education) evaluation. Prepare offer letters and other required forms for adjuncts' hiring and payroll processing. Attend to and manage adjuncts' onboarding and offboarding process. Maintain and ensure compliance to government's regulatory policies and SMU/SCIS policies and guidelines. Plan, forecast and manage adjuncts' budget. Any other admin work related to the function. Guide the SCIS faculty organiser in planning the budget for the conference/event. Lead and manage the tender process for procurement. Manage revenue and expenses of events and conferences to ensure compliance. Liaise with SCIS faculty hosting the event and the external professional organisation of the event on all financial matters. Ensure adherence to relevant SMU policies and government regulatory policies. P&L reporting and transactions reconciliation for events and conferences. Liaise with SMU OFIN to reconcile all revenue and expenses at the end of each event. Prepare reports and statements for audit after the conference/event. Lead the execution (admin and logistics) of the conference/event. Provide administrative and logistics support to SCIS visiting faculty and research talks by visitors. Manage reimbursement of visiting faculty. Other duties as assigned. QUALIFICATIONS University degree with 5 years of relevant work experience or diploma with at least 8 years of experience, preferably in the education industry. Self-motivated with strong interpersonal and communication (both verbal and written) skills. Strong analytical, administrative and problem-solving skills. Good financial accounting/budgeting ability. Able to work independently and at the same time, be an excellent team player. OTHER INFORMATION #LI-KC1 Candidates who do not possess the stipulated qualifications but have relevant work experience may still apply. Remuneration and appointment terms shall commensurate with qualifications and experience. SMU reserves the right to modify the appointment terms where necessary. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - Singapore Management University

title: Technical Business Analyst for ServiceSG (Contract)
description: [2 years contract] ServiceSG aims to transform the service experience for citizens receiving public services. The Service Transformation and Planning team within ServiceSG leads the service transformation work for key citizen journeys. As a Data and Systems Business Analyst (Service Transformation & Planning), you will play a crucial role in overseeing and coordinating various aspects of a tech-ops data project within the business domain to support service delivery transformation. You will: • Data Governance & Data Sharing – Support cross-agency data sharing initiatives by defining clear data exchange processes, governance mechanisms, and technical standards – in compliance with government and privacy regulations. Drive the adoption, use, and alignment of data systems to enable data-ops-tech integration across government agencies and partner entities. Support the design and deployment of interoperable digital and data architectures that facilitate and align with the next bound of the public service vision — to know and serve citizens as one and with heart. • Shaping Service Experience through Insights & Requirements – Lead the engagement of agencies and stakeholders to capture strategic requirements and feedback that shape future digital and data service delivery systems. Design and conduct user research including surveys and focus group discussions to better understand user requirements. Translate operational needs and user behaviours into actionable system requirements and feature roadmaps. Ensure that day-to-day operational insights are elevated into long-term planning, enabling ServiceSG to anticipate demand and design future-ready solutions. • Programme Leadership & Governance – Oversee and steer multiple project workstreams to achieve service transformation outcomes. You will set direction, establish governance mechanisms, and monitor programme health across delivery milestones. Anticipate risks and craft mitigation strategies to ensure alignment with national digital and service strategies. Provide oversight of project reporting, executive briefings, and progress updates for senior leadership and decision-makers. • Cross-Agency Collaboration & Strategic Partnerships – Build and sustain strong partnerships with GovTech, public agencies, and cross-functional teams to co-create tech-enabled solutions that balance citizen experience, innovation, and operational efficiency. You will act as a strategic integrator, aligning diverse agency priorities and fostering whole-of-government coherence in service delivery. • Risk, Foresight & Continuous Improvement – Shape and deliver clear, concise, and compelling reports, dashboards, and presentations for senior management and policy-makers. Ensure that key project information and learnings are systematically captured, disseminated, and translated into strategic insights that inform broader WOG service transformation efforts. • Reporting and Documentation - You will prepare clear & concise reports, dashboards, and presentations for senior management, as well as ensure all key project information is accurately documented and easily accessible when needed. We are looking for motivated individuals who are self-starters in a fast-paced environment and enjoy large systems problem solving. You should be comfortable with the ambiguity in driving large system complex change, action-biased via experimentation and prototyping, and always seeking to bring in new ideas and best practices for the public service. Key Competencies Required • [ Working Effectively with Stakeholders ]: Able to work closely with senior management and other Public Service agencies to drive the service transformation initiative and achieve better coordination and seamless integration of technology and processes. Influence agencies to see beyond their individual agency’s missions, towards a collective purpose of seamless service delivery for the citizens. •[ Thinking Clearly and Making Sound Judgements ]: Strong analytical and critical thinking abilities. Able to work with ambiguity and loosely defined problem spaces and synthesize data points and ground realities to sense-make, propose and implement sound and timely solutions with stakeholders in mind. •[ Creative Thinking and Problem Solving ]: Contextualise and apply design methodologies and processes to uncover insights and solve specific service-related challenges. You would be required to demonstrate creativity in generating solutions to complex issues. •[ Technical and Data Acumen ]: Sound understanding of data systems, governance frameworks, and integration processes. •Prior experience in project management and projects involving using scrum and / or agile methodology / or technology projects will be useful. In addition, prior experience with data infrastructure or technology projects will be advantageous. Work Experience At least 5 years of work experience in at least two of the following areas in service delivery would be preferable: • Data Governance and Management • Service Design and Experience Management • Service Strategy, Policy and Planning • Service Operations and Management • Digital Transformation • Product Management • Project Management Personal Traits Excellent communication, presentation and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to bridge strategic thinking with practical implementation, and articulate complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences.

title: Lecturer / Senior Lecturer, Ridge View Residential College
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description Ridge View Residential College (RVRC) is a vibrant "living-and-learning" community of around 650 NUS students, As part of their residency, sudents participate in a two-year academic programme that complements their majors by supporting four of the the NUS General Education pillars. This programme is integrated into the day-to-day life of the College, which has a strong focus on sustainability and workplace readiness. RVRC's education mission is to cultivate students who are intellectually inquisitive, keen to grow, future-oriented, and value social and environmental responsibility. Job Description : RVRC seeks to appoint a committed individual to complement our team of educators. The successful applicant will, among other things, teach interdisciplinary courses, contribute to curriculum design and development, and engage in out-of-classroom learning and other student-centric pedagogies anchored in the different forms of sustainability and / or workplace readiness.  The appointee will also be expected to contribute to the College's student and residential life programmes, which include advising and mentoring students in various living-and-learning activities. We are specifically seeking applicants with expertise in workplace readiness, although the ideal applicant must have strong interdisciplinary capabilities coupled with a passion for their own field of expertise. The ideal applicant must work work well in a team and be committed to student learning, supporting students in their years of university life, and preparing them for their post college experiences. The successful applicant will be employed as a full-time RVRC faculty member on the University's Educator Track. The appointment is targeted for July 2026. We welcome you to apply to join our committed team of educators! Responsibilities : Design and teach academic interdisciplinary courses, specifically with a focus on intrapersonal and interpersonal skills, cultural intelligence, and / or the future of work. Engage students in out-of-classroom learning (including outdoor learning). Mentor students in formal and informal learning settings. Support the College's mission through service contributions, including leading living-and-learning initiatives. Qualifications Requirements: A PhD (or equivalent). Candidates with teaching experience at the tertiary level are preferred. Fully embracing of interdisciplinary, integrative teaching in and beyond the classroom. Motivation and reflectiveness as an educator, Adaptability to dynamic working environments. Passion for student growth and success. Enthusiasm to engage students in a living-and-learning programme. Interest in organising and participating in out-of-classroom learning activities. Application Documents : Cover letter Detailed CV listing:academic qualificationsemployment history (include all academic and non-academic positions held, including leadership roles, if any)summary of relevant courses/modules taughtpublications and conference presentations (if any)names and contact email of 3 referees. Statement of teaching philosophy and reflections on teaching practice (2-page limit) Evidence of teaching quality, including feedback from students and peer evaluations (if available) Other supporting documents We appreciate your understanding in advance that only selected applicants will be contacted. Review of applications will begin early and proceed on a rolling basis, prior to the conclusion of the application period. Closing date : 22 February 2026

OPSG works hand in hand with community partners, organisations and individuals to promote programmes and initiatives that foster deeper interaction, understanding and appreciation of our multi-ethnic and multi-religious community. We are seeking dynamic individuals with a high level of initiative to join our team as: PROGRAMME MANAGER You will initiate and develop initiatives on youth outreach and engagement, and community engagement. You will also spearhead engagement and development of volunteers/facilitators, build community partnerships and lead initiatives promoting workplace diversity. Key requirements: *  Degree with at least a year of relevant working experience. *  Passion for inter-racial/inter-religious community work *  Proven ability in conceptualising and developing outreach and capacity building programmes for youths, volunteers, community leaders, workplaces and/or educators in areas of intercultural/interfaith understanding, advocacy and cross-cultural engagement *  Track record in volunteer management, partnership development and/or corporate engagement *  Curating facilitated conversations on cross cultural relations and social cohesion *  Experience preferably in: -  Programme/event management, administration, evaluation and social media engagement -  Track record in working with youth-based organisations, schools, grassroots/community organisations, volunteers and/or corporates *  Adaptable, strong interpersonal skills, analytical and objective-driven *  Ability to work well in teams yet competent in managing projects independently If you are a highly motivated individual who has what it takes, we welcome you to join us. Salary will be commensurate with working experience and qualifications. Closing date of application: 20 February 2026 Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: HR Talent Acquistion Coordinator cum Ops Lead
description: Stag Match® Institute (SMI) hosts a wide range of Adult training Courses and Certification - Diploma Qualification and Workshops for both companies and individuals. www.smi.edu.sg We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced HR Talent Acquisition & Operations Lead to join our institute. This dual-role position is critical in ensuring the successful placement of our graduates and the smooth operational coordination of our courses. The ideal candidate will be a proactive networker, an excellent communicator, and possess strong organizational skills. Responsibilities : Talent Acquisition & Learner Placement Learner Placement Strategy: Develop and implement comprehensive strategies to assist graduates in securing employment opportunities relevant to their acquired skills and qualifications. Employer Engagement: Identify, build, and nurture strong relationships with recruitment agencies and companies across various industries to understand their hiring needs and facilitate learner placements. Networking Events: Organize and host engaging networking sessions, career fairs, and industry talks, providing platforms for our learners and potential employers to connect and explore job opportunities. Job Market Analysis: Stay updated on current job market trends, in-demand skills, and industry requirements to better guide learners and tailor placement strategies. Resume & Interview Preparation: Provide guidance and support to learners on resume writing, interview techniques, and career development to enhance their employability. Placement Tracking & Reporting: Maintain accurate records of learner placements and generate reports on placement success rates and employer feedback. Human Resources & Grants Management Company Grants & Incentives: Research and identify relevant government grants, incentives, and funding opportunities available to companies for hiring our graduates. Assist companies in understanding and applying for these grants, acting as a liaison with relevant agencies in Singapore. Internal HR Support: Provide basic internal HR support, including assisting with onboarding of new instructors or temporary staff, maintaining staff records, and coordinating HR-related administrative tasks as needed for the institute. title: Online Education Marketing Specialist
description: Pickering Global Campus is the online learning platform, part of the SagePaths Group. We deliver accessible, high-quality academic and professional programmes designed for learners across Asia and beyond. As we continue expanding our digital education offerings, we are building a dedicated team to drive online growth, brand visibility, and student acquisition. This role is ideal for an individual who thrives in a start-up-style environment—resourceful, hands-on, adaptable—yet appreciates the structure and support of a well-established organisation. If you want to play a pivotal role in shaping a new digital learning initiative, this is your opportunity. Role Brief We are seeking a dynamic and ambitious Online Education Marketing Specialist to lead Pickering Global Campus’ digital presence, growth initiatives, and student recruitment strategy. You will design and execute marketing campaigns from scratch, experiment with new tactics, analyse performance, and directly influence PGC’s overall marketing roadmap. This role requires creativity, data-driven thinking, and ownership of the full digital funnel. Key Responsibilities Digital Strategy & Campaign Execution Develop and execute digital marketing strategies to grow brand awareness, leads, and online enrolments. Create multi-channel content and campaigns (email, social media, paid ads, landing pages) tailored to target audiences. Maximise use of CRM and automation tools (e.g., HubSpot/Brevo) for email marketing and behavioural journeys. Growth & Performance Marketing Run growth experiments such as A/B testing, landing page optimisation, and lead magnets to achieve rapid, measurable results. Monitor and analyse campaign performance using Google Analytics, HubSpot, and internal dashboards. Adapt strategies based on data insights to continually improve ROI and conversion rates. Content, Brand & Web Manage website content and ensure alignment with PGC’s brand direction. Collaborate with internal teams and freelancers (design, content, sales) to ensure cohesive and impactful messaging. Guide social media content planning alongside Brands & Communications for consistent digital presence. Market Research & Strategy Development Conduct market and competitor research to identify new opportunities and emerging trends. Contribute to shaping PGC’s marketing roadmap and prioritise high-impact initiatives. Support the development of new programmes by providing audience insights and demand analysis. Agile Collaboration Work closely with Product, Tech, and Sales teams to ensure marketing initiatives support the learner journey and platform features. Adjust campaigns quickly in response to performance data and operational needs. Take initiative and operate independently in a fast-paced, start-up-like environment. Requirements Minimum Bachelor degree with 2+ years of experience in digital marketing, growth marketing, or performance marketing. Strong understanding of digital fundamentals: SEO, PPC, social media, content marketing, email marketing, and funnel optimisation. Comfortable with analytics tools (Google Analytics, HubSpot, Meta Ads Manager, etc.). Creative problem-solver with a willingness to test new ideas and iterate rapidly. Excellent written and verbal communication skills; attention to detail is essential. Ability to work independently and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial environment. Advantageous: Experience building/launching new online products, start-up experience, EdTech or education industry exposure.
