title: Education Executive
description: Chen Su Lan Methodist Children’s Home (CSLMCH) is dedicated to transforming the lives of children and families in need. Our mission is to foster healing, growth, and self-reliance within our community, emphasizing family reunification and improved psycho-social well-being. About the role A qualified executive with experience in teaching and delivering individualized intervention to join our team of multidisciplinary professionals who embraces the best interest of child and youth. He/she is committed towards the methodical planning, coordinating and executing of all educational-related policies/programmes/activities. The primary role of the executive is to provide tiered intervention to children/youths in consultation with the multidisciplinary team to promote the child/youth’s academic and personal excellence. The Education Executive is also responsible for working closely with key stakeholders to plan, organize and implement programmes and activities for the children and youths, manage volunteers and conduct needs and outcome assessments to measure and enhance programme relevance and effectiveness to bring about quality service and standards. Key Responsibilities: A) Delivers effective and efficient individualised intervention to children/youths Manage children and youths’ behaviour for the purpose of providing a safe and positive learning environment. Create a positive learning culture using a trauma-informed inclusive education approach in the House of Wisdom. Utilize data-based decision-making and evidence-based strategies to provide Individual Educational Plans (IEPs) and intervention plans for the children/youths with their respective case executives and in consultation with the Supervisor. Working with the school teachers to identify learning objectives for lesson planning with differentiated instruction. Plan, prepare, and deliver small group intervention in Social Emotional Learning to develop learners’ social-emotional and life skills. Provide academic support for learners according to their abilities and in alignment with mainstream or special school curriculum objectives. Coordinate and collaborate with colleagues e.g., caseworkers, therapy team and external stakeholders e.g. parent/guardians, schools, tutors, and volunteer tutors to deliver effective interventions to increase academic and personal success. Establish positive, safe, and trusting relationships with children/youths, their families and school teachers to encourage academic and personal progress specific to their needs. Commit to the continual professional development of not less than 50 training hours in any 2 years, which shall consist of at least 10 hours in Pedagogy training to keep current with the latest skills and ensure professional competency, subject to approval by the immediate supervisor. B) Plan, organize and execute programmes and activities relevant and beneficial for the children and youths . To communicate and work with potential stakeholders such as organisations, vendors and volunteers, to bring in appropriate educational programmes that meet the needs of children/youths. Track the attendance of participants of the programme and plan for the tuition scheduling of the volunteers/tutors on a monthly basis. Coordinate and/or support centre’s planned events and programmes. Develop and facilitate programmes that need support. Conduct periodic needs and outcome assessments to evaluate the effectiveness and relevance of key programmes. C) Regularly assesses children’s/youths progress to modify instruction/intervention Make use of relevant data to identify child’s/youth’s needs, modify instruction, and report progress to all stakeholders. Summarize assessment results and incorporates information into an appropriate support plan. Prepare relevant submissions/reports on a termly or ad-hoc basis regarding children/youths’ learning, progress development and recommendations for intervention. Maintain updated and accurate records which adhere to high levels of confidentiality. Coordinate, facilitate, and maintain records on meetings with parents, school teachers, and internal/external stakeholders. D) Provides consultation to volunteer tutors, school teachers, parents, and other stakeholders to foster greater academic and social/emotional skill development across all children and youths Manage volunteers; conduct virtual and on-site training sessions for volunteers. Offers ongoing support or training to volunteer tutor, colleagues, teachers, and parents in order to accelerate child/youth’s growth. Participate in a multi-disciplinary team of professionals by utilizing a problem-solving approach. - Prepare and disseminate professional resources according to child’s/youth’s needs. Establish and maintain ongoing professional collaboration with internal and external stakeholders in order to maximize children/youths’ impact in all areas of development. E) Development of teaching curriculum and resources Ongoing development and review of curriculum and pedagogy with instructional materials to ensure the education programme at House of Wisdom remains dynamic, evidence-based, and responsive to the learners’ varying needs. F) Provide Academic Pathways Guidance & Career Guidance Provide education and career counselling to children/youths based on their learning abilities and career/academic aspirations so as to make good academic choices/career prospects. Coordinate, partner with/network with various Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) or companies for visits, learning journeys or taster workshops. G) Supported Employment - Develop individualized employment plans with youth and incorporate input from the caseworkers when appropriate. Adjust the plan according to the youth’s needs and preferences. Conduct job development and job search activities. Assess the client’s vocational functioning on an ongoing basis. Provide individualized on-site job coaching to assist youths. Provide education and support to employers, which may include negotiating job accommodations and on-site client supervision and support with frequent employer communication. Prioritize employer relations and solutions-based thinking through timely and proactive communication. Use conflict resolution skills to provide timely interventions. Return phone calls, follow up on job leads, and meet with youths consistently to resolve job issues. H) Any other duties Review and update all educational policies, SOPs, programmes, children/youths’ education case file/data/information. Perform the secretariat role for planned and/or ad-hoc meetings where required. Perform administrative tasks related to volunteer programmes and funding applications for programme reporting and audit purposes. Take on any other assignment, project or duty as assigned by the Supervisor. Candidate's Requirements: At least 2 years of experience working within special education, providing learning support or community rehabilitation services for adults or youths at risk. Relevant qualifications in education, special education or psychology. Demonstrate flexibility, strong work habits, and a positive attitude, with a commitment to teamwork and collaboration. Knowledge and experience in profiling and delivering interventions for individuals with social-emotional difficulties are preferred. Experience in administrative tasks, including for fund application for programmes. Attend meetings and willing to take minutes when required. Working Hours Monday to Friday during 9am to 6 pm or 12:30pm to 9:30pm The selected candidate is required to work in the evenings three times a week as needed. What are you waiting for: Apply now and join us today with a dedicated team committed to making a difference in the lives of vulnerable children and families. You’ll have the opportunity to lead impactful initiatives, collaborate with passionate colleagues, and contribute to meaningful community-driven solutions. We work closely with community partners to bring holistic support and care to the children and families. Together we make a difference on the lives of the children in the Home. Together We Nurture Every Child and Family to Shine. Only Shortlisted applicant will be notified.

Why us: Flexible working hours with part-time working models and hybrid options Attractive fringe benefits and salary structures in line with the market Modern and central office space with good public transport connections Can-do mentality and one-spirit culture Varied events and employee initiatives Your documents to start the process: Resume Job references Qualifications (bachelor/ master diploma, etc.) with certificate of grades Motivation letter: Why Synpulse? title: Senior Assistant Director (RTO), A*STAR
description: TRUST is a data sharing and analytics platform that supports the secure use of research and real-world datasets to enable health research data analytics, so as to improve health outcomes for Singapore and support data-driven innovation. As a Communications Lead, you will lead a team on crafting clear messaging around data privacy and health-data innovation, translating technical concepts for diverse audiences, managing media and stakeholder engagement, and developing content that supports Research Trust Office’s mission of enabling secure, responsible use of health data for research and healthcare transformation. Communications Outreach and Education Develop digital content that caters to the platform’s requirements and stakeholders’ interests by working with vendors and/or stakeholders. Coordinate the platform’s public relations matters and crafting of media materials to manage media queries, prepare and support spokespersons. Media materials  include but are not limited to press releases, media factsheets, media, FAQs, and collaterals. At least 5–7 years of working experience in strategic communications, corporate communications and / or patient support / advocacy communications. Proven track record in working with stakeholders to shape strategic communication and messaging to national and/or international audiences. Sensitivity to diverse audiences and demonstrated sound judgement about messaging. Versatile in working independently as well as an effective team player.

title: Lead, Sustainability
description: Translate global sustainability developments and regional trends into business-relevant insights that support the Group’s long-term vision and well-being mandate. Advise on emerging sustainability risks and opportunities and ensure integration into group-level planning and positioning. Maintain a forward-looking, impact-focused approach that evolves with stakeholder expectations, regulatory shifts, and innovation. Maintain sustainability governance frameworks, internal policies, and reporting structures in alignment with global standards (e.g.GRI, ISSB, SBTi, IFC PS, GIIN, etc.) and local regulatory requirements. Support the sustainability reporting process, working closely with relevant departments to collect, analyse, and articulate sustainability performance and disclosures. Support the group sustainability council and conduct meetings once every 2 months to promote sustainability awareness and monitoring of projects’ performance. Perform periodic internal reviews and improvement cycles to ensure sustainability metrics are actionable, transparent, and decision-enabling. Review and approve investment team’s ESG downsides and impact upsides across due diligence and portfolio review. Guide sustainability integration into new business ventures and innovation projects, particularly those related to green or regenerative models. Align sustainability priorities with the Group’s strategic direction, without duplication of brand or marketing functions. Cultivate high-impact partnerships with regulators, industrial experts, and regional and global institutions. Represent the Group in sustainability forums and regulatory dialogues to build visibility and credibility. Engage with philanthropic, academic, and civic partners to drive joint initiatives and knowledge exchange. Build sustainability literacy across the Group through internal engagement, capacity-building programs such as CSR initiatives, and thought leadership. Act as an internal advisor to functions implementing sustainability-related workstreams such as Restore Nature and Wellbeing and EESG Alliance (WEGA). Promote a mindset of conscious leadership and collective responsibility through clear sustainability narratives and values alignment. Contribute to shaping the culture around long-termism, stewardship, and ethical growth Job Requirement: Minimum 10-15 years of experience in sustainability, ESG, or impact strategy roles, ideally with experience across both business and investment environments in Asia. Familiarity with global sustainability frameworks (e.g. GRI, ISSB, SBTi, IFC PS, GIIN, etc.) and experience implementing governance or reporting structures. Strong cross-functional collaboration experience, particularly with investments, strategy, or transformation teams. Demonstrated ability to lead sustainability initiatives independently while aligning with broader business or brand direction. Prior exposure to sectors such as logistics, maritime, energy transition, or purpose-led investment is beneficial. Soft Skills: Proficient in English; Mandarin is advantageous for regional engagement. Strong communication, facilitation, and stakeholder engagement skills across internal and external audiences. Capable of articulating sustainability in strategic, operational, and brand-aligned language. Proactive, adaptable, and able to operate effectively in a matrixed and evolving organizational environment. Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Sustainability, Environmental Management, Business, or related disciplines. Certifications in ESG, sustainability reporting, or climate governance are a plus.

title: Assistant Director (Community and Youth Mental Health Services)
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION The Salvation Army has been touching lives in Singapore since 1935 and we remain committed in serving the underprivileged in the community without discrimination. To us, every life is precious and we actively help those in need regardless of age, race or religion. Throughout the years, we have established a comprehensive network of social services to meet a wide range of needs in our community. Our diverse programmes reach out to children who need care and protection, families in material need, ailing aged requiring quality nursing care and other needy segments in our society. Our spiritual ministry also reaches out to the people in the community to provide counsel to those in need. We are there to help guide those in need towards finding their identity and purpose in life. RESPONSIBILITIES MAIN PURPOSE / OBJECTIVES: The Assistant Director, Community and Youth Mental Health (CYMH) Services is responsible for overseeing and managing the delivery of community services and youth mental health programmes. He/she will provide professional and operational oversight for clinical and social work components in CYMH. This position will supervise the Manager, Youth Development Centre (YDC) to ensure smooth planning and implementation of programmes for youths at the drop in centre, child access services and youth mental health programmes. This will include guiding staff and executing the pivoting of YDC from its current focus to professionally grounded youth mental health and developing the activities and programmes needed from scratch. This position will also provide professional supervision to develop and implement youth mental health programmes that are able to reach a broad spectrum of youths to build resilience as well as supervise the team to provide case management and basic level counselling/triage for low to moderate level of youth mental health concerns. This will include ensuring that funders' KPIs and obligations are met. This position will also supervise the Manager, Kids in Play (KIP) to ensure strategic planning, and provide strong professional oversight to enable KIP to continue to be well established as a clinically sound service provider and continue to grow KIP's social work and clinical practice in the corrections landscape. This position will also front engagements with government agencies such as Prisons and NCSS, using his/her professional standing and capability to build a strong reputation for KIP and enable collaboration with key stakeholders to ensure continual financial support for all KIP programmes. He/she will also work with relevant agencies such as NCSS on assessment/evaluation tools and how they are to be implemented appropriately to ensure KIP is fairly assessed. Key Responsibilities: Oversee and manage the delivery of community services and youth mental health programmes. Provide professional oversight for clinical and social work components in CYMH and KIP. Supervise the Manager, Youth Development Centre to ensure smooth planning and implementation of programmes for youths at the drop-in centre, child access services, and youth mental health programmes. Guide staff and execute the pivoting of YDC from its current focus to youth mental health. Provide professional oversight to develop and implement youth mental health programmes that are able to reach a broad spectrum of youths to develop healthy coping mechanisms, build resilience, and navigate life's challenges. Supervise the Manager, Kids in Play (KIP) to ensure strategic planning, and where appropriate review, for all KIP programmes to ensure KIP's standing in correctional sector remains strong. Foster strong collaboration with key stakeholders such as Prisons and NCSS to ensure continual financial support for all KIP programmes. Maintain close working relations with Prisons authorities to ensure all kids in play continue to develop. Ensure that funder's KPIs and obligations for all community and YMH programmes are met, as well as work with relevant partners such as MER in HQ and NCSS Research to ensure evaluations are fairly executed for. Grow youth mental health community services. Work closely with ED/CYG and AM/Corp and Prog Development to grow funding to sustain and grow community services, in particular youth mental health. Provide practice leadership and supervision for social workers in CYG, as needed. Any other duties as required by TSA or CYG. Key Performance Indicators: Programme Effectiveness: Measure the impact and effectiveness of community services and youth mental health programmes through participant feedback, engagement levels, and outcome assessments. Funder's KPlis and Obligations: Ensure that all funder's key performance indicators and obligations are met, including timely submission of reports and adherence to funding requirements. Staff Supervision and Development: Evaluate the performance and development of supervised staff, including the Manager, Youth Development Centre, and the Manager, Kids in Play (KIP). This includes regular performance appraisals and professional development plans. Collaboration with Stakeholders: Foster strong collaboration with key stakeholders, including maintaining close working relations with Prisons authorities and ensuring continual financial support for all KIP programmes. Programme Implementation and Pivoting: Successfully guide and execute the pivoting of the Youth Development Centre (YDC) from its current focus to youth mental health, ensuring smooth planning and implementation of programmes. Compliance and Standards: Ensure that all administrative requirements meet licensing standards and that work processes and protocols are transparent and understood by all staff. Funding: Ensure funding to enable sustainability of community services and growth of youth mental health. QUALIFICATIONS A degree in social work, psychology, or a related field. title: External Affairs Manager, APAC
description: The External Affairs (EA) Manager, Asia-Pacific (APAC), will play a key role in setting strategy for APAC and leading a team to implement it. The EA Manager will oversee public affairs and public relations efforts across focus countries, supporting in policy matters, media relations, and stakeholder engagement. Working closely with Office/Operating Managers, senior stakeholders and other regional EA teams, the manager will oversee the design and delivery of localized campaigns that reflect national priorities while remaining aligned with MDPI’s global identity. In addition to strengthening MDPI’s media presence and stakeholder networks, the Manager will provide strategic counsel, implement processes and support the growth and development of colleagues. Core Responsibilities Overall Determine and manage the development and implementation of an External Affairs communications team and program in the Asia-Pacific region in line with MDPI’s mission and business goals. Manage public affairs and public relations activities across a portfolio of focus countries, liaising with Office/Operating Managers to cover policy matters, media relations, and broader reputational risks and opportunities. Provide strategic support to country representatives and spokespeople and manage stakeholder engagements. Manage and own EA resourcing for the APAC region, including team development and growth, campaign budgets, and agency requirements. Public Relations Implement PR campaigns to enhance MDPI’s brand visibility across focus countries in APAC, focusing on academic, scientific, and policy-related media. Secure high-quality earned media coverage in top-tier and trade outlets across the region, with tailored messaging for each country market. Localize global narratives and campaigns to suit the cultural, linguistic, and sectoral nuances of key countries. Build and maintain strong relationships with journalists, editors, and media stakeholders in science, academia, education, and research policy. Coordinate with global teams to align regional communications with overall brand and publishing strategies. Provide strategic counsel and training to MDPI representatives in APAC. Public Affairs Map and engage with key institutional stakeholders across APAC — including academic societies, research funding bodies, ministries of education/science, and regulatory bodies. Track and report on relevant policy developments that may affect open access publishing, scholarly communication, and academic research infrastructure. Shape MDPI’s positioning and voice on strategic public policy issues in the region (e.g. open access mandates, national research strategies, publishing reform). Where required, represent MDPI in external forums, working groups, or policy discussions where appropriate. Develop briefing materials, talking points, and responses on sensitive or complex issues relevant to APAC stakeholders. Additional Responsibilities Coordinate efforts and communication with other EA teams and regions. Streamline processes to improve responsiveness and alignment with organizational goals. Provide insights and recommendations based to improve future strategies. Identify opportunities to improve team proficiency in strategic communications. Ensure that all EA activities are consistent with MDPI's brand and communication standards. Requirements Bachelor’s degree and above. Minimum 5 years of experience in Public Affairs or Public Relations, with strong exposure to external communications and strategic initiatives. Minimum 2 years of managerial experience. Proven APAC regional experience, with the ability to work independently and hands-on across multiple markets. Demonstrated experience engaging with government stakeholders. Experience in Singapore, Malaysia and Taiwan markets would be an advantage. Prior experience in agency or publishing industry would be an advantage. Skills and Abilities Excellent command of English; proficiency in additional APAC languages would be a plus. Proficient in Microsoft 365. Attention to detail, confidence in stakeholder engagement, proactiveness, teamwork, and effective problem-solving. Ability to support corporate branding initiatives (e.g., Institutional Open Access Program) and deliver in-house training on communication strategy. What we offer Professional and personal development An international work environment Flexible working hours in the limits of a full-time job Business trips to the other MDPI offices Competitive salary, benefits About MDPI: A pioneer in scholarly open access publishing, MDPI has supported academic communities since 1996 . Our mission is to foster open scientific exchange in all forms, across all disciplines. We operate more than 400 diverse, peer-reviewed , open access journals supported by over 66,000 academic editors . We serve scholars from around the world to ensure the latest research is openly and broadly available. MDPI is headquartered in Switzerland with additional offices in Europe, Asia and North America. We are committed to ensuring that high quality research is made available as quickly as possible. We also support sustainability projects, with sustainability as a key theme in many journals and through the MDPI Sustainability Foundation.

title: International Growth Practice - Manager
description: At PwC, we help clients build trust and reinvent so they can turn complexity into competitive advantage. We’re a tech-forward, people-empowered network with more than 370,000 people in 149 countries. Across audit and assurance, tax and legal, deals and consulting we help clients build, accelerate and sustain momentum. Find out more at www.pwc.com. About the Team Headquartered in Singapore, PwC’s International Growth Practice is a global team supporting organizations to address their internationalization ambitions, whilst achieving rebalanced and resilient growth strategies. We are a truly global team, with satellite offices in Europe and the United States, made up of individuals from over 15 nationalities that reflects our diverse and extensive cross-country and multi-industry experience. The practice caters to a wide array of problem statements ranging from international market expansion, global location assessment, international supply chain transformation, international talent strategy, intra-regional trade flow analysis and strategic point of view development. Our global team focuses on several sectors including healthcare and pharmaceuticals, industrial manufacturing, automotive , consumer goods, telecoms, media and technology, financial services as well as government and global institutions. For more information about PwC’s International Growth Practice, please visit our website: www.pwc.com/internationalgrowth About the role We are looking for experienced candidates with demonstrable experience from at least one (1) or more of the areas listed below: Healthcare and Pharmaceuticals Industrial Manufacturing Automotive Consumer Goods Media and Technology People and Organisation Responsibilities Develop expansion strategies for clients from the sector seeking international expansion or to realign their existing supply chain and production footprints to being more rebalanced and resilient. Typical engagements will focus on developing in depth analysis and insights for: Market expansion strategies (Where to play) – market, consumer and competitor assessments as well as product and services analysis Market entry scenarios (How to win) – Channel strategies, operating model design, partner evaluation and selection. Global and regional site selection for new HQs, R&D sites, manufacturing / production plants Global talent assessments, to assess the availability of talent for growth strategies Supplier portfolio realignment , identifying and assessing suppliers to meet the needs of realigned supply chains and manufacturing. Strategic points of view focused on international growth Provide points of view for publication on opportunities and challenges of global trade and internationalization Lead bespoke client discussions and workshops to senior executives of corporate organizations and governments. Engage in business development and client pursuit efforts-Develop global relationships and build a pipeline of inbound and outbound projects to sell and deliver Work closely with PwC’s global network of teams and connect relevant expertise in PwC to address clients’ unique business challenges Establish strong international network of external stakeholders including clients and industry associations and participate in regional and international conferences and client events About you 8 + years of relevant experience working in our target sectors in either a management consulting setting or within industry in strategy or general management role. Growth markets experience (E.g. Southeast Asia, China, India, Middle East, Latin America, Africa and CEE) Strong experience in delivering on client engagement with multiple stakeholders and teams in different countries Experienced in managing data collection and analysis and resourceful with excellent research and analytical skills; can transform quantitative and qualitative data into insights relating to country, sector and consumer trends, competitive intelligence Possess credentials in business development and sales with ability to develop insightful presentations to support proposals, client discussions and business development pursuits Thrives in a fast-paced and dynamic work environment Possess strong communication and presentation skills Team player comfortable with working in a multicultural and diverse global team

title: KYC Analyst #ESY
description: •	Attached to Private Wealth Business Compliance & Governance Department •	This position sits within the Private Wealth BCCO unit and is responsible for supporting the front office in ensuring timely and compliant onboarding of new clients. The successful candidate is expected to be well-versed in AML/CFT and CDD standards, and familiar with the documentation and regulatory requirements for Private Wealth account types. The role requires a meticulous eye for detail, strong communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple stakeholders and competing priorities. •	Review and assess onboarding documentation to ensure completeness, accuracy, and compliance with MAS regulations and internal policy standards. •	Identify and escalate any deficiencies or red flags in client KYC, source of wealth (SoW), and background information. •	Liaise with Relationship Managers (RMs) to clarify submissions and obtain supporting documentation where required. •	Provide guidance to RMs on KYC requirements and support them in meeting submission standards. •	Ensure proper risk classification based on client profile, SoW, and overall risk factors. •	Coordinate with Financial Crime Compliance (FCC) and other control functions where escalation or additional review is necessary. •	Monitor onboarding turnaround time and ensure cases are processed within SLA. •	Support internal projects and initiatives related to onboarding process enhancements, checklist updates, and policy changes. •	Ensure proper documentation and record-keeping to support audit readiness and regulatory inspections. •	Ability to work independently with minimal supervision, and collaborate effectively with both front office and control functions. Interested candidates, please email your resume to annasee@recruitexpress.com.sg Anna See Sing Yee CEI Reg R25157535 Recruit Express Pte Ltd EA License No: 99C4599 We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted

title: Projects & Partnerships Lead
description: About Preschool Market Preschool Market is a social enterprise dedicated to providing quality learning opportunities for all children. We are a two-time Champions of Good Award recipient conferred by the National Volunteer and Philanthropy Centre (NVPC). We work closely with schools, educators, parents, corporates, and community partners to design meaningful educational experiences and social impact initiatives. As we expand our programmes and national-level projects, we are looking for a driven and capable individual to strengthen our project execution and partnership development efforts. Role Overview The Project & Partnership Senior Executive / Assistant Manager plays a key role in identifying, developing, and managing strategic collaborations that advance Preschool Market’s business growth and social impact. You will oversee projects from concept to execution, ensuring strong alignment with organisational goals, partner objectives, and measurable outcomes such as revenue generation, programme reach, and community impact. This role works cross-functionally and externally to translate ideas into well-executed programmes, build strong partner relationships, and use data-driven insights to optimise partnerships, projects, and engagement strategies. Key Responsibilities 1. Strategic Partnerships & Business Development Identify, evaluate, and develop strategic partnerships with preschools, corporates, public agencies, NGOs, and community organisations Manage the end-to-end partnership lifecycle, from pitching and proposal development to execution and renewal Align partnership objectives with Preschool Market’s business, programme, and impact goals Support revenue-generating initiatives, sponsorships, and CSR collaborations 2. Project & Programme Management Lead projects from concept, planning, and budgeting through execution and post-project evaluation Coordinate internal teams, trainers, vendors, and partners to ensure timely and high-quality delivery Manage project timelines, resources, risks, and deliverables across multiple initiatives Ensure programmes meet agreed outcomes in participation, engagement, revenue, and/or social impact 3. Stakeholder & Relationship Management Act as the key liaison between Preschool Market and partners Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with stakeholders Facilitate meetings, presentations, and progress updates Manage expectations and resolve issues professionally to ensure project success 4. Data, Insights & Impact Tracking Track and analyse project and partnership performance using relevant data and metrics Prepare reports, insights, and recommendations to improve programme effectiveness and partnership value Use data to support decision-making and continuous improvement Support impact measurement and reporting for social and community initiatives Qualifications Diploma or Degree in Business, Education, Communications, Marketing, Social Sciences, or related fields Proven experience in managing projects end-to-end and working with multiple stakeholders Strong relationship-building, negotiation, and communication skills Comfortable working with data, reports, and performance metrics Proactive, resourceful, and able to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment At least 1 year of experience in partnerships, project management, business development, or community engagement is an advantage Interest or experience in education, social impact, or sustainability is an advantage Knowledge or experience in grant research, applications, and reporting is an advantage What You Will Gain Exposure to strategic partnerships and business development in a growing social enterprise Hands-on ownership of projects from ideation to execution and impact measurement Opportunities to work with diverse partners across education, corporate, and community sectors Development in project leadership, stakeholder management, and data-driven decision-making A purpose-driven role where your work directly contributes to children’s learning and community impact

title: Family Outreach and Program Coordinator
description: Company Description Glyph Community is a Singapore-registered IPC charity dedicated to supporting children from lower-income or vulnerable households, aged 5 to 12, through holistic weekend enrichment programs. Beyond fostering social, emotional, and intellectual growth through structured activities like arts, STEM, life skills, and wellness initiatives, Glyph Community now also focuses on uplifting the living standards of the families we serve. Role Overview This role supports the full journey of a beneficiary-from first contact to enrolment, engagement, and renewal. The Programme Coordinator ensures families have a smooth, caring experience while helping Glyph grow its reach through direct outreach and ecosystem partnerships. The main KPI is the number of active beneficiaries. Roles & Responsibilities Daily Tasks Respond to new and existing families via WhatsApp Business Follow up with prospective beneficiaries through WhatsApp, phone calls, and occasional home visits Maintain accurate daily attendance and activity records (Excel) Update CRM entries (HubSpot) to reflect family status and engagement notes Weekly Tasks Teach and manage weekend enrichment classes for children Conduct volunteer briefings and assign session roles Oversee student attendance and collect feedback from families and volunteers Manage centre operations: open, clean, and close the centre smoothly Update weekly lesson slides (PowerPoint) Track volunteer registration and attendance (Giving.sg, Excel) Coordinate with social workers, government agencies, schools, and non-profits for beneficiary referrals and outreach partnerships Monthly Tasks Review and update curriculum slides for relevance and clarity Design and send out the monthly family schedule and centre announcements (Canva, WhatsApp) Ensure all administrative records-attendance, feedback, engagement-are up to date and organised Track beneficiary engagement and handle semester renewals to maintain or grow enrolment This role is ideal for someone who is people-first, clear-thinking, and unafraid to own both the details and the bigger picture. Your success is measured by one thing above all: more children reached; more lives changed. Application Process As part of our selection process, all applicants are required to participate in a volunteering session to assess their suitability for engaging with our beneficiaries. Work Schedule Wednesday – Friday: Work-from-home Saturday – Sunday: Centre operations (Boon Lay / Marsiling) Work Hours: 9:30am – 6:30pm

title: Secondary English Teaching & Curriculum Specialist
description: About the Role At illum.e, we are not just another tuition centre — we are Singapore’s most forward-thinking English education brand, known for our game-changing methodology and class-leading results. As a Secondary English Teaching & Curriculum Specialist, you will join the team driving this standard of excellence. This unique role blends teaching and curriculum innovation , designed for those who aspire to lead the future of English education in Singapore. We are seeking the very best curriculum talents in the nation — educators with the creativity, rigour, and vision to keep illum.e at the forefront of English mastery. Key Responsibilities Teaching & Learning : Deliver illum.e’s signature Secondary English classes (Sec 1–4) with clarity, impact, and inspiration. Build student confidence and drive mastery beyond grades. Curriculum Development : Work with our curriculum team to design and refine cutting-edge lesson materials, assessments, and resources that set national benchmarks in rigour and relevance. Student Progression : Track student performance, adapt pedagogy, and ensure each learner achieves growth with excellence. Collaboration : Partner closely with Curriculum Leads to identify gaps, propose innovations, and co-create modules that shape the future of Secondary English. Leadership Pathway : Take part in professional growth initiatives that prepare you to step into Curriculum Lead roles, where you will shape vision, strategy, and team culture for Secondary English. Who We’re Looking For A strong command of English Teaching Curricula, with a genuine passion for teaching and shaping students' mastery of the language. Visionary educators who want to set the standard for Secondary English in Singapore . Growth-minded, collaborative, and driven to innovate. Prior Secondary English teaching experience is valued; outstanding fresh talent with curriculum potential will also be considered. Why illum.e? Strong Brand, Strong Demand: We don’t chase students. Our reputation brings them to us Proven Impact: Our students regularly top their schools and national exams, and come back to thank us World-Class Materials: Every teacher is equipped with high-impact materials, scaffolds, and model essays with no last-minute scrambling Culture of Mastery and Growth: High-performing team, low politics, and a culture of continuous feedback and mentorship Clear Career Pathways: Whether you want to teach, lead, innovate, or scale EdTech, we’ve built the ladders Future-Facing Brand: illum.e is rapidly expanding into AI tools, online learning, and overseas markets Staff Benefits Competitive salary, reviewed annually Semester-based performance bonuses and retention incentives Annual staff retreats, festive bonding events, and wellness perks Full curriculum and marking support so you can teach with quality, not chaos Fast-tracked growth opportunities into curriculum, academic leadership, or product development Mentorship from experienced Leads, plus direct access to the CEO and Academic Directors Paid training sessions and curriculum workshops Two full rest days each week Flexible work schedules based on availability

title: Senior Manager (Grant Partnership)
description: Avensys is a reputed global IT professional services company headquartered in Singapore. Our service spectrum includes enterprise solution consulting, business intelligence, business process automation and managed services. Given our decade of success we have evolved to become one of the top trusted providers in Singapore and service a client base across banking and financial services, insurance, information technology, healthcare, retail and supply chain. We are hiring a Senior Manager (Grant Partnership) with experience in cross-functional, international environments. This role offers strong growth opportunities and a healthy work–life balance. More details as below: Key Duties & Responsibilities Drive the activities in grant business partnership and quality management, mainly for programmes funded by the SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) grant. Grant business partnership involves engaging with internal and external stakeholders, as well as managing grant administration and governance. Quality management covers the full courseware lifecycle, from submission through training delivery (and includes audits and assessments), to ensure quality assurance for Continuing and Education Training (CET) courses. Be responsible for the preparations of reports and presentations as required for submission to University and SCALE Management, Government funding agencies, etc. Liaise with funding agencies on all matters related to grants received by SCALE. Be responsible for claims submission activities, including gathering claim details and ensuring successful submission to funding agencies’ portals, such as the SSG Funding Management System. Lead continuous improvement projects to ramp up productivity and operational efficiency. Develop, cultivate and promote positive stakeholder engagement practices to continuously improve stakeholders’ experience. Job Requirements: At least 8 years of related working experience in the education industry, with a good understanding of the Singapore CET landscape. Prior experience managing Government-funded grants, especially SSG grants, would be advantageous. Energetic and skilled people manager, adept at leading teams towards achieving common objectives in a fast-paced and challenging work setting. Proven leadership skills to inspire, galvanise and engage staff members at all levels and with external stakeholders, functioning effectively in a complex, uncertain and ambiguous environment. Excellent interpersonal and communication (verbal & written) skills. Results-driven, self-motivated and highly disciplined. Strong team player, and able to work collaboratively with various stakeholders to achieve desired outcomes. Resilient, resourceful with good problem-solving and critical-thinking skills, and thrives in a fast-paced, results-oriented and ambiguous work environment. If you are interested kindly share you latest resume. WHAT’S ON OFFER: You will be remunerated with an excellent base salary and entitled to attractive company benefits. Additionally, you will get the opportunity to enjoy a fun and collaborative work environment, alongside a strong career progression To submit your application, please apply online or email your UPDATED CV in Microsoft Word format to swathi@aven-sys.com Your interest will be treated with strict confidentiality. title: Business Development and Partnerships Executive / Senior Executive
description: Job Description: Business Development and Partnerships Executive / Senior Executive Organisation Overview : ARTDIS (Singapore) Ltd . ( formerly known as Very Special Arts Singapore Ltd ) is the leading charity for arts and disability in Singapore.  Serving close to 800 beneficiaries/artists with disabilities in each year, we champion arts access and professional development for persons with disabilities.  Founded in 1993, we promote visual, performing and literary arts, and have different developmental pathways such as foundational regular classes, progressing into industry mentorship programmes and showcase platforms. Role Description: This role drives the growth of ARTDIS (Singapore) Ltd’s private-sector revenue to strengthen the Charity’s long-term financial sustainability.  It supports partnerships and business development efforts end-to-end, from initial client engagement through to project delivery. The individual will proactively cultivate and manage relationships with donors, corporate partners and new leads, working independently, and collaboratively with the team, to develop and execute initiatives that generate income and strategic value for ARTDIS. Partnership initiatives involve working with corporate partners to align their CSR and ESG objectives with ARTDIS’ mission, through projects such as commissioned artworks, staff engagement workshops, corporate gifting, and artist performances at corporate events. This role requires confidence in engaging the corporate sector, the ability to manage end-to-end partnerships and contracts, and the initiative to unlock new opportunities that support ARTDIS’ programmes and artists. Role Deliverables Grow private-sector revenue through partnerships, sponsorships and business development initiatives Secure and manage corporate partnerships aligned with ARTDIS’ mission and programmes Develop and deliver income-generating projects, from concept and proposal to execution and reporting Lead and support regular event activations, including corporate, fundraising and public-facing events, in collaboration with internal teams Manage end-to-end partnership lifecycles, including negotiations, contracts and delivery milestones Deliver key fundraising initiatives, including annual events and targeted giving campaigns Identify, cultivate and convert new corporate and donor leads into active partnerships Collaborate with internal teams to design and deliver partnership offerings such as commissioned artworks, workshops, corporate gifting and performances Ensure high-quality partner experience and long-term relationship management Track partnership outcomes, revenue targets and event deliverables against agreed objectives Contribute to strengthening ARTDIS’ financial sustainability and growth of unrestricted funding Key Skills, Competencies & Behaviours Proven track record in securing five- and six-figure partnerships through fundraising, sales or business development Strong negotiation and influencing skills, with the ability to engage and close with senior business leaders and decision-makers Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to develop donor-aligned proposals, pitch decks and reports Highly self-motivated and resilient, with the ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities Collaborative team player, able to work effectively across cross-functional teams within ARTDIS Strong alignment with ARTDIS’ mission and commitment to advancing inclusion through the arts Qualifications Minimum two years of relevant experience working with corporate clients in sales, partnerships, fundraising or project management roles Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration or a related discipline aligned to the competencies of the role Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects concurrently Demonstrated interest in, or experience working with, the disability or social sector Ability to work sensitively and respectfully with persons with disabilities We will also consider experienced candidates wishing to work on a part-time basis

title: Student Care Programme Educator (Eunos)
description: About MIJ Hub: My Inspiring Journey Hub (MIJ Hub) is a leading and rapidly growing private school and non-profit organisation (NPO) dedicated to transforming the lives of individuals aged 4 to 40 with diverse learning needs. Through our comprehensive educational programs, vocational training, and community integration initiatives, we have been creating positive change locally for over 13 years. Our mission is to be the ultimate one-stop centre for the special needs community in the region. At MIJ Hub, we are pioneers in innovation. By managing multiple for-profit ventures, including a food and beverage (F&B) arm, we generate sustainable income streams that allow us to provide subsidies, ensuring parents and students have access to affordable, high-quality education. Join MIJ Hub and become part of a vibrant, dynamic team committed to driving financial sustainability, fostering inclusivity, and expanding our reach to make a real difference in the community. Role Summary: The Special Needs Educator plays a critical role in delivering customized educational programs that cater to the unique learning needs of students with special needs. This educator will design, implement, and evaluate individualized education plans (IEPs) for students, ensuring holistic development across academic, social, and emotional domains. By fostering a nurturing and supportive learning environment, the educator will collaborate closely with multidisciplinary teams and engage with parents to drive positive student outcomes. Key Responsibilities: 1. Individualized Education Plan (IEP) Development • Collaborate with specialists (therapists, counselors, etc.) and families to design and implement personalized IEPs for each student. • Ensure the IEP aligns with the student’s goals and addresses academic, social, and behavioral needs. • Regularly assess, review, and adjust IEPs based on the student’s progress and changing needs. 2. Instructional Delivery • Develop and implement differentiated lesson plans that cater to various learning styles and abilities. • Use creative and adaptive teaching methods, including assistive technology, to meet diverse student needs. • Facilitate individual and group learning sessions, ensuring each student is engaged and supported. 3. Progress Monitoring and Assessment • Conduct ongoing formative and summative assessments to monitor student progress and document learning outcomes. • Maintain detailed records of each student’s academic performance, key observations, behavioral progress, and developmental milestones. nclusive classroom environment that promotes student engagement and active learning. • Implement positive behavior management strategies and de-escalation techniques, ensuring a safe learning space for all. • Address challenging behaviors with evidence-based interventions and document behavioral changes as needed. 4. Daily Care and Safety • Supervise and monitor students during classroom activities, outdoor play, and transitions to ensure their safety. • Follow established health and safety protocols to manage risks, prevent accidents, and maintain a safe learning environment for all students. • Be aware of and ready to implement emergency procedures and first aid protocols in case of accidents or health concerns. 5. Collaboration and Teamwork • Work closely with therapists, learning support staff, and other educators to provide a coordinated approach to student’s care. • Participate in team meetings to discuss student progress, share resources, and collaborate on strategies for improved learning outcomes. • Maintain regular communication with parents or caregivers, ensuring they are informed of their child’s progress and development. 6. [Only for SC] Shift Duties • Opening Shift : 7:30 AM to 4:30 PM. • Closing Shift : 9:30 PM to 6:30 PM. • Responsibilities during opening/closing include preparing the classrooms, ensuring safety procedures are followed, and maintaining the environment before and after school hours

title: Senior Social Worker, Families for Life @ Community, ComLink (1 Year Contract)
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Founded in 1978 as Singapore's first family service centre, Allkin Singapore is a community-centric social service agency that believes everyone has the potential to create positive change, regardless of age, race, or background. Through our service offerings, strategic partnerships, and community initiatives, we unlock opportunities for vulnerable individuals and families to overcome life's challenges, build a brighter future, and inspire others to do the same. This is a 1 year contract. RESPONSIBILITIES Assist in the planning, development, evaluation and implementation of family-related programmes Conduct information & referral, home visits and work collaboratively with stakeholders / community partners Deliver evidence-based parenting talks, workshops, programmes and family events for parents and grandparents. Support outreach and engagement efforts with stakeholders such as family coaches, school leaders, educators, community partners, and religious organisations to promote family life education. Conduct thorough needs and risk assessments, provide expert consultations, and support holistic case management. Lead case conferences and supervise intervention plans Maintain accurate casework documentation, approve case assessments, and co-lead reviews of complex cases. Strong interpersonal, communication, writing, organizational and teamwork skills. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Excellent clinical and supervision skills, and effective leadership. title: Sales Support Specialist
description: About MIJ Hub My Inspiring Journey (MIJ) Hub is a leading and rapidly growing private school and non-governmental organization (NGO) dedicated to transforming the lives of individuals aged 4 to 40 with diverse learning needs. Through our comprehensive educational programs, vocational training, and community integration initiatives, we have been creating positive change locally for over 13 years. As we strive to be the ultimate one-stop center for the special needs community in the region, we are proud to open our first international centre, MIJ International in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia., At MIJ Hub, we are pioneers in innovation. By managing multiple for-profit ventures, including a food and beverage (F&B) arm, we generate sustainable income streams that allow us to provide subsidies, ensuring parents and students have access to affordable, high-quality education. Join MIJ Hub and become part of a vibrant, dynamic team committed to driving financial sustainability, fostering inclusivity, and expanding our reach to make a real difference in the community. Job Title: Sales Support Specialist (Part-time) At MIJ Hub, the Sales Support Specialist plays a vital role in driving our sales function. This position offers an exciting opportunity to make a meaningful impact as an individual contributor. It’s ideal for someone who thrives in a customer-focused environment and is eager to bring their extensive retail sales experience to a dynamic and innovative team. In this role, you will drive sales across all MIJ brands, including student enrollment for MIJ Hub, securing donations, and promoting product sales from Ashraf Café and Inspo. You will engage with customers via calls, emails, and WhatsApp to close sales, upsell, and retain customers. Additionally, you will collaborate closely with our marketing team to ensure a seamless experience between our e-commerce platforms and sales efforts. You’ll also be responsible for utilizing HubSpot to manage customer data, track sales activities, and maintain accurate records. Key Responsibilities ● Support Sales Specialist to drive sales revenue across all MIJ brands, focusing on donations and product sales ● Utilize HubSpot to manage customer data, track sales activities, and maintain accurate Records. prepare sales materials, and ensure a seamless customer journey. ● Coordinate with other departments to support smooth and efficient sales operations. ● Address customer inquiries, resolve issues, and maintain high levels of satisfaction. ● Hands-on experience with CMS platforms, including Payload, to support smooth integration between marketing and sales workflows. ●Strong experimentation mindset, comfortable testing variations in messaging, offers, and channels to identify what drives higher engagement and conversions. Requirements ● Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Communications, or a related field. ● Exceptional interpersonal skills with a talent for building rapport and fostering long-term customer relationships. ● Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, adept at engaging with customers across calls, emails, and WhatsApp. ● Proven ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. ● Strong problem-solving skills with a proactive approach to addressing customer needs and concerns. ● Experience with CRM systems (preferably HubSpot), and a good grasp of digital tools to track and manage customer interactions. ● Ability to work closely with cross-functional teams, particularly marketing, to enhance customer engagement and drive sales. Able to commit as 3 days in a week from 9am to 4pm.

Thye Hua Kwan Moral Charities

title: Senior Lecturer (Digital Journalism)

Wee Kim Wee School of Communication and Information (WKWSCI) invites outstanding academics to apply for faculty position as Senior Lecturer in Digital Journalism. title: Senior Executive (Risk & Compliance)
description: Job Summary: This role will be critical in supporting TOUCH Community Services’ commitment to governance, risk management, and regulatory compliance. It involves identifying and mitigating risks, ensuring compliance with Singapore’s regulatory framework for charities, and fostering a culture of compliance and ethical integrity within the organisation through collaboration across teams. Principal Responsibilities and Duties: 1. Risk Management Maintain and update the organisation’s risk register, including operational, financial, and reputational risk management. Conduct regular risk assessments in partnership with programme and corporate teams. Support the development of risk mitigation plans for service delivery, fundraising, and partnerships. Coordinate crisis management planning and support incident investigations. 2. Compliance and Regulatory Adherence Ensure adherence to statutory requirements under the Charities Act, Code of Governance for Charities, and other applicable laws. Develop, review, and implement internal policies to ensure regulatory compliance. Manage filings and submissions to regulatory bodies such as the Commissioner of Charities, MSF, etc. Conduct compliance training for staff, including fundraising ethics. At least 3-5 years of experience in compliance, governance, or risk management, ideally within the social service or non-profit sector. Familiarity with the regulatory landscape for charities in Singapore. Professional certifications such as Certified Risk Management Professional (CRMP) or Certified Compliance & Ethics Professional (CCEP) are an advantage. Core Competencies and Attributes: Governance Expertise Problem Solving Effective Communication Engaging diverse stakeholders Audit Processes Policy Implementation

description: Job Description Youth Work Associate (Fixed Term, Up to Dec 2026) Position Type: Contract | Experience: 1–2 Years | Sector: Youth Services / Education Support Key Focus: School-based Intervention, Mentoring, Life-Skills Facilitation, Stakeholder Engagement. Job Description: The Youth Work Associate (School-based) is stationed within partner secondary schools to provide frontline support for at-risk students (aged 10–16). Grounded in the Positive Youth Development (PYD) framework, the incumbent facilitates social-emotional learning, conducts small to large -group interventions, and serves as a bridging mentor between the school environment and the community. School-based Engagement & Case Support •	Engagement: Build rapport with students through regular presence during in-class curriculum activity and school clubhouse. •	Engagement: Proficient in using creative arts, sports, or digital media to connect with hard-to-reach youths. •	Screening & Intake: Assist in identifying students with socio-emotional needs through school referrals and conducting initial screenings. •	Supportive Mentoring: Provide one-on-one "check-in" sessions to monitor student well-being and progress toward personal goals. •	Crisis Support: Provide immediate emotional first aid for students in distress under the guidance of the School Counsellor or CARE Team Supervisor. ____________________________________________________________________________________________ Programme Facilitation & Delivery •	Life-Skills Training: Support the delivery of structured workshops (e.g., self-management, responsible decision-making, and resilience) to classes or small groups. •	Group work: Facilitate group sessions that promote the "6 Cs" (Competence, Confidence, Connection, Character, Caring, Contribution). •	Experiential Learning: Assist in organizing and leading school-based excursions and holiday engagements that promote pro-social behaviour. ____________________________________________________________________________________________ Stakeholder Collaboration •	School Partnership: Work closely with Student Management Teams, Year Heads, and School Counsellors to align CARE’s interventions with the school’s discipline and pastoral care goals. •	Volunteer Support: Assist in liaising and coordination of volunteers/sponsors during school-based activities. ____________________________________________________________________________________________ Administration and Impact Measurement •	Data Management: Maintain accurate and timely case notes and attendance records for all school sessions. •	Outcome Evaluation: Administer pre- and post-programme assessments (e.g., Programme Evaluation System - PES) to track student development milestones. •	Reporting: Assist in preparing semester-based progress reports for school leadership. ____________________________________________________________________________________________ Professional Qualifications •	Diploma in Social Work, Youth Work, Counselling, Psychology, or a related Social Science discipline. •	1–2 years of experience working with at-risk youth, preferably within school settings. ________________________________________ Technical & Soft Skills: •	Facilitation: Strong ability to manage group dynamics and engage groups of up to 40 participants. •	Communication: Fluency in English (written and spoken). Ability to speak a second language is highly advantageous for communicating with caregivers. •	Digital Literacy: Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel is essential) and engagement tools (e.g., Canva, Mentimeter, Ahaslides). ________________________________________ Personal Attributes •	A strong passion and desire to make a positive impact on youths and young people. •	The ability to work effectively in a fast-paced and dynamic environment, particularly for event management. •	A commitment to continuous learning and a passion for serving community needs. •	Must be resilient and a dedicated team player. •	The role may require occasional work outside of standard hours. Please mention your expected salary and availability. *Only short-listed candidates will be contacted for an interview.

You'll be part of a dynamic team that values innovation and professional growth, offering you the chance to advance your career in a global environment. title: Middle School Learning Support Teacher
description: Position Responsibilities Middle School Educator Responsibilities Foster a sense of student belonging by cultivating joy and purpose throughout the Middle School. Cultivate a caring and culturally responsive learning community in Advisory, as well as the classroom, taking into account physical, emotional, and intellectual well-being of students. Experience and/or demonstrated skills facilitating restorative conversations when supporting student behavior in the learning environment. Collaborate with subject area teachers to co-identify essential learning and use a planning framework with standards, learning targets, real-world issues, and a culturally responsive curriculum. Co-design quality formative, summative and self-guided assessments that provide students with appropriate scaffolds in order to provide opportunities for frequent feedback and communicate students’ growth and achievement. Plan and implement engaging learning experiences that are inquiry-based, collaborative, culturally responsive, and differentiated. Respond to learners’ diverse needs using responsive, measurable, and research-informed interventions and extensions. Collaborate as a member of a Professional Learning Communities (PLCs) that creates a supportive community, plans curriculum, gives and receives feedback with one another, and engages in professional learning and reflection. Actively engage in professional learning and growth through regular observation and feedback cycles with Professional Learning Community Coaches and Principals. Design and implement inquiry-based learning experiences in and beyond the classroom to prompt learners to question, investigate, create, and reflect. Experience with or interest in co-teaching in a flexible learning environment. Demonstrate an understanding of interdisciplinary teaching and learning. Integrate technology in innovative ways in daily instruction to enhance learning. Engage with students in meaningful activities beyond the classroom that extend student learning experiences (coaching, organizing field trips, sponsorship of clubs, events, overseas travel and/or community service projects, etc.). Strive for continuous self-improvement as a life-long learner. Model the Core Values in your interactions with all members of the school community. Lead and engage with students in meaningful activities beyond the learning spaces, which extend learning experiences for students (supervision, overnight Classroom Without Walls trips, sports coaching, field trips, sponsorship of clubs, events, TRi Time, community service projects, etc.) Connect with and be available to parents and students during and outside of school hours (in person and via email communication) Role-Specific Responsibilities Use diagnostic assessment to determine students’ strengths and needs in literacy, numeracy, and/or executive function. Plan data-informed interventions to support the development of reading, writing, math, and executive function skills. Use a variety of progress monitoring strategies to monitor student progress toward their individualized goals Collaborate in Professional Learning Communities to review data and share progress monitoring documentation and planning for next steps for a plan of intervention. Collaborate with PLC members, students, and parents to write & implement Individual Education Support Plans with SMART goals as well as Individual Accommodation Plans. Collaborate with subject-area teachers and implement a broad range of strategies to support students in a co-taught, inclusive classroom. Participate regularly in professional development to enhance knowledge of best practices for supporting diverse learning needs and delivering targeted interventions in reading, writing, mathematics, and executive functioning. Partner with and assist subject-area teachers in developing and utilizing educational resources to facilitate differentiated student access within the classroom. Collaborates with administrators, counsellors, and specialists to support student well-being and to address social-emotional, executive functioning, behavioral, and academic goals. Position Requirements & Qualifications Bachelor’s Degree in a relevant field Master’s Degree in relevant field preferred Teaching license or a degree in education At least two years of full-time teaching experience preferred Standards-based curriculum experience preferred Excellent verbal and written English language skills Confident technology skills for teaching and administrative tasks Working Requirements Sponsoring and/or coaching after-school activities are part of the responsibility of the professional educator. Therefore, faculty shall be available to coach and/or sponsor an athletic team or other extracurricular activity. Each faculty member is expected to do a minimum of one Category 1 activity or sport per year. Required attendance at school professional learning days Chaperone and participate in evening and/or weekend school activities each year, as assigned (including “Back-to-School Night”).

title: Specialist Associate (Health Screening Coordinator)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description The Singapore Population Health Studies (SPHS) is a population-based health research initiative at the Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health, National University of Singapore. SPHS aims to investigate the interactions between lifestyle, physiological, genetic and other omics biomarkers and their impact on health and longevity. In addition, SPHS will serve as a platform to monitor risk factors in the population and gain insight into determinants of health-related behaviours. For this purpose, SPHS includes over 50,000 adult Singaporeans and long-term residents from three major ethnic groups (Chinese, Malay, and Indian), for a better understanding of disease aetiology in urban Asian settings, and the development of improved preventive and therapeutic measures for Singapore and other Asian populations. JOB PURPOSE This role is vital to the health assessment operations of the cohort studies that contribute to a Singapore population biobank. Duties & Responsibilities Participate in operational planning and the development and documentation of Work Instructions (WI) and SOPs for the data collection protocol. Source and coordinate with vendors for the procurement and supply of services or goods required for health screening operations. Perform health screening procedures according to WIs and SOPs. Examples of procedures: anthropometric measurements, blood pressure measurement, retinal imaging, ECG, visual acuity test, hand grip strength measurement, carotid ultrasound and dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DEXA). Ensure participant safety and privacy through adherence to establish guidelines and work practices. Participate in or conduct periodic technical re-assessments to ensure standardization and consistency in data collection and image acquisition. Implement new procedures for future research projects. Opportunity to take on the role of a supervisor to actively train, assess and supervise trainees involved in research operations. Qualifications Degree in Life Sciences / Biomedical Sciences or relevant fields, from recognised institutions 42-hour work week: Monday to Friday (7:45am to 6:15pm) and 2 designated Saturdays (7:45am to 3:15pm) per month. Wednesdays are rest days. Work location: NUS, Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health Ability to interact with people of various age groups and backgrounds. Fluency in spoken Mandarin/Chinese Dialects/Malay/Tamil will be an advantage, to communicate with participants who are only able to converse in these languages. Phlebotomy skill would be an advantage. Attention to detail. Able to work in a fast-paced environment and multi-task. Successful candidate will be appointed on 1-year contract in the first instance, and renewal of appointment is subject to funding and performance. WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR We are looking for a teamplayer capable of self-learning and working in a fast-paced environment, and who is service-oriented and good at time management.

Conduct detailed market and competitor analysis to identify trends, opportunities, and emerging client needs.
