title: Talent & Learning Manager (Fixed Term Contract)
description: The Opportunity… At Moët Hennessy, We Don't Just Create Exceptional Experiences – We Live Them. For generations, our 27 Maisons, including Dom Pérignon, Veuve Clicquot, and Hennessy, have defined luxury. Now, it's your turn. Imagine a career fueled by passion and creativity, where you redefine what's possible. We seek individuals who thrive in collaborative environments, sharing our love for craftsmanship, elegance, and integrity. We celebrate diversity and the pursuit of excellence. Ready to drive learning and talent innovation at a global luxury brand? As Talent and Learning Manager for Moët Hennessy in Asia Pacific, your role will involve partnering with regional and global teams to design and implement strategic L&D initiatives. Your work will directly empower our people, build essential capabilities, and cultivate an engaging and inclusive culture that is critical to our ongoing success. If you're passionate about fostering continuous growth, enhancing critical skills, and nurturing an engaging work environment, this is your opportunity to contribute to our success. This is a 9-momth fixed-term contract. How you will contribute… Learning & Development Strategy and Execution Strategic L&D Leadership: Support the development and implementation of the regional Talent & Learning strategy, aligning with regional and global objectives and leverage existing tools and platforms to foster a culture of continuous learning and critical capability enhancement across Asia Pacific. Capability & Needs Assessment: Conduct thorough skills assessments and needs analyses to identify critical skill gaps and development opportunities, directly informing the design of targeted training solutions that support the regional SOAR strategy. Program Design & Delivery: Lead the end-to-end design, development, and facilitation of a diverse learning calendar, encompassing engaging workshops, digital learning, and experiential programs, ensuring high-quality content delivery. Stakeholder Collaboration & Curation: Act as a strong people partner, collaborating effectively with global and regional teams, and engaging with external vendors to curate and deliver high-quality, relevant learning content. Performance Measurement & Improvement: Establish and implement robust evaluation methods for all learning programs and initiatives, utilizing data and feedback to drive continuous improvement and demonstrate impact. Learning Culture & Engagement: Champion a dynamic, feedback-driven learning culture, actively promoting participative learning and growth initiatives throughout the organization. Talent Development & Management: Strategic Talent Advancement: Drive regional talent investment strategies and programs, focusing on upskilling, leadership advancement, and overall capability building to support the long-term talent agenda. Talent Pipeline & Mobility: Collaborate with HR teams to actively build a robust and diverse talent pipeline within the region, ensuring talent mobility and readiness for future organizational needs. High-Potential Development: Identify and nurture high-potential talent, providing targeted development opportunities to accelerate their growth and prepare them for future leadership roles. Other: Budget Management & Operational Excellence: Manage the L&D and Talent Development budget effectively, ensuring efficient allocation of resources, and overseeing administrative processes to support the smooth execution of all programs and initiatives. Cross-Functional Support & Ad-Hoc Initiatives: Provide responsive support for various HR and business initiatives, including contributing to ad-hoc projects, preparing reports, and handling other duties as assigned to advance organizational talent objectives We are looking for unique and amazing talent. Join us if you have… 8+ years of experience in Learning & Development, Talent Management, or a similar HR function, with a strong focus on training and program delivery. Proven experience in designing, implementing, and evaluating learning programs with experience using LMS platforms Strong understanding of adult learning principles and instructional design methodologies. Excellent communication, presentation, and facilitation skills. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural and fast-paced environment. Proactive, results-oriented, and a strong team player. Proficiency in English; additional regional languages are a plus When you join Moët Hennessy… You embark on a career journey with the leaders of luxury wines, champagnes, and spirits, within the prestigious LVMH Group. You enter a world of possibility where career development opportunities across our 5 global regions and within the entire LVMH Group are unmatched. You join a passionate workforce within our internationally diverse and inclusive teams, exploring the rich heritage of our Maisons and shaping the next chapter of our story You discover our essential relationship with our terroirs and the soils that define our products. Through our Living Soils Living Together program, we are committed to regenerating soils, reducing climate impact, and empowering communities for a sustainable future. You will share in our passion for hosting, the art of living, and creating products that celebrate life’s most memorable milestones, always experienced through responsible tasting. Ready for a world of possibilities? Discover Moët Hennessy today!

title: Senior Associate - Global Trade Advisory
description: Senior Associate – Global Trade Advisory Deloitte has one of the largest Indirect Tax and Global Trade Advisory practice in Singapore, and is regarded within the firm as one of the leading and most successful service lines, with a reputation for being proactive, entrepreneurial, successful and fun to work with. This is a position with Deloitte’s rapidly expanding Global Trade practice, providing tailored advice and solutions to a broad range of clients, both domestic and multinationals looking to gain access into various markets from a regulatory, trade and customs perspective. As an Associate, you will be learning regulations and principles that form the hallmark of international trade, applying principles of the World Trade Organization (WTO) and the World Customs Organization (WCO) on market access and border taxation, working closely with the team in helping companies navigate through tariff and non-tariff barriers, devising appropriate strategies and learning to use proprietary Deloitte technology and tools in the global trade advisory arena. In addition, with increasing environmental and sustainability requirements affecting global trade today, you will also be working with the ESG team providing advisory on carbon tax, CBAM and other extended producer responsibility schemes that are becoming more relevant to global supply chains. Roles and Responsibilities: You will join a team of dedicated customs and international trade specialists, providing advice and hands-on assistance on projects involving the use of Free Trade Agreements (FTAs), HS Code classification, customs valuation and transfer pricing, export controls, supply chain management and applying for trade schemes provided by Singapore Customs and related authorities. Other responsibilities include supporting the team carry out trade compliance reviews arising from business restructurings, M&A, and working on complex customs and trade disputes involving duties, taxes and other WTO-related disciplines. In addition, you will also have the opportunity to participate in the implementation of global trade solutions for clients. This includes delivering compliance and customs solutions, advice and other assistance in the form of solution design, change management, project management and solution implementation. Requirements : You should have a degree with at least 2-4 years of experience with a major in international trade, law, accounting and finance, economics or other supply chain-related fields. Strong interest in varied work and able to meet tight deadlines, presenting complex issues clearly and concisely. Ability to synthesize complex information, critically analyze and articulate thoughts clearly. Excellent research and project management skills, together with interpersonal and communication skills. Entry level graduates who want to pursue a career in international trade are encouraged to apply.

title: SURVEY INTERVIEWER
description: Seeking independent and self driven individuals for market research. To conduct survey interview to members of public in hawker centers. Topics of surevey: Public opinion of the quality of hawker centers and housing in Singapore. Part time and Full time positions available. Flexible working hours. Retirees and students are welcome to apply.

title: Family Mentor
description:
1. Duties i. Case Management a) Conduct regular assessment of youth and caregivers using appropriate assessment tools b) Develop youth-centric case plan for each youth identified according to the programme protocol for follow ups c) Monitor and track attendance at schools and address any barriers to attendance (which may require engaging parents via home visits or other youth or parent engagement platforms) d) Attend and convene case conferences, where necessary, with school teachers and mentors, MDT, and other agencies (e.g. Social Service Offices, Family Service Centres, etc.) to coordinate support for youth and their families at a systemic and case level (e.g. ComLink+) e) Provide last mile delivery of support to families (e.g. accompanying parents and their youth for further health checks) f) Support and ease transitions across different school stages, and withdrawals from the programme g) Document case plans and notes of meetings/case conferences h) Support the needs of other children between in the family if they are not known to any social services ii. Outreach a) Organise, execute and participate in outreach activities to find and recruit potential disadvantaged families or families who need assistance together with volunteers. b) Record the outcomes of outreach to determine status of targeted youth. iii. Intake and Referral a) Receive and process referrals from a variety of sources. b) Seek consent from and determine the eligibility of families for programme components. c) For families in the region who are not eligible for the programme, to refer them to relevant social service agencies. d) Collate and maintain accurate and complete set of data on youth enrolled to the programme. iv. Engagement a) Support the organisation mapping of community needs and assets. b) Collate needs from families overseen by Tasek for possible sponsorship or donation opportunities. c) Assist with disbursement and tracking of sponsored items to youth and families. 2. Job Requirements a) Bachelor's degree in Social Work, Social Sciences or related field. b) Keen to work with disadvantaged youth and families and willing to go the extra mile to support families in need c) Good writing skills for case management andcomfortable with data collation for reporting purposes. d) Strong organisational skills, with an ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines effectively. e) Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with an ability to work collaboratively with various stakeholders including parents, caregivers, teachers, and community partners. f) A passion for uplifting individuals and families in vulnerable or disadvantaged situations, which involves a keen interest in understanding and forming relationships with individuals in need of
support while adhering to professional boundaries. g) Proficiency in Microsoft Office, familiarity with assessment tools such as FAST, CANS and platforms such as Case Connect is an advantage. h) Able to work on irregular hours due to home visit scheduling. In addition, as a Tasek Academy and Social Services staff, you will gain: ● Meaningful Impact: Your work will directly contribute to improving the lives of children and youth in our community, providing them with opportunities for growth and development. ● Professional Development: Gain valuable experience and skills in programme management and mentoring within the social services sector. ● Collaborative Work Environment: Work within a supportive team that values collaboration, innovation, and holistic community development. ● Networking: You will have the chance to network with a team of passionate individuals and partner organisations, potentially opening doors for future collaborations or job opportunities. ● Creativity and Innovation: At Tasek Academy and Social Services, we welcome fresh ideas and perspectives. This is your chance to unleash your creativity to inspire and make a difference in our community. If you are passionate about making a difference in the lives of disadvantaged youths, have experience in programme management and youth work, we would love to hear from you.  Join us at Tasek Academy and Social Services, and let's make a positive impact in our community together.

title: Retail Leasing Executive
description: About us Far East Organization is a Christian Enterprise, which develops real estate and operates businesses by serving with grace, love, integrity and honesty. Together with its Hong Kong-based sister company Sino Group, they are one of Asia’s largest real estate groups, with operations in Singapore, Malaysia, Australia, Japan, China (Mainland and Hong Kong) and USA. Far East Organization is the largest private property developer in Singapore, having developed over 780 developments across all segments of real estate including 55,000 private homes in Singapore since its establishment in 1960. Far East Organization includes three listed entities: Far East Orchard Limited, Far East Hospitality Trust and Yeo Hiap Seng Limited. Far East Organization is the winner of 14 FIABCI World Prix d’Excellence awards, the highest honour in international real estate. In 2022, Far East Organization is listed as top 50 of The Straits Time’s Singapore Best Employers Study. We believe in taking care of the total well-being of our people as they grow with us. We nurture a community where talent is developed and recognised, innovative ideas are encouraged, and achievements celebrated. Our health and well-being benefits provide physical, mental and spiritual support to our people and their families. In addition to annual leave, performance bonus, medical and insurance coverage, you can anticipate the provision of birthday leave, birthday benefits, family care leave, flexible benefits, staff rebates at F&B outlets, and special rates at our hotels, both locally and overseas. We empower you in your engagement with the wider community by providing varied activities to experience different opportunities to serve and contribute to the community. Responsibilities: Identify and prospect for potential tenants through market research, networking and outreach Negotiate lease terms for new leases and renewals Finalise lease agreements and other leasing-related documentation Conduct competitive market research and analysis to understand current trends in the real estate industry Liaise with tenants and building occupants on leasing and tenancy related matters Requirements: Diploma in Real Estate, Business or any other relevant disciplines Preferably with 3 years of experience in retail/commercial leasing Good negotiation and communication skills Ability to build rapport with diverse stakeholders Fresh graduates are welcome to apply for entry role

title: Change Management Consultant (Ref 26159)
description: Responsibilities As a Change Practitioner, you will be responsible for helping develop and execute change initiatives to enable a successful transition to the target state. You will develop metrics to measure the transition and determine appropriate actions. You are expected to be a subject matter expert, collaborate and manage the team to perform effectively. You will be responsible for team decisions, engage with multiple teams, and contribute to key decisions. Additionally, you will provide solutions to problems for your immediate team and across multiple teams. Responsibilities: • Facilitate workshops to gather insights and foster collaboration among stakeholders. • Design and implement communication strategies to ensure all team members are informed and engaged. • Analyze feedback and metrics to continuously improve change initiatives. • Mentor team members to enhance their understanding of change management principles. • Develop comprehensive reports to present findings and recommendations to leadership. Requirements • Degree / Diploma in Computer Systems, Information Systems, or similar field or equivalent education. • 3-5 years experience • Advanced proficiency in Change Management is required. • Advanced proficiency in Journey Management, Organizational Strategy, Stakeholder Management, and Change Analytics is suggested. Licence no: 12C6060

title: Acceleration Manager – Corporate Innovation & Cross-Border Programs
description: As an Acceleration Manager, you will play a pivotal role in supporting XNode’s cross-border innovation and startup acceleration activities between Singapore and China, as well as other international markets. Your responsibilities will include: · Program & Project Execution o Support the planning and end-to-end execution of corporate innovation projects and cross-border acceleration programs. o Coordinate program activities, workshops, pitch events, and stakeholder meetings. o Ensure smooth delivery of program operations, including logistics, scheduling, and reporting. · Startup & Partner Engagement o Facilitate collaboration among startups, corporates, investors, universities, and government stakeholders. o Help organize mentorship sessions, learning events, and networking opportunities for startup teams. o Actively participate in ecosystem-building initiatives and represent XNode in partner engagements. · Research & Business Support o Conduct market research, due diligence, and preliminary assessments for innovation projects and startups. o Contribute to the preparation of proposals, presentations, project documentation, and evaluation reports. o Track project timelines, budgets, and KPIs to ensure alignment with program objectives. We are looking for a dynamic individual who thrives in fast-paced, multicultural, and innovation-driven environments. · Bachelor’s degree in Business, Innovation, Economics, or a related discipline. · At least 3 years of relevant experience in consultancy, project management, stakeholder coordination, cross-border business development, or projects operation. · Strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (written and verbal). · Strong interpersonal communication, project management, organizational, and stakeholder coordination skills. · Comfortable working across cultures and adapting to different business contexts. · Legally entitled to work in Singapore (Citizen, PR, or holder of a valid work pass). Nice-to-Haves · Prior exposure to corporate innovation, startup incubation and acceleration, or international expansion projects. · Familiarity with the Singapore–China innovation ecosystem or Southeast Asia startup landscape. Why Join Us At XNode, you will: · Be part of a leading cross-border innovation platform that bridges Singapore, China, and global markets. · Work closely with startups, corporates, investors, and government agencies to drive real-world business impact. · Gain hands-on experience in program execution, ecosystem building, and international innovation trends. Join a collaborative and entrepreneurial team culture where your ideas and contributions matter.

title: Group Compliance Strategic Planning Analyst
description: The Compliance function is a strategic partner and a trusted business enabler to the Board and senior management of the bank. The Group Compliance Strategic Planning team works closely with internal stakeholders to identify and to assess regulatory risks. This collaboration also includes developing practical solutions that integrate regulations into operational requirements as well as actively shaping and promoting stronger compliance culture and literacy in the Bank. Project Management : Lead and oversee AML and AFC-related projects, ensuring end-to-end management from initiation, planning, and execution through to monitoring and closing. Provide project plans, status updates, and communication strategies. Stakeholder Engagement : Work closely with key stakeholders across compliance, data science, IT, and external vendors to align project objectives and manage dependencies, risks, and issues effectively. AML System & DA Strategy and Oversight : Act as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) for AML systems & DA models, providing insights on systems implementation, and regulatory requirements. Change and Risk Management : Prioritize and manage change requests for AML systems, coordinating adjustments to meet evolving regulatory and operational requirements while identifying and mitigating project risks. Visualizations and Reporting: Develop and maintain visual dashboards to track project performance and AML analytics. Synthesize project data and insights into concise, management-ready reports for decision-making. Special Projects : Support high-priority AFC initiatives, particularly those requiring enhanced analytical capabilities for regulatory or internal intelligence purposes. Contribute to the development of advanced data models to support AFC analytics. Training and Development: Provide guidance and training to compliance and business teams on AML systems and data-driven approaches to AFC. Share best practices on model governance and regulatory analytics. Innovation and Best Practices : Stay updated on emerging data science and analytics trends in AML and AFC. Drive the adoption of advanced techniques, such as machine learning and AI, to enhance system capabilities. Job Requirements: Degree holder 5 to 10 years of relevant working experience in the banking industry, specifically in Regulatory Compliance, AML and AFC.

title: Business Development Executive / Senior Business Development Executive

We partner with business owners, CFOs, and leadership teams to build strong corporate foundations, enhance governance and compliance, and enable sustainable, long-term growth. Our integrated platform spans Audit & Assurance, Accounting & Outsourcing, Corporate Secretarial & Compliance, Tax Compliance & Advisory, Business Advisory, and Digital Transformation. By combining technical depth with commercial insight, we help clients move from operational stability to strategic scale. At OA GOC, we value professionalism, ownership, and long-term partnership — with our clients and with our people. Job Summary As OA GOC advances its growth strategy, the Business Development Executive and Senior Business Development Executive roles are critical to expanding our client base and deepening client relationships. You will originate and convert business opportunities, collaborate with internal teams to deliver integrated solutions, and contribute measurably to group-wide revenue growth within a professional services environment.
