title: Membership Specialist (Membership Acquisition), Member & Corporate Relations
description: The Singapore National Employers Federation (SNEF) is the national trade union of employers, representing more than 3,900 employers and the interests of all sectors of the economy. In the tripartite system, SNEF is the body representing employers and it aims to be the trusted partner of the National Trades Union Congress representing the labour movement, and the Ministry of Manpower representing the Government. SNEF’s vision is to help organisations achieve sustainable growth that is supported by responsible employment practices. Therefore, the federation aims to strengthen industrial and labour relations through advancing tripartism and responsible employment practices. Industrial peace will, in turn, help organisations achieve business sustainability. The Membership Specialist, Membership Acquisition will play a pivotal role in expanding SNEF’s corporate membership base. This function is responsible for prospecting and acquisition of new members, driving engagement with key decision-makers, and ensuring strong conversion results aligned with performance metrics. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced B2B environment, maintains a high-quality prospect pipeline, and demonstrates strong ownership of revenue and growth KPIs. This is a 2-year contract role with provision of extension yearly based on performance. Responsibilities: ·        Develop and execute recruitment strategies for the full membership recruitment lifecycle: Recruitment, Conversion, and Retention of corporate members ·        Establish and maintain a strong pipeline of qualified leads through proactive prospecting, cold outreach, referrals, and participation in relevant business events and industry engagements. ·        Conduct consultative engagement with senior stakeholders (C-suite, HR Leaders) to articulate SNEF’s value proposition and drive membership interest and acquisition. ·        Maintain accurate lead tracking, conversion updates, and follow-through, ensuring high pipeline visibility and data integrity. ·        Analyse recruitment outcomes, market feedback, and business trends to refine outreach plans and enhance membership growth performance. ·        Prepare periodic reports and insights to demonstrate progress against KPIs and recommend strategic actions for continuous improvement. ·        Take on project and task as assigned by the reporting officer. Requirements: Relevant experience in sales, business development, or client engagement (typically 2 years or more), with the ability to demonstrate applied skills. Proven history of achieving or surpassing sales targets, ideally within membership-based, subscription, or professional services environments. Highly motivated, results-driven, and resilient with the ability to operate independently while collaborating effectively within a team structure. Strong relationship-building and communication capabilities, with the confidence to engage and influence external stakeholders at senior levels. Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data to optimise outreach execution and performance strategies. Basic Knowledge in Customer Relationship Management (CRM)

title: Senior Associate, Voice of Brand
description: About KK39 KK39 is the investment arm of Wee Hur Holdings Ltd., a Singapore-listed group with a long-standing reputation in real estate in Singapore and Australia. We are evolving into a professionalised multi-asset investment platform spanning Private Equity, Private Credit, and Venture Capital, and are actively strengthening our industry presence and relationships with LPs, GPs, and ecosystem partners. To support this growth, KK39 is seeking a Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) who will spearhead our branding, communications, and engagement efforts to ensure KK39’s presence in the investment community is visible, credible, and differentiated. Role Overview The Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) will act as KK39’s branding and communications lead, responsible for building a consistent external voice for the firm. This role blends strategic communications, event curation, and stakeholder engagement to elevate KK39’s profile in the investment ecosystem. You will work closely with the investment team, translating KK39’s activities, perspectives, and initiatives into compelling narratives and touchpoints that resonate with LPs, GPs, and partners. Key Responsibilities 1. Brand Strategy & Positioning · Develop and refine KK39’s brand narrative to reflect our unique positioning as a listed-group-backed investment office. · Ensure consistency of tone, voice, and design across all communication channels. · Build branding frameworks (templates, messaging guides, outreach playbooks) that the investment team can easily adopt. 2. Events & Community Engagement · Lead the end-to-end planning, coordination, and execution of KK39-hosted events (eg. LP/GP networking sessions, roundtables, thought-leadership dinners). · Manage event logistics, invitations, attendee engagement, and follow-up communications. · Build and maintain a calendar of industry events and conferences; coordinate KK39’s participation, visibility, and speaking opportunities. 3. Content & Communications · Craft thought-leadership content (LinkedIn posts, press releases, newsletters, and op-eds) to highlight KK39’s activities and perspectives. · Produce post-event summaries and follow-up campaigns to ensure engagement is maximised beyond the event itself. · Coordinate with external partners (media, PR, design vendors) as needed. 4. Stakeholder Engagement · Work alongside the investment team to strengthen relationships with LPs, GPs, and ecosystem partners by creating professional and timely touchpoints. · Track attendee feedback, engagement data, and industry response to measure brand-building effectiveness. · Support development of investor-facing materials (event decks, thought pieces, conference presence). 5. Internal Enablement · Free up the investment team by owning the operational and execution-heavy aspects of branding and events. · Maintain an internal repository of content, event templates, and standard communications for reuse. Job Requirements · Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field. · 3–5 years of experience in branding, corporate communications, or marketing within financial services, investment firms, or professional services. · Strong understanding of brand strategy, positioning, and messaging. · Excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to craft compelling narratives. · Familiarity with content creation for LinkedIn, press releases, newsletters, and thought-leadership pieces. · Ability to manage external vendors (PR agencies, media, design). · Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. · Proficiency in digital marketing tools and social media platforms. · Comfortable working in a fast-paced, multi-asset investment environment.

title: Regulatory Compliance Officer
description: Regulatory Compliance Officer About The Company Sing Investments & Finance Limited has 60 years of lending experience in the financing arena in Singapore. Our core activities include the acceptance of fixed and saving deposits from the public as well as the provision of loans and credit facilities to individuals and corporations, particularly the Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) in Singapore. The relationships that have been built over the years have earned us the reputation as one of Singapore's trusted finance companies. We are seeking dynamic individuals to grow with us and to continue building relationships with our valued customers. We are seeking a dynamic, motivated, and detail-oriented Compliance Officer, who will assist to develop and drive action plans and strategies for the business, engaging both internal/external stakeholders to ensure that they are in full compliance with the company’s prescribed rules, procedures, policies and regulations. The successful candidate will be responsible for tracking regulatory developments, supporting policy governance, maintaining regulatory risk assessments, and assisting in the execution of compliance assurance activities as well as the measures put in place to address the Anti-Money Laundering (“ AML ”), Countering the Financing of Terrorism (“ CFT ”), and Proliferation Financing (“ PF ”). Key Responsibilities: Regulatory intelligence and impact assessment Monitor and track relevant regulatory updates and emerging risks. Assist in impact analysis and communication of regulatory changes to key stakeholders. Maintain a regulatory change tracker and coordinate with business units for timely action. Policy and procedures governance Assist in the periodic review and maintenance of compliance policies, standards, and procedures. Ensure proper documentation, version control, and communication of policy updates. Regulatory risk and control self-assessment (“ RRCSA ”) Support the maintenance of the RRCSA, including periodic updates of risks, controls, and ratings. Facilitate stakeholder engagement for control validations and risk reviews. Compliance assurance and monitoring Assist in the planning and execution of compliance assurance reviews across business units. Document testing results, control effectiveness, and areas for improvement. Follow through on agreed remediation plans. Support AML/CFT/PF Initiatives Assist in improving AML/CFT framework and governance for the business and to provide support in related projects. Management reporting and record-keeping Support the collation and preparation of compliance-related management information and dashboards. Ensure accurate reporting and maintain records for audit and regulatory review purposes. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in law, finance, business, or related field preferably with at least 3 to 5 years’ experience in a compliance role or  years’ relevant experience in a Big 4 advisory firm or the compliance function of a MAS-regulated financial institution. Exposure to regulatory compliance, assurance, internal controls, or policy governance. Strong knowledge of Singapore financial regulations (e.g., MAS Notices, Guidelines). Familiarity with risk management frameworks and assurance methodologies. Digital mindset and fluency Sharp analytical and critical thinking skills. Strong written and verbal communication. Highly organised, meticulous, and initiative taking. High level of integrity and  accountability. Collaborative with a learning mindset and growth-oriented attitude. Ability to prioritise and manage multiple deliverables under tight deadlines. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Remuneration and corporate rank will be commensurate with experience.

title: COURSE DEVELOPER
description: AA International is seeking a talented and creative Course Developer to join our team. The Course Developer will play a key role in designing, developing, and implementing engaging and effective learning experiences for our clients. This role offers the opportunity to collaborate with subject matter experts to create high-quality courses that meet the needs of our target audience and support our company's mission and goals. Responsibilities: Collaborate with subject matter experts, instructional designers, and other stakeholders to identify learning objectives, content requirements, and course outcomes. Design and develop course content, instructional materials, and assessments using a variety of instructional design models, principles, and technologies. Create engaging and interactive learning experiences that incorporate multimedia elements, such as videos, animations, simulations, and interactive exercises. Write clear and concise instructional content, learning objectives, and assessments that align with course objectives and target audience needs. Conduct quality assurance checks and review course content for accuracy, consistency, and alignment with instructional design standards and best practices. Stay up-to-date with emerging trends, technologies, and best practices in instructional design and e-learning development, and recommend new approaches and strategies to enhance course effectiveness. Communicate effectively with stakeholders and project team members to provide updates on project status, solicit feedback, and address any issues or concerns. Assist with the evaluation and assessment of course effectiveness, gather learner feedback, and make recommendations for course improvements and revisions. Qualifications: Minimum Diploma in any discipline. Possess WSQ Diploma in Design and Development of Learning for Performance (DDDLP) or WSQ Diploma in Adult and Continuing Education (DACE) will be an advantage. Experience in course development, or curriculum design, preferably in public training courses. Minimum 5 years' experience in Workplace Safety & Health, Proficiency in instructional design principles, learning theories, and adult learning methodologies. Strong technical skills, including proficiency with instructional design software, e-learning authoring tools, and multimedia editing software. Excellent writing and editing skills, with the ability to create clear, engaging, and effective instructional content. Creative thinking and problem-solving abilities, with a passion for innovation and continuous improvement in course design and development. Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines. Ability to work effectively both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.

title: Assistant Manager, Finance
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION The Salvation Army has been touching lives in Singapore since 1935 and we remain committed in serving the underprivileged in the community without discrimination. To us, every life is precious and we actively help those in need regardless of age, race or religion. Throughout the years, we have established a comprehensive network of social services to meet a wide range of needs in our community. Our diverse programmes reach out to children who need care and protection, families in material need, ailing aged requiring quality nursing care and other needy segments in our society. Our spiritual ministry also reaches out to the people in the community to provide counsel to those in need. We are there to help guide those in need towards finding their identity and purpose in life. RESPONSIBILITIES ACCOUNTABILITY You will be accountable to the Manager, Finance. In the absence, you will be accountable to the Head, Finance. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES Financial Management and Reporting Responsible for assisting in budgeting, financial planning including cash flow management and analysis of the financial performance of the group. Ensure accurate and timely completion in all areas of financial and management accounting activities including but not limited to monthly, year-end closing of accounts, audited financial statements and accounts schedules. Assist in preparation of financial reports and necessary materials for presentation, as needed. Stakeholder engagements Establish collaborative business partnering relationships with various business units and lead the team in improving existing policies and processes and transform it through the active adoption of digital tools for better efficiency and costs savings. Finance operations, systems and processes streamlining Assist to provide supervision over the Finance operations, to review and ensure its quality and accuracy. Develop and implement processes and procedures to enhance workflows of the department to drive continuous productivity improvement on accuracy, efficiency and simplicity. Support and contribute to systems implementation projects and its change management tasks. Support new and on-going projects to enhance Finance process and controls. Ensure compliance with organisation policies, boards, councils' decisions, and government statutory legislations. Others To carry out any other duties as assigned by Management from time to time. QUALIFICATIONS Degree in Accountancy with ACCA/CPA or other professional accounting qualification 5 - 8 years of relevant experience, with at least 3 years in a supervisory role Preferably with knowledge of charity and/or non-profit finance experience Proficiency in Oracle Fusion, MS Office applications (Advanced Excel skills) and data visualisation tools will be advantageous Good internal and external stakeholder management skills Possess strong sense of responsibility, initiative with good interpersonal and communication skills Positive attitude with a strong desire to learn Resilient and confident in management of ambiguity Fast learner who can work independently and as a team Good organisational skills with the ability to multi-task and prioritise projects/task OTHER INFORMATION DUTY HOURS The total work hours is 44 hours per week. Under normal circumstances the hours of duty will be as follows: 8.30am to 5pm - Monday to Friday (1 hour lunch break) It will be at the discretion of your Centre/Department in charge or his/her assigned representatives to determine whether you will be required to work any of the unutilised hours according to the operational needs of the Centre/Department and within the legitimacy of the employment laws where applicable. Working Location Singapore Regional Headquarters - Finance Department 20 Bishan Street 22, Singapore, 579768 By submitting this job application, I affirm that I have given to The Salvation Army true and complete information on this application. No requested information has been concealed. I agree that The Salvation Army (TSA) may collect, use and disclose your personal data, as provided in this application form, or (if applicable) obtained by TSA as a result of your employment, for the following purposes in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 and our data protection policy (available at our website at https://www.salvationarmy.org.sg/data-protection-policy/ ): a) the processing of this employment application; b) the administration of the employment with TSA; and c) sharing of personal information to third party, including but not limited to government agencies, hospitals, vendor(s)/supplier (s), working in collaboration with TSA to support the employment. If you are providing third party's personal data, you hereby declare that you have obtained consent from the named individual(s), for the collection, use and disclosure of his/her personal data by you to TSA, TSA business partners and other third-party service providers. I authorised the organisation to contact references provided for employment reference checks. Please visit our website at https://www.salvationarmy.org.sg/data-protection-policy/ for further details on our data protection policy, including how you may access and correct your personal data or withdraw consent to the collection, use or disclosure of your personal data. If any information I have provided is untrue, or if I have concealed material information, I understand that this will constitute cause for denial of employment or immediate dismissal. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - The Salvation Army Singapore

title: Operations Support Assistant
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION MINDS has been caring for Persons with Intellectual Disabilities (PWIDs) and their families since 1962. Grounded in the belief that all PWIDs have innate abilities and talents, MINDS is committed to empowering them and enhancing their quality of life, while advocating for greater inclusivity for PWIDs as a nation. MINDS is today one of the largest charities in Singapore employing more than 850 staff. With the key focus to expand community-based services, improving the employability of PWIDS, strengthening education, and engendering inclusive community living, MINDS offers a holistic range of services and programmes across the PWIDs' lifespan. These include schools, employment and training development centres, home-based care services and community-based services to cater to the physical, psychological, environmental and social needs of PWIDs. For more information, please visit www.minds.org.sg. RESPONSIBILITIES The Operations Support Staff assists to ensure smooth running in the day-to-day operations of the adult services centre. This includes, but is not limited to, direct care for clients, facility management and maintenance, operational support, support for centre activities and events, and simple administrative tasks. Client Care Assist in client movement, personal hygiene and toileting Clean soiled areas and equipment Assist centre staff on client support as needed. Centre Events/Activities Put up/remove decorations for special festivities and events Packing and distribution of items for programmes and events Arrange refreshments for meetings and functions Facility Management Attend to simple faults and repairs Assist in facility inspections and reporting, including safety checks, faults and defects Assist the Environment Control Coordinator (ECC) in cleaning and disinfection of surfaces in contact with bodily discharge Assist ECC in checking for potential vector breeding areas Administrative Support Sorting, packing and shredding of documents Simple filing of documents Simple data entry QUALIFICATIONS Completed Primary Education At least 3 years of related experience [Any other specific requirements e.g. experience with methodology or previous projects done, etc.] Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - MINDSG LTD

title: Senior Education Executive / Educational Therapist
description: Job Title : Senior Education Executive / Educational Therapist Reports to : Education Manager Supervise : Education Executive Chen Su Lan Methodist Children’s Home (CSLMCH) is dedicated to transforming the lives of children and families in need. Our mission is to foster healing, growth, and self-reliance within our community, emphasizing family reunification and improved psycho-social well-being. Why Join Us: Competitive pay: Motivating financial package and regular bonuses. Competitive benefits and wellness package Career growth and Development Support Work Life Balance And so much more Position Objective: A qualified executive with experience in teaching and delivering individualized intervention to join our team of multidisciplinary professionals who embraces the best interest of child and youth. He/she is committed towards the methodical planning, coordinating and executing of all educational-related policies/programmes/activities. The primary role of the executive is to provide tiered intervention to children/youths in consultation with the multidisciplinary team to promote the child/youth’s academic and personal excellence. The education executive is also responsible for working closely with key stakeholders to train and coach them, develop curriculum, plan, organize and implement programmes and activities for the children and youths, manage volunteers and conduct needs and outcome assessments to measure and enhance programme relevance and effectiveness to bring about quality service and standards. Key Responsibilities: A) Delivers effective and efficient individualised intervention to children/youths - Manage children and youths’ behaviour for the purpose of providing a safe and positive learning environment. - Create a positive learning culture using a trauma-informed inclusive education approach in the House of Wisdom. - Utilize data-based decision-making and evidence-based strategies to provide Individual Educational Plans (IEPs) and intervention plans for the children/youths with their respective case executives and in consultation with the Education Manager. - Working with the school teachers to identify learning objectives for lesson planning with differentiated instruction. - Plan, prepare, and deliver small group intervention in Social Emotional Learning to develop learners’ social-emotional and life skills. - Provide academic support for learners according to their abilities and in alignment with mainstream or special school curriculum objectives. - Coordinate and collaborate with colleagues e.g., caseworkers, therapy team and external stakeholders e.g. parent/guardians, schools, tutors, and volunteer tutors to deliver effective interventions to increase academic and personal success. - Establish positive, safe, and trusting relationships with children/youths, their families and school teachers to encourage academic and personal progress specific to their needs. - Commit to the continual professional development of not less than 50 training hours in any 2 years, which shall consist of at least 10 hours in Pedagogy training to keep current with the latest skills and ensure professional competency, subject to approval by the immediate supervisor. B) Plan, organize and execute programmes and activities relevant and beneficial for the children and youths. - Be the main liaison person to communicate and work with potential stakeholders such as organisations, vendors and volunteers, to bring in appropriate educational programmes that meet the needs of children/youths. - Track the attendance of participants of the programme and plan for the tuition scheduling of the volunteers/tutors on a monthly basis. - Take the lead in coordinating, supporting, executing and providing the oversight to centre’s planned events and programmes. - Develop and facilitate programmes that need support. - Conduct periodic needs and outcome assessments to evaluate the effectiveness and relevance of key programmes. C) Regularly assesses children’s/youths progress to modify instruction/intervention - Develop outcome measures and using data-driven approaches to track the development of learners. - Collect and analyze screening as well as progress monitoring data to identify child’s/youth’s needs, modify instruction, and report progress to all stakeholders. - Summarize assessment results and incorporates information into an appropriate support plan. - Prepare relevant submissions/reports on a termly or ad-hoc basis regarding children/youths’ learning, progress development and recommendations for intervention. - Maintain updated and accurate records which adhere to high levels of confidentiality. - Coordinate, facilitate, and maintain records on meetings with parents, school teachers, and internal/external stakeholders. D) Provides consultation and coaching to supervisees, volunteer tutors, school teachers, parents, interns and other stakeholders to foster greater academic and social/emotional skill development across all children and youths - Recruit and manage volunteers; conduct virtual and on-site training sessions for volunteers. - Serve as a resource and mentor to maintain coaching relationships with the volunteer tutor team/supervisees/interns to ensure positive classroom culture and successful learning outcomes. - Understand each volunteer tutor’s and supervisee’s needs and problem-solve on ways to better improve their experience and support their development. - Implement standards of instructional quality with individual volunteer tutors and supervisees. - Observe/evaluate instruction effectively and provide actionable coaching to support the continuous growth of volunteer tutors and supervisees on instructional delivery and classroom culture. - Participate in a multi-disciplinary team of professionals by utilizing a problem-solving approach. - Prepare and disseminate professional resources according to learners’ needs. - Establish and maintain ongoing professional collaboration with internal and external stakeholders in order to maximize children/youths’ impact in all areas of development. E) Development of curriculum and teaching resources - Design curriculum content closely aligned to Singapore's MOE syllabus and Singapore Examinations and Assessment Board requirements. - Devise and refine curriculum framework and teaching pedagogical framework with instructional materials that meet the learners’ varying needs. - Ongoing review of curriculum to ensure the education programme at House of Wisdom remains dynamic, evidence-based, and responsive to the children’s and youths’ learning needs. - Conduct research and stay informed on the latest examination trends F) Provide Academic Pathways Guidance & Career Guidance - Provide education and career counselling to children/youths based on their learning abilities and career/academic aspirations so as to make good academic choices/career prospects. - Coordinate, partner with/network with various Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) or companies for visits, learning journeys or taster workshops. G) Support Research and Productivity Improvement Efforts - Identify research-related and productivity-improvement-projects information that is of interest to the home. - Conduct a literature review. - Keep proper records of past and ongoing research and productivity improvement projects funded by relevant agencies. - Track various ongoing research and highlight progress to ensure compliance with agreed-upon schedules of evaluation. H) Supported Employment - Develop individualised employment plans with youth and incorporate input from the caseworkers when appropriate. Adjust the plan according to the youth’s needs and preferences. - Conduct job development and job search activities. Assess the client’s vocational functioning on an ongoing basis. Provide individualised on-site job coaching to assist youths. - Provide education and support to employers, which may include negotiating job accommodations and on-site client supervision and support with frequent employer communication. - Prioritize employer relations and solutions-based thinking through timely and proactive communication. Use conflict resolution skills to provide timely interventions. Return phone calls, follow up on job leads, and meet with youths consistently to resolve job issues. I) Any other duties - Review and update all educational policies, SOPs, programmes, children/youths’ education case files/data/information. - Perform the secretariat role for planned and/or ad-hoc meetings where required. - Perform administrative tasks related to volunteer programmes and funding applications for programme reporting and audit purposes. - Work with internship coordinators from IHLs to ensure the successful implementation of the internship programme. - Take on any other assignment, project or duty as assigned by Education Manager. Position Requirements - Minimum of 5 years of teaching and curriculum writing experience - Relevant Bachelor / Master Degree and diploma in education / special education - Excellent lesson delivery skills - Good interpersonal communication and professional collaboration skills. - Experience as a mentor who inspires, develops, and empowers direct reports. - Demonstrate flexibility, strong work habits, and a positive attitude. - Knowledge and experience in delivering the intervention for individuals with various aspects of learning challenges and social-emotional difficulties. - Knowledge of the principles and methodology of effective teaching of inclusive education is preferred. - Ability to work flexible hours including weekday evenings (3 week nights per week) What are you waiting for: Apply now and join us today with a dedicated team committed to making a difference in the lives of vulnerable children and families. You’ll have the opportunity to lead impactful initiatives, collaborate with passionate colleagues, and contribute to meaningful community-driven solutions. We work closely with community partners to bring holistic support and care to the children and families . Together we make a difference on the lives of the children in the Home. Together We Nurture Every Child and Family to Shine. Only Shortlisted applicant will be notified. Interested applicants may send their CV via this portal OR to hr@cslmch.org.sg. (You only need to choose one of these channels to submit your CV.)

title: Donor Relationship Assistant Manager / Manager
description: The Donor Relationship Assistant Manager / Manager (RM) serves as the key point of contact for our community of Donor-Advised Fund (DAF) holders. This role builds trusted relationships, ensures a seamless donor experience, and helps donors translate their philanthropic intentions into meaningful impact. Asia Community Foundation (ACF) is the first Asia-wide platform for collaborative and strategic giving. We enable donors to pool resources, co-create solutions, and drive positive social and environmental impact across the region. As we expand our donor base and manage a growing portfolio of donor-advised funds (DAFs), impact funds, and bespoke giving vehicles, we are strengthening our financial leadership to steward philanthropic capital with the highest standards of accountability, transparency, and impact. Established in 2022 by a group of philanthropists from Asia Philanthropy Circle, ACF was born out of the pressingneed for a regional platform to facilitate cross-border giving in Asia. Our mission is to promote purposeful giving in Asia by connecting donors and nonprofits to achieve greater results than they could on their own. As a community, we believe that we can create positive change and address some of the most pressing socioeconomic and environmental issues facing Asia today. Key Responsibilities: Coordinate onboarding for all new DAF holders, working closely with the rest of the Development and Engagement team to ensure proper setup and documentation of each fund. Act as the day-to-day liaison for donor queries, grant requests, and general support. Build a deep understanding of each donor’s interests, giving patterns, and philanthropic goals. Prepare donor-facing summaries, grant histories, and tailored updates in collaboration with relevant colleagues. Ensure all DAF donor and prospect interactions and updates are  recorded in a timely and accurate manner in CRM systems. Support internal knowledge-sharing to equip the broader team with donor insights and engagement tools. Monitor DAF activity to identify underutilized or inactive funds and recommend appropriate engagement strategies. Collaborate with the larger ACF team to propose thematic giving opportunities, portfolio funds, or curated nonprofit recommendations. Coordinate and organize one-on-one check-ins, or learning events to encourage active giving and deeper engagement with DAF holders. Track and report on donor engagement metrics, including fund activity, event participation, and relationship depth. Any other initiatives or events related to DAF donor engagement and pipeline building as assigned. Requirements: At least 5 years’ experience in relationship management, stewardship, or client services, preferred, background in non-profit or philanthropy is an advantage. Strong interpersonal and communication skills, as well as customer service ethos,  with the ability to build trust and rapport across diverse donor profiles. Excellent organizational skills with attention to detail and follow-through. Collaborative, solutions-oriented mindset and ability to work effectively across teams. Passion for social impact and the mission of advancing giving across Asia. If you’re passionate about creating meaningful impact, we invite you to submit your latest cover letter and CV.

title: Retirement Savings (GSO) Executive
description:
What the role is At #TeamCPF, you’re not just joining a team; you are embracing a culture of excellence, collaboration, and meaningful impact. You will play a pivotal role in empowering over 4 million members to secure their retirement, healthcare, housing needs and better navigate life’s uncertainties. We thrive on sharp minds and insightful decisions. Your ability to analyse and think critically isn't just valued; it's essential. Every choice you make contributes to our collective success. Collaboration is our way of life. We believe in the power of effective partnerships and seamless communications across teams. Together, we amplify each other’s strengths and achieve remarkable results. Our learning never stops. We encourage your inquisitiveness and courage to embrace new challenges head-on. Your agility, readiness to challenge conventions, embrace of data-driven strategies, dedication to learning and applying new skills fuels our innovation and progress. At the core of everything we do lies a genuine desire to make a difference. We serve our community and support each other with compassion, empathy, and unwavering dedications. Every action we take is guided by a deep sense of purpose and a commitment to those we serve. Join us at #TeamCPF! Together, let's redefine possibilities and leave a legacy that echoes for generations. What you will be working on The team you are joining is dedicated to empowering Singaporeans to take charge of their financial health throughout their lives through comprehensive financial guidance products and services. In this role, you will: Provide support to our Senior/ Managers in effectively executing operational activities, launching
innovative financial solutions, and fostering public engagement and awareness. Execute administrative tasks and provide operational support to ensure the smooth running of daily operations, the successful launch of new products and services, and the implementation of initiatives that drive awareness and encourage usage. Handle enquiries from CPF members relating to financial guidance products, services and CPF retirement schemes and provide clear and accurate information. Deliver training workshops on CPF financial guidance products, services and retirement schemes to internal staff. Serve as a knowledge resource personnel at public outreach events to offer insights on our financial
offerings. Proactively identify and communicate potential areas for process improvement to supervisors. Review and implement process enhancements where applicable to optimise workflow and
service delivery. What are we looking for We value the diverse talents
and experiences that each individual brings to the table. While mastery of
every requirement may not be necessary, familiarity and expertise in some of
the following areas will position you for success within this team. Relevant experience in administration or operations function. Interest in financial literacy, and ability to present information clearly and effectively to various
audiences. Ability to handle public inquiries with professionalism and empathy. Self-motivated individual with a strong dedication to delivering exceptional service and
operational excellence. Good organisational skills with proficiency in numerical, written, and verbal communication Proficient in basic productivity tools (e.g., Microsoft Office) and eager to learn and apply new
digital tools, such as generative AI and robotic process automation. The seniority of appointment and actual corporate job title will commensurate with individual work experiences. Position is on a 1-year full-time contract directly under the payroll of CPF Board with an option to renew, contingent upon confirmation and subject to organisational needs. Additionally, there is potential for emplacement into a permanent position. What you can expect Being part of #TeamCPF means embarking on a challenging and rewarding career in a progressive workplace that values productivity and growth. Here’s what awaits you: Opportunities to engage in a mix of formal and informal training, keeping your skills sharp in our ever-evolving technological landscape. Promotion opportunities based on your capability and on-the-job performance. A vibrant community of like-minded and friendly colleagues, where collaboration and creativity thrive. A hybrid work model that offers flexibility for remote work, subject to exigencies of service. Flexible dress code that empowers you to choose your appropriate outfit for the day. A comprehensive rewards package that includes annual leave, pro-family leave, medical and dental benefits, and access to recreational activities.

title: Customer Service and Admin Team Lead
description: Tuition Centre in Ang Mo Kio Requires Customer Service & Admin Team Lead Customer Service & Admin Team Lead Job Description: 1. Team Leadership & Supervision •	Lead, manage, and support a team of three (3) Customer Service & Admin Specialists in their daily responsibilities. •	Lead regular team huddles to brief the team on updates, operational instructions, and expectations. •	Allocate tasks, set priorities, and ensure all work is completed accurately and on time. •	Monitor team performance, provide coaching and guidance, and ensure consistent customer service standards. •	Act as the first point of escalation for administrative issues before involving the Head of Operations. •	Ensure SOPs and workflows are followed, and identify opportunities for continuous improvement to increase efficiency. 2. Centre Management & Frontline Experience •	Oversee daily centre operations, ensuring the centre is neat, organised, and welcoming at all times. •	Greet and engage students and parents in a friendly and professional manner, while ensuring the team upholds the same service standard. •	Manage monthly pantry supply orders (including F&B and cleaning supplies) and coordinate with cleaning vendors to maintain centre cleanliness. •	Take ownership of the centre’s day-to-day cleanliness and overall physical presentation. 3. Daily Operations & Customer Handling •	Handle customer enquiries across all channels, including phone, text, walk-ins, social media, and email. •	Ensure timely responses and satisfactory resolution of customer feedback and concerns. •	Step in to manage complex or sensitive cases and escalate when necessary. •	Prepare and send clear, timely communications to parents and students regarding programmes, schedules, fees, and centre updates, including last-minute changes. •	Assist in organising events and promotional activities (e.g. webinars) to attract new students and retain existing ones, in collaboration with the marketing team. 4. Student Administration, Billing & Records •	Process student registrations, course enrolments, and payment matters accurately and in a timely manner. •	Prepare and issue monthly billing invoices to parents and follow up on payment matters. •	Maintain and audit student and staff records in the iEduCentre system, ensuring data accuracy through both direct input and team oversight. 5. Escalation, Reporting & Support •	Act as the first point of escalation for operational and customer-related issues. •	Report regularly to the Head of Operations on admin operations, recurring issues, and team performance. •	Support ad-hoc operational needs and assist other departments when required. 6. Tutor & Academic Administrative Support •	Assist tutors with administrative matters when necessary, including the printing of class materials and mailing of study resources to students. Requirements : Minimum Diploma Working hours : 4 weekdays (1pm to 9.30pm) + 1 weekend (Sat) (9am - 7pm) Passionate in customer service 2-3 years experience in customer service Experience in a supervisory role will be good Confident and tactful in handling customer’s needs Strong interpersonal and communication skills Positive work attitude, self-driven, independent, team player with a strong service mindset Meticulous and systematic with administrative procedures Skilled in basic office skills (excel, word) and computing skills Prior experience in frontline customer service jobs will be a bonus Able to handle difficult customers confidently

title: Graduate Officer, Compliance
description: At Bank of Communications, we are dedicated to fostering talent and innovation in a dynamic and supportive environment, providing opportunities for growth and development. We are excited to invite enthusiastic and driven fresh graduates to apply for the position of Graduate Officer in our Compliance department. This is an entry-level role designed to provide on the job training and hands-on experience in the chosen field. You will work closely with seasoned professionals to gain valuable insights, contribute to meaningful projects and develop a strong foundation for your career. · Report to Head of Compliance · Assist in monitoring and ensuring adherence to company policies and regulatory requirements · Support the development and implementation of compliance and AML program and initiatives · Conduct research and analysis to identify potential compliance risks · Collaborate with various departments to ensure compliance with internal and external standards · Assist in AML/CFT name screening on a daily basis in a timely manner · Coordinate with relevant departments/personnel to resolve any name screening issues including system issues · Assist in the preparation of AML/CFT name screening statistics and reporting · Assist in the preparation of training and guidance materials relating to name screening · Undertake any other additional tasks and responsibilities assigned by department head REQUIREMENTS · Recent graduates with a Bachelor’s degree from reputable Universities · Strong interest in compliance and eagerness to learn · Strong analytical skills, excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills · Good communication skills both verbal and written with ability to interact comfortably with a variety of stakeholders.

title: Training & Event Executive (Junior Position)
description: A) WHAT WE DO AT VALUE INVESTING ACADEMY A 5-Room resale HDB flat can cost more than S$1 million nowadays. Our official retirement age has been increasing as we live longer. Costs of living have also increased significantly over the years. So, what should most people do? Work harder, and longer? We recognised the challenges faced by everyone residing in Singapore and our Vision at Value Investing Academy (ViA) is to "Support Anyone to Invest at their Fingertips!" so that they can improve their financial well being through value investing which was founded more than 100 years ago by the late Prof Benjamin Graham at Columbia Business School. Our mission is “ We Care to Make You a Better Investor ”. Through our community with thousands of members in Asia and learning platforms, we have so far trained at least 50,000 people in Asia. Countries and cities that we have impacted include Malaysia (Kuching, Kuala Lumpur, Penang), Thailand (Bangkok), Cambodia (Phnom Penh), Vietnam (Ho Chi Min City), Hong Kong, Japan (Tokyo) and of course our own home, Singapore. B) WHY JOIN US 1. We are the leading Value Investing Educational training company that runs the largest Value Investing Programme in Singapore and Asia . You will have a chance to be the pioneer in spearheading new strategies. 2. We are result oriented and we believe in compensation that correlates with results . Our Top Performers in FY 2024 received a total of 8 months bonus. You are the limit! You are the limit. 3. We give you the space to do crazy things as long as it works . We are unlike MNCs or big companies. We grow by breaking the rules. We constantly challenge ourselves to scale new heights by doing new things. If you dislike red tape, this is the place. 4. You will get a chance to meet very successful people in the speaking industry. Our past Keynote Speakers include Mary Buffett, the Internationally Acclaimed Author and Speaker of How Billionaire Warren Buffett invests, Lauren Templeton, etc. 5. You will get a chance to travel overseas in our overseas projects and have a chance to learn new cultures and have Fun! 6. Regular lunches, Movie Shows, Popcorns, Makan, etc! We do these regularly after every project! The best part is we do ALL these Fun things during office hours! 7. Office politics is a dirty phrase, and we eliminate any politics and bureaucracy 8. We constantly DO New Things. If you dislike routines and wanted to learn New Things, you will get a chance. 9. Most importantly, you will learn from the Founder, Cayden Chang, himself. (C) WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR 1. You are someone who has at least 3 years of full-time wortking experience with at least 1 year of experience in organizing events from beginning to the end as a project leader 2. You are independent and proactive in getting things done without much supervision. You are honest, meticulous, responsible and mature in personality 3. You can work over weekends once every month and able to work in weekday evenings around once/twice per month (We love the moon and the stars) and you will be compensated with off-in-lieu. 4. You enjoy working with people (About 60% deskbound, and 40% interaction with our customers) 5. Familiar with Microsoft Office, Excel and PowerPoint 6. Effectively Bilingual (D) WHAT YOU DO 1) Marketing and managing workshops, following up with emails/sms 2) Handling all customer enquiries and managing event on that day 3) Managing all paperwork related to the training 4) Post-Course enquiries and support E) TO APPLY, YOU NEED TO DO THE FOLLOWING 1) Provide cover letter why we should meet you. 2) Email résumé to resume@mindkinesis.com NOTE: Please note that we don't read off-the-shelve résumé.

title: Research Fellow in Cybersecurity
description: 1. Purpose of Position This role contributes to research-based activities in the field of Cybersecurity at Curtin Singapore. 2. Accountabilities and Responsibilities Leadership & Service • Contribute to the development of a collegial and supportive working environment. • Foster and promote relationships with industry and affiliated associations, and the wider community. Research In conjunction with the Head of School of Science, Technology, and Engineering, agree on a program of research that may include but is not limited to: • Undertaking high-quality research in cybersecurity. • Work closely with industry partners on key projects as agreed with the Head of School of Science, Technology, and Engineering. • Participating in applications for competitive funding as appropriate. • Disseminating research findings through seminars, workshops, and conferences. • Publishing in high-quality refereed journals in a manner consistent with disciplinary practice. • Where appropriate, providing effective co-supervision of research students. Other • Engage in appropriate professional development and mentoring programs. • Undertake administrative functions associated with research activities as required. • Model a high standard of professional behaviour consistent with the University Code of Conduct and its Vision, Mission and Values. • Undertake other activities as directed by the Head of School of Science, Technology, and Engineering and/or the PVC and President. 3. Selection Criteria Applicants are not required to address each element of the selection criteria but should provide sufficient information in their application to enable the selection panel to make an informed assessment of their suitability for the role. Selection Criteria for Curtin Singapore Research Fellow Essential 1. A doctoral qualification in Cybersecurity. 2. Evidence of quality research outputs consistent with the discipline (relative to opportunity). 3. Demonstrated high-level communication and interpersonal skills with a commitment to the development of a collegial and supportive working environment and the ability to interact with students and staff with cross-cultural sensitivity. Desirable 1. Demonstrated ability to supervise HDR students (relative to opportunity).

title: EY1/ EY2 Infant Teacher - Islandwide [SL]
description: Responsibilities: Applies child development knowledge to promote and support the learning and holistic development of the infants and toddlers. Leads in planning and implementing a child – centered intentional programme based on a cyclical process of observation, documentation and evaluation to facilitate children’s learning needs and interest in accordance to Early Years Development Framework (EYDF). Establishes and ensures strong partnership with families by communicating and sharing information and knowledge in child development to facilitate the continuity of learning between centre and homes. Complies with government regulations and quality assurance framework. Requirements: Has Certificate in Infant and Toddler Care and Development (CIT) / Higher Certificate in Infant Care (HCIC) / Advanced Certificate in Early Years (ACEY) Has registered with ECDA and attained the EY1/ EY2 certification We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers. EA License No: 20C0270 EA Personnel No: R22110981 EA Personnel Name: Law Mei Fong

title: Assistant Manager, Data & Impact Analytics
description: Why This Role Matters At New Life Stories (NLS), data is not just about numbers — it’s about children, families, and systems of care. As Assistant Manager, Data & Impact Analytics, you will play a pivotal role in turning programme data into credible evidence, meaningful insights, and compelling impact stories that guide decisions, strengthen accountability, and support advocacy. This role sits at the intersection of service delivery, impact measurement, and organisational strategy — ideal for someone who wants their data skills to drive real social change. What You Will Do 1. Data Systems & Infrastructure You will ensure that NLS has strong, reliable data foundations. Manage and maintain our Case Management System (CMS / Salesforce CRM), including user access and data integrity Set up clear, consistent data entry protocols and documentation Train staff on digital tools to ensure quality and consistency of data Maintain PDPA-compliant data storage and security structures 2. Impact Measurement & Analysis You will transform raw data into insights that improve programmes and outcomes. Oversee digital assessment tools measuring child and family outcomes (e.g. literacy, resilience, caregiver burden, quality of life) Track programme performance against outcome targets with internal teams Analyse trends and patterns across programmes, including longitudinal outcomes Lead data cleaning, validation, and quality assurance Identify gaps in data processes and co-develop improvements to strengthen measurement systems 3. Reporting & Stakeholder Accountability You will translate data into narratives that inform, persuade, and comply. Produce impact analytics for donors, funders, and governance reporting Support grant applications and donor reports with robust evidence Develop dashboards, visualisations, and “impact stats” for leadership and partners Contribute core data and insights to the NLS Annual Report 4. Data Governance & Compliance You will safeguard ethical and responsible data use. Ensure full compliance with PDPA and ethical data standards Monitor and enforce data governance protocols across the organisation What We’re Looking For Qualifications & Experience Degree in Statistics, Social Sciences, Economics, Data Science, or related fields Experience in data management, analysis, and reporting Familiarity with impact measurement frameworks and outcome evaluation Prior experience in the social service or non-profit sector is strongly preferred Skills & Attributes Strong ability to explain data clearly to non-technical audiences Comfortable working with cross-functional and multidisciplinary teams Detail-oriented, organised, and proactive Strong written communication and data storytelling skills Curious, adaptable, and able to keep pace with evolving digital tools A collaborative team player with a strong sense of purpose Employment type 2-Year Contract Full-time Why Join New Life Stories Work on meaningful social issues affecting children and families See your data influence real decisions, funding, and systems change Collaborate with practitioners, leaders, and partners across sectors Be part of an organisation that values rigour, compassion, and integrity

title: Communications and Marketing Executive
description: The Communications and Marketing Executive plays a pivotal role in raising brand awareness, showcasing the impactful work of Habitat for Humanity Singapore, and highlighting meaningful experiences of volunteering with our charity. This individual should be a creative and passionate communications professional with a flair for storytelling and a strong eye for visual content. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on creating engaging digital content, with a particular emphasis on photo and video production. You'll have the unique opportunity to connect with our beneficiaries, partners and volunteers, capture their stories, and bring them to life in a way that resonates deeply with our audience, all while upholding our brand guidelines and narrative principles. Job Scope A) Content creation and storytelling: Assist in developing and executing a consistent stream of engaging, quality content for all social media platforms, with a primary focus on visual (photos, videos, graphics) content including but not limited to short-form clips, interviews, testimonials, event highlights, and campaign videos. (e.g. TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube). Proactively identify and develop compelling stories from our beneficiaries, volunteers, and staff that complements visual assets and reinforces brand messaging. Assist in managing a content calendar to ensure timely and relevant posting across all channels. Conduct sensitive and empathetic interviews with beneficiaries, ensuring their stories are told respectfully and authentically, adhering to all privacy, consent and Habitat Singapore’s safeguarding guidelines. Translate complex experiences into relatable and impactful narratives that inspire action and support. Actively showcase diverse activities and positive experience of our volunteers. Develop campaigns that encourage volunteer recruitment and retention. B) Brand Guardianship: Ensure all communications and marketing materials adhere strictly to brand guidelines, tone, and narrative. Maintain a consistent and professional brand image across all platforms and touchpoints. Meticulously protect our brand's integrity and strategic interests across all touchpoints. This includes ensuring consistent and appropriate brand representation with our beneficiaries, in collaborations with corporate partners, and throughout all internal and external communications from the charity itself. C) General Marketing Support: Assist with other marketing and communications initiatives as required, including documents and presentations, email marketing, website updates, and public relations efforts. Collaborate with internal teams to ensure consistent brand messaging. Assist in developing content for fundraising projects and campaigns. Provide support for collaborative projects with stakeholders and partners. A successful candidate would be · A diploma or tertiary graduate holding qualifications in Mass Communications, Marketing, Journalism, Multimedia Arts, or a related field. · Minimum experience of two years in communications, marketing, or a social media role, with a portfolio showcasing video content production. · Demonstrates expertise in creating engaging short-form video content for social media platforms (Facebook, TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube). · Proficiency in video editing software (Adobe Premiere Pro, Final. Cut Pro, CapCut) and graphic design tools (Adobe Photoshop, Canva). · Strong written and verbal communication skills, with a keen eye for detail and good copywriting abilities. · Ability to conduct sensitive interviews and extract compelling stories from individuals, particularly those from diverse backgrounds. · Good command of English and able to speak a second language (Mandarin, Malay, Tamil) and/or local dialects to communicate with. · Good understanding of social media trends, best practices, and analytics. · Ability to work independently and as part of a team · A passion for our charity's mission and a genuine desire to make a positive impact. · Comfortable to uphold, communicate and affirm Habitat for Humanity’s principles (vision and mission); Pls refer to https://www.habitat.org.sg/about-us for details. If you are interested to join us and be part of our exciting and meaningful ministry, please email us your complete curriculum vitae . Do include a cover letter with your salary expectations and email it to hr@habitat.org.sg . Only short-listed candidates will be contacted for an interview. Habitat's Expectations Surrounding Safeguarding: Habitat for Humanity requires that all employees take seriously their ethical responsibilities to safeguarding our intended beneficiaries, their communities, and all those with whom we work. Managers at all levels have particular responsibilities to support and develop systems that create and maintain an environment that prevents harassment, sexual exploitation and abuse; safeguards the rights of beneficiaries and community members (especially children); and promotes the implementation of Habitat for Humanity's code of conduct.

title: Management Trainee, Corporate Services
description: Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition Since 1985, GreenGarden Integrated Services Pte. Ltd. has been providing exceptional cleaning and landscaping services across Singapore. From commercial cleaning to landscaping maintenance and building upkeep, we take pride in delivering quality, reliability, and outstanding customer service. At GreenGarden, we believe in investing in our people—equipping our staff with the skills, knowledge, and mindset to grow and succeed. Join us and be part of a team that values professionalism, teamwork, and continuous learning. We are proud to be recognized as a BizSAFE Enterprise Level STAR, holders of the Clean Mark Silver Logo, and ISO certified, reflecting our strong commitment to safety, quality, and operational excellence. Take the next step in your career with a company that truly cares—join GreenGarden Integrated Services today! Job Summary The Management Trainee (Corporate Services) programme grooms future corporate professionals through an 18-month structured rotation, delivering hands-on experience in financial accounting, procurement, budgeting, compliance, and digitalisation to prepare trainees for permanent roles within the organisation. title: Senior Counsellor
description: Work Location: Primary: West Singapore General Purpose of Position: As a Senior Counsellor, you will play a crucial role in providing guidance and support to individuals facing various challenges. Your responsibilities will include conducting counselling sessions, developing and implementing counselling programmes and overseeing the Counselling Centre and social work staff. You should have a deep understanding of psychological principles, excellent communication skills, strong leadership skills, and a passion for helping others achieve personal and emotional well-being. Summary of Responsibilities/Duties: 1. Counselling Sessions Conduct individual and/or group counselling sessions to address a range of personal, emotional and mental health concerns. Develop relationships with clients to facilitate positive outcomes. 2. Programme Development Design and implement counselling programmes that cater to the diverse needs of our clients. Collaborate with the social work team to continuously improve and innovate the counselling services. 3. Assessment and Evaluation Conduct thorough assessment of clients' needs and develop personalized treatment plans. Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of counselling interventions and adjust strategies as needed. 4. Crisis Intervention Respond to crisis situations promptly and effectively, providing immediate support to individuals in distress. Collaborate with relevant stakeholders to ensure a coordinated response to crisis. 5. Documentation and Reporting Maintain accurate and confidential client records in compliance with ethical and legal standards. Generate reports on counselling activities and outcomes for management review. 6. Leadership & Team Management Provide effective leadership to the team, fostering a positive and collaborative work environment. Supervise, mentor and support the team in their professional development. Conduct regular team meetings to discuss case reviews, share best practices and address any challenges. Demonstrate leadership qualities, empathy, and commitment to provide high quality counselling services. 7. Counselling Unit Development Oversee the development and operations of the centre and evaluation of the unit to enchance service quality and delivery. Review Organizational capability for service delivery and maintain operational efficiency in line with Organization's strategic objectives. Plan operational goals and strategic focus for the unit. Develop supervision guidelines and lead the professional development of social workers, including training requirements Any other duties as assigned by the immediate Supervisor. Qualifications and Requirements Degree in Counselling, Social Work, or Psychology from a recognised institution. Registered or eligible for registration with a professional body (e.g. Singapore Association for Counselling, SASW). 5–7 years’ experience in counselling or social service, including supervision and crisis intervention . Skilled in programme planning , case management , and trauma-informed practice . Strong leadership , communication , and documentation skills. Empathetic, ethical, and passionate about supporting individuals and families in need.

title: Programme Executive
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION The Salvation Army has been touching lives in Singapore since 1935 and we remain committed in serving the underprivileged in the community without discrimination. To us, every life is precious and we actively help those in need regardless of age, race or religion. Throughout the years, we have established a comprehensive network of social services to meet a wide range of needs in our community. Our diverse programmes reach out to children who need care and protection, families in material need, ailing aged requiring quality nursing care and other needy segments in our society. Our spiritual ministry also reaches out to the people in the community to provide counsel to those in need. We are there to help guide those in need towards finding their identity and purpose in life. RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Volunteer Management Assist in volunteer management and conduct orientation programs Train volunteers in conducting active aging activities and buddying initiatives for seniors/clients Encourage volunteers to engage in befriending relationships with seniors to enhance their emotional and social well-being 3. Programme Planning and Execution Plan, organize, implement, monitor, and evaluate the Centre's programs, activities, and events, creating a regular activity calendar Assist and facilitate activities for clients alongside the care team Facilitate or conduct classes for senior lifelong learning 4. Programme Management & Reporting Oversee the management of key programs, ensuring timely execution and achievement of objectives Coordinate and manage events and partner relationships, ensuring clear communication and smooth execution Provide timely and accurate reporting on the progress of programs, including key milestones, risks, and resolutions 5.
