title: Head of Business Development
description: Company Overview Founded in 2009, Bitsmedia develops purpose-driven technology that supports Muslims around the world in practising their faith with ease and intention. The company is best known for Muslim Pro, the leading Muslim lifestyle app with over 170 million downloads globally, offering essential tools and content that guide users in their daily spiritual lives. Within Muslim Pro, Bitsmedia launched Qalbox in 2022, a dedicated video platform that provides educational and spiritually enriching content — from Quranic recitations and tafsir to hadith studies, Arabic lessons and expert-led discussions — making Islamic knowledge more accessible to a global audience. Qalbox also explores faith in modern life, spiritual well-being and personal growth, offering guidance rooted in Islamic teachings to help users connect their faith to everyday experiences. In 2025, Bitsmedia became a certified B Corporation, reflecting its commitment to building products that serve the global Muslim community responsibly and sustainably. Headquartered in Singapore, with offices in Kuala Lumpur and Jakarta, Bitsmedia continues to grow its ecosystem of digital services for Muslims worldwide. At Bitsmedia, we value diversity and inclusivity, striving to create a workplace where all voices are heard and unique perspectives are celebrated. We invite applications from all qualified individuals and are dedicated to fostering an environment where everyone can contribute and thrive. Join us and be a part of a dynamic team where creativity meets technology, and each member plays a pivotal role in shaping the future of digital media. At Bitsmedia, we’re not just pushing boundaries—we’re setting them. Job Summary The Head of Business Development will be instrumental in shaping and executing the strategic growth initiatives for our digital media business. Reporting directly to the Managing Director, the Head of Business Development will focus on expanding market presence, driving revenue growth, and establishing key strategic partnerships. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record in business development, with a strong background in digital media and experience in leading growth initiatives. Responsibilities:- Strategic Planning and Execution: Develop and implement business development strategies to achieve annual revenue target and expand market share. Identify and pursue new business opportunities, targeting an increase in strategic partnerships and collaborations each year. Market Expansion: Conduct market research and competitive analysis to uncover new market segments and growth opportunities. Drive initiatives to enter at least two new market regions or verticals annually. Revenue Generation: Design and execute strategies to increase revenue streams, aiming for an improvement in average deal size and an increase in new client acquisition annually. Track and analyze sales performance, using data to refine strategies and achieve quarterly revenue targets. Partnership Development: Build and maintain strong relationships with key partners, content creators, and digital platforms. Develop and close high-value partnership agreements, contributing to an increase in partnership revenue over the next year. Operational Alignment: Collaborate with internal teams to ensure alignment of business development initiatives with company objectives and operational capabilities. Oversee the integration of new partnerships and business initiatives, ensuring a seamless transition and operational efficiency. Performance Monitoring: Establish and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) related to business development, such as conversion rates, partner performance, and revenue growth. Provide regular reports and analysis on business development progress and outcomes to the executive team. Industry Insight and Innovation: Stay abreast of industry trends, emerging technologies, and digital media innovations to drive strategic growth and maintain a competitive edge. Leverage industry insights to propose and implement innovative business models and revenue-generating opportunities. Leadership and Team Development: Lead and mentor a high-performing business development team, fostering a culture of innovation, collaboration, and results-oriented performance. Set clear performance goals and provide ongoing feedback and development opportunities to team members. Compliance and Risk Management: Ensure all business development activities comply with relevant regulations, intellectual property rights, and data privacy laws. Implement risk management strategies to safeguard the company's interests and mitigate potential business risks. Paid Ads User Acquisition: Utilize knowledge of paid ads user acquisition to drive targeted lead generation and optimize customer acquisition strategies. Incorporate insights from paid ads performance into business development initiatives to enhance overall effectiveness and revenue growth. Corporate Culture and Values: Promote a positive and inclusive work environment, supporting teamwork, professional development, and employee well-being. Requirements:- Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Digital Media, or a related field. At least 10 years of progressive experience in business development or strategic roles within the digital media industry. Demonstrated success in driving growth and establishing key partnerships, preferably in a senior leadership capacity. A strong track record of achieving revenue targets, expanding market presence, and developing high-value business opportunities. In-depth understanding of the digital media landscape, including market trends, business models, and revenue streams. Excellent strategic thinking and problem-solving abilities with experience in formulating and executing growth strategies based on market insights. Exceptional leadership skills with experience in managing and motivating teams to achieve business development goals. Strong communication and interpersonal skills with proven ability to build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders, partners, and clients. Knowledge of digital media platforms, emerging technologies, and industry trends, with a commitment to staying informed about advancements. Proven financial acumen with experience in managing budgets, analyzing financial metrics, and optimizing resource allocation. Proactive, results-oriented mindset with a passion for driving business growth and achieving strategic objectives. We regret that only shortlisted candidate that will be contacted.

GovTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe it is key to innovation. title: Software Developer (Learning Sciences & Assessment Department) [NIE]
description: ABOUT THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF EDUCATION (NIE) The National Institute of Education (NIE), Singapore , is Singapore’s national teacher education institute and we are proud to be an integral part of the nation’s education service. We play a key role in the preparation of teachers and in the provision of teacher professional and school leadership development programmes. We are committed to our vision of being An Institute of Distinction: Leading the Future of Education and our mission to Inspire Learning, Transform Teaching and Advance Research . Read more about NIE here . NIE invites applications for the position of part-time software developer for the project “PhET-style simulations to promote AI literacy”. The project’s goal is to develop a curated library of interactive, high-quality online resources for teachers that puts NIE at the forefront of AI literacy. To work towards this goal, the project will start out by developing customizable web-based simulations and corresponding avatar-based narrative instructional videos catering to three fundamental topics in AI literacy - Personalization using Machine Learning and Neural Networks , Ethical Issues in AIED , Human-AI Collaboration. These simulations would allow student teachers to experiment with relevant critical task features in a hands-on manner, thus improving their understanding of abstract technical concepts in AI and the implications of their usage in AI systems for education. Project evaluation would focus on usability evaluation and learning evaluation of the programmed simulations. Facilitation resources and validated design guidelines for developing effective AI literacy simulations will be made available to educational stakeholders upon project completion. This is a contract position till 30 November 2026. Responsibilities Gathering requirements for simulation design from domain experts Agile prototyping of mock UI/UX interfaces for the simulations Programming and iteratively refining the simulations based on expert feedback and usability testing Building data logging capabilities in the designed simulations Developing facilitation guides to assist stakeholders’ work with designed simulations Working with NIE’s IT teams to host simulations securely and ensure seamless interoperability with existing NIE platforms Managing the project and ensuring timely completion of milestones Requirements Essential A Polytechnic Diploma Full stack software development and debugging experience Knowledge of computer graphics and interface design Fluent in coding with generative AI Solid understanding of fundamental machine learning and AI techniques Creativity, good communication skills and ability to work as team player Able to work independently with minimal supervision Preferred Work, teaching, or corporate training experience in AI for education Familiarity with AI literacy efforts worldwide, in APAC and Singapore Up-to-date with latest advancements in generative AI (for education) Passion for improving teaching and learning with technology Application We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Other Information NIE staff can take chartered buses at their own expense from or near their home to the NIE campus. This is subject to the availability of seats.

title: Assistant Campus Manager
description: Position Objective The Assistant Campus Manager plays a key operational and facilities support role. The  position is responsible for ensuring all aspects of facilities, operations, safety, and vendor management are delivered efficiently, safely, and cost-effectively. Working closely with the Campus Manager, the role partners with academic leadership teams to ensure that the campus environment supports high-quality teaching and learning, meets all regulatory requirements, and reflects each school’s unique identity. Responsibilities Leadership, Collaboration & Stakeholder Management • Support the Campus Manager in partnering with school principals and academic leaders to ensure the campus meets operational, capacity, and functional needs. • Assist with the fair and effective coordination of shared spaces, ensuring all schools are supported equitably. • Build strong relationships with internal stakeholders, vendors, regulatory partners, and third-party service providers. • Provide day-to-day guidance to maintenance, facilities, and soft services teams, supporting employee engagement, deployment, and performance. • Collaborate with the Facilities Directors/Campus Managers across Singapore to align processes, identify  efficiencies, and support group-wide operational standards. Facilities & Maintenance Management Support the planning, execution, and reporting of all preventive, corrective, and reactive maintenance activities. • Oversee the daily management of all outsourced engineering, mechanical, electrical, air-conditioning, along with soft services such as security, cleaning, landscaping, and all general maintenance vendors deployed on site. • Assist in monitoring and reporting on SLA and KPI performance for all facilities teams and services. • Conduct routine inspections of building systems, learning spaces, playgrounds, and soft services operations, ensuring compliance, upkeep, and readiness. • Support the management, including procurement, inventory control, financial tracking, and the annual budgeting process. • Assist in the development and implementation of long-term campus maintenance and optimisation plans (5- and 10-year outlooks). • Contribute to the planning and delivery of renovation works, enhancement projects, and campus improvements. • Laise with local government agencies where necessary (NEA, BCA, URA, SCDF, LTA ect) Health, Safety, Fire & Risk Management: Support the implementation and adherence to Health & Safety, Fire Safety, Evacuation, and Risk Management policies and procedures. • Coordinate with the external Fire Safety Manager and assist in SCDF inspections, rectifications, and renewal of the Annual Fire Certificate. • Partner with academic teams to ensure classroom setups, learning environments, and early-years spaces are safe, compliant, and developmentally appropriate. • Maintain and deliver training for the CERT team, ensuring drills, exercises, and emergency procedures are executed as scheduled. • Support safe work practices by enforcing Risk Assessments and ensuring contractor compliance with all relevant standards and guidelines. • Maintain and keep updated documentation records for all health and safety matters and prepare documentation for all audits. Operations Management Oversee daily operational support services including cleaning, security, logistics, event support, transportation coordination, and general campus upkeep. Provide operational support for school events, activities, and special programmes. • Support tender reviews, RFP processes, and contract administration in partnership with the Campus Manager. • Assist in the ongoing review and improvement of Standard Operating Procedures, service standards, and operational workflows to ensure efficiency and service excellence. • Drive the adoption of technology, innovation, and productivity improvements to enhance campus operations. • Maintain strong relationships with all internal and external stakeholders to ensure smooth, reliable, and responsive service delivery. • Perform any other duties as necessary to support the smooth and efficient operations. Position Requirements •	Excellent communication skills - written and spoken English •	Good interpersonal skills, with the ability to interact professionally with stakeholders of various nationalities. •	Self-motivated, proactive, and creative problem-solving skills to ensure an outstanding customer service delivery is maintained •	Ability to work independently in a fast-paced and challenging environment, manage teams of third-party vendors to ensure an outstanding service is delivered and maintained •	Proficient in IT skills with good MS Office knowledge Qualifications: •	Degree or Diploma in Engineering (Mechanical/ Electrical/ Facilities Management) or equivalent qualification •	Minimum 5 years of experience in a similar role •	Possession of a valid Fire Safety Manager (FSM) certification to perform FSM duties or the willingness to undertake certification will be advantageous •	Knowledge of Singapore laws and regulations relating to Building Management and Regulations including but not limited to Fire Safety •	Possession of a valid Environmental control coordinator would be advantageous Working Hours: 8:00 am – 5:00 pm, Monday to Friday

To Teachers: Prepare materials and activities for lessons, including laminating, photocopying, and setting up games, in line with lesson plans and learning objectives. Assist with organising the classroom and creating displays, such as preparing labels, signs, and other practical tasks to ensure a high-quality learning environment. Help with arranging classrooms before each term begins and dismantling and packing up at the end of the academic year. Take joint responsibility with the class teacher for the educational progress and well-being of every student in the class. Takes part in IEP conferences and reviews, working with parents, School Leadership, Inclusion Coordinators, and other professionals or stakeholders involved with the student. Pastoral & Supervision: To take a proactive role in the pastoral life of the children in the school, working with teachers and senior leaders, promoting the general progress and wellbeing of all pupils in our care; participating in the reward and sanction of pupils, and implementing the School’s Behaviour and Discipline policy. Take responsibility for students' physical needs while encouraging their independence, such as assisting with changing for PE lessons or water play. Accompany students to the medical room and keep the class teacher informed of any accidents or illnesses. Carry out break and lunch duties according to the school duty rota. Perform bus duties following school guidelines for health and safety. Accompany teachers and students on educational outings. Assessment & Reporting: Offer feedback to teachers on students' progress based on group work or informal observations, in Early Years and Primary settings. Assist the class teacher in uploading photos, videos, and information to the communication platform in accordance with school guidelines. Provide prompt and positive feedback to teachers regarding students' learning and achievements. Job Requirements: Minimum Diploma holder or equivalent in Early Childhood Education, Special Education, Counselling, Psychology or related field. Minimum 1- 2 year work experience in a school setting. Experience with EAL/Learning Support class will be desirable. Ally The Outsourcing Team Recruit Express Pte Ltd EA: 99C4599

title: Senior Software Engineer with experience using AI for code development
description: [12 months contract, renewable] About Us The Government Technology Agency (GovTech) aims to transform the delivery of Government digital services by taking an outside-in” view, putting citizens and businesses at the heart of everything we do. We also develop the Smart Nation infrastructure and applications, and facilitate collaboration with citizens and  businesses to co-develop technologies. Join us as we support Singapore’s vision of building a Smart Nation - a nation of possibilities empowered through infocommunications technology and related engineering. Who we are The Alliance for Digital Transformation (ADX) program office’s mission is to empower government agencies in its digital transformation journey through technology and engineering. The ADX program office functions as a cap-center that offers consulting services in product management, design and software development to government agencies. By doing so, ADX builds up the digital competency of its partners on a sustainable basis and a vibrant ecosystem of exchange among the ADX program members. Role & Responsibilities As a Senior Software Engineer, you will play a critical role in designing, developing, and deploying scalable, high-performance software solutions. You will work closely with cross-functional teams, contributing your expertise to ensure the successful delivery of technical projects. Your responsibilities include: Designing, developing, and maintaining robust, scalable, and secure software applications. Collaborating with product managers, designers, and other stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into technical solutions. Driving the full software development lifecycle, including coding, testing, deployment, and monitoring. Ensuring the delivery of high-quality code through code reviews, testing strategies, and adherence to best practices. Mentoring junior engineers and fostering a culture of knowledge sharing and technical excellence. Identifying and resolving performance bottlenecks, scalability issues, and architectural challenges. Staying updated with emerging technologies and trends to bring innovative solutions to the team. Contributing to the technical vision and ensuring alignment with the company’s overall goals. Our Ideal Candidate We’re looking for a passionate and experienced engineer who thrives in a collaborative environment and is excited about solving complex problems. The ideal candidate will have: 7+ years of professional experience in software development with expertise in Node.js or Java. Candidate must have experience using AI for code development in production environment. Proficiency in backend development and database design, e.g. Express.js, PostgreSQL, MongoDB and etc. Expertise in various frontend frameworks, e.g. React.js, Storybook, MUI and etc. Proficiency in cloud technologies, e.g. AWS Lambda, Amazon S3 and etc. Familiarity with DevOps practices and tools, e.g., CI/CD pipelines, Docker, Kubernetes. Experience with MINDEF Commercial Cloud (MCC) will be a plus. Strong analytical and debugging skills, with a proactive approach to identifying and resolving issues. Excellent communication and teamwork skills, with experience working in Agile or Scrum environments. A track record of mentoring team members and contributing to technical decision-making. Eagerness to learn and adapt to new tools, frameworks, and methodologies as needed. A genuine interest in creating innovative solutions and contributing to the company’s growth.

title: Senior Executive, Communications & Resource Development
description: Position Overview Reporting to the Manager, Community Engagement & Partnerships and working closely with the Head, Corporate Services, the Senior Executive, Communications & Resource Development is responsible for driving key areas of KKCS’s communications and marketing work, with a strong focus on media engagement, donor communications and fundraising support. As part of a small Community Engagement & Partnerships team, this role provides ownership and leadership over specific communications and marketing domains, translating organisational priorities into effective narratives, campaigns and stakeholder engagement efforts that support KKCS’s mission, services and fundraising needs. Key Duties & Responsibilities 1. Communications & Fundraising Execution (Domain Ownership) 1.1. Drive the planning and execution of communications and marketing initiatives within assigned domains, including media relations, donor communications and fundraising-related outreach. 1.2. Translate organisational priorities, programmes and impact into clear, compelling communications for internal and external audiences. 1.3. Ensure consistency, clarity and alignment across all communications touchpoints. 2. Donors & Grantors Communications 2.1. Develop and deliver clear, impactful communications to support donor stewardship and grant submission and reporting. 2.2. Partner with internal teams to craft narratives and materials that reflect programme outcomes and funding requirements. 2.3. Ensure timely dissemination of impact updates, donor reports and engagement collaterals. 3. Fundraising & Campaign Support 3.1. Drive communications and marketing components of fundraising initiatives and events, including campaign messaging, publicity, collaterals and post-event communications. 3.2. Support donor stewardship efforts by ensuring timely, meaningful and consistent engagement before and after fundraising activities. 3.3. Deliver timely fundraising strategies and proposals and the required compliance and reporting related work. 4. Cross-Unit Collaboration 4.1. Work closely with service units to surface stories of practice, outcomes and community impact that support communications, media and fundraising needs. 4.2. Serve as a key connector between service units and Corporate Services for communications-related work such as donor and fundraising needs. 4.3. Provide professional input and recommendations on communications, media and donor engagement matters. 4.4. Provide secretarial support to select committees from time to time. 5. Brand & Content Development 5.1. Articulate KKCS’s impact and strengthen brand affinity through high-quality content — stories, videos, collaterals, speeches and digital assets. 5.2. Uphold and safeguard KKCS’s brand guidelines across all communications channels and materials. 6. Management of Communications Channels & Platforms 6.1. Manage and update key communications channels, including website, email communications, social media, SharePoint, general media other digital platforms. 6.2. Manage media engagement efforts, including pitching stories, responding to media enquiries and coordinating interviews and features. 6.3. Build and maintain relationships with relevant media partners and digital platforms to enhance KKCS’s public profile and credibility. 7. Other Duties 7.1. Perform any other duties as assigned by the Executive Director and the leadership team.

title: Marketing & Growth Lead
description: We are a small but fast-growing tuition centre in Singapore, built on strong academic outcomes and modern, content-driven student acquisition. As a lean and ambitious team, we move fast, experiment often, and expect real ownership. We are looking for a Marketing & Growth Lead who will own the end-to-end marketing and sales process, from campaign ideation to student conversion. Role Overview This is a high-ownership role. You will be responsible not just for creating content, but for designing, championing, and executing marketing campaigns that directly drive enrolments. You will plan the full funnel — awareness, engagement, trial, conversion — and work closely with tutors to turn teaching content and TikTok Live sessions into real business growth. This role is ideal for someone who thinks like a founder or growth operator, not a task-based marketer. Key Responsibilities End-to-End Marketing & Sales Ownership Design and own the entire student acquisition funnel Plan how leads are generated, nurtured, and converted into signups Champion marketing campaigns from concept to execution and post-campaign review Take responsibility for outcomes (e.g. enquiries, trial sign-ups, enrolments) Marketing Strategy & Campaign Planning Craft a clear, actionable marketing plan aligned with enrolment targets Plan and launch specific campaigns (e.g. crash courses, exam-season pushes, TikTok Live series, free trial funnels) Identify target segments (students vs parents, level-specific strategies) Track performance metrics and continuously iterate Content Creation & Social Media Plan/design/shoot/edit/create short-form videos, images, captions, and posts Manage posting schedules and content calendars Repurpose TikTok Live content into clips for sustained reach Ensure consistency in branding, messaging, and credibility TikTok Live & Tutor-Led Marketing Plan TikTok Live teaching and engagement strategies as a key growth channel Guide tutors on structuring live lessons for engagement and conversion, communicating value clearly to students and parents, and handling basic sales messaging naturally during live sessions Coordinate live schedules and coach tutors on basic on-screen presence and messaging Beneficial Prior Experience Candidates with experience in one or more of the following will be highly valued: Marketing or growth roles in education, tuition, or enrichment centres Running or scaling TikTok, Instagram, or short-form video accounts Planning and executing lead-generation or conversion-focused campaigns Creator-led marketing, influencer marketing, or live commerce Early-stage startup or small business environments Graphic design, video-editing for social media posts Work Arrangement & Compensation Part-time, flexible hours Hybrid / remote-friendly (some in-person coordination required) Highly competitive pay, with performance-based incentives Clear path to expanded responsibility or full-time role as the centre scales Candidates are required to commit for at least six months

title: Manager, IT Network & Infrastructure
description: Lifelong Learning SG (LLSG) strives to continues to strengthen its internal IT capabilities to support secure, reliable, and compliant operations. The Manager, IT (Network & Infrastructure) is responsible for the day-to-day ownership, governance, and continuous improvement of the organisation’s network, infrastructure, and end-user computing environment, operating within a vendor-managed service model. The role reports to the Head of Digital Services and works closely with external service providers and internal stakeholders to ensure operational stability, cybersecurity, regulatory compliance, and service optimisation. The role also supports the onboarding of officers onto IT systems and standards and contributes to identifying opportunities for improvement, risk reduction, and infrastructure upgrades as part of ongoing operations. Key Responsibilities Network Infrastructure & EUCD Operations Ensure availability, performance, and resilience of infrastructure services. Act as the primary in-house escalation point for infrastructure-related incidents. Coordinate vendors for incident resolution, change implementation, and ongoing support. Maintain accurate infrastructure documentation, network diagrams, and asset records. Vendor & Service Management Operate within a vendor-managed delivery model, retaining in-house ownership and accountability. Manage vendor performance against agreed SLAs and operational requirements. Review and validate vendor technical proposals, designs, and change requests. Coordinate multi-vendor troubleshooting and resolution activities. Ensure vendors comply with organisational IT policies, security standards, and governance requirements. Change, Incident & Problem Management Oversee infrastructure-related change activities, ensuring proper impact assessment, rollback planning, and documentation. Manage and coordinate response to infrastructure incidents and service disruptions. Support root cause analysis (RCA) and follow-up preventive actions. Escalate risks, recurring issues, or service concerns to the Head of Digital Services where necessary. Security, Risk & Compliance Support Support implementation and maintenance of technical security controls in line with PDPA requirements & ISO 27001 and organisational security policies Coordinate remediation of infrastructure- and EUCD-related findings from vulnerability assessments (VAPT) and security reviews. Ensure firewall rules, access controls, logging, and monitoring are properly implemented and reviewed. Support incident containment, investigation, and audit evidence gathering when required. Technical Governance & Continuous Improvement Provide technical input and recommendations for infrastructure standards, configurations, and operational practices. Review infrastructure designs and configurations to ensure alignment with security, resilience, and operational requirements. Identify opportunities for service optimisation, risk reduction, and infrastructure improvements. Support knowledge sharing, documentation, and process improvement to enhance operational maturity. Ad-hoc & Special Assignments Support ad-hoc IT or infrastructure-related assignments as directed by the Head of Digital Services. Participate in cross-functional initiatives involving Facilities, Security, or Business Units where infrastructure input is required. Escalate workload, capacity, or risk impacts early to ensure appropriate prioritisation. Qualifications and Requirements Background and experience in IT, Computer Science, or related discipline. Foundational knowledge of IT infrastructure and networking concepts. Basic understanding of: TCP/IP, LAN/WAN, DNS, DHCP Network and security principles Interest in cybersecurity, infrastructure, and IT operations. Comfortable working with external vendors and service providers. Willingness to learn and follow established procedures. Good communication, coordination, and documentation skills. Able to work under guidance in a structured environment. Candidates who do not have the required qualification or relevant experience are welcome to apply. Successful candidates will be offered a 2-year contract in the first instance and may be considered for an extension or be placed on a permanent tenure. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Middle School Counselor
description: REPORTS TO : Student Services Director JOB GOAL: The Middle School Counselor provides proactive, Christ-centered counseling and academic support for students in Grades 6-8. The counselor promotes emotional well-being, resilience, and academic success through direct counseling services, collaboration with teachers and parents, and schoolwide SEL initiatives. Serving as a student advocate and intermediary, the counselor supports effective communication between students, faculty, administration, and parents. A primary focus is leading the development of the Middle School SEL (Advisory) Program by designing curriculum, equipping teachers, and supporting implementation. All ICS faculty demonstrate Christian character through kindness, grace, and integrity, creating a nurturing space for students. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Counseling & Student Support Provide individual and small-group counseling to support students’ social-emotional, behavioral, and interpersonal needs. Maintain a safe, confidential, and supportive environment that promotes student trust and growth. Implement preventive and responsive interventions using evidence-based, solution-focused, and restorative approaches. Partner with the Learning Support Teacher and MS/HS Principal to address students’ holistic needs through targeted academic, social, and emotional interventions that enhance classroom engagement, organization, motivation, and peer relationships. Refer students to external professionals or agencies when specialized care is required, ensuring coordinated follow-up and communication. Consultation & Collaboration Consult regularly with faculty, staff, and parents regarding student well-being, classroom climate, and behavior support. Partner with teachers to design classroom strategies that promote positive behavioral, social-emotional, and academic outcomes. Support crisis management by providing immediate intervention and follow-up support when necessary. Review records for prospective students to assess eligibility for admission. Social Emotional Learning (SEL) & Advisory Program Design and develop the Middle School SEL Program (during Advisory), including curriculum creation, piloting, and refinement. Train and equip Advisory teachers through ongoing professional development, modeling, and collaborative planning. Collaborate with the ES Counselor to ensure vertical alignment between SEL programs. Integrate SEL practices with the school’s spiritual life, restorative discipline, and student leadership initiatives. Parent & Community Engagement Partner with parents to promote consistent home-school approaches to supporting student development. Offer parent education sessions and workshops on topics such as adolescent development, digital wellness, executive functioning, and social-emotional growth. School-Wide Contributions Support student transitions between Elementary, Middle, and High School, providing guidance and orientation for students and families. Maintain accurate, confidential records and ensure compliance with school policies and ethical standards. Participate in the Service Learning program (Project 4:12) including the capstone project Week Without Walls (WWW) by co-leading a team. Participate actively in faculty meetings, chapel, and school events, modeling the school’s values and Christ-centered mission. Engage in ongoing professional development in counseling, SEL, and related areas. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity and optimism. Perform other duties as assigned. QUALIFICATIONS: A Bachelor’s degree in a related field or equivalent experience [Preferred] Master’s degree in Counseling, School Psychology, or a related field Teaching credential from a US state, Canadian province or from ACSI (exceptions will be approved by the Academic Board) Experience with supporting students with social & emotional needs [Preferred] Minimum 3–5 years of experience in school counseling (international or cross-cultural experience preferred) [Preferred] Experience developing or facilitating SEL or Advisory programs

title: Programme Executive
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION THK MC was incorporated on 13 October 2011 as a charity to provide multiple social and welfare services to the community at large, and achieved an Institute of Public Character (IPC) status on 18 November 2011. THK MC is the charitable arm of Thye Hua Kwan Moral Society, which lives by the mission - To Serve Mankind - to help anyone who needs help regardless of their race, religion, colour, language, creed and culture. title: Head of Compliance
description: About the Organisation Our client is an established bank with a significant and growing presence in Singapore. They are seeking an experienced and dynamic Head of Compliance to lead the Singapore compliance function. This is a pivotal role within the senior management team, offering high visibility and the opportunity to shape the compliance culture. Job Summary Reporting directly to the CEO, Singapore, with a functional reporting line to the Group Head of Compliance at Head Office, you will lead the Singapore branch’s compliance and financial crime prevention framework. You will act as the key interface with the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) and lead a team of compliance professionals to ensure regulatory adherence and risk mitigation. Responsibilities Lead the Singapore Compliance function, embedding a strong compliance culture and governance frameworks across all business units to mitigate regulatory and financial crime risks Develop and execute the AML/CFT framework, including policies, procedures, customer due diligence, transaction monitoring, sanctions screening, and management of suspicious activity reports to ensure regulatory compliance Serve as the primary liaison with MAS and other regulatory bodies, delivering timely, accurate, and high-quality regulatory reporting and submissions Provide authoritative, practical, and timely compliance advice to business leaders and senior management on regulatory requirements, emerging regulations, and compliance risks to support informed decision-making Review and vet legal and compliance-related documentation, including agreements and NDAs, coordinating with internal teams and external counsel to manage legal risk Lead, mentor, and develop the local compliance team, fostering a high-performance culture focused on continuous learning and professional growth Collaborate closely with the Group Head of Compliance to align local policies and procedures with group standards and regulatory expectations Required competencies and certifications Deep, hands-on expertise in AML/CFT and broad regulatory compliance within the banking or financial services sector Proven experience managing and developing compliance teams to achieve operational excellence In-depth knowledge of Singapore banking regulations and MAS expectations, demonstrated through effective regulatory engagement Preferred competencies and qualifications Experience reviewing and negotiating legal documentation to mitigate compliance and legal risks Excellent communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to operate independently and influence senior management A degree in Law, Finance, Business, or a related field Relevant professional certifications such as CAMS or ICA

title: Geospatial Data Engineering
description: About the Role At Kyndryl, you play a key role in shaping how organisations use geospatial intelligence at scale. If you’re excited about designing enterprise‑grade GIS platforms, building modern geospatial applications, and working with advanced spatial analytics, this role gives you the depth, complexity, and career runway to grow into a true GIS expert or architect. Here, you won’t just maintain maps or process data—you’ll design, automate, and modernise full geospatial ecosystems for large enterprises across Singapore and the region. What You Will Do 1) Geospatial Data Engineering You’ll shape how spatial data is structured and managed across the organisation. You’ll: Design and maintain enterprise geodatabases (file/enterprise geodatabases, feature classes, domains, subtypes, topology rules). Build ETL/ELT pipelines for vector, raster, and imagery using ArcGIS Data Interoperability, ArcGIS API for Python, ModelBuilder, or FME. Implement data quality checks, metadata standards, schema versioning, and coordinate system workflows. 2) Services, Applications & Web GIS You’ll create the tools and experiences that users interact with every day. You’ll: Publish and optimise map, feature, and image services on ArcGIS Server. Manage Portal for ArcGIS content, groups, permissions, and sharing models. Build web GIS applications using Experience Builder, Web AppBuilder, Dashboards, Field Maps, Survey123, and StoryMaps. Develop custom web applications using ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript, ArcGIS REST API, and modern web frameworks. 3) Spatial Analytics & Modeling You turn raw data into actionable insights. You’ll: Execute advanced analysis (network, proximity, spatial statistics, suitability, 3D/scene layers) in ArcGIS Pro. Use GeoAnalytics, Raster/Spatial Analyst, Image Analyst, and Notebooks for large-scale analytics. Build repeatable geoprocessing models, notebooks, and scheduled jobs for production analytics. 4) Automation & Integration You ensure the GIS ecosystem is efficient, scalable, and well‑connected. You’ll: Automate admin tasks with ArcGIS API for Python (users, services, content, backups, monitoring). Integrate ArcGIS with enterprise platforms like SQL Server, PostgreSQL/PostGIS, ERP/CRM, data lakes, or APIs. Support real-time/event-driven GIS using GeoEvent Server, streaming services, and IoT feeds. 5) Platform Operations & Security You help keep the GIS platform reliable, secure, and high‑performing. You’ll: Support ArcGIS Enterprise deployments (Windows/Linux, on‑prem, Azure, AWS). Monitor caching, indexing, performance tuning, and ArcGIS Online credit usage. Enforce governance for roles, permissions, SSO/Entra ID, and security policies. Why Join Kyndryl? You’ll be part of a global technology services organisation driving digital transformation across Singapore’s key industries. You’ll get: Opportunities to work on meaningful national-scale or enterprise projects Exposure to cloud, automation, security, and data engineering A culture that values engineering depth, innovation, and continuous learning A supportive environment where your ideas shape the GIS landscape Required Skills & Experience Minimum 5+ years of relevant hands‑on experience in geospatial engineering, GIS development, or enterprise GIS platform management Strong hands‑on experience with ArcGIS Enterprise , ArcGIS Pro , and core Esri geospatial technologies Solid understanding of enterprise geodatabases (e.g., SQL Server, PostgreSQL/PostGIS), spatial data models, and coordinate systems Practical experience with Python for geospatial automation, scripting, or administrative tasks Experience working with web GIS tools or applications (e.g., Portal/ArcGIS Online, Web GIS app builders) Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret, process, and validate complex geospatial datasets Familiarity with GIS system administration concepts such as roles/permissions, security policies, or platform performance tuning Ability to work collaboratively in a technical environment and communicate effectively with non‑technical stakeholders Preferred Skills & Experience Experience with ETL tools such as FME, ArcGIS Data Interoperability, or other data integration platforms Knowledge of ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript or modern frontend frameworks for building custom GIS applications Exposure to GeoEvent Server , real‑time data streams, or IoT‑driven GIS workflows Experience deploying GIS solutions in cloud environments (Azure, AWS, Kubernetes) Familiarity with spatial analytics extensions such as GeoAnalytics, Spatial Analyst, Image Analyst, or Raster analytics Understanding of enterprise integrations with ERP/CRM systems , data lakes, or REST‑based APIs Professional certifications in geospatial technologies, cloud platforms, or data engineering

title: Sales Executive
description: Sales Executive - Institutions Singapore Asia Publishers, a leading provider of educational assessment books in Singapore, is seeking a dynamic and enthusiastic Institutional Sales Executive to join our team. This role is integral to our mission of delivering high-quality educational resources to schools, teachers, and students in Singapore. Job Description: - Develop an in-depth understanding of our extensive range of educational assessment books, including content, unique selling points, and target audiences. - Build and maintain relationships with schools and teachers, understanding their needs and recommending suitable books and resources. - Actively promote our books through presentations, meetings, and events, ensuring our products meet the educational needs of our customers. - Stay informed about market trends, competitors, and customer feedback to identify new opportunities and improve our product offerings. - Assist customers with placing orders, ensure timely delivery, and handle any post-sales inquiries or issues. - Achieve monthly and quarterly sales targets, contributing to the overall growth of the company. Requirements: - Diploma and above - At least 2 years of sales experience preferrably in similar industry. - Strong communication and interpersonal skills. - A passion for education and helping students succeed. - Self-motivated with a proactive approach to sales. - Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Please write in with full resume to sharonyoo@sapgrp.com Please send in your resume to sharonyoo@sapgrp.com Salary will commensurate with experience. Job Types: Full-time, Part-time, Contract, Internship, Freelance Salary will commensurate with working experience in relevant industry.

title: Youth Worker |School
description: The Services required are set out as follows: 1. Outreach and engagement - engage and build positive relationships with identified students during and/or outside of curriculum hours (e.g. night, school holidays, weekends), engage and connect with identified students in school and/or community settings outside of school compound, strongly encourage identified students to attend school or after-school/community programmes, and equip students with essential socio-emotional knowledge and skills; 2.School collaboration – provide proper documentation, relevant feedback and progress of students to school personnel, and provide support and consultation to school teachers on students’ needs and challenges; 3.Case management and Family Engagement – provide case planning, management and counselling support to the family, and conduct family engagement and interventions; 4.Youth Worker for each school to be deployed only to the school; 5.Assignment of Youth Worker should ensure continuity and sustainability in the engagement of the identified students

title: English Teacher (Primary) [Early-Career / Fresh Graduates Welcome]
description: About the Role At illum.e, we are not ‘just another tuition centre’. We are a premium English education brand known for clarity of method, depth of thinking, and consistently strong student outcomes. This role is designed specifically for newly-graduated educators or tutors with limited formal classroom experience who want to be trained properly, mentored closely, and developed into confident, high-impact English teachers. If you have mainly done 1-to-1 private tutoring, this role offers the structure, support, and pedagogy to help you scale your teaching skill professionally. You will focus primarily on teaching excellence and classroom delivery, while being gradually introduced to curriculum thinking through guided exposure — not thrown into the deep end. Teaching locations will also be based on our five branches - Jurong East, Bukit Timah, Novena, Hougang and Marine Parade. Key Responsibilities Teaching & Classroom Delivery Deliver illum.e’s Primary English lessons using our structured lesson plans, materials, and teaching frameworks Build students’ confidence, clarity of expression, and examination readiness through guided practice and feedback Learn to manage small-group classrooms effectively, even if your prior experience has been mostly 1-to-1 Pedagogical Development (Training-Focused) Undergo structured in-house training in lesson delivery, questioning techniques, explanation clarity, and marking standards Observe experienced teachers, co-teach where appropriate, and receive regular feedback on your teaching Develop strong fundamentals in grammar teaching, writing instruction, and comprehension strategies Student Progress & Support Monitor student understanding during lessons and flag learning gaps to Leads Apply feedback from mentors to refine your teaching and communication week by week Light Curriculum Exposure (Supported) Support minor curriculum refinement tasks (e.g. improving worksheets, suggesting clarifications) under guidance No expectation to independently design full curricula or assessments at this stage Who We’re Looking For Fresh graduates or early-career educators with a strong command of English Tutors with mainly 1-to-1 private tutoring experience who want to grow into classroom teaching Clear communicators who enjoy explaining ideas simply and precisely Coachable, reflective, and genuinely eager to improve as teachers Prior MOE or centre experience is welcome but not required Why illum.e? Serious Training, Not Sink-or-Swim You are not expected to ‘already know everything’. We invest time in grooming you into a confident, structured, and respected educator. Strong Brand, Real Demand Students come to illum.e because of trust, results, and reputation — not aggressive sales. Excellent Teaching Support You teach with well-designed materials, clear lesson objectives, and marking guidance — not last-minute scrambling. Low Politics, High Standards A professional culture focused on growth, clarity, and doing the work well. Clear Growth Pathways Strong performers can progress into senior teaching roles, curriculum tracks, or academic leadership over time. Future-Facing Organisation Exposure to AI-assisted learning tools, digital platforms, and long-term expansion plans — without pressure to ‘perform tech’. Staff Benefits Competitive salary with annual reviews Semester-based performance bonuses and retention incentives Paid training sessions, lesson observations, and curriculum workshops Full curriculum and marking support Mentorship from experienced Teaching and Curriculum Leads Direct access to Academic Directors and senior leadership Two full rest days per week Flexible scheduling based on teaching availability Annual retreats, festive gatherings, and wellness initiatives

title: Entrepreneur-in-Residence (EIR) – Social Impact
description: Company Overview We’re not just building startups, we’re solving global challenges. Wu Song Capital is a venture studio and early-stage VC fund focused on education, social impact (ESG), and tech. If you’re passionate about creating change and love the hustle, this is your chance to shape the future. Job Description This isn’t your typical job. As an EIR at Wu Song Capital, you’ll own a startup from Day 1, whether it’s your idea or a venture we’ve sketched out. You’ll start lean, with interns and a barebones team, and build something impactful from the ground up. This role is designed for the doers, the dreamers, and those who know that creating real change requires perseverance, creativity, and a whole lot of grit. Key Responsibilities Create Your Venture: You’ll launch and run a business, leading every aspect of its development Bootstrapping to Scaling: Begin with interns and limited resources but scale your team and operations as you grow Innovate & Build: Develop strategies that will challenge the status quo, create market entry plans, and scale impact across Asia Lead a Movement: Bring your team together as you grow from an agile startup to a purpose-driven organization Network & Fundraise: Engage with customers, partners, and investors, and raise funds to propel your venture forward Embrace the Hustle: This is not a cushy corporate gig, it’s a grind that requires resilience and resourcefulness every day Qualities an EIR Must Possess Purpose-Driven Hustler: You’re driven by making an impact, not just by profits. You’re creative, adaptable, and excited to rough it out to build something that matters Ownership Mentality: You thrive on freedom and responsibility, taking full charge of your business Resilience & Grit: Startups are tough, and you’re tougher. You bounce back stronger, ready to overcome any challenge Experience as a founder or exec is great, but we care more about your entrepreneurial energy and willingness to hustle What We Offer Equity = Your Reward: You’re here for the upside. Significant equity means you benefit from your startup’s long-term growth. Basic Allowance, Big Impact: We’ll cover essentials with a modest allowance, but the real payoff comes from building something incredible. Freedom to Lead: Complete autonomy to drive your venture and create real social impact. Access to Resources & Community: We’ve got the network, mentors, and investors to help fuel your growth. Adventure Built In: Whether you’re working remotely or traveling Asia, this is your chance to shape a bold new future. Application Process Ready to change the world? Email your resume and 2-pager teaser of your idea to careers@wusongcapital.com . We want dreamers, doers, and anyone who believes in creating lasting impact. Stipend $800 - $2,400

title: Tax Compliance & Advisory Manager
description: About OA Group of Companies (OA GOC) OA Group of Companies (OA GOC) is a regional professional services groupheadquartered in Singapore, with an expanding presence acrossMalaysia and the region. We partner with growth-minded businessowners, CFOs, and leadership teams to build strong corporatefoundations, enhance governance and compliance, and enablesustainable growth. Ourmultidisciplinary platform spans Audit & Assurance, Accounting & Outsourcing, Corporate Secretarial & Compliance, Tax Compliance & Advisory, Business Advisory, and Digital Transformation. By integrating compliance, financial insight, and advisory thinking, we help clients move from operational stability to strategic scale. At OA GOC, we believe that strong growth is built on professionalism, ownership, and long-term partnerships, both with our clients and our people. Role Overview We are looking for a highly driven Tax Compliance & Advisory Manager to lead our tax department. You will manage a diverse portfolio of corporate and individual clients, ensuring they remain fully compliant with Singapore’s tax laws while providing high-level advisory on tax structuring, incentives, and cross-border transactions. Key Responsibilities 1. Tax Compliance Management Oversee the preparation and filing of Estimated Chargeable Income (ECI) and Form C / Form C-S for a portfolio of corporate clients. Manage Goods and Services Tax (GST) reporting, including quarterly filings and ASK (Assisted Self-Help Kit) reviews. Ensure timely submission of individual income tax (Form B/B1) and withholding tax filings for non-resident payments. Liaise directly with the Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore (IRAS) regarding tax queries, audits, and voluntary disclosures. 2. Advisory & Strategic Planning Provide technical advice on Singapore’s tax incentives (e.g., PIC, M&A allowance, DTIs). Leadership & Operations Review the work of tax associates and seniors, providing mentorship and technical guidance. Monitor legislative changes in the Singapore Budget and OECD (BEPS 2.0) to assess impact on the client base. Drive business development by identifying upsell opportunities within existing accounts.

title: Lead Cybersecurity Specialist (Governance)
description:
​ Our Client is an established company in
Singapore, who is seeking to recruit a Lead Cybersecurity Specialist (Governance). Lead Cybersecurity Specialist
(Governance) You will be the primary
architect of the security governance and risk management framework., with the mission
to transform GRC from a compliance-heavy exercise into a strategic enabler. You
will ensure that risk management is deeply integrated into the lifecycle of
every digital system, from web applications to critical Operational Technology
(OT) environments. 1. Enterprise Risk
Governance & Management ·       Dynamic Risk Registers: Establish and oversee the security
risk register. You will ensure that registers are not static documents but
"living" tools that accurately reflect the current threat landscape
and project status across all agencies. ·       Senior Management Facilitation: Lead and facilitate
high-level risk conversations with Senior Management. You must be able to
translate complex technical risks into clear business impacts to drive informed
resource allocation and prioritisation. ·       Risk Analysis Framework: Develop a robust framework to guide
agencies in performing consistent, high-quality risk analysis. This framework
should empower agencies to take calculated risks for innovation rather than
defaulting to "no" due to risk aversion. 2. Threat Risk Assessment
(TRA) & Standards ·       Unified TRA Framework: Establish and maintain standards for
conducting Threat Risk Assessments across diverse domains, including Cloud
(GCC), Web Applications, and OT/ICS systems. ·       Crown Jewel Identification: Develop SOPs to guide project
teams in identifying "Crown Jewels" (Critical Information Assets) and
mapping comprehensive threat vectors. ·       Standardisation of Controls: Define common security
configuration standards and ensure that controls are technically effective in
mitigating identified risks, rather than just meeting baseline requirements. 3. Zero Trust &
Architecture Governance ·       Zero Trust Roadmap: Lead the establishment of a Zero Trust
Framework, setting the standards for identity-based security,
micro-segmentation, and "never trust, always verify" architectures. ·       Architectural Advisory: Provide expert GRC input during the
design phase of high-impact systems to ensure security-by-design and alignment
with standards. ·       Technology Application: Evaluate and recommend security
technologies that effectively mitigate specific risks, ensuring that defensive
layers remain relevant against modern threats. 4. Supply Chain &
Ecosystem Risk Management ·       Third-Party Risk Strategy: Establish the framework for
managing risks across the software supply chain and IT vendors. ·       Dependency & Vendor Risk: Develop standards for assessing
the cyber-resilience of third-party partners and managing risks associated with
software dependencies (e.g., Open Source libraries). 5. Audit Excellence &
Systemic Improvement ·       Proactive Readiness: Shift agencies from "reactive"
audit preparation to a state of continuous compliance and readiness. ·       Root Cause Rectification: Oversee the closure of audit
findings, ensuring agencies implement substantive, effective technical fixes
rather than surface-level measures. 6. Stakeholder Management
& Threat Intelligence ·       Education & Advocacy: Partner with key stakeholders to
inculcate a proactive risk management mindset. ·       Threat & Tech Foresight: Keep abreast of evolving Actor
TTPs (Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures) and technology changes. Periodically
review the relevancy of existing defences against the latest threats. Requirements Experience ·       Years of Experience: 10 to 12 years in Cybersecurity GRC,
Information Security Risk Management, or Security Architecture. ·       Domain Breadth: Proven experience in managing risks across IT
and Cloud environments; exposure to OT (Operational Technology) systems is a
significant advantage. ·       Regulatory Knowledge: Deep familiarity with security policies
(e.g., Instruction Manual on IT Management) and international standards (e.g.,
NIST, ISO 27001). Technical Skills ·       Risk Methodologies: Mastery of risk assessment methodologies
(e.g., TVRA) and the ability to translate technical vulnerabilities into
business risk. ·       Security Technologies: Strong technical understanding of
various Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) components and cloud security
technologies. Such as Firewalls, EDR, IAM, SIEM, CSPM, CWPP, CASB and secrets
management etc. ·       Threat Awareness: Ability to map technical controls to the
MITRE ATT&CK framework to ensure defensive coverage. ·       Offensive Security: Proficiency in manual and automated
testing tools; deep understanding of the MITRE ATT&CK framework and common
TTPs. ·       Certifications: Professional certifications such as CISM
(Certified Information Security Manager), CRISC (Certified in Risk and
Information Systems Control), CISSP, OSCP or OSWE (Offensive Security Web
Expert) are highly preferred. Soft Skills ·       Strategic Influence: Ability to educate and persuade senior
stakeholders (CIOs/Project Owners) on the importance of rigorous risk
governance. ·       Critical Thinking: Ability to look past surface-level audit
compliance to find and fix underlying systemic issues. ·       Lifelong Learner: A genuine passion for staying updated on
the latest security technologies and evolving cyber threat landscapes. ·       Risk Articulation: Exceptional ability to
"translate" deep technical issues (e.g., zero-day vulnerabilities,
configuration drifts) into business risk for non-technical senior executives. JJ Consulting Services EA Licence No.: 12C6207 Applicants are invited to send in a MS Word resume to jobs@jjconsulting.com.sg stating
position applying for/present/expected salaries and earliest available date. We thank all applicants in advance and regret that only short listed candidates
will be notified.

title: SAP Master Data Lead
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Project Portfolio and Master Data Lead to join our Technical Team in Neste Tuas Singapore. In this job you will: Lead a team of  Document Integrity Controllers in managing Neste Digital Assets, including SAP Master Data, Engineering Document Depository and other Information Management System (IMS) applications. Function as the Project Portfolio controller to ensure data and information are accurate in SAP, and that approval processes and governance are consistently followed. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job: Master Data & Document Management Leadership : Providing leadership to Document Integrity Controllers in managing life-cycle documents and SAP Master Data. Accountable for SAP Master Data integrity and act as MDG Data Owner Accountable for Document Management system integrity and accessibility by end users. Facilitate data, documents and information handover from projects and MOCs initiatives. Drive continuous Improvement and clarity related to SAP MDG and document management systems, working closely with key stakeholders at site and globally. Provide comprehensive support to end-users, including training, troubleshooting issues and managing system administration tasks. 2. Project Portfolio Control and Support: Supporting Asset Development and Portfolio Manager in ensuring site project portfolio is updated in SAP (PPM and PS) Coordinate monthly site investment jury and portfolio review meeting. Support Development Manager/Engineer in setting up project, releasing WBS, and monitoring of the project budget, schedule and resources utilisation. Collaborate with cross site and global project portfolio team in implementing standardise improvement in portfolio system and processes. Liaise with finance team to ensure the accuracy of the project spending and forecast. Report creation and monitoring of relevant project portfolio information in Power BI Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Education: Diploma, Degree, or Master's in Engineering, Science, or Information Technology. Experience: 7–10 years of experience in the Process Manufacturing/Engineering industry, with a minimum of 2 years of experience in people management. Technical Expertise: Strong SAP skills, particularly in Project Portfolio Management (PPM), Project System (PS), Master Data Governance (MDG), and/or Information Management System (IMS). Knowledge and experience in Document Management Processes, IT, and Quality Systems. Knowledge of industry standards and regulations in this domain is an advantage. Good computer and business solutions software skills; experience with SAP and Google tools is considered an advantage. Soft Skills & Competencies: Quick learner, independent, with good analytical and problem-solving skills. Good Communication skills to different organizational levels. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application by 20 February 2026. We will do our best to contact you within 2 weeks after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The first interviews are preliminarily scheduled to take place soon. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: High School Counselor
description: REPORTS TO : Student Services Director JOB GOAL : The High School Counselor provides proactive, Christ-centered counseling and academic guidance for students in Grades 9-12. The counselor promotes emotional well-being, resilience, and academic success through direct counseling services, collaboration with teachers and parents, and schoolwide initiatives. Serving as a student advocate and intermediary, the counselor supports effective communication between students, faculty, administration, and parents. A primary focus is guiding students through college and career planning. All ICS faculty demonstrate Christian character through kindness, grace, and integrity, creating a nurturing space for students. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Counseling & Student Support Provide individual and small-group counseling to support students’ social-emotional, behavioral, and interpersonal needs. Maintain a safe, confidential, and supportive environment that promotes student trust and growth. Implement preventive and responsive interventions using evidence-based, solution-focused, and restorative approaches. Partner with the Learning Support Teacher and MS/HS Principal to address students’ holistic needs through targeted academic, social, and emotional interventions that enhance classroom engagement, organization, motivation, and peer relationships. Refer students to external professionals or agencies when specialized care is required, ensuring coordinated follow-up and communication. Consultation & Collaboration Serve as a student advocate and liaison, fostering open communication among students, teachers, parents, and administration. Partner with teachers to design classroom strategies that promote positive behavioral, social-emotional, and academic outcomes. Support crisis management by providing immediate intervention and follow-up support when necessary. Academic, College, and Career Counseling Provide students and families with personalized academic, college, and career counseling throughout high school, supporting course selection, graduation planning, and postsecondary preparation. Guide students through the college exploration, application, and decision process, integrating faith-based discernment and reflection. Plan and coordinate college fairs, scholarship information sessions, and financial aid workshops in partnership with universities and external organizations. Implement and refine students’ High School and Post High School plans on an annual basis through individual counseling that results in a written academic learning plan for each student. Design, deliver, and evaluate developmentally appropriate lessons focused on executive functioning, academic success, emotional wellness, resilience, and college/career readiness (during Advisory). Manage student schedules, grades, transcripts, and College Board records, ensuring data accuracy and alignment with graduation requirements. Oversee the College Board Advanced Placement (AP) program and coordinate all relevant courses and assessments. Coordinate the administration of standardized assessments (PSAT, SAT, AP exams, etc.) and use data to inform counseling and academic planning. Review academic records for prospective students to assess eligibility for grade level admission. title: Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on adopting Neste Operations Excellence Management System (OEMS) and translate it to Singapore refinery Integrated Management System (IMS) with ISO 9001, 14001, 45001, SS651 and best practices. You will provide your expertise to lead our Singapore Refinery IMS, ensuring safety compliance are analysed, updated and sustained. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the Singapore Safety department. Your key stakeholders will range from engineers, supervisors and front-line personnel in Singapore to the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. As the local administrator of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System, you are expected to manage, analyse, trend, gain insights and learnings from Refinery unsafe conditions, near misses, incidents and audit management. In our Safety team, you will report to the Safety Manager and support the safety team in safety-related tasks and achieve our common goals. Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Audits Lead & coordinate audits, such as ISO certification, recertifications, internal/external audits, regulatory audit (eg. SS651), Corporate Insurance Survey and Neste OEMS (Operations Excellence Management System) Principle / compliance audit and self assessment. Ensure audits action closure, preparation and follow up are sustainable. IMS (Integrated Management System -  Neste OEMS, SS651, ISO 9001, 14001, 45001) Lead, develop and implement Singapore refinery IMS Prepare and facilitate annual Management Review, support the Management Representative, follow up annual Management Review recommendations and track area of improvement closure Be the NMS (Neste Management System) Space-owner and liaise with the cross functional team on QMS  activities (i.e.NMS Space/procedure/Audits) Gatekeeping/Maintaining NMS documentation framework to ensure compliances are met throughout the certification period. Compliance/Legal Register Review quarterly Legal Register related to Health, Safety, Environmental legal requirements & ISO Standards Work with relevant stakeholders to interpret legal requirements Review compliance activities on a monthly basis Owner of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System Own Singapore refinery NCR System, processing of NCR reportings, programs owner and be its user administrator Lead & drive Near Miss reporting program such as data trending, analysis, proposals and  recommendations for corrective and preventive actions Incident Learning Own and manage the Incident Learning processes (i.e. reporting, classification, investigation & actions) to ensure compliance with corporate, regulatory & site requirements Advise stakeholders on incident classification and reportable incidents Participate in incident investigations as Safety SME or as team leader Lead cross functional team to trend and propose improvement plans to reduce incident rate and improve safety culture Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 8 years safety working experience in petrochemical or chemical industry Bachelor’s Degree in chemical / process / process safety engineering; having a Masters Degree in a related field is highly desired Strong understanding and knowledge to lead, support, implement and operationalise IMS to the workforce Able to effectively lead organization in audit preparations, execution, follow up closure and analysis of long term effectiveness, durability and on-going compliance of implemented actions Ability to interpret corporate and regulatory requirements and effectively translate them into practical, organization-specific solutions while ensuring stakeholder alignment Experienced incident investigation leader or facilitator Strong communication, writing and analytical skill Registered WSHO is an added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request). We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com
