title: High School Science Teacher
description: REPORTS TO: Middle & High School Principal JOB GOAL: A High School Science teacher at ICS must have a strong understanding of the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) and have experience closing the achievement gap. They must develop engaging and innovative lessons that allow our students to grow academically, socially and emotionally. The teacher should be confident in incorporating technology (including emerging technologies such as GenAI) and hands-on learning experiences into the classroom with enthusiasm and also believe in collaboration with other colleagues, promoting understanding and communication in a diverse learning and cultural environment. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Plan, prepare, and execute appropriate lessons, utilizing appropriate resource materials and activities. Follow the prescribed scope and sequence of curriculum and assessment as scheduled. Employ a variety of instructional aides, methods, and materials that will promote best practices in order to reach the whole child as stated in the ICS mission statement. Assess student learning against the appropriate standards on a regular basis and provide progress reports as required. Identify the needs of students through diagnostic procedures and align instructional objectives to these needs. Effect student learning through mastery of the subject material by utilizing valid teaching techniques to achieve curriculum goals within the framework of the school’s philosophy. Eager to explore and learn about emerging technologies, such as Generative AI, with a commitment to integrating them effectively into the classroom to foster critical thinking and guide students in developing ethical and responsible use. Ensure Biblical Worldwide Integration (BWI) is mapped throughout the curriculum. Complete lesson/unit plans, Atlas, ESO documentation, and PLC expectations within specified time frames. Maintain student records as required by the school. Communicate with parents, students, and other professional staff regarding student progress (behavioral and academic). Follow school policies, procedures, rules, regulations, and guidelines and the provisions of the contract. Maintain proper discipline in the classroom and on the school premises utilizing teacher created discipline plans in conjunction with a school-wide discipline plan. Inform the administration if/when unable to fulfill any duty assigned. Prepare adequate information and materials for a substitute teacher. Plan and lead a student Chapel when called upon. Supervise extracurricular activities, organizations, and outings as assigned. Complete the Christian Philosophy of Education in-service program within the first three years of teaching at ICS. Attend and participate in devotions, teachers’ meetings, evening programs (if involving his or her students or as requested by the administration), and other meetings as assigned. Carry out supervisory duty as assigned (before school, break, lunch, after school). Maintain a classroom in a clean and educationally stimulating environment. Maintain regular and accurate attendance and grade reports that provide comprehensive information for each student’s learning progress. Keep students, parents, and the administration informed of students’ progress or deficiencies, and provide sufficient and timely notice to parents when students fail to meet learning standards. Submit all appropriate reports to the administration, guidance office, and business office as required by school regulations or as requested. Work with the administration in implementing all policies, procedures, and directives governing the operation of the school. Complete professional development programs as required by the administration and/or ICS Board. Maintain teaching credentials as required through sponsoring agencies (state/provincial education department, ACSI, etc.). Acquire and maintain teaching credentials as required by the laws of Singapore, NICS, and/or ICS. Demonstrate the character qualities of enthusiasm, courtesy, flexibility, integrity, kindness, self-control, perseverance, and punctuality. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity, and optimism. Develop and maintain rapport with students, parents, and staff by treating others with friendliness, open-mindedness, dignity, and consideration. Respectfully submit to and demonstrate loyalty to constituted authority. Maintain a personal appearance that models cleanliness, modesty, good taste, and is in agreement with school policy. QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s Degree Teaching credential from a US state, Canadian province or from ACSI (exceptions will be approved by the Academic Board) Expertise in working with students and teaching Advanced Placement (AP) courses Knowledge of specific subject(s)

title: Programmes Executive / Assistant (Outdoor Education)
description: POSITION OVERVIEW As a Programmes Executive (Outdoor Education), you will play a key role in the planning, coordination, and delivery of outdoor adventure programmes, camps, and expeditions under The Singapore Scout Association (SSA). You will be responsible for ensuring programmes are well-designed, safely conducted, and aligned with Scouting values, educational objectives, and/or award requirements. In this role, you will work closely with youths, volunteers, facilitators, schools, parents and stakeholders to deliver meaningful and impactful outdoor experiences that supports development. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Programme Planning & Coordination • Plan, design, and coordinate outdoor adventure programmes, camps, expeditions and experiential learning activities. • Ensure programmes align with organisational values, learning objectives, MOE/NYAA frameworks and/or award requirements where applicable. • Develop, review and refresh programme content to meet the evolving needs of participants, schools and stakeholders. • Customise programmes according to participant profiles, age groups, learning outcomes and risk levels. • Prepare detailed programme schedules, activity outlines, lesson plans, facilitator guides and supporting materials. • Conduct site recces and feasibility checks to ensure suitability of locations and activities. • Support budgeting, costing and resource planning for programmes. • Perform any other roles and responsibilities as assigned by the Supervisor and/or Executive Director. Event Logistics & Stakeholder Coordination • Coordinate logistical requirements including transport, accommodation, meals, equipment, permits and venue bookings. • Liaise with schools, vendors, outdoor facilities, facilitators, volunteers and parents to ensure smooth programme delivery. • Source, brief and coordinate external vendors and instructors where required. • Manage participant registrations, consent forms, medical declarations, risk acknowledgements and administrative documentation. • Prepare and disseminate pre-programme briefings, joining instructions and contingency plans. • Ensure timely and clear communication with all stakeholders before, during and after programmes. • Track logistics timelines and ensure all deliverables are met within schedule. On-Ground Supervision & Programme Delivery • Lead, brief and support facilitators, instructors and volunteers on programme objectives, safety protocols and roles. • Supervise on-ground operations to ensure smooth programme flow, discipline and participant engagement. • Facilitate or co-facilitate activities when required, ensuring quality learning experiences. • Provide real-time coordination, problem-solving and decision-making during programme delivery. • Act as the main point-of-contact on-site for schools and stakeholders. Safety, Risk & Compliance • Conduct comprehensive risk assessments and hazard analysis for all outdoor and adventure-based activities. • Ensure compliance with SSA safety policies, SOPs and relevant national regulations (e.g. MOE guidelines, Workplace Safety & Health requirements). • Ensure facilitators and instructors adhere to safety standards, ratios and operational guidelines. • Oversee emergency preparedness, first-aid readiness, evacuation plans and incident response procedures. • Document and report incidents, near-misses and safety observations in a timely manner. • Support safety audits and reviews to enhance organisational risk management practices. Monitoring, Evaluation & Reporting • Collect, analyse and consolidate feedback from participants, facilitators, volunteers and stakeholders. • Evaluate programme effectiveness against learning outcomes, safety standards and operational objectives. • Prepare post-programme and post-event reports, including outcomes, observations and recommendations. • Track programme data, attendance and performance metrics for reporting purposes. • Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance programme quality, safety and delivery standards. • Contribute to the development of best practices, SOPs and training resources. WORKING ARRANGEMENT 1. 5-day work week 2. office hours, with flexibility required for camps, events, and outdoor programmes 3. May include overnights, evenings, weekends or public holidays based on programme requirements REQUIRED SKILLS & ATTRIBUTES 1. Strong interest in outdoor education, camping and adventure-based programmes. 2. Enjoys working with youths, volunteers and diverse stakeholder groups 3. Strong organisational, coordination, and problem-solving skills. 4. Able to work independently and as part of a team in dynamic environments. 5. Comfortable with on-ground operations and field-based work. 6. Responsible, safety-conscious, and able to manage multiple tasks effectively WHY JOIN US? 1. Medical and dental benefits 2. Annual Wage Supplement (AWS) 3. Opportunity to contribute to a meaningful youth development organisation 4. Hands-on involvement in impactful outdoor and experiential learning programmes

title: Nanyang Assistant Professor
description: Nanyang Assistant Professorship (NAP) Seeking The World’s Smartest Minds to Address Humanity’s Biggest Challenges Young and research-intensive, Nanyang Technological University (NTU Singapore) is ranked among the world’s top universities. NTU has a vibrant research eco-system with a high proportion of early career researchers. It has a healthy environment for interdisciplinary research collaboration and the research is well funded. The Nanyang Assistant Professorship scheme provides the opportunity for outstanding early career researchers and exceptional scholars from Singapore and around the world, who aspire to a research leadership role at NTU. Successful NAP awardees will be given a tenure-track Assistant/Associate Professor appointment and granted a substantial start-up grant (SUG) to lay the foundation for their research ventures within the university. NTU invites outstanding early-career researchers (postdoctoral fellow or equivalent) to apply for an appointment as a Nanyang Assistant Professor. Eligibility criteria Applicants must be early career researchers, holding a PhD or MD or equivalent degree, obtained within the last 10 years. Clinician scientists engaged in translational and/or clinical science, encompassing areas such as bench-to-bedside research, clinical trials, epidemiology, digital health, health services research, and interventional studies in any medical discipline, are encouraged to apply. A strong track record of early achievements and the potential to lead an independent research group are required. Eligibility extends to both new external applicants and NTU Faculty (not holding the rank of Associate Professor or Professor) who have joined within the last 12 months or within 3 years after PhD, whichever comes later. Existing NTU Faculty are limited to a single application attempt. Selected candidates must be committed to taking up an independent research appointment at NTU. Benefits NAP Awardees will enjoy a comprehensive set of benefits designed to support and enhance their academic and research at NTU: An attractive remuneration package. A tenure-track appointment and opportunities in leading roles in the University's new wave of multi-disciplinary and integrative research projects. Access to excellent R&D infrastructure, featuring state-of-the-art laboratories provided by the University, as well as opportunities for research funding. Each NAP Awardee will receive a start-up research grant (SUG), which will be determined based on the awardee's research track, as outlined below: STEM: Up to SGD 1.5 million startup research grant and scholarships for hiring up to 5 PhD students SHAPE: Up to SGD 1 million startup research grant and scholarships for hiring up to 2 PhD students A dedicated White Space Fund of up to SGD 250,000, within the budget cap stipulated above. NTU is an equal opportunity employer. The University is dedicated to the goal of building a culturally diverse and pluralistic university comprising faculty and staff who are committed to research, teaching and working in a multicultural environment. We invite individuals of diverse gender, ethnicity, and other under-represented groups, including those with disabilities, to apply. Application Process Applications for NTU NAP can be submitted throughout the year. Interested applicants are welcome to send their CVs and other supporting documents via this link . Click here for the application guidelines and required documents to be submitted. The application evaluation will usually take around 2-3 months. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified/contacted by the relevant schools

title: AVP, Risk Manager – Wealth Management
description: Position Overview - The role is focused on establishing the Conduct & Control Department as a proactive business partner and a key contributor to the Bank’s business strategy. - Acts as the first line of defense to ensure risk-based, sustainable, and efficient strategies are implemented to address Operational, Business, and Conduct risks in the APAC region. - Works closely with the Wealth Management (WM) business in APAC to manage and mitigate risks. Primary Role Responsibilities - The candidate will support the Head of Governance, Advisory & Surveillance, WM APAC Conduct and Control, in maintaining a robust risk framework for Operational and Business risks in collaboration with WM in APAC (Hong Kong and Singapore). - The position is based in Singapore and operates within the first line of defense (1 LoD). Key Responsibilities 1. Incident Investigation and Analysis - Ensure timely lodging and escalation of incidents. - Lead and coordinate in-depth investigations of operational risk incidents across Wealth Management in Singapore and Hong Kong. - Conduct structured fact-finding and evidence analysis to determine root causes and contributing factors. - Assess downstream and upstream impacts, ensuring proposed actions address immediate, past, and future issues. - Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of remediation actions and preventive measures. - Perform trend analysis to identify opportunities to strengthen the control framework. 2. Escalation and Governance - Ensure timely escalation of incidents to Senior Management in accordance with defined thresholds and governance requirements. - Prepare clear and concise incident summaries, analyses, and reporting materials for management and governance committees (e.g., ICC, ORCC). 3. Stakeholder Coordination - Serve as a key coordination point between Front Office, Operations, Compliance, Legal, Finance, IT, and other stakeholders for incident matters. - Liaise with Head Office and regional counterparts to ensure consistency in incident handling and alignment with Group expectations. - Facilitate effective ownership and accountability for incident remediation. 4. Reporting and Continuous Improvement - Produce incident reports (e.g., quarterly reporting) and trend analyses for management. - Contribute to the review and enhancement of incident-related procedures, escalation matrices, and governance frameworks. - Support internal audits, inspections, and ad-hoc risk reviews related to operational incidents. - Assist in various risk reporting requirements for different forums and to Head Office. 5. Risk Assessment and Reporting - Participate in the review and collation of annual Enterprise Risk Assessments, such as Risk Cartography Self-Assessment and Risk Identification. 6. Ad Hoc Projects - Undertake ad hoc projects as assigned by the Head of Conduct and Control – Governance, Advisory and Surveillance. Requirements - Strong understanding of risk control frameworks and operational risk management. - Excellent stakeholder management and collaboration skills. - Structured, detail-oriented, and methodical approach to work. - Strong analytical skills with the ability to assess and articulate risk impacts. - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present investigation findings clearly and concisely to governance forums. - At least 5 to 8 years (or more) of relevant experience in Operational Risk, Incident Management, Internal Control, Audit, or related risk functions, preferably within private banking or wealth management. - Proven experience in operational incident investigation, including fact-finding, timeline reconstruction, root cause analysis, and remediation assessment. - Sound understanding of Operational Risk Management frameworks, internal controls, and governance within the private banking sector. - Strong data analysis skills, with the ability to work on various dashboard reporting for Management and investigative work.

title: Head of Membership, Asia
description: POSITION PURPOSE The Head of Membership, Asia is part of the CASE Global membership team. This position is responsible for membership growth, engagement, and retention across Asia, including key markets such as South East Asia (e.g. Singapore, Malaysia), Hong Kong and China, India, and West Asia (e.g., Lebanon and the U.A.E.). This includes, but is not limited to, account management of member institutions, managing membership products and services, and the regional strategy for acquisition, engagement, and retention of members. The position is accountable for the identification and cultivation of sponsorship opportunities that support strategic priorities and regional programs. The position works closely with partners, volunteers, and internal teams to build lasting relationships and advance regional impact. In a small office, all staff are expected to be involved in developing, supporting, delivering and driving all CASE business. This role has responsibility for membership revenue, including meeting established targets for growth and retention of members. The core functions of this position include but are not limited to the following: Membership Strategy Lead the Asia-Pacific membership retention and growth strategy in Asia, working closely with the Asia-Pacific Executive Director and colleagues across the Global Membership team to achieve targets identified in CASE’s Global Membership Growth Plan. Identify and build relationships with potential partners to support membership engagement and growth. Maintain responsibility for achieving an annual revenue goal and identifying opportunities for new revenue streams. In consultation with the Senior Director, Member Strategy and Marketing, manage the membership acquisition strategy, with particular emphasis on emerging markets and identifying potential new markets. Membership Engagement : Develop and own membership retention plan to improve retention rate across the membership in Asia. Manage the provision of the first line of customer service for all membership enquiries, meeting internal standards for response time and quality of response. Working with the Executive Director, AP and the Senior Director, Member Strategy and Marketing, develop and manage a regional membership stewardship programme, including regular touch points, to improve engagement with particular emphasis on at-risk members Manage an institutional or individual onboarding plan for new members Volunteer Stewardship Establish good relationships with volunteer leaders in Asia to ensure goals and outcomes support CASE’s priorities and align with our strategic direction. Lead the strategic recruitment of new volunteers and coordinate induction activities in Asia. Support volunteers in their roles in fulfilling engagement activities, networking activities, and other projects between meetings. Liaise with colleagues responsible for educational programmes in the Asia-Pacific region and in the global Membership, Volunteerism, and Engagement Division to align activities and deliver a consistent volunteer experience in Asia. Fiscal Management/Budget Responsibilities/Judicious Use of Resources Manage the annual budget assigned for the role and prepare periodic reports as requested. Exercise fiscal responsibility in the utilisation of all CASE resources. Liaison Responsibilities Work cooperatively and diplomatically with all divisions and regions. Maintain flexibility to work across different time-zones, including late evenings, as and when required. Liaise with CASE colleagues in other parts of the world, especially those in membership, marketing, communications, and volunteer engagement, to ensure the best sharing of ideas, expertise, and knowledge. Incorporate tasks and/or projects related to objectives as directed by the CASE Strategic Plan. Image Responsibilities In keeping with the CASE Values Statement, ensures that CASE is well represented by exercising a high degree of professionalism, accuracy, and ethics, in all activities, services, and products. POSITION REQUIREMENTS Education & Experience Educated to degree level. Fluency and competency in writing in English. Proficiency in a relevant regional language strongly preferred (e.g., Mandarin, Cantonese, Arabic or Hindi). Evidence of business development and/or marketing experience to deliver against income and/or numerical targets at a team level and independently Evidence of managing a team, budgets, and targets. Experience in institutional advancement, e.g., alumni relations, communications, fundraising, or advancement services, preferred. Skills & Abilities A demonstrated interest in advancing education in the nonprofit sector. A strong commitment to contributing to an inclusive organizational culture. An understanding and interest in engaging with a broad range of cultures and countries. Excellent relationship-building, account management and member services skills, demonstrating evidence of working with and developing opportunities for volunteer, supporter and/or member engagement Excellent time management and prioritization skills with ability to manage multiple projects. High comfort level with IT systems and good working knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (including Access), Outlook and Database Management. Ability to communicate effectively with people of all levels and work well as part of a team. Self-starter with ability to take ownership of and work autonomously on initiatives. Strong commercial, market-led approach. Ability and willingness to travel 2-3 times a year in the region. Readiness to work unsocial hours on occasion (e.g., over weekends at conferences). Maintain a high standard of excellence. Ability to embrace CASE values and work collaboratively with enthusiasm and energy.

title: Assistant Director (RTO), A*STAR
description:
Research Trust Office is a data sharing and analytics platform that supports the secure use of research and
real-world datasets to enable health research data analytics, so as to improve health outcomes for Singapore and support data-driven innovation. JOB OVERVIEW We are looking for a strategic, collaborative and nurturing leader to lead research enablement as one of the
three key teams under Research Trust Office Operations. You will play a key role in improving user experience, supporting platform functionality design & enhancement, and use case onboarding. As the Research Enablement lead, you will champion the use of the Research Trust Office platform for health
research analysis and proactively engage users consisting of researchers from public and private research  cosystem (e.g. universities, research institutions, public health care institutions, industries etc.) to uplift their
proficiency in navigating the platform, and perform health and biomedical research data analysis. As user advocate, you will play a critical role in analysing users’ needs, and coupled with domain knowledge of
health research analysis, provide critical insights to guide the enhancement of platform design and development of the platform roadmap. Operationally, you will also work closely with the sub-teams within Research Trust Office and appointed service
providers to address user queries for efficient resolution. JOB RESPONSIBILITIES Use Case Onboarding Lead training activities including the training materials development such as demo data, data science notebooks and videos, as well as delivering the training. Support user manual and help content development. Apply domain knowledge to support the quality assurance process of data release. User Feedback and Guide Platform Roadmap 4.  Lead engagement with research users to understand the needs and issues while using the Research Trust Office platform. 5. Work cross-functionally with different team members to perform user feedback review and provide report to support the solution development and upgrade. 6. Design and prototype solutions to demonstrate the usage scenarios using the Research Trust Office platform and genomics tools available on the platform. User Support 7. Respond to and follow-up users’ requests related to platform usage and resources, analyse, and map the requests to the features supported by the Research Trust Office platform. JOB REQUIREMENTS Minimum degree in Bioinformatics, Biomedical informatics, or related field with strong computational and statistical foundation required. Advanced degree (Master's/PhD) in relevant discipline is advantageous. At least 7–10 years of experience in bioinformatics, biomedical data science, or related fields, with demonstrated expertise in artificial intelligence, multi-omics analysis, and large-scale data processing. Strong background in clinical or pharmaceutical data analytics preferred. Strong proficiency in Python programming for data science and analytics; R for statistical analysis and modelling; Shell scripting and CLI tools. Extensive experience (minimum 5 years) with statistical analysis, predictive modelling, data mining techniques, and/or in applying statistical and machine learning techniques in healthcare research. Knowledge of Core AWS services and solutions. AWS certification is advantageous. Knowledge of the following will be highly regarded: Genomics research & genomics analysis tools, statistical software packages including STATA, SPSS, and MATLAB. Exceptional interpersonal and communication abilities with demonstrated experience in stakeholder engagement, professional presentations, and collaborative team environments.

title: Manager, Business & Program Development
description:
The Manager, Business & Program Development, will provide strategic leadership to enhance the
organization’s overall growth.  This role involves spearheading business development efforts, creating and executing impactful programs, and fostering collaboration to drive revenue and achieve organizational objectives. Additionally, the Manager will lead the business development and program team in diversity and SMART MedTech, and Sustainability Initiatives in Southeast Asia to raise awareness and attract talents for the industry. This is a full-time position and reports directly to the Director, Business & Program
Development at SEMI SEA Office. THE IDEAL CANDIDATE: The ideal candidate can communicate internally and externally to meet the company’s overall target goal. The candidate is self-driven and collaborates closely with other senior leaders to drive the organization’s overall success. CORE RESPONSIBILITIES: · Increase programs relevancy and value to members – Develop new content for programs. Work closely with program committees, as they set program objectives and develop content. Assist in preparing meetings and project plans to meet program needs. Ensure quality of speakers and presentation materials. Create and develop new digital programs –increase values to members. Provide strategies for SEMI Southeast Asia – Play a vital role in contributing to strategizing for the expansion of the SEA region market, encompassing budget management and performing ROI analysis.  Take the lead in Key Account Management to ensure sustained growth and success. Manage, develop and execute the business growth and Go-to-market strategies for new service. Conduct and execute business plans for new SEMI products - services and platforms to support SEMI’s larger span of the global electronics manufacturing supply chain in areas such as semiconductor equipment
and materials, semiconductor manufacturing, automotive electronics, medical electronics, flexible and hybrid electronics, MEMS, Advanced Packaging (+ other IoT markets). Develop strategic partnerships (industrial, Public, Associations)
for new segments (horizontally and vertically). Assess and prioritize SEMI’s targeted engagement in vertical and horizontal/adjacent supply chain segments. Strategic Planning – Contribute to the development of organizational strategies to drive sustainable growth. WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE: Successful implementation of impactful programs. Work closely with senior management to align business and program
development initiatives with overall company objectives. Identify new business opportunities and partnerships to expand market
presence. CRITICAL SKILLS: Working with regional offices and partners to identify target client markets and developing outreach strategies. Ability to create and communicate a long-term vision while balancing short term goals Provide effective leadership to the business and program development teams. Demonstrate sound judgement in evaluating creative ideas and suggestions and adeptly manage the processes with effectiveness and efficiency. PREFERRED KNOWLEDGE OR PRACTICAL EXPERIENCE: Education - B.S./B.A. degree in semiconductor or related field or an equivalence in working experience. Minimum 5 years of experience in business development or related roles. Knowledge of the semiconductor industry is mandatory with good contact points in E&E ecosystem. Strong collaboration and communication skills as demonstrated by ability to work cross-functionally with internal stakeholders. Ability to collaborate effectively and articulate clear organizational vision. ABOUT SEMI SEMI is the global industry association representing the electronics manufacturing supply chain, connecting over 3,000 member companies and 1.3 million professionals worldwide. SEMI members are responsible for the innovations in materials, design, equipment, software, devices, and services that enable smarter, faster, more powerful, and more affordable electronic products. Since 1970, SEMI has built connections that have helped its members prosper, create new markets, and address common industry challenges together.

title: Pan-Asia Equities Reporter - Singapore
description: Bloomberg News is one of the biggest financial and business news organizations in the world. We lead the way in breaking news and providing analysis on financial markets, from real-time coverage to in-depth features. Known for our accuracy, speed, and insight, we deliver journalism investors can act on, and that commitment drives us to keep innovating. We are seeking an experienced and motivated reporter to join our Asia equities team, based in Singapore. From a boom in artificial intelligence stocks to the rising influences of retail investors, this role offers a front-row seat to the forces reshaping Asia's equity markets. You will work closely with a team of expert reporters and editors in Singapore and across the region, covering the daily ebb and flows of Asian markets while helping explain their global significance to Bloomberg’s worldwide audience. The role involves breaking news, writing sharp real-time analysis, and producing ambitious step-back features. You will also develop and maintain strong relationships with key market participants, including leading fund managers focused on Asian assets, and regularly publish interviews with influential voices in the region. We’ll trust you to: Break news and write insightful stories analyzing market trends on a deadline Build and maintain a strong source base across the region from traders to money managers Monitor stock and index moves and explain the drivers behind them Mine the Bloomberg terminal for smart and actionable data-driven stories Participate in live coverage of events such as central-bank decisions and earnings, highlighting the stock-market impact Collaborate closely with colleagues on the Equities Team globally and other members across Asia Markets colleagues Engage audiences through multimedia storytelling and discussions on Bloomberg’s digital platforms Serve as deputy team leader for pan-Asia stories, coordinating with the South Asia equities team leader to ensure timely, comprehensive coverage of major Asia regional market trends Bring creativity and innovation to story framing, writing, and presentation You need to have: At least 7 years of recent financial journalism experience At least 3 years of experience working in a real-time breaking news environment Experience covering Equities market as a reporter or equivalent experience at Bloomberg Proven ability to make and build sources in financial markets A track record of team work and collaboration An ability to write quickly, clearly and accurately under tight deadline pressure A bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience Demonstrated continuous career growth within an organization Fluency in both written and spoken English Life at Bloomberg is many things, but it’s never dull. We’re a truly global business with a truly diverse workforce. We pride ourselves on being open, inclusive and collaborative, and provide a work environment that inspires our employees to be their best. We offer competitive compensation, comprehensive healthcare coverage, and programs to support you and your family through different life stages, from parental leave to generous retirement savings plans. If this sounds like you: Apply if you think we're a good match. We'll get in touch to let you know what the next steps are, but in the meantime feel free to have a look at this: www.bloomberg.com/professional Learn more about our office and benefits: Singapore | www.bloomberg.com/professional www.bloomberg.com/singapore

title: Managing Director, Head of ASO Systems Planning (JOB ID: 10074861)
description: As the Managing Director, Regional Head of the Systems Planning Department, you will play a crucial role in shaping and executing the IT strategy for MUFG across the Asia Pacific (APAC) region. This position is pivotal in driving the strategic vision and ensuring the alignment of IT initiatives with the overall business objectives. You will lead the Systems Planning Department, overseeing the development and implementation of high-level IT strategies, policies, and frameworks that support the bank's growth and operational efficiency. Reporting directly to the CIO for APAC, you will collaborate with senior leadership and various stakeholders to ensure that IT strategies are effectively integrated into business plans, fostering innovation and driving the bank's digital transformation. Your role will encompass strategic planning, business relationship management, project management, and the optimization of IT investments to enhance business performance and resilience. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute the regional systems planning strategy in alignment with business goals, global IT strategy, and regulatory requirements. Provide top-level direction and vision for the IT landscape in the APAC region. Establish and maintain strong relationships with business partners, ensuring that IT initiatives meet business needs and drive value. Act as a key liaison between IT and business units to align technology solutions with business strategies. Lead the Project Management Office (PMO) to ensure successful delivery of IT projects, aligning them with business priorities and ensuring adherence to project management standards. Oversee the facilitation and support of system projects across the region. Optimize the allocation of IT resources, including budget management, to support strategic objectives and operational efficiency. Manage the system investment budget allocation and utilization for the Asia and Oceania region. Ensure the development and implementation of robust business continuity and disaster recovery plans to safeguard the bank's operations. Manage administrative tasks for the department, including expense control and human resource management. Oversee the preparation of management materials and support for regional IT training programs. Ensure adherence to local regulations and internal policies, maintaining robust governance frameworks. Coordinate with IT Risk Management and other departments to uphold IT governance standards. To liaise and collaborate with stakeholders at the Tokyo Head Office to ensure effective integration of IT strategies into business plans, and present these strategies in Japanese during meetings and conferences. Requirements: Minimum of 15 years of experience in IT systems planning and technology, in the financial industry. Experience in systems planning and emerging technologies, in business relationship management, project management, and IT investment planning. Minimum JLPT N1 Certification (or equivalent business-level Japanese proficiency) with demonstrated experience communicating with Japanese stakeholders and senior management, including acting as a key point of contact across Tokyo Head Office and APAC. Comprehensive understanding of business strategies and challenges, with the ability to align IT initiatives with business goals. Demonstrated leadership capabilities, with the ability to manage and inspire a diverse team in a complex and dynamic environment. Excellent organizational and communication skills, with the ability to effectively engage with stakeholders at all levels and present complex technical concepts clearly. Proven ability to plan and execute enterprise-level changes, with a deep understanding of enterprise architecture and industry trends. We regret to inform that only shortlisted applicants will be notified.

title: Director, FCR Integrated, Intelligence & Investigations (i3), ASIA
description: Job Summary The employee is a team member of Group Financial Crime Risks Investigations (GFCRI). The role will: Oversee, review and advise on projects and investigations to identify and mitigate financial crime risk; the causes, control issues and remediation. Key responsibilities include, setting and continued evolution of investigative methodologies, training, coaching and mentoring of investigators, and the delivery of compliant, thorough and insightful products to a range of clients and stakeholders internal and external to the bank. Act as part of GFCRI’s extended leadership team to support the setting and delivery of GFCRI’s OKRs and strategy, and contribute more broadly to the delivery of the CFCR Function of the Future. The role is expected to also support and lead on activities outside of investigations, with a view to generating a more integrated GFCRI across its (and FCR’s) various component activities. Key Responsibilities Strategy •    As part of GFCRI, support setting and implementation of OKRs, strategy, direction and leadership for the Investigations team, consistent with the vision and strategy for FCR and in support of the Group’s strategic direction and growth aspirations. •    Leverage GFCRI tools, standards and resources to identify and mitigate financial crime risk across the Group, with a particular emphasis on previously unknown client risk Business •    Lead and manage investigators and their investigations and projects. Advising directing and reviewing their investigations. •    Experienced in financial crime investigations, open source research, transactions analysis and report writing. •    Have a good understanding of global money laundering with an expertise in Asia region. •    Mentor, coach and train junior investigators and reports. •    Represent the Bank and GFCRI in internal and external workshops, briefings and forums. •    Work with stakeholders to understand their requirements and deliver solutions. •    Produce reports and policy type documents for senior stakeholders. Risk Management •    Provide subject matter expertise on priority and emerging financial crime topics and issues, drawing on financial crime intelligence and law enforcement and industry networks, professional qualifications and other experience. •    Manage and develop further relationships with GFCRI clients and stakeholders to ensure effective communication regarding relevant significant and key investigations. •    Assess and mitigate risks arising from products / segments / geographies / customers / transactions. •    Identify intelligence for typology studies and risk mitigation plans. •    Identify and assess financial crime risk indicators through various internal / external sources and the media; map and assess those risks, quantify the potential impact and escalate where necessary. Advise relevant clients and stakeholders on outcomes of financial crime risks identification and assessment methodologies. •    Promote the use of data and apply data analytics to large data sets in order to identify new and emerging financial crime risks. •    Analyse significant financial crime risk events and the potential network (e.g. SARs, non-compliant transactions, production orders) to ensure that all connected parties, particularly cross-border are identified and reported at the appropriate level internally as well as externally across all relevant jurisdictions. •    Manage end-to-end financial crime client investigations initiated by external sources (regulators, agencies, authorities or other external organisation), and internal events as applicable. •    Escalate to senior management serious regulatory breaches (or where risk tolerances have been breached) promptly. •    Ensure remedial actions (e.g. Regulatory reporting, client exits, additions to watchlists) are recommended, approved by applicable Risk Framework and Process Owners and implemented quickly. •    Partner with legal counsel for advice on technical matters. •    Provide investigative expertise to support management of Significant Regulatory / Legal cases / Speak up events. •    Conduct root cause analysis on control / other failures to ensure lessons are learned across the bank. •    Provide intelligence to GFCRI clients on specific clients, client-types and emerging risk typologies, and engage in external fora where appropriate. Governance •    Support senior oversight of GFCRI. •    In the event of serious regulatory breaches, or where risk tolerances have been breached, ensure senior management and relevant regulators are informed and that actions are taken quickly to remediate and/or activities are ceased. •    Prepare and cascade lessons learned from investigations as applicable. •    Propose control improvements, enhancements and simplifications where appropriate. •    Produce high quality written reports of findings, including observations and recommendations, suitable for internal and external stakeholders, including regulators where applicable. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    As part of the extended leadership team, lead GFCRI to achieve the outcomes set out in the Bank’s Conduct Principles: [Fair Outcomes for Clients; Effective Operation of Financial Markets; Financial Crime Prevention; The Right Environment]. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key stakeholders •    Head, GFCRI •    Head, GFCI Investigations •    Head GFCRI Intelligence •    GFCRI Team Leads •    FCC Country and Cluster Heads •    Senior FCR Leadership •    Senior CFCR Leadership •    CFCR Advisory •    FCSO •    Risk Framework and Process Owners •    Segment Business Heads/Teams Other Responsibilities Embed Here for good and Group’s brand and values in GFCRI; Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. Our Ideal Candidate •    Professionally qualified – Preferably Graduate or Postgraduate in a numerate or research discipline. •    Experienced in managing and leading teams of investigators. •    10+ years of relevant experience in a bank, law enforcement,  regulatory, financial services, compliance, legal or audit function with a financial crime related focus. •    Excellent understanding of international money laundering and financial crime across ASIA. •    CAMS / ICA certification is an advantage. •    Have an advanced knowledge of the Group, the Policies, Procedures and Standards relating to Financial Crime Compliance, as well as knowledge of the major regulations and legislation on AML/CTF and sanctions. •    Experience of developing and leading teams. •    Strong analytical experience working with large data sets to identify trends, patterns and typologies. •    Interest in geo-political affairs and anti-financial crime industry developments. •    Proactive, creative and critical thinker, with the ability to adapt to different requirements, challenge status quo, challenge robustly but constructively and to build strong instrumental relationships with key stakeholders and clients. •    The employee is obligated to conduct internal training for all members of GFCRI and FCR when needed. •    CAMS / ICA certification is an advantage Role Specific Technical Competencies • Risk Management •    Knowledge of processes, tools and techniques for assessing and controlling an organization's exposure to risks of various kinds; ability to apply this knowledge appropriately to diverse situations. • Data Gathering and Reporting •    Knowledge of and ability to utilize tools, techniques and processes for gathering and reporting data in a particular department or division of a company. • Big Data Management •    Knowledge of and experience with day-to-day management, operations and support of a big data application, systems and platforms. • CFCC Policies and Standards •    Knowledge of and experience with CFCC policy and standards • Cross-functional Collaboration •    Knowledge of collaborative techniques and approaches; ability to promote a culture of continuous improvement and working together across functions to solve business problems and meet business goals. • Business Acumen •    Knowledge, insight, and understanding of business concepts, tools, and processes that are needed for making sound decisions in the context of the company's business; ability to apply this knowledge appropriately to diverse situations. About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. title: Director, Business Development, Industrial
description: The Director of Business Development is to support the overall growth in Asia working closely with the Senior Vice President and the Asia Leadership team to expand the AtkinsRéalis current presence in the Industrial and Project & Program Services (PPS) markets as well as Singapore as the regional hub to achieve the Vision 35 strategic plan. The role is responsible for leading and executing business development strategies across the Industrial and Project & Program Services (PPS) sectors in Asia. The role encompasses client relationship management, marketing and communications, BD team leadership, performance reporting, and strategic growth planning. In addition to current responsibilities, the role includes oversight of business development across all sectors in Singapore, with a focus on cross-sector collaboration and cross-selling initiatives. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Planning & Execution · Develop and implement regional business development strategies for Industrial and PPS sectors. · Lead cross-sector BD efforts in Singapore, promoting integrated service offerings and cross-sales. · Identify and pursue strategic growth opportunities across Asia. Client Relationship Management · Maintain and expand relationships with key clients and stakeholders. · Ensure client satisfaction through proactive engagement and strategic alignment. · Support account retention and growth initiatives. Marketing & Communications · Collaborate with marketing teams to develop sector-specific campaigns and materials. · Represent the company at industry events, conferences, and client engagements. · Ensure consistent brand messaging across regions and sectors. Team Leadership & Collaboration · Lead and manage the Asia BD team, fostering a collaborative and high-performance culture. · Promote cross-sector coordination, particularly in Singapore. · Work closely with sector leads, country managers, and global BD teams to align efforts. Reporting & Performance Management · Track and report on BD performance metrics, pipeline development, and conversion rates. · Provide regular updates to senior leadership on market trends and strategic initiatives. · Use data-driven insights to refine BD strategies and improve outcomes. Qualifications: · Extensive experience in business development, preferably within Industrial and PPS sectors. · Strong understanding of the Asia market and client dynamics. · Proven success in cross-sector and cross-regional BD leadership. · Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills. · Demonstrated ability to lead diverse teams and drive strategic initiatives. Preferred Attributes: · Strong client orientation and commercial acumen. · Strategic and analytical mindset. · Collaborative leadership style. · Adaptability in a dynamic, multi-cultural environment.

title: Computing and Cybersecurity Teacher
description: Key Responsibilities: ●     Teach core and applied computing modules, with a strong focus on cybersecurity and Python programming. ●     Design learner-centred lessons that build skills in secure coding, threat analysis, and ethical hacking. ●     Guide students through real-world projects aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals. ●     Lead and mentor student talent through CCAs such as coding clubs, cybersecurity competitions, and SST Inc. Girls-In-Tech. ●     Lead female students in overseas education partnerships and programme. Minimum Qualifications and Requirements: ●     A recognised Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Cybersecurity, or a related field. ●     Proficiency in cybersecurity concepts (secure systems, threat mitigation, ethical hacking) and Python programming. ●     Strong classroom facilitation skills and a commitment to applied, inquiry-based learning. ●     Openness to overseas travel for collaborative student programmes. Preferred Qualifications: ●     Industry certifications such as CompTIA Security+, CEH, or CISSP. ●     Experience in curriculum design, student mentorship, or managing CCA programmes in cybersecurity or computing. ●     Familiarity with EdTech platforms, cloud tools, and innovative teaching pedagogies. We strongly encourage all candidates who are passionate advocates for inclusive technology education and supporting GIRLS-IN-STEM initiatives to apply.

title: High School Design Technology Teacher
description: REPORTS TO: Middle & High School Principal JOB GOAL: A High School Design Technology teacher at ICS must have a strong understanding of the ISTE standards and have experience closing the achievement gap. This position is specifically for someone with a background in STEM, however other applicants will be considered. They must also develop engaging and innovative lessons that allow our students to grow academically, socially and emotionally. The teacher should be confident in incorporating technology (including emerging technologies such as GenAI) and hands-on learning experiences into the classroom with enthusiasm and also believe in collaboration with other colleagues, promoting understanding and communication in a diverse learning and cultural environment. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Plan, prepare, and execute appropriate lessons, utilizing appropriate resource materials and activities. Follow the prescribed scope and sequence of curriculum and assessment as scheduled. Employ a variety of instructional aides, methods, and materials that will promote best practices in order to reach the whole child as stated in the ICS mission statement. Assess student learning against the appropriate standards on a regular basis and provide progress reports as required. Identify the needs of students through diagnostic procedures and align instructional objectives to these needs. Effect student learning through mastery of the subject material by utilizing valid teaching techniques to achieve curriculum goals within the framework of the school’s philosophy. Eager to explore and learn about emerging technologies, such as Generative AI, with a commitment to integrating them effectively into the classroom to foster critical thinking and guide students in developing ethical and responsible use. Ensure Biblical Worldwide Integration (BWI) is mapped throughout the curriculum. Complete lesson/unit plans, Atlas, ESO documentation, and PLC expectations within specified time frames. Maintain student records as required by the school. Communicate with parents, students, and other professional staff regarding student progress (behavioral and academic). Follow school policies, procedures, rules, regulations, and guidelines and the provisions of the contract. Maintain proper discipline in the classroom and on the school premises utilizing teacher created discipline plans in conjunction with a school-wide discipline plan. Inform the administration if/when unable to fulfill any duty assigned. Prepare adequate information and materials for a substitute teacher. Plan and lead a student Chapel when called upon. Supervise extracurricular activities, organizations, and outings as assigned. Complete the Christian Philosophy of Education in-service program within the first three years of teaching at ICS. Attend and participate in devotions, teachers’ meetings, evening programs (if involving his or her students or as requested by the administration), and other meetings as assigned. Carry out supervisory duty as assigned (before school, break, lunch, after school). Maintain a classroom in a clean and educationally stimulating environment. Maintain regular and accurate attendance and grade reports that provide comprehensive information for each student’s learning progress. Keep students, parents, and the administration informed of students’ progress or deficiencies, and provide sufficient and timely notice to parents when students fail to meet learning standards. Submit all appropriate reports to the administration, guidance office, and business office as required by school regulations or as requested. Work with the administration in implementing all policies, procedures, and directives governing the operation of the school. Complete professional development programs as required by the administration and/or ICS Board. Maintain teaching credentials as required through sponsoring agencies (state/provincial education department, ACSI, etc.). Acquire and maintain teaching credentials as required by the laws of Singapore, NICS, and/or ICS. Demonstrate the character qualities of enthusiasm, courtesy, flexibility, integrity, kindness, self-control, perseverance, and punctuality. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity, and optimism. Develop and maintain rapport with students, parents, and staff by treating others with friendliness, open-mindedness, dignity, and consideration. Respectfully submit to and demonstrate loyalty to constituted authority. Maintain a personal appearance that models cleanliness, modesty, good taste, and is in agreement with school policy. QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s Degree Teaching credential from a US state, Canadian province or from ACSI (exceptions will be approved by the Academic Board) Experience working with students Knowledge and experience teaching STEM is preferred.

title: Programme Manager (Educational Support)
description: Role Overview The Programme Manager (Educational Support) is pivotal in designing, implementing, and overseeing the academic support programmes. This position requires strategic planning, stakeholder engagement, and the management of tutors and academic coaches to ensure high-quality educational support for students. The role also involves mentoring children and youth, fostering their learning motivation, and strengthening home-school-community partnerships. Key Responsibilities ● Plan, implement, and oversee academic support programmes, ensuring alignment with Tasek’s mission and Positive Youth Development framework. ● Recruit, onboard, and coordinate the deployment of tutors and academic coaches, providing ongoing support and professional development to enhance their teaching effectiveness. ● Mentor children and youth, supporting their personal development, learning motivation, and academic progress. ● Work closely with schools, social service agencies, and families to identify learning needs and tailor academic interventions accordingly. ● Engage stakeholders, including parents, universities, and partner organisations, to strengthen home-school-community collaboration and enhance the programme’s impact. ● Organise and manage workshops, tutoring sessions, and enrichment activities to complement academic learning and holistic development. ● Oversee administrative tasks, including attendance tracking, programme documentation, and reporting on programme effectiveness. ● Analyse data and generate reports to evaluate learning outcomes and improve programme strategies. Pre-requisite ● A degree or diploma in education, psychology, youth work, social work, communications, or related fields. ● Experience in mentoring, tutoring, or working with children and youth in an educational or community setting is highly desirable. ● Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate with educators, volunteers, and other stakeholders. ● Ability to work independently and as part of a team, demonstrating initiative and problem-solving skills. ● Commitment to fostering a positive and inclusive learning environment for all children and youth. ● Flexibility to work on evenings and weekends.

title: Senior System Administrator
description: About Us Green Link Digital Bank is Singapore's inaugural wholesale digital bank focusing on supply chain finance, mainly serving MSMEs and aiming to help MSMEs grow and improve digitization. Responsibilities Gather business and/or application requirements on the infrastructure, conduct impact analysis, suggest design/re-design to integrate the change into the existing environment. Manage systems changes through established change request process & provide status reports to the relevant parties. Respond promptly to incident, investigate & provide temporary &/or permanent resolution of incidents escalated. Provide timely status updates to relevant parties. Conduct root cause analysis & implement pro-active measures. Monitor effectiveness of implemented measures. Monitor & measure the performance & availability of systems proactively; implement corrective actions identified to improve performance & availability. Monitor the agreed service level (e.g., service request, system availability), document & maintain the configuration of the systems; provide regular reporting to relevant parties. Execute service continuity measures, e.g., backup/restore procedures & disaster recovery plan dry run to ensure continuous operation of the business. Manage budget, resources, and schedule for system implementation/operation activities; ensure deliverables meet client requirements and quality standards. Established facility management standards/best practices to ensure operation consistency across project/facility management teams. Ensure the management of systems adhere to ITIL best practices/ITSM and methodologies (e.g., change management, release management, incident management, problem management, configuration management). Actively monitor system and network performance, ensuring high availability and reliability of infrastructure components. Manage and track IT assets throughout their lifecycle, ensuring accurate documentation and compliance with organizational policies. Coordinate and execute patch management processes for systems and applications to safeguard the IT environment against vulnerabilities. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure infrastructure changes are planned, tested, and documented according to established change management protocols. Generate and analyse performance metrics and reports, proactively identifying areas for improvement and optimisation. Develop and maintain system monitoring solutions to alert the team of potential issues before they impact operations. Provide technical guidance to team members while fostering a collaborative and knowledge-sharing environment. Stay abreast of industry trends and emerging technologies to recommend enhancements to our IT infrastructure. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field (or equivalent experience). Minimum of 5 years of experience in an IT infrastructure support role. Hands-on experience with containers and Kubernetes operations (deploy/upgrade, networking, storage, security, backup/DR). Strong knowledge of server technologies (Windows Server, Linux), networking (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP), and virtualisation (VMware, Hyper-V). Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure) is a plus. Technical certifications in cloud and system technologies (e.g., CCNA, Microsoft Azure Administrator Associate, VCP, VMCE, CEH) are advantageous. Proficient in scripting and automation tools (PowerShell, Bash, Python). Familiarity with ITIL processes and best practices in IT service management. Excellent troubleshooting skills with a problem-solving mindset. Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work collaboratively. Motivated team player with attention to detail, capable of working independently as well. By submitting your application, you have given your consent for us to collect, use and retain your data for a year as according to our prevailing data retention and PDPA policies. Should you have any enquiries on our data policy, please contact us at CustomerSupport@glbank.com.

title: Lead Centre Manager (Active Ageing Centre)
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Founded in 1976, Home Nursing Foundation (HNF) is a leading charity that provides home healthcare services in Singapore, regardless of our patients' ability to pay. Dedicated to all-round support for our patients, HNF delivers comprehensive care programmes to look after patients' emotional well-being and healthcare needs.Our HNF Wellness Day Care & Rehabilitation Centres offer an array of social activities, social support and holistic healthcare services to help our elderly stay independent in their own community. RESPONSIBILITIES Administration & Facilities Oversee the overall operations and performance of a cluster of Active Ageing Centres (AACs) and directly manage the AAC @ Serangoon - including its day-to-day administration, programmes, and community caf - to ensure smooth, safe, and engaging service delivery for seniors. Lead and support a team of AAC Supervisors to foster well-being and a supportive team culture. Assist HOD in staff recruitment and training of the team. Work collaboratively with AAC supervisors to align practices and maintain consistent service standards across Centres. Ensure operational compliance through the development and review of standard operating procedures, contingency and emergency plans, in line with prevailing policies. Manage incidents, audits, public enquiries and feedback, and related documentations. Monitor centre statistics (attendance, submission of reports on attendance to programmes, befriending statistics) and ensure timely, accurate reporting to management and authorities (e.g. AIC/MOH). Coordinate facility maintenance, procurement, vendor management, ensuring timely resolution of operational issues and cost-effectiveness. Plan manpower deployment across centres to ensure optimal resourcing and coverage. Community Caf Management Oversee the daily operations of the community caf Guide the team in caf management, budgeting, procurement, supplier management and volunteer engagement. Review caf performance, financial accountability and sustainability to ensure alignment with HNF's community engagement goals. Centre Service Planning, Implementation and Evaluation Manage the planning and implementation of outreach and engagement initiatives to residents in the AAC service boundary. Support innovation and local relevance in programme design Oversee the befriending and buddying programme Promote the Asset-Based Community Development approach to recruit, train and activate residents as community champions and volunteers. Oversee volunteer management, including recruitment, training and maintenance of an updated database. Lead programme evaluation efforts to assess outcomes and identify areas for improved effectiveness. Referral Coordination and Community Partnership Strengthen relationships with community partners, volunteers and donors to advance AACs' objectives through collaboration and resource mobilisation. Build and maintain strong partnerships with healthcare and community stakeholders (e.g. Polyclinics, GPs, SSO, SGO) to facilitate seamless service referrals and integrated care. Represent HNF at sub-regional and inter-agency platforms such as ICCS (Integrated Community Care Services), contributing to collaborative planning. Support the coordination of cross-centre initiatives and ensure alignment with HNF's organisational goals and community strategies. QUALIFICATIONS Diploma or Degree in Social Work, Gerontology, Nursing or Healthcare related OTHER INFORMATION We are looking for a Lead Centre Manager to lead a cluster of Active Ageing Centres (AACs) in HNF. The incumbent will report to the Head, Centre-Based Services and should possess the following: minimum 5 years of relevant work experience and has proven experience in team leadership, operations management, and stakeholder engagement. prior experience in cafe/restaurant management (will be advantageous) as the AAC under the Manager's direct charge will have a community cafe. approachable and enjoy engaging and working with seniors You'll make a difference in many lives, and while challenging, the job offers a sense of personal fulfilment like no others. Embark on a meaningful career with HNF today! Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - Home Nursing Foundation

title: Senior Cybersecurity Talent & Leadership Development Specialist
description: The staff is responsible to architect and lead our initiatives for growing and retaining top-tier cybersecurity professionals. •	The role is responsible for the end-to-end design and management of frameworks that empower our entire cybersecurity workforce to achieve its full potential. •	A primary emphasis will be placed on building a robust talent pipeline for our current and future Chief Information Security Officers (CISOs) and ensuring they possess the advanced competencies required to protect the organization. T •	This position sits at the intersection of human capital strategy, cybersecurity, and data analytics This covers the following: Comprehensive Talent & Career Framework Design: •	Develop and operationalize a comprehensive talent mobility framework that outlines clear career pathways, advancement criteria, and growth opportunities for the entire cybersecurity division. •	In collaboration with senior leadership, define and map the critical skills, competencies, rotational assignments, and mentorship opportunities required for all key roles, with a specialized track for aspiring and current CISOs. Holistic Learning & Development (L&D) Strategy: •	Design and manage a robust L&D framework tailored to the unique needs of cybersecurity professionals at all levels. This includes a specialized curriculum mapped to the advanced skills and competencies required for the CISO role. •	Establish and implement systems to track employee and CISO competency levels, certifications, and professional development progress to identify skill gaps and inform learning strategies. Program Management & Stakeholder Engagement: •	Lead the execution of all talent development programs, serving as the main point of contact for participants, CISOs, and key stakeholders across the organization. •	Interface directly with cybersecurity leaders and staff at all levels to promote program participation, provide guidance, and gather feedback for continuous improvement. Data-Driven Reporting & Strategic Insights: •	Establish key performance indicators (KPIs) and benchmarks to track the success, impact, and timely delivery of all talent development initiatives. •	Leverage data analytics to transform program data into clear, insightful reports and visualizations (e.g., using Tableau, Excel) for management. •	Provide data-driven recommendations to inform strategic decisions regarding talent investment, succession planning, and overall workforce development. Requirements: •	A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Human Resources, Organizational Development, or a related field is required. •	Experience: A minimum of 8 years of proven experience in learning and development, talent management, talent mobility, or a related human capital field is essential. •	Deep expertise in designing and implementing large-scale career frameworks and professional development programs. •	Strong stakeholder management and communication skills with a demonstrated ability to collaborate with and influence leaders and employees at all levels. •	Exceptional analytical mindset with the ability to translate complex data into compelling visual stories and strategic insights. Required skills: •	High proficiency in data analysis and visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, MS Excel). •	Experience with a programming language for data analysis, such as Python, is highly desirable/preferred. •	A professional certification in data analytics, human resources (e.g., SHRM-CP), or cybersecurity (e.g., Security+) would be a distinct advantage. Job location: Punggol/ Buona Vista/ Hybird Working days: Monday to Friday 830-530pm Contract role: 1 year (Immediate to 01 December 2026) Salary: $7460 to $11940 EA License No: 96C4864 Reg. No.: R24121728 EUNICE WOO JING QI

title: Senior Finance Analyst
description: Key Responsibilities 1. Financial Modelling & Investment Analysis Build and maintain development and acquisition financial models (DCF, IRR, NPV, equity waterfall, sensitivity analysis). Analyse GDV, construction costs, land cost, financing costs, exit values and cash-flow timing. Evaluate different ownership and investment structures (direct holding, SPV, JV, fund structures). Stress-test assumptions under various market, interest-rate and sales-velocity scenarios. 2. Singapore Property Market Analysis Conduct micro-market research by district, sub-market, and asset class (residential, industrial, commercial). Track URA data, transaction trends, price indices, rental yields, take-up rates, and supply pipelines. Assess the impact of government policies (ABSD, BSD, SSD, LTV limits, cooling measures, GLS/IGLS supply). Monitor URA Master Plan and infrastructure developments affecting asset values. 3. Deal Evaluation & Due Diligence Support feasibility studies for new developments, redevelopment and value-add opportunities. Review key due-diligence inputs: valuation reports, cost plans, sales assumptions and consultants’ reports. Assess project risks (planning, construction, sales, financing, regulatory) and propose mitigation strategies. 4. Investment Committee & Stakeholder Support Prepare investment memoranda, IC papers, and executive summaries for senior management and investors. Translate complex financial outputs into clear insights and recommendations. 5. Portfolio & Performance Monitoring Track actual vs forecast performance for live projects and stabilised assets. Analyse deviations in costs, sales velocity, pricing and cash flows. Support periodic asset revaluation and portfolio reporting. Requirements Technical & Analytical Strong proficiency in Excel financial modelling (advanced formulas, scenario analysis). Solid understanding of real estate finance metrics: IRR, NPV, DSCR, LTV, ICR, GDV. Familiarity with Singapore property regulations and tax framework. Ability to interpret market data and translate it into investment insights. Professional Background Degree in Finance, Real Estate, Economics, Accounting, or related discipline. 2–5 years of experience in real estate investment, development, advisory, valuation, or fund management. Experience covering Singapore property is strongly preferred. Soft Skills High attention to detail with strong commercial judgement. Clear written and verbal communication skills (able to brief senior stakeholders). Ability to work across multiple projects and tight timelines. Strong ethics and professionalism in handling confidential information.

title: GENERAL MANAGER (BUSINESS  DEVELOPMENT)
description: We are seeking a highly versatile and entrepreneurial General Manager to oversee our investment property portfolio and spearhead our expansion into the China-Singapore nightlife and real estate sectors. The ideal candidate will bridge the gap between Singapore’s regulatory environment and Mainland China’s investment landscape, managing everything from capital attraction to physical renovation and procurement. Key Roles & Responsibilities: 1) Investment & Capital Raising: Identify and engage high-net-worth investors and corporate entities from Mainland China to invest in Singapore’s vibrant nightlife and entertainment industry. 2) Tenant Acquisition: Strategically source and secure premium tenants from Mainland China to lease our 4-storey commercial property, ensuring a high-yield and synergetic tenant mix. 3) Project Design & Concept Planning: Conceptualize, plan, and design diverse nightlife operations (e.g., clubs, lounges, KTVs). Translate business visions into functional, high-aesthetic interior design layouts. 4) Renovation & Project Management: Oversee full-cycle renovation projects. Act as the primary liaison with general contractors to ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to Singapore’s building standards. 5) International Procurement & Logistics: Negotiate directly with suppliers in Mainland China for furniture, lighting, sound system and specialized equipment. Manage the end-to-end supply chain, including quality control, shipping, and customs clearance into Singapore. 6) Strategic Investment Research: Conduct market research to identify lucrative real estate or business investment opportunities within Mainland China for the company’s portfolio. 7) Regulatory Compliance: Ensure all operations and tenant activities strictly adhere to Singapore’s nightlife regulations, liquor licensing, and MOM/URA/SCDF requirements. 8) Cross-Border Legal & Market Advisory: Provide expertise on Mainland China’s legal frameworks, trade policies, and market trends to mitigate risks in cross-border transactions. Requirements / Qualifications 9) Market Expertise: Deep familiarity with both the Singapore nightlife ecosystem and the Mainland China business landscape. 10) Language Proficiency: Fluency in English and Mandarin (written and spoken) is essential to negotiate legal contracts and deal with stakeholders in both regions. 11) Technical Knowledge: Experience in interior design, renovation workflows, and managing construction contractors. 12) Regulatory Knowledge: Strong understanding of Singapore’s licensing (Category 1 Public Entertainment, Liquor Licenses) and China’s foreign investment laws. 13) Network: An existing network of investors or hospitality operators in Mainland China is highly preferred.
