title: Senior Speech Language Therapist
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION MINDS has been caring for Persons with Intellectual Disabilities (PWIDs) and their families since 1962. Grounded in the belief that all PWIDs have innate abilities and talents, MINDS is committed to empowering them and enhancing their quality of life, while advocating for greater inclusivity for PWIDs as a nation. MINDS is today one of the largest charities in Singapore employing more than 850 staff. With the key focus to expand community-based services, improving the employability of PWIDS, strengthening education, and engendering inclusive community living, MINDS offers a holistic range of services and programmes across the PWIDs' lifespan. These include schools, employment and training development centres, home-based care services and community-based services to cater to the physical, psychological, environmental and social needs of PWIDs. For more information, please visit www.minds.org.sg. RESPONSIBILITIES Working with stakeholders, we are looking for a skilled and compassionate Senior Speech Language Therapist to join our team and help our clients, predominantly school-aged children with developmental disabilities (e.g. ID, ASD), thrive academically, socially and functionally. 1. Assessment, Therapy Planning and Implementation: Conduct thorough assessments to evaluate speech, language, and communication needs tailored to clients with complex needs. Develop and implement customized therapy plans that address each client's specific challenges and goals, using evidence-based approaches. Deliver targeted individual/group speech and language therapy interventions that support clients in improving their communication skills, social interactions, and academic performance. 2. Supervision Conduct supervision sessions for therapists, providing guidance and support to enhance their professional development and clinical skills. Oversee quality assurance activities and participate in clinical audits to ensure adherence to professional standards. Monitor and manage risks related to therapy practices, ensuring compliance with safety standards and maintaining high-quality service delivery. 3. Collaborative and Teamwork: Collaborate with special education educators, and other therapists to integrate therapy goals into classroom activities and individual learning plans. Provide training and support to educators and caregivers, such as use of AAC or screening, hence to equip them with strategies to reinforce communication skills outside of therapy sessions. Assist educators and the speech therapy team in designing and planning of curriculum for special education within this population. Work closely with the SLT Lead to develop and evaluate therapy programs, ensuring they align with best practices and client needs. 4. Administrative and Operational Tasks: Perform essential clinical administration tasks including detailed documentation of therapy sessions and outcomes, scheduling, and managing therapy materials. Contribute to the smooth operation of the therapy department by participating in meetings, coordinating with other staff members, and supporting departmental initiatives. 5. Professional Development and Research: Engage in ongoing professional development through workshops, courses, and training to stay current with the latest advancements in speech-language therapy. Support SLT Lead in research initiatives, as well as to participate in research activities to contribute to the development of innovative practices and improve therapeutic outcomes. QUALIFICATIONS Bachelor/Master's degree in Speech-Language Pathology from an AHPC recognised institution. Proven 5 years of experience working with school-aged children who have intellectual disabilities and autism. Entry level therapists are welcome (supervision will be provided). Strong ability to work effectively within collaborative, interdisciplinary teams. Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to adapt therapy approaches based on individual needs. Empathetic, patient, and passionate about supporting diverse learners in an educational setting. OTHER INFORMATION Why Join Us? Impactful Work: Make a significant difference in the lives of PWIDs by helping them achieve their communication and learning goals. Supportive Team: Be part of a collaborative and dedicated team focused on creating an inclusive and engaging learning environment. Professional Growth: Benefit from ongoing training and development opportunities to advance your skills and career. Inclusive Culture: Join an organisation that values diversity, equity, and inclusion. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - MINDSG LTD

title: Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk (Listed FI)
description: Lico Resources is partnering with a prominent financial market infrastructure provider at the heart of Singapore’s capital markets. This organisation plays a pivotal role in safeguarding systemic stability and is committed to upholding the highest standards of enterprise risk management. A unique opportunity has arisen for an experienced and detail-oriented risk professional to join the Operational/Enterprise Risk team in a highly visible role. This position offers broad exposure to group-wide operations and senior stakeholders, contributing directly to the effectiveness of the enterprise risk framework across critical business and support functions. Key Responsibilities Enhance and implement the enterprise risk management (ERM) framework in alignment with global standards such as COSO and ISO 31000. Prepare materials and coordinate inputs for senior governance forums, including Board-level risk and audit committees. Investigate operational risk incidents, perform root cause analysis, and track remediation actions to prevent recurrence. Develop and maintain dashboards, risk appetite indicators, and emerging risk summaries for executive reporting. Support enterprise-wide change initiatives by evaluating risk implications of new processes, technologies, and services. Provide input into product and service risk assessments, particularly for new or significantly altered offerings. Collaborate with resilience and technology teams to integrate enterprise risk perspectives into testing outcomes, recovery planning, and critical dependency mapping. Coordinate responses to regulatory reviews, audits, and internal assurance exercises, ensuring follow-through on recommendations. Ensure data quality and consistency across GRC systems; support workflow optimisation through process and tool enhancements. Deliver targeted risk training sessions and facilitate awareness-building engagements across the organisation. Qualifications & Experience **Essential:** A recognised Bachelor's degree in Finance, Business, Engineering, Information Systems, or a related discipline. 4–6 years of relevant experience in enterprise risk, operational risk, internal audit, or control functions within financial services or market infrastructure environments. Strong grasp of ERM practices including RCSA, KRIs, risk appetite frameworks, incident analysis, and regulatory expectations (especially MAS guidelines). Excellent communication and analytical skills, with the ability to produce concise, insightful reports for executive audiences. Adept at working across functions and facilitating risk workshops with stakeholders from varied disciplines. Proficient in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint; working knowledge of GRC platforms (e.g., Archer, ServiceNow GRC) is beneficial. **Preferred:** Exposure to data visualisation tools such as Power BI or Tableau. Professional certifications such as FRM, PRM, CRM, CIA, CISA, or equivalent. ### Personal Attributes Highly accountable, with a proactive and structured approach to execution. Calm and dependable in fast-paced, regulatory-driven environments. Team-oriented with strong influencing and relationship-building skills. Upholds high standards of integrity and sound judgment, aligned with the organisation’s role in maintaining market confidence. --- If you are interested in this role, please send us your updated resume today to nicole@licoresources.com quoting reference number **A09012**. title: Industrial Hygienist
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Industrial Hygienist to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on preventing occupational illness and disease by anticipating, recognizing, evaluating, and controlling environmental factors arising in the workplace which may cause sickness, impaired health and well-being, or significant discomfort to the workforce. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the safety team, stakeholders from other functions and departments including the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. You will provide your expertise to our Refinery on matters of industrial hygiene (IH) and occupational health & safety (OH&S). In our Safety team, you will report to the Safety Manager. As the technical authority and expert in IH and OH&S, you manage and lead the refinery in IH and OH&S function to reduce operational risk exposure to the workforce through governance of OEMS principles, regulatory compliance and requirements. Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Risk Identification, Evaluation and Control Proactively anticipate and recognize chemical hazards, physical hazards, biological hazards, and ergonomic hazards across refining areas Design, execute, and manage comprehensive Industrial Hygiene (IH) monitoring programs . Conduct qualitative and quantitative exposure assessments for airborne contaminants, chemical exposure, noise & physical agents and ergonomic risk Interpret monitoring results against local (MOM/WSH) and internationally recognized (ACGIH/OSHA) standards Conduct routine Occupational Hygiene site inspections and audits, focusing on exposure risks, ventilation systems (LEVs), and chemical storage/handling procedures Control Measures and Management Systems Recommend, implement effective industrial hygiene engineering as key control strategies and evaluate its effectiveness Oversight chemical inventory, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) management, and risk assessment process for hazardous substances Develop and enforce stringent chemical handling procedures Administer and manage IH Management system and programs such as Ventilation Systems, Hazard Communications, PPE standards, Ergonomic standards, heat stress program, medical examination & surveillance program, Hearing Conservation Program (HCP), including noise mapping, audiometric testing, noise control implementation, process and office ergonomic program Conduct industrial hygiene risk assessments for process work area, offices, laboratory, recommending necessary adjustments and controls measures Strategizing Regulatory Compliance (PEL, STEL, TWA, C) and Translating to Operable Compliance Translate PEL, STEL, OEL requirement into practical, executable operational compliance Deep working knowledge of Singapore and international acts, regulations, requirements related to industrial hygiene topics Ensure strict adherence to permissible/occupational exposure limit and relevant regulations and Codes of Practice relating to occupational health and safety Prepare detailed industrial hygiene reports, regulatory submissions and internal performance metrics. Maintain accurate records of all monitoring data, calibration records, and exposure assessments Provide technical support and expertise during health-related emergency response situations (e.g chemical spills) Training and Collaboration Develop and deliver targeted training programs on industrial hygiene topics, including chemical management and hazard communication, PPE use, respiratory protection, noise conservations, heat stress, ergonomics Collaborate closely with cross functional teams to integrate industrial hygiene into daily operations, projects and Process Safety Management (PSM). Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Technical expert in industrial hygiene, occupational health role and operationalized industrial hygiene into day to day operations Minimum 10 years experience as a practicing industrial hygienist in a refinery, petrochemical or chemical manufacturing industry; track record in industrial hygiene monitoring (chemical, physical and biological to personal and area exposure)  IH engineering control, IH Management System Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in industrial hygiene highly desired Professionally certified industrial hygienist (CIH) or registered industrial hygienist (RIH) or equivalent. Candidates without IH certification but demonstrate mastery and proven track record in IH will be considered Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request) We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Director, Transactions Management
description: About the role You will deliver Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management (TM) services to some of the world’s largest companies. This position calls for an energetic, detail-oriented professional with excellent project management, analytical, communication and collaboration skills as well as experience of managing real estate strategies and transactions across Asia Pacific. Comfortable in working towards achieving performance targets, you will be responsible to deliver a range of possible real estate and workplace solutions to clients looking for the next generation benefits that come from a vested partnership with JLL. Portfolio Strategy & Planning – Country, City and Metro plans, Global Initiatives Assessment of portfolio performance, portfolio rationalization and divestment, lease vs. ownership structures, cost reduction initiatives and bid support to achieve business change. Lead planning process to develop strategies to lower real estate costs and improve workplace effectiveness. Identify opportunities for optimization, cost saving and improved ROI in the Client portfolio Conduct fact-based analysis using occupancy, portfolio, real estate, and business data to align stakeholders on informed real estate plans. Through strong business relationships and clear, compelling business cases, challenge status quo thinking to drive unexpected portfolio optimization opportunities Develop overarching strategies related to workplace, organization, process, sustainability, operations and finance to govern the portfolio and set a foundation for city master plans Analyze inputs from the strategic planning process and on-account systems (business requirements, costs, facility information, cap-ex requirements, forecasts, occupancy data, lease and market data) Analyze and make recommendations with respect to strategic dispositions, investment and market opportunities, hold versus sell analysis, feasibility studies, asset review and market studies, highest and best use analysis, acquisition and advice analysis and valuations for sale/leaseback purposes Develop multiple practical options supported by strong business cases that optimize NPV and enhance productivity and innovation Be a SPOC for all Portfolio related client inquiries and a representative in global calls and initiatives Manage expectations, timelines and delivery against Client requirements, represent both JLL and the Client’s interest on the long term Transaction Management You will execute a wide variety of real estate transactions, including capital and lease acquisitions, sale and leasebacks and disposition transactions through local Markets teams across Asia, demonstrating a high level of proficiency and knowledge. You will actively engage with client’s senior stakeholders to define objectives and agree requirements at the outset of a strategy and transaction. Financial Acumen You will need to: Prepare and evaluate financial analysis documents. Support and contribute to the client’s budget forecasting. Forecast revenue and initiate and close-out projects to meet monthly cashflow plan. Monitor and closely control legal budgets and resolve billing queries. Relationship Management You will build strong and sustainable working relationships and help the client navigate through the JLL delivery platform in an efficient and productive manner. You will be the face of Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management towards the Client both regionally and globally. People Management You possess strong leadership skills to manage brokers across geographies and actively engage across the client’s wider corporate real estate team and internal stakeholders in a collaborative manner. You demonstrate a high level of proficiency and knowledge in portfolio management and possesses the ability to orchestrate complex activities across multiple countries/properties/services at once to accomplish goals and objectives. Commercial Acumen You will understand account profitability, formulate an account plan to achieve revenue and performance targets whilst meeting client service standards. Armed with a sharp business acumen, you will leverage on technology and new products within JLL to deliver the next generation of benefits for our client. Operating a Global Account Can you see the bigger picture? Can you juggle regional delivery that aligns to a global strategy? As the TM lead for Asia on a Global Account, you are proactive in bringing Asia into the forefront, highlighting successes or potential issues whilst providing tangible solutions for the client. Can you communicate clearly and guide the client and your team on the best way forward for best in class processes and portfolio strategies at an account level? Sound like you? Here is what we’re looking for: Great Communicator Can you get your ideas across clearly both verbally and in writing? You’ll be working with many diverse interest groups so the way you share information and your ability to persuasively convey information is highly important. A high EQ is must for this role. You will have exceptional client management, communication and presentation skills and the ability to absorb and translate broad concepts and ideas into clear and logical communications. Ambitious You will be results-oriented with confidence to liaise effectively with all levels of management. Have commitment to collaboration and teamwork, and would thrive to exceed team goals. Resilient Transaction Management is a highly dynamic part of JLL’s business. It’s fast paced and you’ll need to be fast paced as well. You’ll need a positive mind set and a ‘can do’ attitude when multi-tasking driving change and delivering efficiencies. Naturally proactive, you’ll possess the innate ability to identify risks in delivery before it becomes an issue. At JLL, we have a continuous improvement mentality across our business and we’ll be looking for you to have the same. A flexible, resourceful and proactive individual who is able to produce results and guide the team to deliver the same. You will have the ability to work well under pressure and support the team without compromising quality. Experienced You will be an experienced real estate professional with a minimum of 10-12 years’ experience who has delivered positive results across the APAC region. You will be familiar with working with fast moving MNC clients with a regional presence and multiple service lines and possess prior experience in managing a team.

title: Research Fellow (Engineering - Mechanical/ Clean/Renewable Energy/ Building related)
description: Established in 2010, the Energy Research Institute @ NTU (ERI@N) is a pan-university research institute that focuses on systems-level research for tropical megacities. It performs translational research that covers the energy value chain from generation to innovative end-use solutions, motivated by industrialisation and deployment. ERI@N has multiple Interdisciplinary Research Programmes which focus on translational Research, Development & Deployment which focus on specific area of the energy value chain, and a number of Living labs and Testbeds which facilitate large scale technology deployment enabling validation and demonstration of real-world applications. For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/erian You will be part of a dynamic research team working on topics relevant to the Sustainable Built Environment (SBE). The SBE team focuses on building energy modelling and simulation, decarbonization strategies for the built environment and R&D of green building technologies such as HVAC systems, thermal energy storage, data-driven building control systems and other novel solutions for the urban built environment sector. This position is to recruit a member of the research team to work on a project on sustainable indoor farming. Key Responsibilities : You will need to actively support SBE projects at ERI@N in terms of project R&D, technology evaluation, project coordination, and stakeholder interfacing. The role will include the following: Perform as subject matter expert for ongoing projects e.g. in energy modelling and/or embodied carbon assessment; coordination with project partners, industry and other external stakeholders. Provide inputs for conceptualization of research proposals related to sustainable urban solutions, green buildings, low-carbon district level solutions and agritech applications. Lead in preparation and submission of new research proposals. Conduct research, analysis and assessment on embodied carbon for buildings, urban farming and other applications. Lead technical analysis and research on green building technologies, decarbonization strategies for new and existing buildings and smart building systems incorporating AI/ML/energy analytics. Engage stakeholders at management and working levels internally as well as with external funding agencies, collaborators and industry partners, including delivering presentations and guiding discussions or workshops as necessary. Lead in authoring of technical publications for completed or ongoing research projects. You will be an integral part of the SBE team and will need to participate in project discussions especially related to project implementation details. Job Requirements PhD degree in engineering (e.g. Mechanical, Clean/Renewable Energy or Building related) or sustainable buildings design. Experience in sustainable design/analysis for the built environment, Green Mark or LEED projects, M&E construction and development (min 2yrs preferred). Proficient in MS Office applications, BIM software and energy modelling software. Should be a very hands-on person, willing to take on non-routine tasks. Possess good communication (written and verbal) skills. Able to manage multiple stakeholders and work under tight timelines. Positive attitude, ability to work independently as well as in a team. Familiarity with green building technology, HVAC system study and optimization, sustainable building design, building energy modelling, CFD modelling, BIM and embodied carbon/lifecycle assessment for built environment applications is preferred. Knowledge in EN 15978 or similar standards for carbon assessment or lifecycle analysis. Prior experience in sustainability standards and green building accreditations would be advantageous. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: RGM/Pricing Manager
description: About The Role As a Revenue & Pricing Manager at Versuni, you will play a key role in shaping and executing revenue growth management and pricing strategies across the Singapore and Malaysia markets. You will collaborate closely with commercial teams to optimize trade terms, pricing, promotions, and channel segmentation. Your expertise in pricing execution and analytics will ensure compliance with commercial policies while driving business efficiency and performance. The Impact You’ll Make Lead the execution of revenue growth and pricing strategies that align with market-specific objectives. Collaborate with global teams to implement best practices, analytical tools, and strategies that strengthen local capabilities. Translate global recommended retail prices into local markets while ensuring compliance with global standards. Design and implement promotional strategies tailored to each customer to drive profitability, following global promotional best practices. Co-develop a 12-month promotional calendar with key account managers and trade marketing teams to align with market priorities. Enhance trade spend effectiveness by ensuring optimal return on investment (ROI) and implementing best-in-class pay-for-performance models. Implement customer segmentation models to refine pricing and promotional strategies for different customer groups. Ensure pricing compliance by monitoring and enforcing policies across channels, adhering to Versuni’s commercial policy and local regulations. title: Market Strategist & Presenter
description: So, who are we? Hello, we're IG Group. We are a publicly traded FTSE250 FinTech company who run mobile, web and desktop platforms that help our clients trade stocks & shares, leveraged products, Futures & Options and Crypto. We are ambitious. Over 340,000 people already use our platforms. We're global with offices in 18 countries and products in 16 regions. We're hungry to move faster, ship better product for our customers and grow our user base. We believe in high autonomy, and we want people who are looking to do things differently in order to create better experiences for our customers. We work in cross-functional teams and are laser focused on increasing the number of active clients we serve to drive sustainable growth. Your role in the team We are seeking a dynamic and insightful Market Strategist and Presenter to join our team. You’ll be the market face of IG Group in Singapore, delivering actionable insights, building thought leadership, and engaging with the media and the trading community. You’ll also help support our content strategy and represent IG in media and public forums – bringing expert-level credibility and personality to every screen, page, and platform you’re on. As a Market Strategist & Presenter at IG Group, you’ll be both the voice and face of the company—delivering sharp, actionable insights that resonate with traders. You’ll shape our market narrative and drive client engagement through timely analysis, compelling reports, media appearances, engaging seminars, and social content. This is a high-impact, high-visibility role for someone who thrives on real-time market moves and knows how to transform analysis into actionable insights. What You’ll Do Market Commentary : Deliver daily and weekly market commentary, providing actionable insights on macroeconomic trends and trading strategies. Content Creation : Write articles covering significant market events, including US earnings, BOJ meetings, and Singapore banks' earnings. Live Hosting : Host live shows and video content on social media channels to discuss market trends and events. Social Media Engagement : Provide regular content updates across Twitter, Telegram, and Instagram to grow the company’s social media presence. Media Representation : Act as the go-to spokesperson for market views in external media, including radio and TV interviews, to share insights and build brand credibility. Webinars and Live Streams : Host regular market webinars and live streams to drive trader engagement and education. Client/ Public Events : Support and participate in client events to enhance engagement and foster brand loyalty. Who We’re Looking For You’re a natural communicator who thrives in the market cycle and knows how to turn complex themes into compelling stories. You're equally comfortable writing macro commentary, appearing on Bloomberg or CNA, or jumping into a live market call on social. You understand the stakes, the speed, and the scale of what IG clients want – and you're ready to meet that moment, every time. What You’ll Need for This Role Education: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in finance, Economics, Business, or a related field. Professional certifications such as CFA, CMT, or similar are highly desirable. Experience : A minimum of 3 years of direct experience in a market analysis or related role within the financial industry, OR Certifications : Successful completion of the RES5 and CM-CMP examinations. Strong understanding of macroeconomic trends and financial markets. Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to distill complex information into clear, actionable insights. Proven ability to create engaging written and video content for diverse audiences. Comfortable with public speaking and media appearances, including radio, webinars, and TV interviews. Proficiency in leveraging social media platforms (Youtube, Telegram, Instagram) to build brand presence. Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities effectively. How we work We try to take a thoughtful approach to our ways of working as a company. When it comes to how we deliver, there are 5 things we want everyone to do to drive high performance, better learning and career satisfaction: Lead and Inspire: Drives trust, alignment, and enthusiasm Think Big: Focus on the problems that most impact commercial outcomes Champion the client: Understand and prioritise client's needs Deliver at pace: Push for fast, sustainable growth; Raise the bar: Take ownership, be accountable and share feedback We believe that diversity is vital to success, it fuels creativity, drives innovation and sets us up for global success. We're committed to building teams with a variety of perspectives and skills to help us realise our vision and strategy, that's why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join us on this journey. Learn more about our D&I approach here . The Perks Your growth fuels our success! Thrive with tailored development programs, mentoring opportunities with leaders, and clear career progression. Expand your network through committees, sports and social clubs. Enjoy extra time off for volunteering and community work. Flexible working hours and work-from-home opportunities. Performance-related bonuses. Insurance and medical plans. Career-focused technical and leadership training in-class and online, incl. unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning platform. Contribution to gym memberships and more. A day off on your birthday. Two days’ volunteering leave per year. Join us for this exciting journey. Apply now!

title: Business Development Executive / Senior Business Development Executive
description: About OA Group of Companies (OA GOC) OA Group of Companies (OA GOC) is a regional professional services group headquartered in Singapore, with an expanding presence across Malaysia and the region. We partner with growth-minded business owners, CFOs, and leadership teams to build strong corporate foundations, enhance governance and compliance, and enable sustainable growth. Our multidisciplinary platform spans Audit & Assurance, Accounting & Outsourcing, Corporate Secretarial & Compliance, Tax Compliance & Advisory, Business Advisory, and Digital Transformation . By integrating compliance, financial insight, and advisory thinking, we help clients move from operational stability to strategic scale. At OA GOC, we believe that strong growth is built on professionalism, ownership, and long-term partnerships — both with our clients and our people. Why This Role Exists As OA GOC enters its next phase of growth, we are intentionally building a high-calibre Business Development team to drive long-term client partnerships and group-wide revenue expansion. This is not a transactional sales role. The Business Development function at OA GOC exists to work alongside business owners and finance leaders, understand their challenges early, and shape integrated solutions across the OA GOC platform. The role plays a direct and visible part in the firm’s growth trajectory. Role Overview We are hiring Business Development Executives and Senior Business Development Executives to support and accelerate OA GOC’s growth. This role is suitable for individuals who are commercially driven, disciplined, and motivated by measurable outcomes. You will be responsible for originating opportunities, building trusted relationships, and converting prospects into long-term clients. You will operate in a professional services environment where credibility, clarity, and relationship quality matter. Key Responsibilities Identify, qualify, and convert business opportunities across SMEs and mid-market clients Build trusted relationships with founders, CFOs, finance leaders, and senior executives Drive outreach through targeted campaigns, networking, events, referrals, and partnerships Prepare and present structured proposals and presentations aligned to client needs Own and manage your sales pipeline with discipline, forecasting accuracy, and accountability Work closely with internal delivery teams to shape solutions and support client success What Success Looks Like in This Role Building a consistent and high-quality opportunity pipeline within the first 3–6 months Developing repeat and referral relationships with clients Demonstrating strong conversion discipline and commercial judgement Progressing from opportunity execution to owning key accounts, sectors, or growth initiatives Making a visible contribution to group-level revenue growth Who We Are Looking For We welcome candidates from different professional backgrounds, particularly individuals who are: Former accountants, auditors, tax agents, or corporate secretarial professionals seeking a transition into business development Business development professionals looking for a more strategic, professional services-oriented role The ideal candidate will demonstrate: Strong drive, ownership mentality, and coachability Professional communication and relationship-building skills Confidence engaging with senior stakeholders Comfort being measured by outcomes and results Discipline in pipeline management and follow-through For Professionals Transitioning from Accounting, Audit, Tax or Corporate Secretarial This role is well-suited for professionals who understand financial, compliance, and governance frameworks and want to apply that knowledge commercially. OA GOC provides structured support to help you develop sales and client-engagement skills while retaining the professionalism and credibility you have built in your technical career. You will not be expected to “hard sell”, but to position value, ask the right questions, and build trust with clients. Career Development High performers in this role can progress into senior business development leadership, key account ownership, sector-focused growth roles, or group-level commercial functions . Advancement is merit-based and tied to performance, capability, and leadership potential. What We Offer Clear and structured career progression Mentorship and ongoing professional development Competitive base salary with uncapped incentive compensation A professional, performance-focused team environment A meaningful role in shaping the growth of OA GOC Why Join OA GOC This is an opportunity to build a long-term career in business development within a respected professional services group that values professionalism, ambition, and impact. title: Service Delivery Manager/Service Senior Manager
description: NCS is a leading technology services firm that operates across the Asia Pacific region in over 20 cities, providing consulting, digital services, technology solutions, and more. We believe in harnessing the power of technology to achieve extraordinary things, creating lasting value and impact for our communities, partners, and people. Our diverse workforce of 14,000 has delivered large-scale, mission-critical, and multi-platform projects for governments and enterprises in Singapore and the APAC region. As a Service Senior Manager, you will manage and govern application and systems operations (Application Manager Services, AMS) for NCS Telco+ SBG’s clients. The role carries primary accountability for AMS delivery excellence, while also supporting platform evolution, customer success, solution delivery, and growth initiatives. The SSM will operate as a techno-functional leader, combining strong understanding of procurement business processes with hands-on experience across different systems and SaaS platforms. The role requires the ability to manage multiple customers simultaneously, each with different maturity levels, priorities, and operating models. What will you do? Service Delivery & AMS Governance (Primary KPI) Own end-to-end AMS delivery and governance for multiple e-procurement customers. Ensure delivery against SLAs, KPIs, and contractual commitments across accounts. Lead L2 governance covering incident, problem, change, and release management. Drive operational reviews, service improvement plans, and risk mitigation. Act as the escalation point for delivery, service quality, and customer satisfaction. Multi-Customer & Stakeholder Management Manage multiple customer accounts concurrently with clear prioritization and governance. Serve as the primary delivery interface for senior stakeholders across all domains and functional areas per client setup. Balance competing priorities while ensuring consistent service quality across customers. Build trusted-advisor relationships and support long-term customer success. Onshore–Offshore Delivery Leadership Lead and coordinate delivery with offshore AMS and delivery teams across India, Philippines and China. Ensure effective planning, workload management, quality assurance, and documentation. Support capability building, mentoring, and performance management of delivery teams. Drive standardization, best practices, and continuous improvement. Solutioning, Delivery & Growth Support Support pre-sales activities including solutioning, estimations, pricing, and proposal development. Contribute to delivery planning for enhancements, rollouts, and transformation initiatives. Provide advisory input on digital procurement operating models and best practices. Support internal leadership initiatives and cross-account collaboration within NCS. The ideal candidate should possess: 10+ years of experience in IT Systems and Application Management support services. Proven experience managing AMS delivery for multiple customers. Strong techno-functional expertise in Payment / Payment wallet and other IT systems. Hands-on experience with IT systems, having completed entire SLDC / Agile implementations of at least 5m SGD in Implementation value and Managed operations of multi-year AMS contracts worth at least 10m SGD. Solid understanding of Managed Operations / AMS methodologies such as ITIL v4, COBIT, SRE Certifications, FitSM, ISO/IEC 20000 and similar. Experience leading onshore–offshore delivery models, preferably with India-based teams. Strong customer-facing, governance, and stakeholder management skills – preparation and presentation of Project and Executive Steerco. Hands on experience managing at least team size of 8 persons (direct reports) Must have demonstrated application of AMS governance via best practice ITSM tooling and have deep understanding and experience in Service Requests, Change Management, Cyber Security and Enhancement and Development. Used industry leading ITSM tool and ITIL v4, COBIT, SRE Certifications, FitSM, ISO/IEC 20000 certifications or equivalent Experience with RFPs, pre-sales solutioning, and pricing models. Background in large enterprise, regulated, or multi-country environments. Familiarity with APAC customer landscapes (Singapore, Australia preferred). Ideally have had at least 4 years of experience managing and running Managed Operations at Singtel or Singtel Group Leadership & Behavioural Competencies Ability to manage complex, multi-customer delivery environments. Strong ownership and accountability mindset. Excellent communication and executive presence. Ability to bridge business, functional, and technical perspectives. Collaborative, adaptable, and delivery-driven leadership style. We are driven by our AEIOU beliefs—Adventure, Excellence, Integrity, Ownership, and Unity—and we seek individuals who embody these values in both their professional and personal lives. We are committed to our Impact: Valuing our clients, Growing our people, and Creating our future. Together, we make the extraordinary happen. Learn more about us at ncs.co and visit our LinkedIn career site. Scam Alert We are aware of fraudulent job offers and impersonations of NCS recruiters. Phishing emails using convincing-looking but fake addresses are also commonly used to trick you into thinking that they come from official NCS sources. Please note that all official communications from NCS Group will only be sent from verified corporate email addresses. Always check that the sender’s email address ends with the genuine NCS domain, @ncs.com.sg and beware of extra letters, symbols or misspellings. When in doubt, verify the sender’s identity by contacting us at reachus@ncs.com.sg .

title: Lead/Principal Software Engineer
description:
We partner with other firms to accelerate their product capabilities through embedded modernisation and innovation initiatives whilst establishing permanent in-house teams that drive broader transformation. The firm brings together exceptional engineering, product, and design talent to work directly within agencies, delivering world-class solutions and building sustainable capabilities that serve Singapore. As a member of this firm, you'll leverage your expertise to deliver high-impact modernisation projects while mentoring the firms teams and establishing permanent product capabilities that create lasting change for other firms As a Lead / Principal Software Engineer, you will: • Advise and guide client agencies from problem definition through to solution design, in both greenfield and brownfield implementations. •  Advise and guide agency partners from problem definition through to solution design, in both greenfield and brownfield implementations •  Address complex, high-impact challenges at a national scale, driving innovation that improves efficiency and effectiveness across other firms •  Champion engineering excellence by influencing the adoption and continuous improvement of engineering standards and best practices across the firm. •   Serve as the lead architect working with the firm and industry
partner teams to ensure smooth handover from recommendations to implementation—owning the outcomes •  Establish and mentor permanent engineering teams within
agencies, transferring technical expertise and engineering best
practices •   Develop standardised approaches for assessments, discovery, and solution design, grounded in the firm and industry best practices You’ll thrive here if you •  Enjoy untangling complex technology and business problems •  Can translate complex tech into clear, non-technical language •  Focus on value and outcomes, not just technical solutions •  Influence through trust, collaboration, and clarity •   Love working upstream to define problems and shape the right solutions Requirements; •   10+ years of experience in multi-squad development teams with a career progression to Architect •   Adaptable with experience working in different industries, in-house development, consultancy. •   Curious and a problem-solver - understands the need to balance building things right with building the right things •   Actively mentors and cultivates future technical leaders, fostering a culture of continuous learning and excellence •   Exceptional communicator and collaborator - able to communicate effectively with technical and non-technical audiences, with a belief that working across teams and organisations is more effective than operating as a solo unit The firm is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering
an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe it is key to innovation.

title: SENIOR RELATIONSHIP MANAGER
description: Job Mandate Relationship Managers are high-level professionals who manage an affluent customer’s wealth holistically. They will be directly responsible for interfacing and engaging with clients on a day-to-day basis with the objective to grow and protect the clients’ wealth for the enjoyment of their successors and their heirs. With consultative process, Relationship Managers gather information about the client’s needs & wants, then tailor a personalized strategy that uses a range of financial products and services. They customize investment strategies to address each client’s unique circumstances as well as specific preferences for income, liquidity, and risk. Where appropriate, they pursue cross-border investment opportunities to enhance returns and provide additional diversification. The Relationship Manager (RM) is expected to achieve the annual business targets on business development, relationship management and customer profile balances based on more complex investment requirements while ensuring accurate and timely completion of the entire sales and compliance process. The Relationship Manager is primarily responsible for achieving business targets through the following: 1. Acquisition of new high net worth customers 2. Generation of additional volume from existing customers across sales channels 3. Development and engagement of strong relationships with assigned customers 4. Delivery of excellent customer service through investment products and service offerings Trust Advisory Referrals DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES A. Business Development 1. Achieves business targets on acquiring, on-boarding and deepening of Mass affluent customers. 1.1. Identifies and promotes a robust pipeline of prospective customers. 1.2. Sets appointments proactively and meets with customers to establish their needs & requirements. 1.3. Pursues marketing and solicitation of investible funds from existing and new accounts. 1.4. Develops proposals in accordance with the requirements of each account, in line with the client’s objective and needs relative to yield safety, liquidity and diversification. 1.5. Submits proposals and ensures follow-ups in a timely manner including preparation of call reports to apprise management of the account status. 1.6. Conducts client presentations. 1.7. Networks with customers in order to create new business contacts. 2. Liaises proactively with branch partners for referrals and cross-sell opportunities. 3. Develops and deepens relationships with assigned customers. 4. Engages support of specialists to provide technical advice to customers when necessary. 5. Analyzes trends in the market as well as customer profiles to identify potential opportunities for the organization. B. Client Relationship Management 1. Monitors customers’ accounts and portfolios providing proactive, timely, relevant market updates and advice through regular calls and meetings, thus building long-term relationships. 2. Seeks to establish and understand customers’ real needs and expectations. 3. Executes sales and investment strategies/activities effectively. 4. Maximizes each client or branch interaction by building on customer cues to gain commitment to products, services, and solutions. 5. Builds and develops relationships with customers –e.g., accompanying them at social/lifestyle events. 6. Reviews accounts regularly to ensure the growth of the aggregate fund level.

title: Finance / Accounting Trainer (Freelance/Part Time)
description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Delivery of Core Accounting Concepts: Double Entry, Accounting Standards & Accounting Cycle •	Financial Statement Analysis: Guide learners on how to read and interpret the Profit & Loss (Statement of Comprehensive Income) and Balance Sheet (Statement of Financial Position). •	Interpretation of financials: Teach calculation of key financial ratios (Current Ratio, ROI, Gross Margin) to assess business health. •	Data Analytics & Visualization: Guide learners on extracting raw financial data and transforming it into actionable insights. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person, virtual, or hybrid). •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. Administrative Responsibilities: •	Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner. •	Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data. •	Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards. Learner Support and Engagement: •	Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve. •	Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment. •	Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students. Requirements •	ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required. •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. •	Professional certification (e.g., CA, ACCA, CPA) is a plus. •	At least 3–5 years of practical accounting experience. •	Strong presentation, facilitation, and interpersonal skills. •	Prior training or teaching experience is highly desirable. •	Ability to simplify complex financial concepts for diverse learners.

title: IT Risk Specialist
description: As a Governance Risk and Compliance Specialist to join our team, this role is crucial in developing and maintaining a robust culture of technology and cybersecurity risk governance across our organization. The ideal candidate will have at least 5 years of relevant experience in ICT  cybersecurity, data security, audit management, governance, and risk compliance management. He or She will be responsible for providing expert advice on cyber security requirements, reviewing and establishing ICT policies, and supporting various aspects of our tech governance framework. Responsibilities : Develop the culture of Tech risk governance and management across the organisation, and ensure proper accountability in the management, tracking and reporting of tech and cyber risks Provide subject matter advice to internal stakeholders on cyber security requirements that the organization is required to comply with, including internal policies and standards, as well as policies and standards from Government and Regulators Review and establish ICT policies and process controls and conduct compliance checks Recommend the re-engineering and streaming of processes to enhance effectiveness of controls implemented Present management reporting to stakeholders, with analysis of data and trends, and recommend next steps Enhance training and other materials in ICT risk management, document case studies and good practices Track and monitor tech  projects and initiatives to meet compliance requirements. Track and monitor of Key  Risk Indicators and Control Self-Assessment as part of Tech governance framework. Track and monitor incident reporting, including reviewing, monitoring, and reporting on the corrective measures and improvement areas. Participate in consultation and conduct gap analysis against new or revised requirements. Assess and seek waiver approvals for deviations and risk treatment strategies. Organise risk forums, including monitoring of action plans. Coordinate and facilitate IT / cyber security audits. Track remediation plans to address audit findings. Follow up on remediation actions, security and risk assessments with respective stakeholders and project and application managers. Requirements At least 5 years relevant experience in ICT cybersecurity, data security, audit management, governance, risk and compliance management Relevant certifications in IT governance, IT audit, cyber or data security (e.g. CISSP, CISM, CISA etc.) preferred. Ability to work with cross-functional, multi-disciplined team to operationalise monitor security policies and procedures. Knowledge of Instruction Manual 8 (IM8) and CSA Cybersecurity Code of Practice preferred. Strong exposure to ICT Governance in a Cloud environment (AWS Certified personnel preferred) Technical knowledge of security vulnerabilities, validation of remediations and risk assessments. Relevant experience in data visualisation and analytics.

title: Director of Marketing & Communications
description: The Opportunity Step into a pivotal leadership role where luxury meets innovation. As Director of Marketing & Communications , you will be the architect of Conrad Singapore Marina Bay’s brand story — shaping how the hotel is seen, experienced, and remembered. This is your chance to drive commercial success while positioning the property as Singapore’s premier destination for milestone moments, from glamorous weddings to world-class conferences. What You’ll Do Lead with Vision : Design and execute a fully integrated marketing and communications strategy that fuels revenue growth, elevates brand equity, and amplifies Hilton Honors contributions. Be the Storyteller : Craft compelling narratives and sales enablement materials — presentations, proposals, digital assets — that resonate with corporate clients, MICE planners, and couples seeking unforgettable celebrations. Own the Digital Stage : Drive engagement across all owned channels — website, CRM, email, and social media — ensuring every touchpoint reflects the hotel’s luxury positioning. Champion Social Media : Define and deliver a bold social strategy that sparks conversation, builds advocacy, and keeps Conrad Marina Bay at the center of Singapore’s lifestyle scene. Guard the Brand : Protect and elevate Conrad’s voice and visual identity, ensuring consistency across every guest-facing moment, from pre-arrival inspiration to post-stay loyalty. Shape Perception : Oversee PR, media relations, influencer collaborations, and agency partnerships to strengthen share of voice and reputation. Deliver Results : Track, analyze, and report on marketing effectiveness using KPIs such as ROAS, CTR, CPC, and conversion — ensuring every campaign drives measurable impact. Lead with Purpose : Inspire, coach, and develop a high-performing Marketing & Communications team, fostering creativity, collaboration, and accountability. Key Outcomes Achievement of commercial metrics: Revenue Generation Index (RGI), total revenue, Hilton Honors contribution . Growth in Weddings, MICE, and social events through targeted storytelling and campaigns. Elevated online reputation and guest sentiment across platforms. Seamless execution of PR, digital, and creative deliverables with agency partners. A culture of innovation and excellence within the Marketing & Communications team. Why This Role Matters This is more than a marketing position — it’s about shaping the future of a landmark hotel in one of Asia’s most dynamic markets. You’ll have the platform to create unforgettable guest experiences, drive commercial success, and leave a lasting mark on Singapore’s luxury hospitality scene. Qualifications - External What are we looking for? A Director of Marketing & Communications serving Hilton Worldwide Brand hotels and vacations is always working on behalf of our Guests and working with other Team Members. To successfully fill this role, you should maintain the attitude, behaviors, skills, and values that follow: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business or related field. Minimum 3 years in a leadership role within the hospitality industry (hotels/resorts) will be an advantage. Proven track record of developing and executing successful, result-driven marketing strategies. Expert knowledge of digital marketing, content marketing, analytics and social media tools. Exceptional communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills. Strong team player who collaborates well with stakeholders. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and multi-task. What will it be like to work for Hilton? Hilton is the leading global hospitality company, spanning the lodging sector from luxurious full-service hotels and resorts to extended-stay suites and mid-priced hotels. For nearly a century, Hilton has offered business and leisure travelers the finest in accommodations, service, amenities and value. Hilton is dedicated to continuing its tradition of providing exceptional guest experiences across its global brands .  Our vision “to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality” unites us as a team to create remarkable hospitality experiences around the world every day.  And, our amazing Team Members are at the heart of it all!

title: Finance Director
description: Shape strategy. Lead finance. Create meaningful impact. We are conducting a confidential executive search for an accomplished and forward-looking Finance Director. This is a rare opportunity for a senior finance professional to join a non-profit purpose-driven organisation with a strong track record of service excellence. The ideal candidate will bring experience in complex, multi-stakeholder settings and a passion for strategic financial leadership, operational excellence, and long-term sustainability. About the Role The Finance Director will provide strategic direction and oversight of the organisation’s financial functions for various entities, supporting a broad range of programmes and services for the community. This role ensures long-term financial sustainability, compliance with relevant regulations, and effective stewardship of resources. Responsibilities span financial planning, budgeting, reporting, funding, and audit activities, in close coordination with internal teams and external stakeholders including government organisations and statutory boards. Core Areas of Responsibility 1. Strategic Financial Leadership Develop and execute a financial roadmap aligned with the organisation’s mission and goals. Guide the executive/senior leadership and governance bodies on performance, planning, and financial risk. Perform scenario modelling and financial analysis to support strategic decisions. Offer expert financial input to key functional leaders and operations teams 2. Financial Planning and Budget Oversight Lead and coordinate the annual budgeting process across departments. Track and report on financial performance and recommending interventions as needed. Ensure delivery of timely and accurate financial reports throughout the fiscal year. 3. Revenue Optimisation and Funding Strategy Monitor financial indicators and streamline revenue processes for service units. Manage relationships and negotiations related to funding frameworks with external partners and Government organisations. Provide analysis and insight into funding model changes and long-term financial viability. Ensure compliance with grant requirements and manage billing and collection functions. 4. Governance, Compliance & Risk Uphold financial and statutory compliance in accordance with regulatory standards. Oversee external and internal audits and manage communications with auditors and authorities. Identify financial risks and implement proactive mitigation controls. 5. Procurement & Capital Resource Planning Supervise procurement strategy to achieve cost-effectiveness and control compliance. Lead planning and budgeting for infrastructure, equipment and technology investment. 6. People & Cross-Functional Leadership Build and mentor a capable finance team committed to continuous improvement. Collaborate across departments to ensure financial alignment with service delivery goals. Foster effective communication and integration across operational units. What We are Looking For 11 to 15 years of progressive senior finance and leadership experience. Current or prior expereince in a Community Healthcare or acute public Hospital environment involving multiple funding sources and regulatory oversight and compliance will be HIGHLY ADVANTAGEOUS. Demonstrated experience in managing Government ministry/statutory board subventions, including budgeting, utilization tracking, reporting, and compliance with funding requirements and audit standards. Solid experience in financial analysis, budgeting, grant management, and audit readiness. Strong leadership and communication skills with experience collaborating across functional areas. Comfortable navigating complex stakeholder environments. Proficient in both written and verbal communication. Preferred Background Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. CPA or equivalent professional certification preferred. Postgraduate qualifications such as an MBA or MHA will be an added advantage. The selected candidate can look forward to a competitive remuneration package consisting of basic salary, 13th month AWS and variable/performance bonuses. Interested and suitably qualified applicants, please apply here or email to:- jerry.loo@recruit-inc.com Reg No: R1550439 EA Licence No: 14C7334 We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

description: Activate Interactive Pte Ltd (“Activate”) is a leading technology consultancy headquartered in Singapore with a presence in Malaysia and Indonesia. title: Global Banking & Markets, Transformation Office, Analyst, Singapore
description: ABOUT GLOBAL BANKING & MARKETS Our core value is building strong relationships with our institutional clients, which include corporations, financial service providers, and fund managers. We help them buy and sell financial products on exchanges around the world, raise funding, and manage risk. This is a dynamic, entrepreneurial team with a passion for the markets, with individuals who thrive in fast-paced, changing environments and are energized by a bustling trading floor. Global Banking & Markets: Transformation Office Are you a looking for a new opportunity to showcase your intellectual curiosity, advisory and project management skills? Do you have a strategic mindset and the ability to influence change? Do you wish to be valued for your innovative approach to problem-solving? We have an excellent new position available within our Transformation Office team for Global banking & Markets. The Global Banking & Markets Transformation Office Team is searching for dedicated individuals who thrives in a collaborative and challenging environment to join our team. Global Change Managers serve as program or product managers on high priority initiatives within the Global Banking & Markets Division. You will work alongside other Change Managers, Product Managers, Sales, Strats, Engineers and Designers to ensure solutions are designed and implemented for the Global Banking & Markets Division in true ‘front to back’ fashion. You will leverage your technical expertise and project management skills to influence and drive the team to deliver these complex projects within budget and with a high quality. HOW YOU WILL FULFILL YOUR POTENTIAL Work with Sales, Trading, Engineering and Operations teams to develop and implement strategic complex initiatives to enable business growth, regulatory compliance and control improvements Leverage project management methodologies (e.g. Waterfall, Agile) and governance tools to provide transparency to senior stakeholders on project scope, timelines, budget and delivery risks Identify and manage project risks from inception to completion while driving resolutions and risk & issue mitigants Perform detailed analysis to enhance business, system & operational workflows Facilitate communication on key project matters (incl. reporting and escalations) across GBM teams, including Sales, Operations, Engineering, Legal, Compliance Provide oversight on the project book of work, ensuring cross project dependencies, risks, and budget are proactively managed Facilitate the performance of quality assurance testing, migration plans and post implementation reviews alongside product SKILLS & EXPERIENCE WE’RE LOOKING FOR Bachelor's Degree 1-3 years’ relevant experience, preferably in a business analysis / project / program management function Strong analytical and business analysis experience, including process analysis Strategic thinker able to identify cross functional issues, provide innovative solutions, and communicate the implications of solutions across existing processes, systems and teams, e.g. costs, risk, service Project management skills including project scope definition; project issues, challenges, risks definition; status reporting; User Acceptance Test execution A proven track record of scoping and driving delivery of projects. Has delivered results in a cross functional team setting, on time and to budget Experienced in driving collaboration and facilitate discussions with stakeholders to perform problem solving and solution analysis Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, written and oral; confident in interaction with senior stakeholders PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Knowledge of products and processes related to the financial services industry would be an advantage, as is related experience in technology strategy or in a client service or business function and / or management consulting Core knowledge base of Global Banking & Markets Demonstrate understanding of Financial Services & Operations processes and procedures Familiarity with project management methodologies and concepts, Project Management related certifications preferred but optional (e.g. PMP, Prince2, Agile, etc.) Experience developing products using customer and design-centric practices Knowledge of automated testing tools and frameworks, Agile Testing practices Experience on tools like JIRA and JIRA Portfolio or similar applications Knowledge of OKR frameworks and roadmaps, as well as other governance and project planning tooling ABOUT GOLDMAN SACHS At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world. We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs.
