title: Relationship Manager
description: PropertyGuru is Southeast Asia’s leading PropTech company, and the preferred destination for over 32 million property seekers monthly to connect with over 50,000 agents monthly to find their dream home. PropertyGuru empowers property seekers with more than 2.1 million real estate listings, in-depth insights, and solutions that enable them to make confident property decisions across Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand and Vietnam. PropertyGuru.com.sg was launched in Singapore in 2007 and since then, PropertyGuru Group has made the property journey a transparent one for property seekers in Southeast Asia. In the last 18 years, PropertyGuru has grown into a high-growth PropTech company with a robust portfolio including leading property marketplaces and award-winning mobile apps across its markets in Singapore, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand as well as the region’s biggest and most respected industry recognition platform – PropertyGuru Asia Property Awards , events and publications across Asia. About the Role As a Relationship Manager, you will play a critical role in expanding PropertyGuru’s customer base by engaging and supporting mass market agent clients. This role is hunter-focused: you will drive new business acquisition through proactive outreach, effective prospecting, and strong product pitches. You will be responsible for building early-stage relationships, understanding agent needs, and helping them get started on our platform. You will recommend relevant products, and ensure every agent receives professional, high-quality service. This role is ideal for someone energetic, driven, and eager to grow a career in sales within the PropTech industry. As a Guru you will be accountable for: Acquire new clients through high-volume cold calling and proactive outreach to mass market agent clients. Conduct high-volume cold calling and outreach to build a strong pipeline of prospects and generate new revenue opportunities. Be comfortable with face-to-face engagements, including client meetings, networking sessions, and training events, representing PropertyGuru professionally and confidently. Deliver effective product pitches and demonstrations, clearly explaining how PropertyGuru’s solutions can support agent success. Build early-stage relationships with agent clients, providing guidance and support as they start using the platform. Identify client needs and recommend suitable products, ensuring agents understand the value and features of our solutions. Support account onboarding and activation, including helping new clients set up, navigate their account, and optimise their usage. Maintain strong service standards, ensuring mass market clients receive professional and timely support. Gather market feedback and competitor insights to help improve product offerings and sales strategies. Collaborate with team mates to surface opportunities, share market insights, and support go-to-market initiatives. Hit and exceed monthly sales and activity targets, demonstrating consistency, resilience, and drive. Be a strong team player, work and communicate well with team mates, sharing insights, and supporting team initiatives to contribute to a positive and cohesive working environment. We're looking for someone who: Experience in sales, telesales, customer service, or business development, preferably in a high-volume or fast-paced environment. Strong hunter mentality, motivated, persistent and results-oriented. Confidence in cold calling and client engagement, with the ability to deliver clear pitches and handle objections effectively. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build rapport quickly with agent clients. Positive, energetic, and resilient, with a strong desire to learn and grow in a commercial role. Organised and self-driven, able to manage your time, track activities, and follow through consistently. Team player, willing to collaborate, share insights, and contribute to the overall success of the commercial team. Ability to work effectively with minimal supervision, demonstrating maturity, accountability, and strong organizational skills. You can prioritize tasks, manage time independently, and consistently deliver results in a fast-paced environment. Our commitment to you: Hybrid flexible working that focuses on outcomes over hours. Holistic rewards package covering your financial, physical & mental health. Multi-directional career development across all levels. Inclusive benefits like equal paternity leave, supporting all employees in work-life balance.

title: Safety Engineer
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Safety Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on supporting and reporting to the Singapore Refinery Operational Safety Manager in the area of operational safety, workplace safety, Emergency and Crisis Management to deliver full compliance with Corporate Safety, local and regulatory safety requirements. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the safety team, refinery engineers, supervisors and front-line personnel in Singapore and the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. You will provide your operational safety knowledge to our Refinery on matters of operational safety, such as Safety Leadership cultural shift, critical field controls & verifications, Contractor Safety, Workplace Safety & Health Committee, Fire Safety Management, Emergency Response and Crisis management. In our Safety team, you will be required to support the Safety Manager and safety team in safety-related tasks and achieve our common goals. Together with you and your safety knowledge, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Safety Engineer Drive and execute operational safety, workplace safety, emergency response and crisis management Ensure refinery legal right to operate safely is delivered Support refinery safety manager in strategic and tactical safety planning, implementation and execution of safety programs Operational Safety Support and participate in refinery operational risk assessments, safety critical job and deviations reviews Support corporate and refinery implementation of creating safety together to strengthen refinery safety cultural shift Ensure the executions of critical safety risk controls, monitoring, trending, verifying and communicated for learnings & improvement Workplace Safety Together with refinery key stakeholders, implement contractors and suppliers safety management Support the Turnaround Safety Lead and team in turnaround planning, preparations executions and lesson learnt Support Workplace Safety & Health Committee initiatives and programs Support and execute site fire safety management Incident Management Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Prepare, maintain emergency response plan and execute training of company emergency response team to mitigate incidents, emergencies and compliance to National CERT Audits and Standard Support preparation of refinery crisis leader and crisis management team training and dive into the role and responsibility of crisis management team support during crisis Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 6 years of safety working experience in the oil, gas or petrochemical industry Experienced to support and operationalise workplace safety, fire safety, emergency response and crisis management Good understanding of local WSH regulations and have experience dealing with local regulators such as MHD, MOM, SPF, SCDF, etc. Good communication, writing and analytical skill Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in Safety highly desired Registered  WSHO and FSM, Noise Monitoring Officer added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application. We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Senior Manager - HR Quality, Compliance & Audit and Strategic Program
description: Job Description Role Summary Join our dynamic team at Micron, where we are dedicated to driving outstanding quality, compliance, and governance in our human resources practices. Based out of Asia, this role offers an outstanding opportunity to lead and innovate within a world-class organization renowned for its dedication to excellence. As the Quality, Compliance and Governance Senior Manager for People Organization, you will lead the global Quality Management and Labor Laws Compliance agenda within the People organization and orchestrates strategic programs that enable compliant, consistent, and scalable HR service delivery. You will design and maintain a robust quality framework (process standards, controls, audit readiness), ensure adherence to global and local labor requirements across multiple countries, and drive cross-functional programs that improve employee experience, reduce risk, and enhance operational efficiency. You will assume the role of HR System Level Process Owner (SLPO), serving as the primary actor accountable for the system level process operations and governance. The ideal candidate blends deep HR operations expertise, strong knowledge of labor law/compliance, and program management excellence. Key Responsibilities 1) Quality Management , Labor laws Compliance & Risk management Build and develop a high-performing team that effectively manages and supports Quality Management System, RBA Labor Compliance, and Governance in People Organization. Review, evaluate, and maintain all process controls to meet Quality Audits and RBA Labor compliance requirements. Collaborate with various departments including Global Quality and Site Process Coordinators in People Organization to successfully implement controls and monitoring systems for Quality Management System. Support various audits such as ISO/IATF, ISO 27001 and Micron’s internal audits. Develop strategies, tools and methodologies to ensure Micron manufacturing sites comply with RBA Labor requirements. Perform detailed audits, including RBA internal assessments and due diligence audits on recruiting agents and suppliers to assess and improve RBA compliance levels. Promote the use of standard methodologies for compliance and risk management, while also providing education on these practices. title: Analyst – Investor Relations & Corporate Development
description: About CapBridge CapBridge, a member of FOMO Group, is a leading digital investment platform headquartered in Singapore. As a Capital Markets Services licensee, CapBridge is regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to deal in capital markets products, including securities and collective investment schemes, and to provide custodial services. It is also an exempt financial adviser licensed to issue or promulgate analyses and reports on investment products. CapBridge enables HNWIs and institutional clients to invest in traditional and digital assets via its one-stop digital investment platform, providing highly curated, top-quality, and institutional-grade opportunities to meet clients' diverse asset allocation needs. Through CapBridge's associated company, FOMO Pay Pte Ltd, a regulated MAS Payment Services Act (PSA) Digital Payment Token (DPT) entity, qualified clients can also invest into CapBridge curated products using DPTs, providing a seamless bridge between Traditional Finance (TradFi) and Web3 Finance. About the Role! This role is ideal for individuals who are intellectually curious, commercially minded, and motivated by building—not just advising on—the future of financial services. We are seeking a highly analytical and execution-focused Analyst to support the Investor Relations and Corporate Development function. This role will work closely with the senior Investor Relations lead to support fundraising, investor communications, financial analysis, and strategic initiatives across the group and its operating companies. The role requires strong financial rigor, including hands-on financial modelling, alongside the ability to develop, structure, and storyboard high-quality investor and executive-level presentations. Key Responsibilities Investor Relations & Fundraising Support · Support fundraising initiatives by preparing high-quality investor materials, including pitch decks, 	financial models, and investor updates. · Assist with investor outreach tracking, follow-ups, and pipeline management. · Coordinate and manage data rooms and due diligence materials for fundraising processes. · Conduct market, investor, and peer benchmarking research to support capital-raising efforts. Presentation Development & Storyboarding · Own the development, structuring, and storyboarding of investor presentations in collaboration with senior 	leadership. · Translate complex financial and strategic information into clear, compelling, and investor-ready narratives. · Create executive-level PowerPoint presentations for investors, board meetings, and internal stakeholders. · Ensure consistency of messaging, visuals, and data across all investor-facing materials. Financial Analysis & Modeling · Build and maintain detailed financial models, including forecasts, scenario analyses, and valuation models. · Analyse group and operating company performance to support investor reporting and strategic decisions. · Prepare sensitivity analyses and commercial assessments to support fundraising and strategic initiatives. Corporate Development & M&A Support · Support the evaluation of acquisition opportunities through financial analysis, market mapping, and 	benchmarking. · Assist in preparing investment memoranda and internal decision-making materials. · Support transaction execution processes in coordination with internal teams and external advisors. Reporting & Stakeholder Support · Support quarterly and ad hoc investor reporting, including performance summaries and KPI 	dashboards. · Coordinate inputs across finance, legal, and operating teams to ensure accuracy and alignment of 	investor materials. · Maintain a high standard of quality, accuracy, and professionalism across all communications. Qualifications & Experience · Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Economics, or a related discipline. · Minimum of 3 years’ experience in investment banking or a Big Four audit firm, with exposure to M&A 	advisory, transaction services, or corporate finance. · Proven ability to develop investor-ready presentations, including structuring and storyboarding 	complex financial and strategic narratives. · Strong financial modeling, valuation, and analytical capabilities. · Advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint (or equivalent tools). · Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with strong attention to detail and visual clarity. · Ability to perform effectively under tight deadlines and manage multiple workstreams concurrently. · Comfortable working with senior executives, investors, and external advisors. · Highly proactive, hands-on, and execution-driven. · Fluent in both English and mandarin to manage Chinese stakeholders. Our Perks and Benefits: · Competitive Compensation and Benefit Package · Group Hospitalisation and Medical Insurance · Fun and Dynamic Environment with plenty of learning and growth opportunities · Career Advancement · Professional Development Training Workshops · Team Building Sessions

Ability to work independently and customise learning plans based on the student's progress and needs. Patience, empathy, and a passion for helping students achieve their potential. Why Join Us? No More Sudden Cancellations : We collect deposits before the first session, so you don’t have to worry about last-minute cancellations. No Wasted Trips : If a client doesn’t show up, you will still receive compensation for the first session. One-Time Administration Fee : Any additional sessions or direct referrals won’t incur further administrative fees from Special Minds. Boost Your Profile : Gain visibility through the free promotion of your profile on our social media platforms, helping you connect with more clients. Meaningful Career : With the growing demand for special needs education (SEN) in Singapore, this is your opportunity to gain valuable experience and build a meaningful career helping students with unique needs. Additional Income Opportunity : Take on assignments that fit your schedule and preferences to earn extra income in your free time, or choose full-time opportunities for more consistent earnings! Compensation: Part-Time Tutor : Up to $60/hr Full-Time Tutor : Up to $90/hr Early Childhood Educator : Up to $70/hr MOE/Special Ed Tutor : Up to $140/hr Enrichment Specialist (dance, art, swimming, etc.): Up to $120-150 per session Therapist (speech, occupational, physiotherapy, etc.):Up to $200-220/hr Psychologist : Up to $250/hr Counsellor : Up to $150/hr If you’re passionate about education and want to make a difference in the lives of students with learning or special needs, join us at Special Minds. title: Research Executive
description: About Dentsu Group (dentsu) Dentsu Group Inc. (Tokyo: 4324; ISIN: JP3551520004) is a global holding company established on January 1, 2020. The Group operates through two main networks: dentsu japan network, overseeing agencies in Japan, and dentsu international, headquartered in London, managing operations outside Japan. Operating in around 145 markets with 65,000 professionals, the Dentsu Group delivers integrated, client-centric communications, media, and digital solutions. Its five global leadership brands—Carat, dentsu X, iProspect, Dentsu Creative, and Merkle—alongside the Dentsu Japan Network, including Dentsu Inc., offer end-to-end services across media, creativity, customer experience, and content. Role Purpose Our client is seeking a Research Executive – Intelligence to support the delivery of data-driven insights primarily within the B2B category. This role sits within the Research & Data Strategy team and supports marketing and business decision-making through robust analysis, insight development, and clear storytelling. The successful candidate will work closely with senior team members to translate complex data into actionable insights, supporting research-led programmes, testing and optimisation initiatives, and the delivery of high-quality insight outputs. This role offers strong development opportunities for someone looking to grow their expertise in research, analytics, and insight-led strategy. Responsibilites • Analyse quantitative data (e.g. surveys, intent data, performance datasets) and qualitative data (e.g. interviews, open-ended responses) to identify patterns, tensions, and opportunities. • Synthesize findings across multiple data sources into end-to-end insight reports with clear conclusions and implications. • Apply statistical reasoning and advanced analytical techniques, including segmentation, propensity modelling, and correlation analysis, when required. • Support questionnaire design, script testing, and validation, including routing, logic, and user experience. • Manage fieldwork processes, ensuring data quality, quota management, and progress tracking. • Conduct desk research and market scanning to uncover relevant secondary data, trends, and contextual inputs. • Prepare charts, outputs, and narrative frameworks to support insight-led storytelling. • Lead defined sections of insight and strategy presentations, clearly articulating implications and recommendations. • Use Q for data setup, cleaning, and analysis. • Use Excel where appropriate for exploratory analysis, modelling, and validation. • Pull and interpret data from relevant audience and market insight tools to support B2B initiatives, including ABM, segmentation, and targeting. • Support ongoing testing and optimisation initiatives and contribute to continuous improvement of insight delivery. • Lead day-to-day client and stakeholder communications, providing clarity and direction throughout project delivery. • Collaborate closely with strategy, activation, creative, and client leadership teams to translate insight into integrated solutions. Experience & Skills • Minimum 2+ years’ experience in research, preferably within B2B. • Strong experience with quantitative and qualitative research methodologies. • Strong analytical skills, with the ability to translate data into clear conclusions and compelling stories. • Experience using Q, Displayr, or SPSS. • Experience across a range of research project types, such as brand, customer satisfaction and loyalty, segmentation, market assessment, concept testing, thought leadership, and ABM/ABX. • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. • High attention to detail and accuracy. • Ambition to develop into a B2B research consultant with increasing client ownership and commercial responsibility. We welcome applications from all individuals, regardless of race, ethnicity, nationality, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, marital status, or any other protected characteristic. Beyond recruitment, we strive to create an environment where everyone feels respected, supported, and empowered to bring their authentic selves to work.

Demonstrates independence, personal effectiveness, and strong time management skills.

title: Syndicated Loans, Indonesia Market - Global Investment Banking
description: Who We Are: As Singapore’s longest established bank, we have been dedicated to enabling individuals and businesses to achieve their aspirations since 1932. How? By taking the time to truly understand people. From there, we provide support, services, solutions, and career paths that meet their individual needs and desires. Today, we’re on a journey of transformation. Leveraging technology and creativity to become a future-ready learning organisation. But for all that change, our strategic ambition is consistently clear and bold, which is to be Asia’s leading financial services partner for a sustainable future. We invite you to build the bank of the future. Innovate the way we deliver financial services. Work in friendly, supportive teams. Build lasting value in your community. Help people grow their assets, business, and investments. Take your learning as far as you can. Or simply enjoy a vibrant, future-ready career. Your Opportunity Starts Here. Why Join Play a key role in shaping the loan origination and syndication strategy for one of Asia's leading banks. As an Executive Director in our Global Investment Banking Division, you'll have the opportunity to work on complex, high-profile transactions, develop deep relationships with clients, and contribute to the growth of our loan business. How you succeed To excel in this role, you'll need to combine strong technical skills with commercial acumen and a deep understanding of the loan market. You'll work closely with our origination, syndication, and distribution teams to identify opportunities, structure transactions, and execute trades. Your ability to analyze complex financial data, think critically, and communicate effectively will be essential in driving success in this role. Position Overview: The candidate will be a pivotal member of the Loans Origination (Indonesia) team within Loan Capital Markets, with a primary focus on independently originating and executing syndicated loan transactions specifically within the Indonesian market. Deep expertise and a strong network in Indonesia’s syndication and capital markets are essential. What you do Proactively originate and structure syndicated loan deals tailored to the unique dynamics and regulatory environment of the Indonesian market. Utilize an extensive and well-established network within Indonesia’s syndication markets to identify and secure high-quality lending opportunities. Conduct in-depth analysis of Indonesian clients’ financing needs, encompassing transaction due diligence, debt capacity, and market liquidity, while staying abreast of local pricing trends and market developments. Provide strategic recommendations on financing solutions that align with Indonesia’s economic conditions, regulatory framework, and market practices. Prepare comprehensive financing proposals, credit memoranda, term sheets, cash flow models, information memoranda, and investor presentations, leveraging local market insights and collaborating closely with junior team members. Lead the end-to-end execution process for Indonesian deals, including syndication launch preparation, documentation review, investor engagement, and deal closing, ensuring compliance with local regulations and market standards. Collaborate effectively with internal teams and external partners to facilitate smooth transaction execution from mandate through closing, with a focus on the Indonesian market landscape. Partner with the distribution team to develop underwriting, pricing, liquidity, and syndication strategies that reflect the nuances of Indonesia’s capital markets. Mentor and guide junior team members, sharing deep knowledge of the Indonesian market to build team capability. Who you work with Global Investment Banking gives you the way into Capital Markets, Corporate Finance, and the Mezzanine Capital Unit. The team offer wide-ranging funding solutions, across key markets, ensuring local customers benefit from international expertise. In return, the chance to build deep experience, across the entire project lifecycle, exposure to multiple industries, and an international career. Who you are Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related discipline. Minimum of 15 years’ relevant experience in corporate banking, investment banking, and/or loan syndication, with a strong track record specifically in the Indonesian market. Demonstrated expertise in credit analysis, loan structuring, loan syndication, and capital markets within Indonesia. Strong analytical skills with meticulous attention to detail. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build and maintain relationships in Indonesia’s financial sector. Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast-paced environment. Client-oriented mindset with a passion for delivering innovative financing solutions tailored to Indonesian clients. Proficiency in Bahasa Indonesia is highly desirable. What we offer: Competitive base salary. A suite of holistic, flexible benefits to suit every lifestyle. Community initiatives. Industry-leading learning and professional development opportunities. Your wellbeing, growth and aspirations are every bit as cared for as the needs of our customers.

Throughout the years, we have established a comprehensive network of social services to meet a wide range of needs in our community. Our diverse programmes reach out to children who need care and protection, families in material need, ailing aged requiring quality nursing care and other needy segments in our society. title: KYC Specialist Mandarin Speaking (Corporate Banking)
description: Location: Singapore (Hybrid) Type: Full-time EXPERIENCE: 3-8+ years in AML CDD policy and standards, financial services (preferably with banks) Vertical: Financial Markets _______________________________________________________________________ Summary As a member of the KYC/AML Client Outreach Specialist (“COS”) team, you will serve as a support member for the client relationship teams, partnering to meet Anti Money Laundering (AML) Client Due Diligence (CDD) Refresh requirements. As a COS, you will partner with product sales and other banking or markets relationship teams, manage a client refresh population, reach out for key AML/CDD documentation, and act as an escalation point for service or operational challenges that may arise in KYC refresh. COS analysts act as the primary point of coordination between Banking/Markets and AML Refresh Operations teams to ensure timely and accurate compliance to policy and regulatory requirements in KYC refresh. A COS is the client advocate, helps to simplify client interaction and deliver the most seamless, holistic service relationship across the firm. You will ensure the accuracy of documentation, and screening, obtain approvals from Compliance and Business and complete refresh cases in a timely and accurate manner. Responsibilities · Support annual KYC refresh for Banking and Markets clients · Execute with client perspective while balancing risk to the firm · Support a culture of teamwork and collaboration focused on goals and delivery · Adhere to standards for timeliness and accuracy of AML CDD case completion · Master CDD requirements applicable to the banking clients and guide clients and bank staff in complying with them · Understand and adopt process recommendations and procedural updates derived from changes to policy, technology adoption and process enhancements · Coordinate with partners in other regions and LOBs to ensure partnership in global KYC initiatives · Review the industry group client hierarchy, looking for inactive accounts to be closed, linkage errors within client structures and merge/purge of client entities · Assist in tracking both Annual refresh population and other KYC related populations in partnership with coverage team · Ability to communicate in Mandarin with Chinese clients for business purposes Eligibility Requirements · 3-8+ years of experience in financial services preferably with banks · Previous experience in client facing roles and must demonstrate a strong client focus · Experience with AML CDD policy standards and industry practices in AML CDD · Understanding of the operational aspects of products and services used by Banking clients · Proven stakeholder relationship/influence management skills to achieve swift adoption through close partnerships · The ability to find ways of solving or pre-empting problems · Understands and respects cultural differences · Bachelor’s degree · AML CDD knowledge for legal entities in and across multiple global jurisdiction

title: Head, Philanthropy
description: The Head, Philanthropy will play a critical role in driving TPC’s expansion into Corporate Philanthropy. We are seeking an experienced and visionary Head, Philanthropy to lead, manage and drive project development efforts of our new multi-faceted philanthropy foundation setup, which focuses on Education, Healthcare and Climate/Environment. This includes uplifting identified communities, which includes youth, working adults and parents with their school going children. He/she would possess a combination of professional experience, skills, and qualities that align with the complexities of philanthropy, community development, and stakeholder engagement. He/she will be working with social service organizations, government ministries agencies, NGOs, educational institutions, businesses, community leaders, and grassroots organizations to create sustainable and impactful initiatives that empower these underserved communities. Lead the Strategic Development of the Philanthropy’s setup (through TPC’s Corporate Philanthropy strategy) and Financial Management Develop a comprehensive and overarching philanthropic strategy that aligns with TPC Group’s vision, mission and values, ensuring a focus on TPC’s well-being mandate Develop a sustainable financial plan and resource mobilization strategy which involves budgeting, forecasting, and resource allocation, to ensure the efficient and effective utilization of funds. Collaborate with a wide range of stakeholders through a comprehensive Community Partnership Strategy for Advocacy & Engagement Establish and nurture partnerships with through multi-sector partnerships with a wide range of stakeholders, including social service organizations, government agencies, NGOs, educational institutions, businesses, community leaders, and grassroots organizations Represent the philanthropy/foundations in conferences, panel discussions, and public speaking engagements related to philanthropy, community development, and social impact. Continually raise awareness about its mission and impact. Design, Manage and Evaluate the Philanthropy Foundation’s programs Develop a comprehensive program management framework that encompasses all aspects of the foundation's initiatives, including planning, execution, monitoring, and evaluation. Team Leadership Build and manage a dedicated team of professionals, including program managers and support staff. Job Experience: Proven track record of at least 15 years in nonprofit leadership roles, with a focus on philanthropy, corporate social responsibility (CSR), community development, or social services. Demonstrated success in creating and implementing impactful philanthropic strategies, particularly in underserved communities in Singapore. Enterprising and resourceful Strong network within the non-profit sector, including relationships with Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) - funded social service organizations, local businesses, and community leaders. Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build and maintain strong partnerships. Experience in managing diverse teams and driving collaboration across multiple stakeholders. Solid understanding of grant writing, donor relations, and fundraising techniques. Passion for community upliftment, social justice, and making a meaningful impact on the lives of marginalized individuals Outstanding communication and interpersonal skills, with an authentic and confident presence that builds credibility and trust Highly organized, with the ability to manage multiple streams of work at high quality Excellent project management skills, with the ability to work with multiple stakeholders and deliver under tight timelines A mature, humble, professional, and hands-on team player Alignment with TPC’s values; appreciate the importance of balancing one’s level of competence, consciousness and wellbeing in delivering sustainable results.

title: Sr Manager, Workforce Development
description: The Senior Manager of Global Assembly & Test Central Workforce Development serves as the strategic leader for Workforce Development (WFD) across all Assembly & Test (AT) sites. As part of a central function, this role is responsible for establishing governance, harmonized standards, and best‑practice frameworks that elevate workforce capability, readiness, and learning excellence across the global AT network. Acting as both a steward and enabler of the Global Workforce Development Standard, the Senior Manager leads a Center‑of‑Excellence (CoE) that partners with site WFD teams, functional leaders, and Global Talent Management to deploy consistent processes, scalable initiatives, and enterprise-wide development programs. This role ensures that AT’s workforce development processes remain future-ready, globally aligned, data-driven, and tightly integrated with corporate talent strategies - including onboarding, technical training, learning governance, skills frameworks & analytics, and capability-building initiatives. Key Responsibilities 1. Lead Global Workforce Development Strategy & Governance Define and drive AT-wide WFD strategy aligned to global business, operations, and talent priorities. Govern adoption of the Global Workforce Development Standard, ensuring consistent processes for training, competency models, training documentation governance, LMS governance, audits, and compliance across all AT sites. 2. Standardization, Best Practices & CoE Leadership Lead best‑practice sharing, cross-site forums, and communities of practice to uplift WFD maturity, performance consistency, and innovation. Drive standardization for operator, technician, and engineer training frameworks across regions, including certification requirements and documentation control processes. Partners with sites WFD, SMEs and engineering leads to ensure technical content, SOPs, and process documentation remain accurate, standardized, and audit-ready. 3. Partner with Global Talent Management and other functions on Enterprise Programs Drive and support deployment of global learning initiatives as well as systems or process improvements across AT. Ensure that AT needs are understood and integrated into corporate program design. 4. Workforce Analytics, Metrics & Reporting Establish AT-wide metrics for training effectiveness, competency readiness, and compliance. Provide data-driven insights and recommendations to AT leadership and global stakeholders. 5. Change Leadership & Capability Building Lead organizational change related to new WFD standards, systems, and initiatives—supporting communication, adoption, and sustainability. Build capability across site WFD teams through coaching, training, and leadership of global WFD councils. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in HR, Organizational Development, Engineering, Business, or related field or equivalent work experience. 8+ years of experience in Workforce Development, Learning & Development, or similar domains. Proven experience leading global or multi-site functions, with strong governance and standardization skills. Demonstrated success partnering with cross-functional and global stakeholders. Proven ability in workforce development project and program management. Preferred experience Experience in semiconductor or advanced manufacturing environments. Strong understanding of competency modeling, training frameworks, or learning management systems.

title: Deputy General Manager, Regional Compliance (ASEAN)
description: Overview We are seeking a senior compliance leader to design and enforce a unified governance framework across our ASEAN subsidiaries. Reporting to the Regional Head of Legal & Compliance, you will act as a strategic partner to the business, ensuring that our cross-border network expansion (IPLC, IP Transit, SD-WAN) and multi-country operations align strictly with the diverse legal, tax, and telecom licensing requirements of the region. Key Responsibilities: Regional Regulatory Strategy: Oversee the regulatory roadmap for entering and operating in ASEAN markets (including Indonesia, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand, and Philippines). Manage the acquisition and maintenance of necessary telecom licenses (e.g., NFP/NSP/NTC) and serve as the primary escalation point for regional regulators. Cross-Border Operational Governance: Harmonize "General Affairs" and HR compliance across subsidiaries. Ensure local offices adhere to their respective labor laws (e.g., Employment Act, Omnibus Law) regarding hiring, expatriate visas, and statutory benefits. Data Privacy & Sovereignty: Lead the regional data privacy strategy. Navigate complex data sovereignty laws to ensure the compliant transfer of customer data and internal records between ASEAN entities and Regional HQ. Commercial Risk Management: Sit on the Joint Governance committee to review complex multi-country connectivity deals. Audit vendor and partner contracts to ensure "back-to-back" liability protection and adherence to anti-corruption (ABC) standards. Internal Audit & Control: Conduct regular compliance audits of local ASEAN offices, focusing on procurement integrity, safety (WSH/EHS) standards, and financial reporting. Stakeholder Management: Translate regional strategy into local compliance roadmaps and act as the bridge between local ASEAN teams and the Group Headquarters. Job RequirementsExperience & Qualifications: Bachelor’s Degree in Law, International Business, or a related discipline. A Master’s degree or professional compliance certification is an advantage. Minimum 12 years of experience in Compliance, Legal, or Regional Operations, with at least 5 years in a leadership role within the Telecommunications or ICT industry. Proven track record of managing compliance or operations across multiple ASEAN jurisdictions (experience with Indonesia, Vietnam, or Malaysia is highly valued). Technical Skills & Knowledge: Strong understanding of cross-border telecom regulations (IPLC, VPN, Internet) and the licensing frameworks within Southeast Asia. Familiarity with regional data privacy laws (PDPA, GDPR principles) and data sovereignty requirements. Knowledge of regional labor regulations and "General Affairs" management. Other Requirements: Business-level proficiency in English and Mandarin is required. This role necessitates frequent communication with Product and Engineering teams at our Regional Headquarters in China, as well as the review of technical contracts and legal policies in Mandarin. Willingness to travel within the ASEAN region (approx. 30-40%) as business needs require. Legally eligible to work in Singapore.

title: Markets Non-Financial Risk Oversight Lead
description: JOB SUMMARY The Markets Governance team supports the governance of the Markets and Financing Securities Services (FSS) businesses with respect to non-financial risks ensuring alignment with Bank Policy and Enterprise Risk Management Framework.  This will include chairing and / or supporting and / or setting standards for meetings that support country and central governance.  The role will also be responsible for reviewing and leading pan Markets existing risk and control framework, remediation activities for alignment. Roles & Responsibilities As such this role will: •    Support the holistic Markets/FSS governance framework, through understanding Market’s strategy, actions under the overall strategic plan, the flow of reporting information within Markets and understanding the delegations of authority / stakeholder landscape for the businesses. •    Lead pan Markets deliverables and initiatives, to align with group and CIB strategy. This is performed through review of existing framework, leading multiple Markets-wide initiatives, and being the coordinator for the Global FSS Non-Financial Risk Committee. •    Collaborate with stakeholders to drive the pan Markets agenda and its related Book of Work •    As directed by business, drive the right risk culture within Markets and /or FSS and support implementation of various related Initiatives. •    Own the global Markets Product Risk Management Process, driving change, efficiency and strong risk management discipline •    Manage communication of status updates to key stakeholders including Senior Management through presentations and reports. •    Provide robust oversight to support adherence to relevant internal and external policies across Markets and / or CIB Strategy •    Provides input and maintains consistency with Bank / CIB Conduct standards and policy. Business •    Good understanding of the Markets and FSS business, how Markets operate within Group, how it aligns with CIB strategy and the external market environment in which we operate including Regulators. •    Good understanding of Risk standards and Policy relevant to the Markets/FSS business and scope of the role. •    Awareness and understanding of the wider Markets/FSS business, in which the Group Operates. This role leads a team that provides core support to the business by providing risk management support. Processes •    Responsible for implementing processes to ensure continued adherence to Policies and Standards relevant to the Markets business. •    Responsible for improving efficiencies and timeliness of relevant processes. •    Provide guidance and analysis for FSS related matters across the Markets Process Universe and process owner support as required. People & Talent •    Responsible for continuously developing a team of strong risk managers. •    Responsible for leading through example and building the appropriate culture and values. •    Sets appropriate tone and expectations for the team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners. •    Responsible for driving training and communications to promote awareness and continuous learning for risk, control, and governance aspects. •    Responsible for ensuring the provision of ongoing training and development of people and ensure that holders of all critical functions are suitably skilled and qualified for their roles ensuring that they have effective supervision in place to mitigate any risks. Risk Management •    Oversee the non-financial risks which can impact the business. Ability to connect the themes and issues across products, functions and locations. •    Monitor control performance, behaviour, trends and internal/external events to proactively identify non-financial risks. •    Perform reviews and assessments of risk metrics (losses, near miss incidents, control effectiveness) to identify top themes and trends. •    Responsible for driving cross-business risk initiatives in support of Target Operating Model. •    Contributes to defining annual priorities for periodic review process as a form of First Line Assurance (i.e. “peer or targeted reviews”). •    Identify opportunities for rationalisation of controls and streamlining of risk administration processes, e.g. Risk Self Assessments (RCSA), (i.e. “control simplification”). •    Responsible for risk tooling and associated processes working in partnership with Group OR and vendors (e.g. Incident Management, KRI Dashboards, etc.) •    Oversee risk oversight documentation (top risk profiles, risk committee materials) and monitoring day-to-day risk treatment progress (residual risk assessments, risk treatment plans, management action plans). Governance •    Promote the development of risk-oriented culture within the organization. •    Accountable for the effective running of all Markets governance committees including FSS Non-Financial Risk Committees •    Drive standards and consistency in 1-down regional/country/function forums relevant to role and providing consistent escalations to 1-up forums/committees. •    Representative at required other unspecified Markets Governance forums and Committees as required to provide input relevant to role. •    Ensure Bank policies and standards are fit for purpose for FSS seeking changes and deviation where required. •    Create, set and evaluate governance standards, frameworks and processes to ensure they are fit for purpose to effectively manage Markets business risk. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Markets Business Heads •    Markets Group and Regional COO teams •    Markets Functional Heads •    Markets and Group CFCC •    Group OR •    Markets MT Other Responsibilities •    Live by the Here for Good promise and the brand values as stated for Group, Business and Country •    Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. •    Responsible for developing a strong working relationship across all Markets risk and risk ops teams and locations. •    Responsible for leveraging the opportunity provided by Corporate Social Responsibility to enhance the Group’s internal and external reputation and indirectly influence the bottom line. •    Responsible for promoting the Group’s brand and Here for good with employees, clients and regulators. •    Responsible for performing other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. •    Responsible for maintaining effective communication with key stakeholders, and staff. Our Ideal Candidate •    Strong risk management and controls background – Individual with more than 10+ years of experience in risk management and compliance. Degree education in related discipline is preferred. •    Significant experience in managing Operational, Compliance, Cyber or FCC risks in first or second line of defence. •    Significant experience in presenting and writing papers to senior management, up to and include Board Level committees. •    Working experience in supporting the Markets and Funds and Securities Services businesses •    Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment. •    Ability to articulate complex issues concisely and communicate effectively. •    Collaborative and creative problem solver, while being outcome focused. •    Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills •    Strong analytical skills with an ability to understand complex workflows and excellent attention to details. •    Comfortable working in a relatively unstructured and multi-cultural environment Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Markets Business and Products (FSS, Markets) •    Operational Risk Standards •    Governance and Supervision •    Coordination between business and functions •    Change Management •    Process Optimization •    People Management •    Data Analytics About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: •	Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do •	Never settle, continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well •	Are better together, we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter, we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. •	Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance, with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. •	Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. •	Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. •	Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits •	A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. •	Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation, one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

title: Global Employee Relations Operations Senior Manager
description: Global Employee Relations Operations Senior Manager Micron is seeking a forward‑thinking Senior Manager to lead global Employee Relations (ER) operations, governance, analytics, and technology enablement. Based in Singapore — supporting global time‑zone coverage and operational resilience — this role strengthens the global ER operating model through improved processes, programs, systems, compliance frameworks, and data‑driven insights that empower ER teams worldwide. This position works in close partnership with a peer based in North America to ensure seamless global coordination, shared stewardship of enterprise‑wide ER priorities, and balanced operational coverage around the clock. This role does not manage day‑to‑day individual ER cases. Instead, it designs, governs, and advances the global systems, policies, analytics, and technology capabilities that enable regional ER teams to operate consistently, effectively, and in alignment with business and compliance expectations. Key Responsibilities Oversee global ER operational performance, including visibility into caseloads, distribution patterns, trends, quality indicators, process compliance, efficiency measures, customer‑experience themes, and support for escalations. Lead global ER programs such as ER leader training, accommodations processes, surveys and exit interviews, proactive ER interventions, and the ongoing evolution of ER digital resource platforms (SharePoint and other knowledge repositories). Own and enhance global ER procedures — including the Investigations SOP, Consultations Playbook, workforce reduction and severance compliance workflows, Anti‑Harassment & Discrimination policy content, Team Member Handbook governance, and the global ER procedures repository — while strengthening ER governance through rigorous documentation management, version control, auditability, and scalable global operating standards. Drive ER‑owned audit activities (e.g. ER Quality & Compliance Audit, SOP Compliance Audit) and support multifunctional audit programs (e.g., Labor‑related components of the RBA Code, Internal Audits, local regulatory reviews, and sustainability‑linked governance requirements). Lead ER analytics and reporting, including dashboards, automated reporting mechanisms, predictive insights, trend analyses, and executive‑ready data storytelling. Oversee the global ER case management system, ensuring data integrity, workflow optimization, system enhancements, and alignment with evolving operational and compliance standards. Advance technology and AI adoption within ER, including workflow automation, AI‑assisted insights generation, digital platform enhancements, and updates to ER policies to address AI‑related risks such as fraud, malicious use, and data‑integrity concerns. Provide oversight of all ER projects globally — leading global ER initiatives and ensuring alignment, execution, and governance, while also tracking and maintaining visibility into local ER projects across regions. Support global business initiatives (e.g., organizational restructuring efforts) through ER‑related alignment, documentation updates, and change‑management contributions. Qualifications Required Minimum 10 years of experience in Employee Relations, HR Operations and Labor Compliance (all three) in the semiconductor manufacturing industry. Experience ( Demostrated success) in leading or supporting global or multi‑regional ER initiatives, including organizational restructures, with proven ability to build, manage, and enhance global ER policies, procedures, and compliance frameworks. Strong analytical capability, including dashboard development, data interpretation, and insights‑driven storytelling to drive proactive ER programs and interventions. Experience with HR technologies, specifically EthicsPoint case management system, Workday, SharePoint, PowerBI and other data analytics tools, and using AI effectively to drive quality and efficiency. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with ability and experience to influence across cultures, geographies, time zones, and functions. Preferred Experience with AI‑enabled tools, workflow automation, or digital transformation within HR/ER. Background in audit methodologies, compliance programs, or risk governance. Experience in global policy harmonization or scaling enterprise‑level operating model changes. Familiarity with cross‑functional audit programs such as RBA or sustainability‑related reporting.

title: Compliance Officer (Regulatory & Licensing)
description: 📖About Us 🔹We are a high-growth Web3 payment infrastructure company redefining how users and businesses interact with stablecoins and digital assets. 🔹Backed by licensed financial institutions, and trusted by leading Web3 partners, we provide crypto-fiat payment rails, card-issuing infrastructure, and CaaS (Cards-as-a-Service) solutions for exchanges, wallets, protocols, and global fintech innovators. 🔹Our team comes from top global financial institutions, tier-one tech companies, and leading fintechs across APAC and Europe — bringing deep expertise, a builder mindset, and a shared passion for shaping the future of compliant, scalable financial infrastructure. About the Role We are seeking a seasoned Compliance Officer (Regulatory & Licensing) to support the expansion of our payment services globally. You will be at the forefront of a global compliance team, conducting independent regulatory licensing research; diving into new areas, regions or jurisdictions; and collaborating with the internal team to develop solutions for obtaining and maintaining licenses in different jurisdictions. What You Will Do Research and analyze licensing requirements for DCS's expansion into international markets, such as Europe, Australia, Middle East, Japan and Latin America. Independently analyze the licensing process for new regions, ensuring compliance with local regulatory requirements. Assist in identifying expansion strategies, target opportunities and considering its impact to the business. Collaborate with legal, compliance, business and product teams to prepare documentations related to licensing in new jurisdictions. Work closely with external legal counsel, actively challenging and guiding them to develop the most effective and commercially viable regulatory arrangements that enable DCS’s business to go to market across different regions/jurisdictions. Manage regulatory submissions and respond to inquiries from regulatory agencies covering majority topics such as business proposals, compliance and AML/CFT matters, IT compliance, operational and outsourcing arrangements. Provide ongoing support for maintaining licenses in existing and new markets as regulations evolve. What You Bring Bachelor’s degree in Law, Banking, Finance, or related disciplines. Minimum 3 years+ of relevant experience in fintech, payments, regulatory compliance or licensing within the financial institution. Strong understanding of financial regulatory frameworks and licensing processes across multiple jurisdictions. Strong research skills with the ability to independently dive into new areas of licensing and regulatory requirements. Exceptional project management capabilities. Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with a focus on problem-solving. Detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment. Proficiency in English is a must

title: Senior Data Scientist
description: Who We Are Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact. To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive. We Are BCG X We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions. What You'll Do Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions. As a Data Scientist, you'll be part of our rapidly growing team. You'll have the chance to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across a variety of industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions. Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, scrappy, and communicative. We are looking for talented individuals with a passion for data science, statistics, operations research and transforming organizations into AI led innovative companies. Successful candidates possess the following: Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience) Significant experience applying advanced analytics to a variety of business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients Have strong project management skills What You'll Bring Master’s Degree or PhD with significant relevant experience providing advanced analytics solutions. The degree should be in computer science, applied mathematics, statistics, machine learning, or a related data centric field. Demonstrated deep technical and data science expertise, acute strategic and analytical skills, ability to lead and persuade, drive and energy, and desire to work in a project-based environment on strategic issues. Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner Strong record of professional accomplishment and leadership. Fluency in Python Additional info Ability to travel based on client and business needs.

title: Senior Executive Officer II, Customer Experience
description: ​We are looking for a passionate and professional Senior Executive Officer to join our Customer Experience team. Based in Singapore and reporting to the Senior Manager, the incumbent will oversee the day-to-day operations of the team's initiatives for customer segments, working along-side teams to integrate content and design customer engagement activities into cohesive initiatives aimed at increasing customer retention and market diversion from illegal operators. However, we also recognize that each individual has a unique blend of skills. Whether your strength is in customer segmentation or business analytics, we want to talk to you. What You'll Do You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather insights and data relevant to specific market segments. You will develop and maintain segment profiles and personas based on data-driven analysis and customer research. You will conduct in-depth analysis of each customer segment to understand their unique needs, preferences, and pain points. You will identify opportunities for personalisation and targeted communication initiatives within each segment. You will evaluate the effectiveness of segmentation strategies and recommend adjustments based on performance metrics and customer feedback. You will conduct competitive analysis to identify strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats within target segments. You will monitor illegal market activities and industry trends to anticipate changes in segment dynamics and market conditions. You will present findings and recommendations to key stakeholders to support strategic decision-making and resource allocation. You will stay abreast of emerging technologies and methodologies in segmentation analysis and incorporate best practices into existing processes. You will develop targeted marketing campaigns and content tailored to the specific needs and preferences of each customer segment. You will author and submit campaigns for regulatory approvals. You will leverage the CRM system, digital and remote channels, and retail touchpoints to deliver personalised experiences for customer engagement. You will conduct A/B testing and multivariate testing of automated campaign elements to optimise performance and conversion rates. You will monitor campaign performance, analyse results, and optimise future campaigns based on insight. You will collaborate with Customer teams to ensure campaign alignment and seamless customer experience. Who You Are You have a Degree in Marketing, Business Analytics, Computer Science, or related discipline. You have at least 3 years of experience in customer segmentation, data analysis, and/or marketing. You have a proven track record of using customer data and insights to drive marketing campaigns with results in improved customer engagement and retention. You possess hands-on experience with customer data platforms, analytics tools, and data visualisation techniques. Experience with Qlik Sense is an advantage. You have a strong understanding of customer behaviour, purchasing patterns, and market trends. You possess the ability to collaborate cross-functionally with Customer, Channels and Product teams to translate customer insights into actionable strategies. You possess excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders. You have creativity and innovation to develop engaging and personalised campaigns that resonate with target audiences. You have attention to detail and a commitment to delivering outcome driven campaigns. Advanced certifications in Analytics is preferred and experience in gaming products and services will be beneficial. Benefits Competitive salaries Flexi Benefits Staggered working hours Medical Insurance Corporate Mobile Plans Singapore Pools welcomes you not for how you look, where you come from, or differences you may have. We want you here for who you are. Diversity at the company helps us see a greater picture represented by different voices, helping us in contributing back to the society. So, feel free to express who you are, and be proud of your heritage and personal experience as you begin your journey with us! Singapore Pools was established by the Government on 23 May 1968 to provide safe and trusted betting to counter illegal gambling. As a not-for-profit organisation, all of Singapore Pools' surplus is channelled to Tote Board to fund a wide range of causes in social service, community development, sports, the arts, education and health. Currently, Singapore Pools contributes about $2 billion annually to the Government in the form of taxes and duties, and for the funding of good causes.

title: AI Finance / Accounting Trainer (Freelance/Part Time)
description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Design and develop AI training content, including both core/beginner and advanced levels. •	Prompt Engineering for Finance: Teach learners how to construct precise prompts to generate financial summaries, draft overdue payment emails, and interpret P&L variances using LLMs (Large Language Models). •	Excel & AI Integration: Guide learners on using AI features within spreadsheets (e.g., Copilot for Excel) to clean data and generate formulas automatically. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person)making complex AI tools accessible and relevant to learners. •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. Administrative Responsibilities: •	Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner. •	Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data. •	Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards. Learner Support and Engagement: •	Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve. •	Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment. •	Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students. Requirements •	ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required. •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. •	Prior experience teaching (preferably 3 years) or training adult learners in AI / digital technology. •	Professional certification (e.g., CA, ACCA, CPA) is a plus. •	At least 3–5 years of practical accounting experience. •	Strong presentation, facilitation, and interpersonal skills. •	Prior training or teaching experience is highly desirable. •	Ability to simplify complex financial concepts for diverse learners.

title: 25919494 Citi Private Bank, Know Your Customer Quality Assurance Ops Specialist
description: At Citi , we get to connect millions of people across hundreds of cities and countries every day.  And we've been doing it for more than 200 years.  We do this through our unparalleled global network.  We provide a broad range of financial services and products to our clients – whether they be consumers, corporations, governments or institutions – to help them meet their biggest opportunities and face the world's toughest challenges. Shape your Career with Citi Citi Private Bank (CPB) is dedicated to serving the world’s wealthiest individuals and their families.  From 52 locations across 20 countries, we offer our services to more than 14,000 ultra-high net worth clients from nearly 100 nations.  Our clients include entrepreneurs, business leaders, executives, their heirs and families, whom we offer customized services to help protect and responsibly grow their wealth.  Our unique approach focuses on fewer, larger and more sophisticated clients with an average net worth above $100 million. Job Description: The Citi Private Bank Client Know Your Customer Quality Assurance Operations Specialist is an operations subject matter expert in Know Your Customer (KYC), Anti-Money Laundering (AML), Account Opening, and Documentation Review. This role is responsible for supporting the client onboarding process for Citi Private Bank clients. The specialist will deal with problems independently and has the latitude to solve complex issues. The role requires integrating in-depth specialty area knowledge with a solid understanding of industry standards and practices. A good understanding of how the team and area integrate with others in accomplishing the objectives of the sub-function/job family is essential. This position applies analytical thinking and knowledge of data analysis tools and methodologies. It requires attention to detail when making judgments and recommendations based on the analysis of factual information. The role typically deals with variable issues with potentially broader business impact and applies professional judgment when interpreting data and results. The specialist must be able to break down information in a systematic and communicable manner. Developed communication and diplomacy skills are required to exchange potentially complex or sensitive information. This role has a moderate but direct impact through close contact with the businesses' core activities. The quality and timeliness of service provided will affect the effectiveness of their own team and other closely related teams. Job Responsibilities: Responsible for performing independent Client Onboarding KYC (Know Your Customer) due diligence functions for new and existing clients across various teams, including private bankers, operations, and compliance. Review client profiles and collaborate with the banking team to fulfill onboarding requirements, ensuring full compliance with the Bank’s AML/KYC standards, local laws, and regulations. Perform client name screening disposition, sanctions review, and Enhanced Due Diligence. Identify and highlight suspicious money laundering activities, deficiencies, and other non-compliance issues to management. Prepare client risk assessments and various MIS (Management Information System) reports for management, to facilitate relevant reviews and strengthen control processes. Conduct ongoing gap analysis on policies and standards against existing processes, identify improvement initiatives for streamlining the KYC and Onboarding process, and ensure guidelines/procedures are up to date. Coach junior team members and provide guidance in exception handling. Act as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) to senior stakeholders. Focus on the timely and accurate delivery of all responsible functions, as well as providing superior customer service and resolving customer issues, guided by service level agreements and efficiency/accuracy standards. Manage the day-to-day volume processing, including daily management of in-process, pending, and service-related activities, ensuring requirements are clearly defined to support all scenarios of KYC account opening and maintenance. Establish and maintain close working relationships with clients, stakeholders, and within the team to facilitate open and direct communication regarding issues, needs, queries, and system setups, ensuring these are responded to efficiently, promptly, and professionally.
