title: APAC Business Process Specialist
description: Company Overview Founded in Hong Kong in 2016 by the French couple Delphine Lefay and Diego Dultzin Lacoste, OnTheList is a leading premium flash sales concept in Asia and the Middle-East. OnTheList works to minimize the problem of excess stock by turning past-season inventory into sustainable opportunities. OnTheList works directly with retail brands and official distributors, turning old inventory into opportunity by providing a sustainable way for brands to sell past-season items that would have otherwise ended up in landfills, while providing the best deals and steep discounts to our OnTheList members. Since launching in Hong Kong in 2016, OnTheList has hosted over 5,200 flash sales and has built a community of over 1.3 million members across all regions. We’ve since expanded to Singapore, Australia, Malaysia, Korea, Shanghai, Beijing, Thailand, and Dubai. Sustainability is rooted in who we are and motivates us to continue our mission. To date, we have saved over 8.2 million items from ending up in landfills. Forming OnTheLove, our CSR initiative, has allowed us to deepen our commitment to minimizing our carbon footprint while working with local organizations and hosting regular charity sales. At OnTheList, our values guide everything we do: Family Spirit is driven by humility, fostering genuine connections with our community. Panache reflects our passion for delivering exceptional experiences, while sustainability is rooted in our commitment to creating a positive impact on the world around us. Excellence is driven by the cooperation of our teams. Job Summary Under the direction of the founder of OnTheList, this role streamlines sourcing and purchasing processes across geographies, builds and monitors operational reporting and tools, and identifies productivity improvement opportunities among internal stakeholders. Responsibilities: - Analyze existing sourcing and purchasing processes across markets to identify dysfunctions and disparities, enabling targeted optimization Develop and present actionable recommendations to align management teams on process improvements Design updated operational processes using video and written materials, and deliver training to teams across APAC and the Middle-East to ensure consistent adoption Lead continuous improvement initiatives by researching and integrating best practices internally and externally Coordinate project deliverables and ensure quality standards are met throughout implementation Evaluate current reporting tools, templates, and forms against target processes to identify enhancement opportunities Prepare and coordinate operational reporting and KPIs, and conduct specific analyses upon request to support data-driven decision-making Train teams on new tools and developments, and monitor their effective utilization to maximize productivity Collaborate with relevant teams to write detailed functional specifications and operational procedures for data management systems Optimize data filing, sharing, and team collaboration processes to improve productivity and cross-functional communication Develop and update collection planning based on regional and process-specific requirements, aligning all stakeholders to ensure cohesive execution Monitor adherence to collection plans, identify bottlenecks, and recommend corrective actions to maintain operational efficiency

title: Lead Cybersecurity Specialist (Security Operations)
description:
​ Our Client is an established company in Singapore, who is seeking to recruit a Lead Cybersecurity Specialist (Security Operations). Lead Cybersecurity Specialist (Security Operations) You will be the primary
architect of the security governance and risk management framework., with the mission
to transform GRC from a compliance-heavy exercise into a strategic enabler. You
will ensure that risk management is deeply integrated into the lifecycle of
every digital system, from web applications to critical Operational Technology
(OT) environments. 1. Incident Management &
Response Standardisation ·       Unified Playbooks: Establish and maintain Incident Response
(IR) playbooks for diverse threat scenarios (e.g., Ransomware, Data
Exfiltration, Cloud breaches). ·       Crisis Leadership: Provide direct guidance and technical
oversight to agencies during High and Critical severity incidents, ensuring
timely reporting and effective containment. ·       Incident Governance: Work with CIOs and CISOs to establish
clear command structures and roles, empowering leaders to make difficult,
high-stakes decisions during a crisis. 2. Operational Readiness
& Resiliency Testing ·       Advanced Exercises: Design and oversee high-quality Tabletop
Exercises (TTX) for various stakeholders (system owners, SIROs, CISOs, CIOs).
You will evaluate external vendors to ensure these exercises are realistic,
comprehensive, and push the limits. ·       Chaos Testing: Drive the adoption of chaos testing across
agencies to validate the adequacy of resiliency plans and identify hidden
failure points in critical systems. ·       Capability Building: Continuously assess the operational
readiness of the Family and lead initiatives to bridge identified gaps in
incident management. 3. Continuous Monitoring
& Asset Governance ·       Centralised Monitoring: Ensure all systems are effectively
onboarded to central monitoring services. Work with system owners on overcoming
challenges encountered during onboarding. ·       Asset Visibility: Partner with CIOs to maintain a robust and
updated IT asset inventory, ensuring that "you cannot protect what you do
not know." ·       Custom Threat Scenarios: Provide expert guidance for agencies
with unique threat use cases or specialised systems (e.g., OT/ICS) that fall
outside standard monitoring coverage, helping them build bespoke detection
capabilities. 4. Vulnerability &
Attack Surface Management ·       Full-Spectrum SOPs: Establish Standard Operating Procedures
for vulnerability management across on-premises, cloud (GCC), and OT
environments. Ensure that there are proper procedures for managing unpatched
vulnerabilities. ·       Attack Surface Scanning: Ensure agencies deploy adequate
internal and external scanning tools. You will oversee the workflow for finding
prioritisation and validate that patches are applied and effective. 5. Advocacy & Education ·       Resilience Culture: Educate stakeholders on the critical
importance of Response and Business Continuity Planning (BCP). ·       Stakeholder Inculcation: Foster a mindset of "assumed
breach," ensuring project owners and leaders understand their roles in
threat monitoring and incident management. Requirements Experience ·       Years of Experience: 8 to 10 years of deep experience in
Cybersecurity Operations, SOC Management, or Incident Response. ·       Crisis Management: Proven track record of leading or
providing technical oversight in high-pressure, high-severity security
incidents. ·       Domain Expertise: Experience managing security operations
across complex hybrid environments (On-premise, Cloud, and OT). Technical Skills ·       Incident Response & Forensics: Mastery of IR
methodologies and a strong understanding of digital forensics and malware
analysis. ·       Threat Intelligence & TTPs: Deep knowledge of the threat
landscape and the ability to map monitoring use cases to the MITRE ATT&CK
framework. ·       Vulnerability & Exploitation Research: Deep understanding
of the CVE (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) system and CVSS scoring.
Knowledge of exploitation techniques and the mechanics of how vulnerabilities
are weaponised by threat actors. Ability to assess the
"exploitability" of a vulnerability within the specific context of
the environment to prioritise remediation. ·       Detection Technologies: Proficiency in SIEM, SOAR, XDR, and
EDR technologies. Ability to evaluate the relevancy of existing monitoring
tools against evolving threats. ·       Cloud Operations: Strong understanding of monitoring and
responding to incidents within Government Commercial Cloud (GCC) and native
cloud environments. ·       Certifications: Professional certifications such as GCIH
(GIAC Certified Incident Handler), GCFA (GIAC Certified Forensic Analyst),
CHFI, or CISSP are highly desirable. Soft Skills ·       Command & Control: Ability to remain calm and provide
clear, authoritative guidance during high-stakes security crises. ·       Diplomacy & Education: Skill in translating operational
needs into strategic priorities for CIOs and CISOs. ·       Strategic Foresight: A strong interest in emerging security
technologies and the ability to proactively adapt monitoring strategies to
counter new actor TTPs. JJ Consulting Services EA Licence No.: 12C6207 Applicants are invited to send in a MS Word resume to jobs@jjconsulting.com.sg stating
position applying for/present/expected salaries and earliest available date. We thank all applicants in advance and regret that only short listed candidates
will be notified.

title: Research Fellow / Engineer (Food Engineering) - AZN5
description: As a University of Applied Learning, SIT works closely with industry in our research pursuits. Our research staff will have the opportunity to be equipped with applied research skill sets that are relevant to industry demands while working on research projects in SIT. The primary purpose of this role is to deliver on a collaborative research project, Proteins for Singapore (P4SG), where you will be part of the research team to establish and understand the structuration of algae/soybean/mung bean proteins for high quality alternative protein foods including through high moisture extrusion. Key responsibilities will include: Explore innovative methods for food process optimization including the use of AI and machine-learning Develop and execute methods for characterizing and linking the texture and structure of alternative protein foods Design, execute, and interpret experiments independently. Participate in and manage the research project with Principal Investigators (PI), and the research team members to ensure all project deliverables including periodic progress reports are met. Author papers in high-quality journals and contribute to grant writing in areas that are related to the research. Carry out Risk Assessment, and ensure compliance with Work, Safety and Health Regulations. Coordinate procurement and liaison with vendors/suppliers. Work independently, as well as manage a team of junior researchers and students to ensure proper operation and maintenance of equipment. To communicate and liaise with any internal and external stakeholders to ensure project deliverables are met. Any other ad-hoc duties assigned by supervisor. Ideal qualities of the candidate include: Able to build and maintain strong working relationships with people within and external to the university. Self-directed learner who believes in continuous learning and development Highly proficient in technical writing and presentation Strong analytical and critical thinking skills Strong initiative and takes ownership of work The candidate should have relevant competence in the areas of processing technology, physical and structural analysis, material formulation, and mathmatical modeling. The candidate should also have a PhD or other advanced degree in a food or engineering related field. Knowledge of food formulation, sensory analysis, extrusion technology, machine learning, and rheology will be advantageous.

title: Executive Assistant
description: The Future of Advisory Starts Here At APCO, we create un/common ground where business, policy, and society converge to shape the future. The traditional consultancy model is evolving at an unprecedented pace—leaders need more than insights. They need integrated advisory to navigate complexity, protect their license to operate, drive growth, and make a lasting impact. Our Singapore hub sits at the center of this transformation, helping organizations adapt to geopolitical shifts, the integration of AI in consulting, and rapidly changing market forces. We are looking for an Executive Business Partner to the Global Senior Director & Market Leader—a structured, highly organized, and execution-focused professional who can manage internal coordination, track key priorities, and ensure seamless execution across multiple business functions. This is not just an executive support role. It is a high-impact, operational role that requires proactive problem-solving, strong internal diplomacy, and the ability to bring structure to fast-moving priorities. The ideal candidate is someone who thrives in high-intensity environments, excels at managing workflows, and serves as the central knowledge hub for a high-performance leadership team. If you want a front-row seat to strategic decision-making, exposure to high-level business challenges, and the opportunity to work at the center of a global advisory firm, this role is for you. What We’re Looking For A high-agency driver who ensures that priorities, workflows, and stakeholder engagement are managed effectively. Execution-driven – Keeps workflows moving and ensures nothing falls through the cracks. Meticulously organized – Manages schedules, key priorities, and follow-ups with precision. Strategic communicator & internal diplomat – Manages relationships across teams and functions. Discreet and professional – Handles sensitive matters with judgment and confidentiality. Problem-solver – Anticipates challenges, fixes inefficiencies, and ensures follow-through. A knowledge hub – Compiles, organizes, and maintains critical information, ensuring accessibility. Key Responsibilities 1. Leadership Support & Special Projects Support the Senior Director in managing and prioritizing strategic initiatives and high-priority projects. Draft meeting agendas and prepare materials for key meetings. Assist in business development efforts, including tracking opportunities and preparing presentation slide drafts. 2. Crisis & Issue Management Act as an early warning system by flagging potential risks and operational bottlenecks. Coordinate responses to urgent issues, ensuring timely and effective resolution. Maintain discretion and manage confidential matters with utmost professionalism. 3. Process Optimization & Efficiency Identify inefficiencies in workflows and propose process improvements. Develop and implement systems to enhance productivity and execution speed. Ensure that best practices are documented and institutionalized within the team. 4. Operational & Knowledge Management Maintain key documents, reports, and background materials, ensuring easy access to critical information. Manage internal workflows related to facilities, office operations, and vendor coordination. Track meeting outcomes, deadlines, and follow-ups, ensuring strategic priorities stay on track. Serve as the central knowledge hub, organizing and structuring information for leadership. 5. Internal Coordination & Stakeholder Management Act as the internal point of contact, ensuring smooth communication across teams. Track key priorities and ensure stakeholders are updated and aligned. Facilitate coordination across business functions, ensuring execution on internal and external initiatives. Serve as an internal diplomat, managing relationships and ensuring collaboration across leadership teams. 6. Executive Operations & Scheduling Own and manage the Global Senior Director’s calendar—optimizing time and balancing priorities. Coordinate complex scheduling, travel, and stakeholder meetings with efficiency. Filter and prioritize internal and external engagements, ensuring effective time management. Ensure the Senior Director is always briefed, prepared, and focused on the right priorities. What You Will Gain This is a fast-moving, high-exposure role where you will: Develop elite operational and execution skills—learning to manage high-level workflows. Gain firsthand exposure to business strategy, global trends, and leadership decision-making. Build internal influence and stakeholder management expertise—becoming a trusted go-to person. Operate at the core of a high-performance advisory firm, gaining insight into how integrated consulting functions. This is a career-accelerating role for someone who thrives in structured, high-intensity environments and wants to grow into senior operations, business strategy, or chief of staff roles. Qualifications & Experience Minimum of 5 years’ experience in executive support, operations, or internal coordination. Strong stakeholder engagement and process improvement skills. Excellent written and verbal communication—able to manage priorities and align teams. Experience in consulting, corporate operations, or professional services is a plus. Strong understanding of business operations from a finance perspective is an advantage. Proficiency in Microsoft Office, Google Suite, and task management tools. Careers at APCO Through our recruitment, hiring and retention efforts, APCO is committed to building teams to bring the best possible expertise and robust perspectives to our clients. We embrace differences in experience, viewpoint and ideas, starting with our values and extending to our behaviors, policies and practices. We aim to create a positive work experience and welcoming environment where everyone feels they belong and can thrive.

Processes ·    Support efforts to establish and maintain effective processes to ensure that policies and standards to address CFCR risks are effectively implemented across CIB Client Coverage. Provide second line oversight and challenge to ensure the continued effectiveness of CIB Client Coverage owned processes. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. title: Value Advisor - Adobe Digital Experience (JAPAC)
description: Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! Imagination for Everyone Adobe is a place where outstanding people work. Our employees recently voted us onto the Great Place to Work list for the 10th year in a row! We’re proud to offer market-leading employee benefits developed with your lifestyle in mind. Highlights include health insurance, global days off, a wellness fund, market-leading parental leave, access to our Employee Stock Purchase Program, and programs that support ongoing career growth. We proudly empower our employees to get involved with corporate social responsibility. We offer a broad range of chances to create a bigger impact through philanthropy, employee, and community engagement. We understand that people set us apart in our business, and that's why we want to meet you. The Opportunity You’re the person who turns Adobe’s platform into business outcomes customers can see, measure, and act on. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, data, and executive influence. Your mandate: help our most strategic Digital Experience customers in the JAPAC region quantify value, accelerate time-to-impact, and build solid reasons that drive expansion, retention, and advocacy. Partnering with Customers and Adobe teams (e.g., Account teams, Ultimate Success, Professional Services), you will anchor Adobe’s work in hard numbers and strategic narratives — not fluffy marketing claims. This role will become an expert in how Adobe’s products deliver measurable business outcomes, develop scalable value tools, and generate thought leadership to encourage customer growth and innovation. What you’ll do Value Discovery & Framing Lead structured discovery with senior customer collaborators (CMOs, CDOs, CIOs, CFOs) to identify growth, efficiency, and experience opportunities tied to Adobe’s DX portfolio. Conduct Value Measurement Workshops to align key customer collaborators on priority use cases and the measurement framework/important metrics needed for Value Realisation. Build compelling, data-driven value hypotheses anchored in industry benchmarks, customer data, and Adobe telemetry. Impact Development Collaborate with customers to showcase return on investment, cost savings, efficiency improvements, and revenue impact directly tied back to Adobe technology investments. Translate technical capabilities into financial outcomes using clear economic models, Adobe tools, and benchmarks. Work together with Adobe Teams such as Sales and Customer Success to assist with renewals, expansion, and executive alignment discussions. Value Realisation & Measurement Propel progress within Adobe's Value Realisation Acceleration (VRA) framework by addressing challenges and tracking value results across consumption, use cases, and N$R. Attend frequent value meetings with Adobe teams to grasp the context more clearly and assist them in advancing value more rapidly. Attend customer meetings e.g., SBR or equivalent experience with executives to help the Adobe teams demonstrate realised return on investment and identify new value pathways. Help promote adoption of Adobe’s value methodology throughout the ecosystem and JAPAC customers. What you need to succeed: 5–10+ years in value engineering, management consulting, transformation, strategy, finance, or similar roles. Champion of value methodology. Proven success building arguments for enterprise technology, digital marketing or transformation programs. Strong financial modelling capability. Ability to translate complexity into crisp, executive-ready insights. Comfortable engaging with senior executives at large enterprises across JAPAC. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Demonstrated ability to influence with authority across cross-functional teams. Familiarity with Adobe Experience Cloud or related marketing/commerce/customer experience technologies to drive personalization and optimization programs (ideal, but not required). Past experience in program management, scaled agile delivery or similar as a squad member; Scrum master experience beneficial.

title: Technical Manager, EV Charger Development

Are you excited by the challenge of building critical infrastructure that supports the nation’s transition to electric vehicles? EV-Electric (EVe) Charging Pte Ltd is the organisation behind Singapore’s public EV charging network. Wholly owned and fully backed by the Land Transport Authority (LTA) , we are leading the nationwide deployment of EV chargers and the electrical infrastructure upgrades that make cleaner transportation possible. As demand for EVs accelerates, we’re looking for passionate, detail-driven engineers to join us and help shape the future of sustainable mobility. About the Role As Technical Manager, Charger Development , you will play a key role in the design, deployment, and commissioning of EV chargers across public car parks island-wide. You will work closely with internal teams, contractors, and government agencies to ensure projects are delivered safely, efficiently, and to the highest standards. This is a hands-on role for someone who enjoys seeing projects move from concept to reality—and knowing their work benefits commuters. schedule and to specification Support electrical and civil-related site matters during infrastructure upgrades Tender & Contract Management Develop tender specifications, technical requirements, and supporting documentation Ensure deployment works comply with contract requirements, relevant codes of practice, and regulatory standards Testing, Commissioning & Compliance Manage and support testing and commissioning of EV Charging Points (EVCPs) Coordinate with relevant agencies on approvals and inspections Ensure all testing, commissioning, and handover documentation is properly completed and maintained Team & Project Support Support operational and administrative functions within the team Lead or support ad-hoc projects and continuous improvement initiatives What you are expected to demonstrate in this Role Business Awareness & Execution – Understand industry trends, align work with organizational goals, and suggest practical improvements Innovation & Problem Solving – Propose creative ideas, test new approaches, and refine solutions Evidence-Based Decision Making – Analyze data, identify patterns, and make sound technical recommendations Collaboration & Teamwork – Work respectfully with colleagues, share knowledge, and contribute to team success Stakeholder Engagement – Communicate effectively with contractors, agencies, and partners to build trust and alignment Adaptability & Growth Mindset – Embrace change, learn from challenges, and continuously develop your skills What We’re Looking For Qualifications & Experience Tertiary qualification in an Engineering discipline At least 2 years of relevant working experience in engineering, project management, or site supervision Candidates with less experience will also be considered if they demonstrate strong organisational skills, teamwork, and leadership potential Skills & Attributes Experience in project coordination, site supervision , and administrative support Strong interpersonal and communication skills to work with diverse stakeholders High attention to detail and strong documentation discipline Proficient in Microsoft Office applications A proactive, responsible, and solutions-oriented mindset Why Join EVe? Work on nation-critical infrastructure with visible impact across Singapore Be part of the EV transformation journey in a fast-growing sector Enjoy the stability and purpose of an LTA-backed organisation Collaborate with passionate professionals committed to sustainability and innovation If you’re ready to apply your engineering skills to something that truly matters, we’d love to hear from you. Join us and help power Singapore’s electric future. ⚡🚗

title: Actuarial (Financial Services) Senior Consultant, Risk Consulting
description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. The Opportunity As part of our life actuarial team, you’ll focus on high-impact opportunities to apply your knowledge and experience that will shape our services. You’ll build valuable relationships with clients and develop strong capabilities, through both formal training and working with senior mentors and talented colleagues. You will be at the forefront of issues that are shaping the insurance industry in Asia and gain a wide exposure to a diverse range of actuarial projects. Your Key Responsibilities As a Senior Consultant in the Actuarial team, you will be assisting and leading the delivery of a wide range of client engagements, leading or working as part of a project team, relating to IFRS 17, Appointed Actuary work, actuarial valuation, pricing and product development, actuarial modelling, Merger & Acquisitions (M&A) and due diligence. You will also be performing evaluation in areas related to financial risk, product distribution and capital issues. You will also work with advanced financial modeling approaches and systems, e.g. designing valuation and Asset Liability Management (ALM) frameworks. Skills and Attributes for Success Good understanding of economic or market issues and the ability to interpret their impact on clients Possess strong interpersonal and teaming skills Has strong analytical and creative skills Leverage technology to continually learn, improve service delivery and maintain our leading-edge best practices To Qualify for the role, you must have Min 3 years of experience in the life actuarial team or an actuarial consulting firm Newly or Nearly qualified with either the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries (UK), the Society of Actuaries (US) or the Institute of Actuaries of Australia Good knowledge of Prophet software Good written and verbal communication skills Ideally, you’ll also have Depth of experience in at least one of the following areas: Financial modeling Financial and regulatory reporting Product and distribution strategy ERM / Solvency II M&A due diligence What we look for Highly motivated individuals with excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to prioritize shifting workloads in a rapidly changing industry. An effective communicator, you’ll be a confident team player that collaborates with people from various teams while looking to develop your career in a dynamic organization. What working at EY offers We offer a competitive compensation package where you’ll be rewarded based on your performance and recognized for the value you bring to our business. We also offer you: Support, coaching and feedback from some of the most engaging colleagues around Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.

title: Supervisor (FOH)
description:
Elephant Grounds is an award-winning Hong Kong-born lifestyle brand renowned for its specialty coffee, Japanese influenced cuisine, and café culture. Founded in 2013 by Gerald Li and Kevin Poon, the brand began as a coffee kiosk within a retail shop and has since grown into a fully integrated coffee roastery, artisanal bakery, and restaurant concept with operations in Hong Kong, Manila, Jakarta Q1 2026, and now Singapore. Our coffee program is led by an award-winning team - including a champion roaster
and other specialty coffee professionals - with a relentless dedication to building a sustainable coffee program. Elephant Grounds focuses on the alchemy of space - design, music, and light - and the impact they have on guests, creating an unmistakable energy and vibe. We are a growing hospitality group preparing to launch the first local outlet of a renowned international café concept known for its specialty coffee, seasonal menus, and modern ambiance.  This launch marks a strategic expansion of our diverse F&B portfolio, we aim to deliver quality, consistency, and innovation at every customer touchpoint. We are looking for passionate individuals to join us as Supervisor.  If you’re driven by dedication and a love for exceptional culinary experiences, come be part of our team and help shape this extraordinary journey at Elephant Grounds. Job Responsibilities 1) Service Excellence & Brand Culture ·    Greet and assist customers with warmth and professionalism, ensuring a welcoming
atmosphere and providing timely service. ·    Present menus, make recommendations, take orders, and serve food and beverages, ensuring a high-quality dining experience. ·    Address customer concerns and complaints with a positive attitude, ensuring guest
satisfaction. ·    Lead the FOH team to promote organic, heartfelt hospitality in line with Elephant
Grounds’ core values. ·    Maintain uniform standards and FOH grooming – proper attire and appearance must be upheld at all times to present a professional and consistent brand image. ·    Assist with food preparation and multitask effectively during busy periods. 2) Roster Management (FOH & BOH) ·    Oversee weekly and monthly planning of staff rosters across Front of House and Back of House. · Submit weekly roster and timesheets for all casual and permanent part-timers to HR. · Submit month-end documentation to HR, including: - Final amended roster. - Shift allowance report for eligible staff. - Timesheets for all part-time employees. - Scanned and properly archived records. - Inventory Tracking & Controls. - Conduct monthly kitchen inventory, documented via Google Sheets for traceability    and ease of access. - Perform weekly bar inventory reconciliations against POS sales data and update stock sheets accordingly. - Oversee bar month-end inventory, also tracked via Google Sheets. 3) Operational Maintenance & Supplies ·    Establish and maintain a comprehensive checklist for daily, weekly, fortnightly, and
monthly maintenance audits across the restaurant. ·    Ensure all supply storage cabinets are regularly reviewed, cleaned, and restocked to
par levels.  Supplies include: - Cutlery, sleeves, takeaway wares, napkins. - Stationeries, gloves, masks, cleaning tools. 4) POS & Delivery Merchant Oversight ·    Regularly review and clean up the POS system to ensure all items are accurate and
current. ·    Ensure product images are uploaded for every POS item (applicable to kiosk and QR
online ordering). ·    Standardize item listings and pricing across all delivery platforms, ensuring consistency and accuracy. 5)    Any other duties as and when assigned by the management. Job Requirement ·    Strong team player with a customer service-oriented mindset. ·    Ability to lead a small team and maintain composure during peak hours. ·    Food Safety Hygiene Certification required. ·    Ability to work flexible hours/shift, including weekends and public holidays. Must Have Factor ·    Team player with a proactive, hands-on approach. ·    Calm under pressure and reliable in fast-paced environment. ·    Eager to learn and grow with a dynamic culinary team. Thank you for your interest in this position. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Understand the IT management policy, quality management policy and security guidelines to ensure the development processes, procedures and system are designed to comply with these policies and guidelines. Manage and resolve day-to-day IT related issues pertaining to application, infrastructure or security, to facilitate smooth running of daily operations. Knowledge and Skills Tertiary education in Computer/Computer Science or Electronics Engineering or Information Technology or equivalent. Project Management Certification or equivalent. Hands-on experience in at least 3 full project development life cycles or project maintenance is preferred for the experienced ones. Ability to work both independently and as part of a team with professionals at all levels. Strong analytical, conceptualisation and problem-solving skills and able to prioritise and manage multiple tasks. Good design, coding, testing and debugging skills. Relevant experience in requirement elicitation techniques such as design thinking, persona analysis and customer journey mapping etc. Relevant experience in facilitating user requirement workshops. Relevant experience in web-based development and mobile IT projects. Results and customer oriented. Experience in Commercial Cloud AWS with focus on the following key area ECS Fargate AWS Lambda AWS RDS AWS QuickSight AWS EC2 AWS Cloudwatch AWS S3 Experience in DevOps/AGILE environment Agile Certification like Certified Scrum Master is preferred. Working experience in government environment will be advantageous. Familiarity with governance, adoption of digital services in enterprise IT environments. Work experience and strong troubleshooting skills in resolving application, infrastructure (including desktop, server, network) and security related issues in a (Singapore) government environment is preferred. Ability to take a broad, strategic perspective as well as drill deep to understand business needs and challenges. Team player with strong organization and people handling skills. Excellent communication skills, both oral and written, with the ability to pitch ideas and influence stakeholders.

title: Associate Director
description: Ethos BeathChapman (EBC) is a global group of executive talent and recruitment experts in Australia, Asia and EMEA, with a broader network beyond. We provide solutions across key recruitment markets including Financial Services, Fintech & Technology, Finance & Corporate Functions, Legal, Commercial and Life Sciences - our vision is to connect the world’s exceptional companies to exceptional people. At the heart of EBC is how we deliver upon that vision via our progressive behaviours and forward-thinking way of performing. We look to break tradition and truly embrace modern technology, data and analytics as well as best in class enablement support to deliver market leading executive recruitment solutions, tailored for our clients and candidates. We are living in a changing world – and here at EBC we strive to approach it in a changing way Overview: As an Associate Director, you will be in an exceptional role unlike elsewhere: one which truly aims and is designed to take already very strong performers to the next level of exceptional performance. Embark on your strategic journey to build, grow or manage a team and/or hold a significant role in the highly adept market management of your remit for EBC. Work closely alongside extremely talented and experienced business leaders in EBC, as well as hold a key gateway and relationship with our sister companies like Chapman Consulting Group (CCG) and international affiliations/lead networks. You will hold responsibility to tailor outcomes and shape strategy for yourself, team and EBC as a business with your regional clients. Use your passion for innovation to work intently with internal stakeholders and our Enablement function (talent engagement, marketing, data, research, client directors, training and development) that sets EBC uniquely apart to take already strong performers to exceptional performance levels whilst plunging yourself into our progressive ways of working, mastering our AI technology and state-of-the-art cloud-based CRM platform. At Associate Director level, EBC engages with you increasingly about rounding your ability to make big impact at macro levels and top performing attributes – whether leadership abilities, account management, proposing innovative solutions to clients (across retained, SoW, projects, contracting), use of internal technology or revenue performance. You will be groomed and challenged towards full Directorship and be expected to make contributions a little more broadly around the business, helping impact EBC at large. Experience and Qualifications: We are looking for an experienced, successful and entrepreneurial-minded executive recruiter who is well-regarded for the deep knowledge of your specialised recruitment domain, outstanding work ethics, collaborative nature and exceptional client delivery. You demonstrate emerging leadership qualities and superior fee generation abilities, speak knowledgably and passionately about your specialised recruitment market and most importantly, the opportunities and key current day levers therein. The ideal candidate should have at least 6 to 10 years of recruitment experience in a fast-paced recruitment environment. Candidates with prior experience from recruitment agencies, in-house or RPO teams will be preferred. Our Culture: A career with EBC revolves on several key principles. Firstly, joining a mature, high performing group of experts requires people with a strong strategic, highly focussed and collaborative approach both internally and across all clients and candidates. Secondly, our commitment to new ways of working and enablement, data-driven decision-making and AI technology is not just held by upper management but is lived and breathed throughout the organisation. We aim for all new joiners to hold good passion and commitment to innovate, share new ideas and work in forward thinking ways like these. Lastly, and perhaps most importantly, we are a boutique who values internal relationships as much as external. Being persistent in delivering a world-class service and interactions across colleagues, clients and candidates alike is paramount. Throughout your journey, we are committed to your success. On hand you will always have the high powered and finessed support – not just from the experienced mentors on your team at your doorstep, but also the supporting technology, our lead network and our best-in class enablement support function. If you aspire to be exceptional, reach out to us with your resume at hello@ethosbc.com This is what your career at EBC could look like: www.ethosbc.com/careers About the Company: Established 2005 in Sydney, Ethos BeathChapman’s mid-sized agility and solutions-focused approach enables us to flex where traditional recruitment agencies have failed, and scale beyond the reach of small boutiques with executive recruitment solutions that address the client’s specific needs. In Asia, our multidisciplinary expertise covers specialised functions across Banking & Financial Services, Fintech, Technology, Legal & Compliance, Industrial, Consumer & Healthcare recruitment markets, among others. Part of Will , EBC has five offices worldwide in Australia, Singapore, Hong Kong, Netherlands and the UK. We own Quay Appointments in Australia. www.ethosbc.com | www.linkedin.com/company/ethos-corporation| #BeExceptional BeathChapman Pte Ltd EA Licence: 16S8112

title: Trade Finance Executive
description: Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition Headquartered in Singapore, Agrocorp International is a leading integrated agricultural commodity and food solutions provider with presence in 16 countries and 25 offices. For over 30 years, Agrocorp has played a distinct role in nourishing the changing world with our agri-food supplies and solutions. To better serve our vision of nourishing a changing world, we’ve grown in size and business verticals from farm partnerships to processing plants and to retail market distribution. Our product range has widened to include a wide variety of commodities & food ingredients. In retail space, Sansar foods offers a wide range of premium pulses and HerbYvore is our plant based food range which includes non diary cheese, eggless egg, vegan ice cream etc. Job Summary The Trade Finance Executive is responsible for managing and processing trade finance transactions related to import and export operations. The role ensures compliance with international trade regulations, bank policies, and documentary credit rules while providing efficient support to clients and internal stakeholders. Responsibilities Manage end-to-end processing of trade finance documents including issuance, advising, amendment, and settlement of Letters of Credit (LCs) Execute Documentary Collections for import and export transactions, ensuring timely payment to suppliers Process Bank Guarantees and Standby Letters of Credit accurately and within required timelines Track and monitor LC issuances, confirmations, discounting, expiry dates, and negotiate related terms to mitigate risks Apply for loans under trade transactions and follow up to secure necessary approvals and disbursements Monitor trade transactions for due dates and discrepancies, proactively initiating follow-ups to resolve issues Provide internal guidance and support on trade finance transactions to resolve operational challenges and ensure smooth processing Coordinate effectively with banks, clients, shipping companies, and internal departments to facilitate seamless trade operations Maintain and update accurate records and Management Information System (MIS) reports for daily reporting to management Support back-office functions by performing timely data entry and assisting team members during absences Collaborate closely with Trade Finance team members and cross-functional teams to deliver unified support and achieve operational goals Skills & Competencies Demonstrate strong knowledge of trade finance products and apply ICC rules (UCP 600, URC 522, ISP98) to ensure compliance and accuracy Apply excellent attention to detail and analytical skills to identify and resolve discrepancies in trade documents Communicate clearly and coordinate efficiently with multiple stakeholders to drive transaction completion Manage multiple trade finance transactions under tight deadlines while maintaining accuracy and compliance Utilize MS Office and trade finance systems proficiently to support transaction processing and reporting Maintain a compliance-oriented mindset to uphold regulatory and bank policy standards

title: Senior Medical Social Worker
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION About NTUC Health Co-operative Limited NTUC Health Co-operative Limited (NTUC Health) is an NTUC enterprise that provides a comprehensive and integrated suite of quality and accessible health and eldercare services to meet the growing needs of families and their dependents. Building on close to three decades of experience and expertise, NTUC Health is among the largest providers of active ageing, senior day care, nursing home, and home personal care services in Singapore. We also serve vulnerable seniors in selected areas in Singapore through Community Case Management Services (CCMS), CREST mental health support, and a sheltered senior group home. In addition, we run a family medicine clinic. Our purpose is to achieve ‘health for life'; enabling healthy and fulfilling years by being a trusted partner of seniors and their families in our community. For more information, please visit ntuchealth.sg or follow us on Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn. Services: Senior Day Care | Home Care | Nursing Home | Active Ageing and Senior Fitness | Rehabilitation and Physiotherapy | Family Medicine Community Support: CCMS (Community Case Management Services) | CREST (Community Resource, Engagement and Support Team) | Henderson Home RESPONSIBILITIES The Nursing Home is committed to a Person-Centred Care model, aiming to transform care environments to promote genuine human connection and resident empowerment for our seniors. The Senior Medical Social Workers are integral to this model, providing psychosocial and financial assessments, casework, and interventions. They also collaborate with the multi-disciplinary team of stakeholders to review individual residents' medical, nursing, therapy, and social aspects as part of delivering person-centric care.  This support is crucial throughout the residents' journey, from their admission to their entire stay in the Home. In addition, Senior Medical Social Workers provide supervision and mentorship for the Medical Social Workers, executives and interns in the department. They also lead projects and initiatives within the Home and across the Residential Care Division to improve the care of the residents. You will need to: Admissions 1. Screen referrals from AIC and work with the nursing teams and relevant stakeholders to assess suitability of the referral. 2. Co-ordinate and complete onsite referral assessment for any care concerns, if necessary. 3. Provide supervision and support to the case management executive(s) and care concierge to complete the pre-admission processes. Case work and clinical interventions 1. Work with the Multi-disciplinary team and next of kin to ease the resident's transition and adaptation to the new living environment. 2. Provide bio-psychosocial spiritual (BPSS) assessments and evaluate resident's financial status, and to develop comprehensive care plans with the Multi-disciplinary team. 3. Provide psychosocial interventions and counselling support to the resident and/or their next of kin, and facilitate communication and visits to preserve family bonds. 4. Facilitate family conference and conversation for discussion on care planning eg discharge, advance care planning, lasting power of attorney, deputyship; optimising family involvement in residents' care journey. 5. Provide caregiver support resources to support families where required. 6. Manage complex social cases independently, collaborating with community stakeholders to support the overall wellbeing of the resident. 7. Review supporting documents for referral to other care options and  collaborate with multi-disciplinary team for assistive equipment as recommended by the therapist where required as part of discharge planning. Financial Assistance Schemes 1. Work with resident or next-of-kin on assessing eligibility for subsidies and financial considerations 2. Review cases and facilitate meetings for cases requiring financial assistance (Deviation, Medifund, MFEC - complex cases) 3. Guide and assist Medical Social Worker and case management executives in applying  Means Test, AIC Long Term Care schemes, MFEC - straightforward cases) Reports and administrative duties 1. Work with Case management executive to ensure residents' profile and movement are updated in database(s) 2. Review next-of-kin details and update residents' social report 3. Collaborate with Finance to review residents accounts receivable status when required 4. Manage and supervise team members in case management team 5. Collaborate with the Centre Director and other Medical Social Workers to conduct regular reviews of departmental workflows and processes Team leadership and mentorship 1. Provide clinical supervision for social work interns on placement 2. Provide clinical and mentorship for peers in the team and across the Residential Care Division 3. Lead continuous improvement projects and initiatives to improve the health and wellbeing of the residents QUALIFICATIONS You should: Have a degree or Post Graduate Diploma in Social Work Be registered with Singapore Association of Social Workers (SASW) Social work in healthcare background preferred Have at least 5 years of relevant experience in intermediate and long term care settings Good interpersonal and problem-solving skills Caring, positive, compassionate and self-disciplined individual Have experience in leading and supervising a team of medical social workers and executives Lead and participate in clinical projects and initiatives for continuous improvements Proficient in Google Workspace (i.e. Gmail, Google Docs, Sheets, Slide) OTHER INFORMATION It would be great if you: Able to communicate effectively with clients who are more comfortable speaking in their preferred dialects. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - NTUC Health

title: Sales Manager
description: Company Description Paradox Singapore is an urban resort that offers guests a sophisticated yet playful blend of traditional refinement and modern elegance. Located in the vibrant river district of Clarke Quay, the hotel is perfect for those looking for a stylish home base during business travel, or a quiet sanctuary to relax and unwind. Our comfortable rooms and spacious suites feature picturesque views of the city's skyline and are equipped with thoughtful amenities for laid-back style without any fuss. Role Description This is a full-time on-site role for Sales Manager at Paradox Singapore. The role will participate in all sales activities and work in line with the sales and marketing plan to exceed hotel revenues and target. Responsible for maximizing and driving revenues for own portfolio to increase market share for the hotel. Deliver the Hotel revenue and profit growth by maximizing revenue for both rooms and conference & banqueting. Act as the main liaison person between clients/ planners, and the hotel for all bookings and groups, from planning to guests’ stay to after-sales stages Responsibilities : Sales Strategy & Execution: Meet or exceed individual sales targets while contributing to the team’s overall revenue goals. Identify and pursue new business opportunities, focusing on partnerships with corporate clients, event organizers, associations, tour operators, travel agencies, and airlines within the assigned portfolio. Collaborate in developing and implementing effective sales strategies to meet personal and team targets. Regularly analyze market trends and competitor activities, adjusting sales strategies to stay competitive. Client Relationship Management: Based on assigned portfolio, build and maintain strong relationships with key corporate procurement teams, meeting planners, incentive buyers, bookers, airlines, tour operators, and wholesalers. Develop and execute strategies to attract new clients while retaining existing ones, ensuring long-term business relationships and repeat bookings. Promote the hotel through consistent sales activities, including face-to-face visits, entertainment, hotel tours, product presentations, and attending key networking events. Engage with guests from major event organizers staying at the hotel to ensure satisfaction and foster loyalty. Resolve client issues or concerns promptly to maintain positive and lasting relationships. Actively support and participate in all client-related engagement activities to enhance client satisfaction and loyalty. Business Development: Prospect for new clients, manage incoming leads, and work diligently to close sales deals aligned with revenue targets. Identify low occupancy periods and develop targeted strategies to maximize room sales during these times. Provide recommendations to regain lost accounts and implement special programs to boost volume, occupancy, average rates, and overall revenue from these accounts. Develop a targeted prospect list to attract business from competitor hotels and increase market share. Continuously identify and source new business opportunities across all revenue-generating areas to maximize overall hotel revenue. Maintain a keen awareness of competitors' strengths, weaknesses, and pricing strategies to stay ahead in the market. Stay informed about market dynamics, client demographics, seasonal trends, and industry developments to ensure the hotel remains competitive. Account Management: Review and analyze account portfolio regularly, presenting performance analysis and forward action plans to sales leader. Monitor key accounts, bookers, and customers, ensuring continuous engagement and satisfaction. Sales Operations: Oversee the preparation and management of all sales contracts, proposals, and agreements, ensuring accuracy and compliance with hotel policies. Work closely with the Revenue Management team to optimize room inventory and pricing strategies in alignment with market demand. Coordinate with other departments (e.g., Banquet, Operations, Front Office) to ensure seamless service delivery and high levels of client satisfaction. Ensure all incoming correspondence and telephone inquiries are handled efficiently and in a timely manner. Represent the hotel at trade shows, sales missions, industry events, and networking opportunities to enhance brand visibility and generate leads. Participate actively in hotel meetings, contributing to the development of broader hotel strategies and initiatives. Marketing and Promotional Activities: Partner with the marketing team to develop promotional materials, online content, and campaigns aimed at driving business from target segments. Ensure all sales materials, presentations, and proposals reflect the hotel’s brand identity and messaging. Attend key sales and PR-related events to effectively promote the hotel. Administrative Duties: Maintain accurate records of client interactions, sales activities, and group details using the hotel’s CRM system. Prepare regular reports on sales performance, client feedback, and market trends, providing insights for continuous improvement. Offer feedback and suggestions to enhance sales processes and achieve better results. Ensure compliance with all hotel policies, procedures, and relevant legal and regulatory requirements. Establish and regularly update mailing lists and databases to support sales activities. Other Responsibilities: Stay updated on industry trends and best practices to maintain a competitive edge in the market. Perform any other duties that may be assigned by Management, demonstrating flexibility and a strong commitment to the hotel's success.

title: Operations Executive (24/7 Shift)
description: About Us AsiaNext is an institution-only, global exchange for digital assets. A first-of-its-kind, combining the innovation of crypto with the robust security of traditional finance, we offer a comprehensive platform for trading and tokenising a diverse range of securities and non-bankable assets. Our services include listing, trading, clearing, settlement, and custody. A joint venture between SBI Digital Asset Holdings Co., Ltd. (SBI DAH) and SIX Group AG (SIX), AsiaNext is built to meet the growing demand from institutional investors for a safe venue to trade digital assets. Where applicable, our business activities will be subject to the strict regulatory oversight of the Monetary Authority of Singapore, and we adhere to the highest standards of corporate governance and internal controls across all activities. Leveraging the extensive networks of SBI DAH and SDX between Asia and Europe, as well as their joint expertise, we have built AsiaNext since 2020. Our vision is to become a trusted, market-leading exchange whose members can safely trade a wide variety of digital assets, permitting greater institutional access and driving the new era of digital finance worldwide. Why Join Us? With SIX, the Swiss stock exchange, and Japan’s finance conglomerate SBI DAH as joint shareholders, AsiaNext has significant financial backing. The team at AsiaNext are seasoned professionals from the finance industry and are passionate about digital assets, innovation and excellence. We listen to one another; we learn together, and we collaborate with a diverse ecosystem. Join AsiaNext and be part of a dynamic, fast-growing team with opportunities for professional growth and development. You'll have the chance to work on cutting-edge projects that are shaping the future of finance About This Role: AsiaNext seeks to develop a fair, orderly and transparent exchange that facilitates members and participants to have equal access to market information and transparent trading rules. This role will be performed on shift work basis where the candidate will be primarily tasked to monitor the continuous smooth running of the platform processes, hourly settlement run, and work on the market surveillance role in monitoring, identifying and escalating instances of market abuse or misconduct and other manipulative practices that compromise the fair, orderly and transparent exchange operations of the trading platform. Responsibilities Act as the first line of client support on Member’s account or trade related queries. Monitor platform processes and ensure the system batch run is executed successfully for the hourly settlement run covering critical process such as MTM margin calculation and risk limit notification. Activate contingent procedure to mitigate any system or data feed outage that affect the system batch run and hourly margin computation. Monitor member’s cash balance and margin limit position and follow up with Members proactively to manage their margin call if it is triggered. Monitor member’s credit or concentration limit and follow up with Members to manage their risk limit exposure if it is breached. Escalation to Ops management on any major operation process, system run or data feed failure for coordinated and prompt resolution. Perform level 1 check on market surveillance alerts triggered and forward to Ops Management for level 2 review on alert closure or further escalation if the alert event affects the working of a fair, efficient and transparent market. Use Python, R, and SQL to extract data, perform simple analysis, and generate a basic report. Able to use MIS/monitoring tools such as PowerBi, Zabbix, Graffana etc. Prior experience of conducting user acceptance testing (either manual or automated) Qualifications And Experience Education Diploma in banking and finance, business management/Higher NITEC in business. Experience and Skill Prior experience working in a related role in the banking/securities services/fintech/payment industry will be desirable. Good knowledge of relevant regulatory and market conduct rules and other related regulations will be preferred. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Proven analytical and eye for details Collaborative, self-discipline, and team focus Ability to pick up the learning with the training provided and work in a fast-paced environment Willing to work in different shifts to cover the 24x7 operations Rotate on fortnightly basis :- Time: 7.30am to 7.30pm (Day Shift) 7.30pm to 7.30am (Night Shift) Employment Benefits Competitive staff benefits - annual leave, life insurance, and medical insurance (includes dependents)

title: APEx (Athletic Performance and Exercise Center) Coordinator
description: Overview The APEx facility (Athletic Performance & Exercise) is Singapore American School’s on-campus health and wellness center. Singapore American School seeks a dynamic individual to oversee the programming and operations of this facility. The APEx Coordinator reports directly to the High School Director of Athletics & Activities, and is responsible for establishing and maintaining developmentally appropriate programs for SAS students, and staff; supervising all activities taking place inside the APEx facility; and monitoring to ensure that equipment is used safely and that programs are followed; and consults, supports, and assists the sports medicine staff with injured student-athletes' rehabilitation plans. The APEx Coordinator will: Create a positive, inclusive, and safe approach towards training and remaining physically active for life (regardless of age and ability). Prepare our SAS students for the rigors of High School athletics through educationally sound pedagogy, youth athlete development, and physical preparation in pre-season, in-season, and off-season Create an annual LTAD physical development framework for each varsity sport Assist with injury prevention- prehab and rehab Available for consultation of the PE curriculum as needed Lead Strength and Conditioning programming for co-curriculars Provide health & fitness opportunities for the SAS Community (Faculty/Staff/Parents/Alumni) Manage the APEx Budget Manage membership of and access to APEx Create, implement, and adhere to all SAS policies, such as student safeguarding and facility/equipment usage Sponsor APEx student committee And finally, model and promote “the Eagle Way”. ​Note: This role is expected to commence in July 2026. Contract and hours of work This is a full-time, 10-month Support Staff position that requires working from Mondays to Saturdays (a total of 40 hours per work week). The work schedule shall be set by the Director of Activities & Athletics in conjunction with appointed SAS supervisors. This will be structured to ensure smooth programs and operations during APEx opening hours, which will include weekdays, weekends, and school holidays. Opening Hours of APEx : Monday to Friday - 6:00am-6:00pm / Saturday - 8:00am-1:00pm Responsibilities: Hire, supervise, and schedule the work hours and responsibilities of the part-time APEx staff to ensure all operating hours are supervised Work in cooperation with the Activities & Athletics Directors and associated teams in the rehabilitation and strengthening of injured athletes Collaborate with coaches as needed to arrange sport-specific training regimes for team members Manage the APEx facility Prescribe fitness programs for the SAS Community through the development of and implementation of an SAS Community Health & Performance Program (including but not limited to bootcamp training, semi-private training, 1-to-1 personal training, and remote coaching options) Educate center users on proper use and technique – including but not limited to in-service sessions for PE department faculty Assist APEx Facility users in the case of a medical emergency Develop systems for tracking and monitoring user progress and coordinate any communication/feedback/reevaluation and documentation between students, coaches, and faculty Liaise with external vendors and internal maintenance/ operations teams to ensure all the equipment is running properly and to ensure a well-kept facility Provide strength & conditioning educational content for the athletics programs and physical and health education programs Create a system for the opening of APEx during some school holiday periods Minimum Qualifications:​ Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field Experience and understanding of youth athlete development in a school setting Excellent organizational and management skills. Demonstrates competence in managing a database and student/fitness records Excellent interpersonal skills and ability to handle multiple responsibilities and priorities concurrently. Excellent English communication skills, both written and oral. Positive work attitude and initiative. Ability to work cooperatively and provide assistance as necessary to other team members

title: Compliance Executive
description: 1. Position Summary The Compliance Executive is responsible for ensuring that Kim Seng Huat (KSH) complies with all regulatory, safety, environmental, and internal policy requirements. This role provides hands-on support in maintaining proper documentation, coordinating regulatory submissions, monitoring operational compliance (including warehouse and SCDF requirements), and supporting internal audits across the KSH group. The position requires high attention to detail, strong follow-up skills, and the ability to work closely with HR, Operations, Warehouse, EHS, Finance, and external agencies. 2. Key Responsibilities A. Regulatory & Statutory Compliance •	Ensure compliance with relevant Singapore laws and regulations including: •	MOM Employment Act & HR-related statutory requirements •	SCDF Fire Safety regulations, storage & usage permits •	NEA, BCA, LTA, JTC, SPF (as applicable to KSH operations) •	Maintain an updated compliance calendar with all renewal dates (e.g., SCDF Fire Certificate, storage permits, forklift diesel/MDT tank declarations). •	Prepare and submit documents for regulatory audits, inspections, and renewals. •	Liaise with government authorities on compliance matters. B. Internal Policies & Governance •	Assist in drafting, reviewing, and updating internal SOPs, handbooks, and compliance guidelines. •	Ensure all departments adhere to company policies, including HR, warehouse, safety, and operational procedures. •	Conduct periodic internal compliance checks and report findings. •	Support policy harmonisation across KSH, HH Stainless, and related entities (if required). C. EHS, SCDF & Warehouse Compliance •	Monitor compliance with safety and environmental requirements (e.g., PPE, forklift operations, fire extinguishers, spill response readiness). •	Maintain SCDF-related documentation (fire safety plan, storage of flammable materials, diesel usage, forklift diesel tank declarations). •	Track maintenance and servicing of equipment related to compliance (e.g., fire extinguishers, emergency lights, alarms, diesel tanks, spill kits). •	Support Site Supervisor/Warehouse team on compliance actions and rectification works. D. Audit & Documentation •	Coordinate internal and external audits (e.g., SCDF inspection, HR audit, ISO if applicable). •	Maintain organised records of compliance reports, licences, permits, contracts, and inspection documents. •	Ensure timely closure of audit findings. •	Maintain version control of compliance policies and SOPs. E. Training & Communication •	Support the rollout of compliance briefings and induction for new employees. •	Communicate updated regulatory guidelines or new requirements to relevant departments. •	Assist in training sessions related to safety, compliance, and internal controls. F. Cross-Functional Support •	Work closely with HR, Admin, Finance, Warehouse, Operations, and Management on compliance-related tasks. •	Provide support during incidents (e.g., spillage, SCDF non-compliance, MOM inspection) including documentation and reporting. •	Provide regular compliance status reports to Management. 3. Requirements Qualifications •	Diploma/Degree in Business Administration, Compliance, Occupational Safety & Health, Engineering, or related fields. •	WSQ/NEBOSH/ISO-related certifications are an advantage.

title: Fund Accounting Assistant Manager
description: We believe that challenges are better solved together. That's why you'll join a diverse, global community of solvers - an unexpected mix of people that come together to build trust in society and solve important problems. With us, you are encouraged to lead with your heart and values, and where your unique skills are developed and put to work in unexpected and exciting ways, superpowered by technology. Our Fund Office Support Services in Asset and Wealth Managed Services (“AWMS”) is expanding rapidly and we are looking for seasoned professionals to join us. We are Singapore’s leading Asset and Wealth Management team, and you will experience a dynamic and fast-paced working environment. You will be responsible for a portfolio of fund manager, family office and asset owners clients (the “clients’) and will work as part of a team to achieve and meet their needs. You will be responsible for a portfolio of clients, leading and driving the engagements to meet objectives and budget successfully. You will also act as the main liaison and point of contact for all matters relating to these clients. Effective day-to-day management of clients; building and maintaining client relationships is crucial. You need to direct and coach junior members and work together as a team to ensure that all deadlines and milestones are met as planned. In addition to taking on a leadership role, you will also be expected to assume preparer responsibilities for certain assignments where necessary. You will also ensure that you and your team hold yourself to the highest standard, in terms of work quality, risk management, issue escalation/resolution and client delivery. You need to understand the client’s issues and manage the expectations of different stakeholders by translating expectations into quality & balanced output/deliverables. You need to work closely with other functions (i.e., Tax, Valuation, etc) within PwC and address client needs by bringing relevant expertise from the firm to client discussions, while also converting service opportunities into proposals/business. You will also be responsible for enhancing the fund accounting (FA) and transfer agency (TA) operational and organisational readiness, and to ensure successful and smooth expectations. Specific responsibilities include the following: Fund Accounting Responsible for providing and/or reviewing quality accounting/bookkeeping and fund administration service for complex/non-complex open ended/closed ended/evergreen Alternative Investments Funds (Private equity/Venture Capital/Real Estate and Fund-of funds), including family offices (the “Funds”). Prepare/review of monthly/quarterly/semi-annual/annual/ Net Asset Value (the “NAV”) reporting pack which includes detailed trial balance, balance sheet, statement of operations, investment, bank reconciliation and investment holdings report. Prepare/review financial statements and related FRS/IFRS/RAP 7/US GAAP disclosures in accordance with accounting standards. Oversee and facilitate audits of the Funds and liaise with the auditors to ensure timely completion of audit. Perform follow-up with clients as necessary on accounting records and issues and develop solutions to these issues. Ensure optimal level of client service matters and all deliverables are on time and meet set quality standards. Prepare operational and system procedural manuals to ensure that all operational risks are well under control and adequate for the day-to-day activities. Transfer Agency Services Review and maintain shareholder/investor register. Prepare/review capital call/distribution/redemption/conversion/share transfer calculations in a timely manner. Prepare/review documentation for capital activities to ensure AML compliance. Management of commitment agreements and subscription forms including monitoring of commitments, drawdowns, and uncalled commitments. Deliver the organisation's FATCA/CRS/K1 service offering to our clients. Investors Services Oversees new client onboarding and verify proper fund and fund structure set ups within the accounting system in accordance to governing documents (i.e., PPM, LPA, etc) and ensure smooth coordination and delivery of end-to-end client acceptance and onboarding process. Liaise, discuss, and resolve issues surrounding client onboarding with fund managers and stakeholders. Maintain and enforce adherence to the Fund’s policies and procedures in accordance with their business line operating model to safeguard clients’ interest Prepare/review of investor onboarding documentations to ensure AML/KYC compliance. Take care of day-to-day operation of the funds and following up with third parties such as investors and fund managers. Take charge in the organisation of board meetings and shareholder’ meetings. Overall responsibility for client relationships, inquiries, and meetings. ​ Team Management Coach and supervise junior staffs, review their work, and provide feedback for improvement to guarantee timely delivery and quality of work. Monitor and track team performance to meet the requirements set out in the SLA. Plan resources in line with business demands and may participate in the recruitment and selection process. Establish plan for new client setup and transitions. Assist client in resolving complex fund accounting issues and/or other fund administration matters. Keep senior management abreast of all material issues affecting the processing of deliverables and ensuring that these issues are resolved on time in accordance with the SLA. Identify and highlight opportunities for internal process improvement and increase in operational efficiency. Assist in billing of service fees to clients and monitoring the collection of service fees from client. Assist in maintaining internal controls as per SSAE 16 requirements. FA/TA operational and organisational readiness and system implementation Enhance and revise as appropriate the FA and TA operational and organisational readiness and system implementation to meet changing needs and requirements. As part of the role, there is a strong focus on improving work processes by identifying pain points and proposing actionable recommendations – enhance through the use of technology – to drive greater operational scalability and efficiency Manage project resource and budget, scope, risk, schedule and progress, performance, deliverables, issues, escalations, and conflicts ATTRIBUTES & SKILLS: You hold a Bachelor and/or master’s degree in Accounting or Economics. You have a minimum 6 years of relevant experience in alternative investment fund administration, Big 4 audit firm or domiciliation. You have good knowledge and understanding of the family office; and asset and , wealth and management industry landscape and operations (including valuation (in private companies, debts, etc), accounting and payment procedures). You have good knowledge about Singapore fund structures (i.e., Singapore Variable Capital Company (VCC), Singapore Private Limited (Pte. Ltd.), SG limited partnership and unit trusts). You have good knowledge in accounting for investment products, ideally with fund-of-funds, single-manager funds, venture capital, real estate and private equity funds will be considered an asset. Competent accounting knowledge in Singapore FRS/IFRS/ RAP 7 and US GAAP standards. Previous experience in compliance and KYC procedures. Familiar with Singapore regulatory filing requirements. Professional, confident, and highly motivated self-starter, with an ability to handle multiple engagements/projects in a fast-paced environment with tight deadlines, positive attitude to learn and strong team player. Possess strong values, personal integrity, and emotional intelligence. Meticulous and attention to details, possessing an analytical and enquiring mind-set. Possess team spirit, a capacity to build strong client relationships and take initiative to act without waiting for direction (when appropriate). Advanced level MS excel, proficient with MS Word, MS PowerPoint. Knowledge and prior experience on Allvue System a plus. A professional designation such a C.A., C.P.A., or C.M.A. etc., would be an asset.

title: Internal Auditor
description: INTERNAL AUDITOR We are a leading multi-family office headquartered in Singapore which provides our clients with Wealth Advisory and Asset Management services and a full suite of wealth management solutions. Reporting to CEO, you will be responsible for the internal audit function of the Company from audit planning to audit report finalisations. Job responsibilities: Your responsibilities include provision of compliance support on all, but are not limited to, the following: · Work independently to develop in house audit function to lead, plan and conduct audit planning and projects including conducting process and control walkthroughs, developing testing criteria, reviewing and analyzing data and evidence, and identifying and defining issues; · Responsible for conducting the full audit cycle for operational, process and risk-based audit field work and report on all aspects of business risk and internal control status, ensuring full adherence by the Company and all Staff to Regulatory Requirements, Compliance Procedures and Standard Operating Procedures (“SOPs”); · Review and evaluate adequacy and effectiveness of all aspects of the Company’s internal control systems and operation procedures in compliance with corporate policies, guidelines and regulatory requirements; · Identify areas of risk and provide recommendations for improvements; · Work closely with management in promptly communicating issues and concerns as they relate to audit assignments and the general operation of the respective departments; · Ensure all required submissions with all the regulators are checked and in proper order; · Prepare clear and concise audit reports on observation and recommendations to management for review; · Improve and enhance processes, propose timely interventions; · Ensure audit plan is followed through and completed on schedule; · Conduct follow-up audit on corrective action taken to address the inefficiencies and prepare summary status reports; · Be fully updated and conversant on sector’s rules, regulations, best practices, tools, techniques and performance standards; · Work together with Compliance officer to implement and instil a compliance culture and awareness within the organisation; and · Lead/ assist in any ad hoc projects as necessary. Skill Requirements/ Qualifications: · Possess Degree in Accounting / ACCA accreditation or an equivalent. · Minimum 3 years audit experience in a public accounting firm or internal audit experience. · Proven knowledge of auditing standards and procedures, laws, rules and regulations · Prior experience in audit in the financial services industry will be an advantage. · Meticulous and adaptable with ability to multi-task in a fast-paced environment. · Able to work independently without close supervision. · Excellent interpersonal and communication skills If you aspire to grow and excel with a high growth organisation, please forward us your CV to Recruitment@tauruswealth.com.sg .
