title: Full Time Career and Job Placement Officer cum Operation
description: We are seeking a highly motivated and versatile Career and Job Placement Officer cum Operations Executive/Manager to play a dual role in our educational institute. This pivotal position focuses on maximizing job placement success for our learners in relevant industries while also providing essential operational and administrative support for our training programs. The ideal candidate will be a proactive, organized, and excellent communicator, capable of managing multiple priorities effectively in a fast-paced environment. Key Responsibilities A. Career & Job Placement (60%) The primary focus is to achieve high placement rates for our graduates by: Industry Liaison: Build, maintain, and strengthen relationships with industry partners and employers to identify relevant job opportunities for learners. Learner Placement: Facilitate the job placement process for graduates, including career counselling, resume reviews, interview preparation workshops, and managing a robust placement tracking system. Recruitment Events: Assist in planning, coordinating, and executing recruitment and career events (e.g., job fairs, networking sessions) specifically for our SCTP (SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme) graduates in partnership with Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs). B. Operations & Trainer Management (40%) Provide essential administrative and logistical support to ensure the smooth operation of our training programs: Adjunct Trainer Management: Manage a roster of adjunct trainers, which includes handling logistics for onboarding, contract renewal, administrative support, and serving as a primary point of contact. Trainer Deployment Support: Assist in the smooth deployment and scheduling of trainers for courses conducted at various IHLs, ensuring all logistical and regulatory requirements are met. Course Follow-ups: Actively assist in follow-ups on course-related matters, including communicating with trainers, learners, and partners regarding schedules, materials, feedback, and necessary documentation. Administrative Support: Support the team with general administrative tasks, procurement of training materials, and maintenance of operational records. Required Qualifications & Competencies Education: Diploma or Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Education, or a related field. Experience: Minimum 2-3 years of relevant experience in career services, recruitment, HR operations, or training administration, preferably within the education sector. Proven track record in building and maintaining professional relationships with corporate partners. Skills: Excellent communication and interpersonal skills (written and verbal) to engage effectively with learners, employers, and trainers. Strong organizational skills with meticulous attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and experience with a CRM or Learner Management System (LMS) is a plus. Attributes: Proactive and resourceful with a problem-solving mindset. Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively within a team. Passion for helping individuals achieve their career goals. Other Duties Any other projects or duties as assigned by the Senior Management Team (SMI management) to support the overall strategic goals of the institute.

title: Teacher of Middle School Drama [Part-time]
description: UWCSEA Dover has a part-time vacancy for August 2026 for a Teacher of Middle School Drama (ages 11­-14). The successful candidate must have a tertiary qualification in relevant subjects and significant experience teaching Drama to 11 -14 year olds. The appointee will be an excellent communicator with students, staff and parents; and also will be both creative and innovative and have a high level of resilience. The candidate also must be flexible if the need arises to teach a High School Drama class. Please note that to apply for this role, you must have an internationally recognised qualification and reside in Singapore. You must also be readily available from August 2026. Main Responsibilities Teachers should be willing to contribute positively to the collaborative culture of the Drama Department and broader Middle School team (ages 11-14 years old). Teachers construct learning experiences that challenge students, support diverse learners in each classroom, and demonstrate a strong commitment to concept-based teaching and learning. Teachers ensure assessments support ongoing learning and provide timely, relevant and appropriate feedback to students on formative and summative assessments. Teachers demonstrate effective classroom management that shows an understanding of the academic, social and emotional dimensions of learning. Teachers keep parents informed about the learning of their children, and be proactive in communication with parents. Expectations and Standards Follow the UWCSEA Professional Code of Conduct and Safeguarding Code of Conduct . Experience in and willingness to direct extra curricular productions is essential for this role. Experience in supporting students in developing work for examination would be a significant advantage. Teachers should be willing and eager to participate in the five key elements of a UWCSEA education: Academics;  Activities;  Outdoor Education; Personal and  Social Education and Service Academic expectations include enthusiastic participation in Professional Learning. Pastoral expectations include the leadership of a mentor class, unless the teacher is exempted due to other responsibilities. On average the College expects a yearly contribution of two sessions per week in each season to Activities and/or Service, or equivalent for part time positions The College expects that teachers will participate enthusiastically in the curricular Outdoor Education experiences which are relevant to their students and will also occasionally volunteer for holiday Outdoor Education trips and/or Service trips. A commitment to diversity and inclusion is essential alongside a willingness to adapt and develop pedagogy to meet the needs of our learners. Applications The UWC Mission is to make education a force to unite people, nations and culture for peace and a sustainable future.  We are committed to creating a community in which all members feel safe and secure, and know that their authentic selves and individual differences are accepted with respect and care. At UWCSEA, we acknowledge that diversity, equity, inclusion and justice (DEIJ) is an ongoing and evolving process and strive to create and sustain a sense of belonging for all members of our community, past, present and future. All qualified applicants, including those who identify as a part of marginalized group(s) and individuals with (dis)abilities are all encouraged to apply.  Our school is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of all the students in our care and expects all applicants to share this commitment. title: Equities Developer C++ AVP
description: Purpose of the role To design, develop and improve software, utilising various engineering methodologies, that provides business, platform, and technology capabilities for our customers and colleagues. Engage in complex analysis of data from multiple sources of information, internal and external sources such as procedures and practises (in other areas, teams, companies, etc).to solve problems creatively and effectively. Communicate complex information. 'Complex' information could include sensitive information or information that is difficult to communicate because of its content or its audience. Influence or convince stakeholders to achieve outcomes. All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave. We are seeking a strong C++ developer for designing and developing ultra-low-latency end-to-end Direct Market Access (DMA) trading platform. As a Barclays Developer within Equities QPS (Quantitative Prime Services) Technology, you will join a team focused on building out the functionality and technology of the SubM , SUBM-R, SubM Native systems for our flagship product offering for giving ultra-low latency to a very niche domain of very high volume and low latency sophisticated Equites trading clients, mostly Hedge funds as well as develop Cash Connectivity Platform for our Equities Cash Business. Key Accountabilities Design and development of industry-leading, ultra-low-latency Equities trading systems. Individual should be taking up the full responsibility and drive multiple deliveries at a time. Work closely with existing leads to review current platform architecture and propose and implement changes geared towards performance improvements, simplification, and technical debt reduction. Be a go to person for Business/ Desk and provide estimates for tasks including design, development, and testing. Holistic simulation and testing of the trading system for high reliability. Support analysis, testing, and operations teams. Participate in code reviews and architectural decisions. Mentor junior developers. Problem Solving Working with business analysts and project managers to understand the requirements. Analysing those requirements to find the most appropriate technical solution and creating a technical specification where needed. Developing a pragmatic software solution as per the requirement specifications. Unit / system / regression / performance testing of the solutions. Fixing bugs coming out of System / UAT testing. Reviewing code produced by others. Supporting the implementation of each new code. Resolving any post-implementation issues as needed. Participating in post-project reviews to improve the project lifecycle where necessary. Essential Skills/Basic Qualifications: Excellent 5+ years of experience with expertise in C++ and conversant with Modern C++ topics and features. Expert in data structures, algorithms, multi-threading, and C++ standard library. C++ performance and low latency understanding to make latency reduction on the critical path of flow. Good working experience of Linux/Unix operating system and Linux commands. Working experience, collaboration in the regional and global team structure. Desirable skills/Preferred Qualifications: Front office trading development on the critical path to the exchange and may have developed low latency system specially for APAC markets. Proven experience in working in the Investment banking, working with directly with Traders and Desk Heads. Algorithmic Trading experience with a High Frequency Trading Firm. User Space Networking: Solar flare open onload. Lower level TCPIP Experience. You may be assessed on key critical skills relevant for success of the role including job-specific technical skills and interpersonal skills. Barclays is required by law to confirm that you have the Legal Right to Work in any role that you apply for. If you currently hold a work visa sponsored by Barclays, or you would require sponsorship from Barclays, you must declare this as part of your application. title: Manager | Credit Risk
description: Join our Credit Risk division, which is responsible for analysing and assessing all credit and equity exposures taken by Macquarie to minimise the risk of loss arising from failure of counterparties to repay loans or honour contracts. Our division is also responsible for undertaking reviews of counterparties and transactions, and establishing appropriate limit frameworks. On principal transactions, we work with the deal team to understand risk versus reward profiles and develop risk mitigation strategies. At Macquarie, our advantage is bringing together diverse people and empowering them to shape all kinds of possibilities. We are a global financial services group operating in 31 markets and with 56 years of unbroken profitability. You’ll be part of a friendly and supportive team where everyone - no matter what role - contributes ideas and drives outcomes. What role will you play? You will conduct in-depth credit reviews and analyses of Commodities and Global Markets' counterparties in the Asia region, focusing on corporate clients. You will collaborate with global credit offices to secure credit approvals, partner with Macquarie's business groups to evaluate new products and transactions, and provide well-founded recommendations to credit approvers. Additionally, you will assist in structuring transactions, reviewing legal terms, and offering general advice and analysis. You will perform annual and new reviews of counterparties, stay updated on portfolio developments, and address ad-hoc credit requests. You will also maintain strong connections across the commodities market to stay informed of the latest news and developments. What you offer Over 5 years of regional Asia credit risk experience in a large financial institution and/or commodity trading house Physical commodities trading experience including energy products and trade finance Strong background in transactional credit risk analysis and approvals of OTC derivatives across commodities, including familiarity with negotiating ISDA and CSA documentation Proven experience in preparing counterparty credit reviews, credit recommendations and presenting to senior risk management Comfortable planning and re-prioritising workload in an unpredictable environment Confident and able to provide independent credit judgement and articulate this succinctly to the senior management team High degree of ownership, delivery and timeliness in a fast-paced, output focused environment Ability to build strong, open, dialogue-based relationships across a range of stakeholders. We love hearing from anyone inspired to build a better future with us, if you're excited about the role or working at Macquarie we encourage you to apply. What we offer At Macquarie, you’re empowered to shape a career that’s rewarding in all the ways that matter most to you. Macquarie employees can access a wide range of benefits which, depending on eligibility criteria, include: 1 wellbeing leave day and 5 additional paid Flexi-Leave days per year 20 weeks’ paid parental leave for primary caregivers along with 12 days of paid transition leave upon return to work and 6 weeks’ paid leave for secondary caregivers 2 days of paid volunteer leave per annum and donation matching Option to purchase additional annual leave Benefits and initiatives to support your physical, psychological and financial wellbeing including, comprehensive medical and life insurance cover and additional flexible wellbeing credits Access to a wide range of learning and development opportunities, including reimbursement for professional membership or subscription Hybrid and flexible working arrangements, dependent on role Reimbursement for work from home equipment About the Risk Management Group Our Risk Management Group works as an independent, and centralised function, responsible for independent and objective review and challenge, oversight, monitoring and reporting in relation to Macquarie’s material risks. We are a global team that aims to manage the risks of today and anticipate the risks of tomorrow. title: Senior Environmental Designer
description: Atelier Ten, www.atelierten.com, is looking for a full-time Senior Environmental Designer to join our Singapore office. We are an international environmental design consultancy creating high-performance, sustainable, award-winning buildings. Buildings that give back to the wider environment, do not waste resources and make people feel good. You will work on the creation of highly innovative and sustainable solutions involving a mix of technologies. We encourage the design of highly innovative and sustainable solutions that incorporate a mix of technologies. About the role We are looking for a Senior Environmental Designer with sustainable design experience and specialist knowledge in building energy optimisation to consult on projects. You will be responsible for delivering environmental and sustainability advice on projects for both internal and external clients. This will involve consulting on environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials and waste, with emphasis on building energy optimisation. This consultancy will be demonstrated through sound analytics including building physics. Projects will range from masterplanning to detailed design of specific buildings across all scales and locations of the globe. Work will often be coordinated with an approach to the mechanical and electrical servicing concept, and where the project is for an internal client you will support design engineers to achieve this. You will be responsible for leading and maintaining a range of projects, leading and maintaining environmental design inputs on a range of projects for internal clients and/or providing specialist knowledge to other projects/teams. Using industry benchmarks and internal standards, you will ensure the timely delivery of considered and accurate project information. Where projects are not being delivered to internal clients you but also provide a point of contact between Atelier Ten and our clients/collaborators. You will also be responsible for leading and co-ordinating the internal efforts of junior staff across Atelier Ten, providing appropriate frameworks and tools, agreeing appropriate standards and co-ordinating delivery against these standards. We are looking for someone who has experience working in a fast-paced office environment with the ability to manage multiple deadlines and work as part of a team as well as having the initiative to work under limited supervision. You are proactive, self-motivated with strong interpersonal skills and comfortable working with colleagues on all levels. Key accountabilities Technical Ability Demonstrate specialist knowledge in building energy optimisation with at least 2 or more practices’ expertise Demonstrate a proven ability to provide consultancy for environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, building energy optimisation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials, waste and building physics Demonstrate an empathy with Atelier Ten’s approach Committed to the development of high-quality, high-performance and sustainable buildings via the appropriate technical standards, costs and programme Help to frame client briefs in terms of environmental and sustainable aspirations Deliver technical solutions in accordance with client brief Support and nurture junior/graduate members of the team Demonstrate an awareness and understanding of other disciplines Project management Liaise with client, architect and other members of the internal & external design team with clarity and confidence Organise junior staff to achieve solutions within agreed time scales Maintain project overview: explain the status of relevant section of a project at any time Be aware of both our agreement and the contractual relationships on the project Mentor junior staff Marketing Appreciate and assist in the need to generate business Report any feedback or issues concerning the project Foster positive relationships with clients and other members of the design team Skills Natural leader and can operate as part of a team Self motivated Ability to work with minimal supervision Ability to prioritise and to think strategically about the timing and delivery of project information Ability to structure the delivery of project information against evolving client brief in such a way as can be clearly explained to senior managers at the start of a project Strong writing skills for producing written reports Effective IT skills for producing relevant material, including knowledge and application of building physics packages Effective IT skills to develop new and bespoke assessment methods for project specific tasks and rendered in a manner that allows re-use Qualified candidates ideally possess Bachelor’s or higher degree in a field related to building engineering, environmental building design, architectural engineering, building services engineering or building science Candidates who are Chartered or working towards Chartership are highly desired 5+ years’ professional experience in an engineering or sustainability consulting firm Proven track record of leading and executing dynamic thermal modelling and energy modelling analysis Experience in daylight modelling, computational fluid dynamic (CFD), parametric modelling and programming Proficiency in IES VE, Radiance and Microsoft Excel Skills with softwares such as Grasshopper, eQuest, EnergyPlus, AutoCAD, Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, InDesign) and Rhino or general programming or data visualization skill Proven track record of successfully co-ordinating and delivering sustainable & environmental design input to projects within the public and private sector, with special focus on building energy performance Proven track record of engaging and negotiating with senior management and peers on projects Proven track record of engaging with and influencing peers outside of formal management relationships Practical knowledge of managing small project teams coupled with the understanding to select frameworks/tools that are appropriate to particular circumstances Strong writing skills for producing written reports Applicants should have demonstrated skills in computer and hand drafting, have proficiency in written and spoken English, and good verbal, inter-personal and writing skills are required Ability to work under limited supervision Ability to prioritise work loads Excellent analytical skills Strong ability to multitask

Monitor the performance of risk models, ensuring their appropriateness and maintaining the integrity and accuracy of risk data. Participate in stress testing exercises to evaluate margin adequacy under extreme market scenarios, including reverse stress testing and sensitivity analysis for key risk parameters Develop and leverage upon advanced analytics tools and models, e.g. tools and models related to Artificial Intelligence (AI) Design test cases and perform testing for risk systems development and system upgrades Contribute to the development and successful delivery of visual analytics initiatives. Risk Culture Contribute to the establishment and promotion of risk culture within the Company including through daily responsibilities, awareness efforts and training efforts Contribute to the culture of governance and risk oversight within the Company Qualifications: Degree in Risk Management, Business, Economics, Mathematics, Science, Engineering or IT, or a related discipline Working level competency in Excel VBA/SQL/Python and Microsoft Office applications Understanding of derivatives products including futures and options, margin methodologies, and collateral management principles preferred Strong attention to detail with a focus on process execution Keen interest in financial, commodity and energy markets and derivative instruments Key player in an agile and diverse environment that emphasizes proactive collaboration and risk identification Quick learner with excellent written and communication skills, team orientation, and ability to work with counterparts in different organizational areas Self-starter who takes initiative to improve the current state of things and is adaptable to change Prior relevant professional experience in risk management, data analysis, portfolio management, and/or risk systems in prime brokerages, fund houses or clearing houses preferred

title: Junior Fund Accountant
description: Our Fund Office Support Services in Asset and Wealth Managed Services (“AWMS”) is expanding rapidly and we are looking for motivated professionals to join us. We are Singapore’s leading Asset and Wealth Management team, and you will experience a dynamic and fast-paced working environment. You will be responsible for a portfolio of fund manager, family office and asset owners clients (the “clients’) and will work as part of a team to achieve and meet their needs. You will be responsible for a portfolio of clients engagements to meet objectives and budget successfully. You will also act as the liaison and point of contact for these clients. Effective day-to-day management of clients; building and maintaining client relationships is crucial. You need to ensure that all deadlines and milestones are met as planned. You will also ensure that you hold yourself to the highest standard, in terms of work quality, risk management, issue escalation/resolution and client delivery. You need to understand the client’s issues and manage the expectations of different stakeholders by translating expectations into quality & balanced output/deliverables. You need to work closely with other functions (i.e., Tax, Valuation, etc) within PwC and address client needs by bringing relevant expertise from the firm to client discussions. Specific responsibilities include the following: Fund Accounting Responsible to onboard new clients and set-up new funds and fund structures into the accounting system in accordance to governing documents (i.e., PPM, LPA, etc). Responsible for providing quality accounting/bookkeeping and fund administration service for open ended/closed ended/evergreen Alternative Investments Funds (Private equity/Venture Capital/Real Estate and Fund-of funds), including family offices (the “Funds”). Preparation of monthly/quarterly/semi-annual/annual/ Net Asset Value (the “NAV”) reporting pack which includes detailed trial balance, balance sheet, statement of operations, investment, bank reconciliation and investment holdings report. Drafting of financial statements and related FRS/IFRS/RAP 7/US GAAP disclosures in accordance with accounting standards. Oversee and facilitate audits of the Funds and liaise with the auditors to ensure timely completion of audit. Perform follow-up with clients as necessary on accounting records and issues and develop solutions to these issues. Assist senior fund accountants to take care of day-to-day operation of the funds which includes resolving accounting issues and client service matters. Ensure optimal level of client service matters and all deliverables are on time and meet set quality standards. Transfer Agency Services Responsible for the maintenance of the shareholder/investor register. Prepare capital call/distribution/redemption/conversion/share transfer calculations and process these in a timely manner. Co-ordinate documentation for capital activities to ensure AML compliance. Management of commitment agreements and subscription forms including monitoring of commitments, drawdowns, and uncalled commitments. Deliver the organisation's FATCA/CRS/K1 service offering to our clients. Investors Services Assist with new client onboarding and ensure proper fund and fund structure set ups within the accounting system in accordance to governing documents (i.e., PPM, LPA, etc). Assist senior fund accountants in liaising, discussing, and resolving issues surrounding client onboarding with fund managers and stakeholders. Maintain and enforce adherence to the Fund’s policies and procedures in accordance with their business line operating model to safeguard clients’ interest Prepare investor onboarding documentations to ensure AML/KYC compliance. Take care of day-to-day operation of the funds and following up with third parties such as investors and fund managers. Assist in the organisation of board meetings and shareholder’ meetings. Responsible for maintaining client relationships, inquiries, and meetings. Operational Support Assist in setting up new client accounts and transitions by preparing required documentation and ensuring accurate system entries. Support resolution of routine fund accounting and administration issues by coordinating with relevant stakeholders. Provide timely updates to senior team members on any material issues affecting deliverables. Monitor and track performance to meet the requirements set out in the SLA. Contribute to identifying process improvement opportunities and suggest ways to enhance operational efficiency. Help maintain internal controls and compliance with SSAE 16 requirements through accurate record-keeping and adherence to procedures. FA/TA operational and organisational readiness and system implementation Assist in maintaining and updating Fund Accounting (FA) and Transfer Agency (TA) operational processes and system setups to meet evolving business needs. Support efforts to improve work processes by identifying inefficiencies and suggesting practical enhancements, including leveraging technology for greater scalability and efficiency. Provide administrative and documentation support for system implementation activities, ensuring accurate data entry and adherence to established procedures. ATTRIBUTES & SKILLS: You hold a Bachelor and/or master’s degree in Accounting or Economics. You have a minimum 1 to 3 years of relevant experience in alternative investment fund administration, Big 4 audit firm or domiciliation. You have good knowledge and understanding of the family office; and asset and wealth and management industry landscape and operations (including valuation (in private companies, debts, etc), accounting and payment procedures). You have good knowledge about Singapore fund structures (i.e., Singapore Variable Capital Company (VCC), Singapore Private Limited (Pte. Ltd.), SG limited partnership and unit trusts). You have good knowledge in accounting for investment products, ideally with fund-of-funds, single-manager funds, venture capital, real estate and private equity funds will be considered an asset. Competent accounting knowledge in Singapore FRS/IFRS/ RAP 7 and US GAAP standards. Previous experience in compliance and KYC procedures. Familiar with Singapore regulatory filing requirements. Professional, confident, and highly motivated self-starter, with an ability to handle multiple engagements/projects in a fast-paced environment with tight deadlines, positive attitude to learn and strong team player. Possess strong values, personal integrity, and emotional intelligence. Meticulous and attention to details, possessing an analytical and enquiring mind-set. Possess team spirit, a capacity to build strong client relationships and take initiative to act without waiting for direction (when appropriate). Advanced level MS excel, proficient with MS Word, MS PowerPoint. Knowledge and prior experience on Allvue System a plus. A professional designation such a C.A., C.P.A., or C.M.A. etc., would be an asset.

title: Head of Marketing, Singapore
description: IG Singapore is looking for a Head of Marketing, becoming a permanent member of our team. Working in the heart of the CBD on Raffles Place, you will be joining a team of passionate individuals seeking to grow our Singapore business and provide exceptional client service to our clients. So, who are we? Hello, we’re IG Group. No, not Instagram – though we're a pretty big deal ourselves. We’re a global, FTSE 250-listed company made up of a collection of progressive fintech brands in the world of online trading and investing. The best part? We’ve snapped up many awards for our top-class platforms, forward-thinking products, and incredible employee experiences. We believe in financial freedom. And our purpose is to help ambitious people achieve it. Around 400,000 people use our groundbreaking technology and forward-thinking platforms to trade the financial products they know and love. Did we mention we have offices in 18 countries? Yes, when you join IG Group, you'll have the chance to work across multiple brands, with people all over the world. Join us for an exciting future and let’s innovate together! Your role in the team We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Head of Marketing to lead our marketing efforts. Reporting directly to the CEO, this role will oversee a team and be responsible for driving the demanding growth of our new brokerage product while continuing to scale the existing CFD business. This is a key leadership position that requires both strategic vision and hands-on execution. You will own all marketing channels—digital, affiliates, social, PR, partnerships, and offline—and partner closely with sales, client servicing, and product teams to deliver measurable growth. The role is critical in shaping our regional brand presence, client acquisition, and engagement strategies, while ensuring marketing initiatives are compliant, data-driven, and aligned with business priorities. What you’ll do Lead and oversee all marketing channels, ensuring strategies are tailored to local market dynamics while aligned with global brand positioning. Manage the budget for markets and being responsible for cost effectiveness, resource allocation and measurable ROI. Drive the demanding growth of IG’s new brokerage product, while continuing to scale the existing CFD business. Partner closely with regional teams, performance team, sales, client servicing,  and product teams to ensure marketing initiatives directly support business objectives and client needs. Develop and execute integrated marketing strategies to drive awareness, acquisition, engagement, and retention across digital, performance, social, partnerships, PR, and offline channels. Manage agencies on record for communications, creative development and media buys. Continuously  track market trends and competitor activity to adapt strategies, seize  opportunities, and mitigate risks. Oversee  and inspire a team of marketers, building a high-performance, innovative, and collaborative culture. Uphold  regulatory and brand compliance standards across all campaigns and communications. Who we’re looking for You’re curious about things like the client experience, the rapid developments in tech, and the complex world of fintech regulation. You’re also a confident, creative thinker with a knack for innovating. We know that you know every problem has a solution. Here, you can try new ideas, and lead the way in creating inspiring experiences for our clients and everyone around you. We don’t fit the corporate stereotype. If you want to work for a traditional, suit-and-tie corporate that just gives you a pay cheque at the end of the month, we might not be for you. But, if you have that IG Group energy and you can stand behind what we believe in, let’s raise the bar together. What you’ll need for this role Minimum 10 years in tech or finance marketing, across traditional, social and digital channels. Minimum 3 years of equivalent leadership experience managing a team and a budget of US>$10M. Degree in marketing, business or finance. Strong understanding of investment & trading products (CFDs, FX, Crypto, Stocks) as well as an interest in the financial markets. Familiar with Gen AI tools and CRM systems such as Braze & Salesforce. Strategic mindset with a bias for action. Strong analytical skills, with a demonstrated ability to optimise and pivot on marketing plans for maximum effectiveness. Regional experience in Malaysia, HongKong, Taiwan, Thailand Fluency in English is a requirement, proficiency in Mandarin or Bahasa is a significant advantage to engage customers from Chinese/Bahasa native countries. How you’ll grow When you join IG Group, we want you to have more than a job – we want you to have a career. And you can. If you spot an opportunity, we want you to chase it. Stretch yourself, challenge your self-beliefs and go for the things you dream of. With internal and external learning opportunities, and the tools to help you skyrocket to success, we’ll support you all the way. And these opportunities truly are endless because we have some bold targets. We plan to expand our global presence, increase revenue growth, and ultimately deliver the world’s best trading experience. We’d love to have you along for the ride. The perks It really is more than a job. We’ll recognise your talent and make sure that you can still have a life – at work, and outside of it. Networks, committees, awards, sports and social clubs, mentorships, volunteering opportunities, extra time off… the list goes on. Performance-related bonuses Insurance and medical plans Career-focused technical and leadership training Two volunteering days per year One day of birthday leave per year Contribution to gym memberships and more Flexible working hours and work-from–home opportunities Where you’ll work We follow a hybrid working model; we reckon it’s the best of both worlds. This model also feeds into our secret ingredients for innovation: diversity, flexibility, and close connection. Plus, you’ll be welcomed into a diverse and inclusive workforce with a lot of creative energy. Ask our employees what their favourite thing is about working at IG, and you’ll hear an echo of ‘our culture’! That’s because you can come to work as your authentic self. The things that make you, you – like your ethnicity, sexual orientation, faith, age, gender identity/expression or physical capacity – can bring a fresh perspective or new skill to our business. That’s why we welcome people from various walks of life; and anyone who wants to help us realise our vision and strategy. So, if you’re keen to connect with our values, and lead the charge on innovation, you know what to do. Apply now

title: Associate
description: About the role We are searching for dynamic and ambitious Associate level talent to support Atelier Ten’s continued success and future growth in Singapore and APAC. As a member of the senior management team, you will be responsible for managing and developing a project team within Atelier Ten. This role supports the Technical Directors in understanding current and likely future resource requirements on ‘live’ projects and planning for the future project pipeline. In supporting the allocation and management of resources across projects, you will also be responsible for overseeing and managing the team’s profitability and technical capabilities, alongside identifying and reporting any technical and financial shortfalls. As a team leader, you will be responsible for managing the design and delivery of projects within the team, maintaining a range of projects and providing specialist knowledge to other projects/teams. Using industry benchmarks and internal standards, you will ensure not only the timely delivery of considered and accurate project information but act as a senior point of contact between Atelier Ten and its clients & collaborators. You will also be expected to identify business development opportunities, secure new business and support the overall marketing effort. You will be responsible for upholding the integrity of ISO 9001, leading, developing and co-ordinating the internal efforts of team members and junior staff across Atelier Ten on their specific projects, identifying and providing training & support where needed, and guidance on frameworks & tools, agreeing appropriate standards and co-ordinating delivery against these standards. You will conduct regular meetings with the team to discuss staff matters, technical issues, delivery and issues arising from the team’s interaction with external parties together with encouraging knowledge share across the business. Key accountabilities Team Leader · Support the allocation and management of project resources · Understand current project resource requirements · Work with Technical Directors to identify future resource requirements and any recruitment needs · Work with the QEMS Leader on upholding and refining quality management systems · Work with Human Resources on training needs · Oversee management of overall team profitability and technical capabilities · Maintain consistency of project management across all projects in the team · Conduct regular team meetings creating a platform for team members to raise issues and share knowledge Technical ability · Be committed to Climate Positive outcomes across all design disciplines · Demonstrate specialist knowledge in a discipline of engineering and sustainable buildings via the appropriate technical standards, costs and programme · Deliver technical design solutions in accordance with client brief · As an experienced engineer take the lead for coordination between the disciplines within the office and to external consultants · To advise and ensure graduate engineers are given proper development goals and mentoring together with training and development plans Project management · Be committed to upholding the integrity of project management within Atelier Ten · Liaise with client, architect and other members of the design team without day-to-day Director or Technical Director level support · Confidently represent all disciplines · Organise team, disseminate information and clearly explain technical requirements, deliverables and programme · Be able to delegate and maintain control of project timelines and budgets · Manage professional relationships with clients, peers, team and administration staff · Financially aware of project costs and programme of projects in the team, the need for additional services and financial control · Be able to provide update and current status of projects at any point in time clearly, concisely and accurately to Directors and Technical Directors · Manage any feedback or issues concerning a project and escalate as necessary · Be able to develop a thorough knowledge of our fee agreements and the contractual status and relationships on the project · Be able to prepare fee arrangements for projects · Be able to recruit staff to resource projects as necessary in line with project budgets · Manage the project managers in the team · Manage the finances in the team, including fees, invoicing, debtors, resource costs, updating finance & resource projections regularly and report to Directors and Technical Directors · Programme the work in the team; forecasting resource, identifying the requirement for external contractor support and liaising with other specialist input teams within the organisation to ensure profitable and timely delivery of projects Business Development · Appreciate the need and assist in business development efforts · Foster relationships with potential clients, fellow professionals and industry associations · Maintain market intelligence and pipeline opportunities; and generate leads · Support Directors and Associate Directors in following leads; be available to prepare submissions, make presentations, entertaining and preparing marketing material · Secure external speaking / lecturing opportunities Qualified candidates ideally possess · Bachelor’s or higher degree in a field related to building engineering, environmental building design, architectural engineering, building services engineering or building science · Candidates who are Chartered or working towards Chartership are highly desired · GM AAP/ GM AP are highly desired · Candidates with LEED or WELL accreditation are highly desired · 8 - 10 years’ professional experience in an engineering or sustainability consulting firm · Proven track record of leading and executing dynamic thermal modelling and energy modelling analysis · Experience in daylight modelling, computational fluid dynamic (CFD), parametric modelling and programming · Proficiency in IES VE, Radiance and Microsoft Excel · Skills with softwares such as Grasshopper, eQuest, EnergyPlus, AutoCAD, Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, InDesign) and Rhino or general programming or data visualization skill · Proven track record of successfully co-ordinating and delivering sustainable & environmental design input to projects within the public and private sector, with special focus on building energy performance · Proven track record of engaging and negotiating with senior management and peers on projects · Proven track record of engaging with and influencing peers outside of formal management relationships · Proven track record of both resource and project management · Practical knowledge of managing small project teams coupled with the understanding to select frameworks/tools that are appropriate to particular circumstances · Strong writing skills for title: Facilities Manager (SFSM)
description: About the Role We are seeking an experienced Facilities Manager cum Senior Fire Safety Manager (SFSM) to oversee the full spectrum of facilities, maintenance, safety, and fire protection operations across our campus. This role is ideal for candidates who thrive in a higher‑learning environment, ensuring that campus users—students, staff, and visitors—enjoy a safe, efficient, and well‑maintained environment. Key Responsibilities Facilities Management Oversee preventive, corrective and reactive maintenance for building systems and infrastructure. Develop and implement FM SOPs, maintenance plans, audits, and incident response procedures. Optimise use of space, assets, resources, and utilities to support operational efficiency. Conduct regular site inspections to ensure quality, compliance and seamless daily operations. Vendor & Contract Management Manage service providers to ensure quality performance and compliance with SLAs. Review quotations, tenders, maintenance contracts and procurement requirements. Monitor contract performance, cost control and budget adherence. Fire Safety Management (SFSM Duties) Lead the organisation’s fire safety strategy in accordance with SCDF requirements. Oversee fire protection systems, Fire Certificate renewal, equipment readiness and maintenance. Conduct risk assessments, hazard identification, CERT equipment management and emergency drills. Ensure campus‑wide adherence to fire safety regulations and best practices. Compliance, Safety & Environmental Standards Ensure compliance with SCDF, MOM, WSH, QEMS and internal standards. Prepare safety, environmental and incident reports; conduct investigations when required. Promote sustainability initiatives and smart FM solutions. Stakeholder Engagement Liaise closely with school departments, management, contractors, and external agencies. Provide timely response to feedback, incidents, and operational requests. Team Leadership Lead and develop facilities teams, ensuring adequate manpower, training and performance. Encourage continuous improvement, innovation and service excellence. Requirements Minimum 5 years of relevant experience , preferably in a higher‑learning institute or campus environment. Mandatory : Valid Senior Fire Safety Manager (SFSM) certification. Diploma/Degree in Facilities Management, Engineering, Building Services or related fields. Strong knowledge of building systems, fire safety regulations and FM operations. Experience in vendor management, contract administration and FM digital systems (e.g., CMMS). Strong communication, analytical, stakeholder management and problem‑solving skills. Able to lead teams, handle emergencies and manage multiple priorities. *Note: Only shortlisted candidate will be contacted for interview. Thank you for your understanding and application*

title: HR Manager
description: We are seeking an experienced, highly passionate and driven HR professional to join our HR team at Mandai Rainforest Resort by Banyan Tree . This will be an excellent opportunity for HR professionals who want to further their career trajectory, especially in a managerial capacity. The selected candidate will play a pivotal role in advancing the Human Capital policies and practices in the Resort. The incumbent will deepen and advance their HR career by driving various spectrums of HR including Business Partnering, Compensation & Benefits and Labour Relations. Key Responsibilities Partner with the HR Director to ensure progressive HR practices across all human resource functions. This includes recruitment, onboarding, policy development, and ensuring compliance with labor regulations. Recruitment and Manpower Planning - Develop and execute a comprehensive recruitment strategy to attract top talent for the Resort. In performing this role, the HR Manager will engage with various stakeholders, including community leaders, agencies etc. to broaden our reach and share our opportunities will all segments of the community. Policy Development – Establish and / or review all HR policies and standards, ranging from salary structures, performance management, benefits, etc, and importantly, to ensure that they comply with all statutory regulations. The HR Manager will also be responsible for developing communication strategies to ensure all team members are aware of the Resort’s policies and procedures. Culture and Employee Relations – Foster a culture that encapsulates the Resort’s Values and Ethos. As part of the role, the HR Manager will be responsible for fostering a positive work environment, one which encourages collaboration, engagement and a passion for continuous learning & upskilling. The incumbent will serve as a strategic Business Partner for the Resort and implement employee engagement initiatives and address any employee concerns. Job Requirements Degree or Diploma in Human Resources, Business Administration or a related field. Proven track record of 7 to 10 years’ experience in a similar capacity. Strong knowledge of local labor laws and HR best practices. Strong analytical, interpersonal, communication and organizational skills. Able to handle matters independently, and able to exercise self-motivation to complete projects.

title: Senior Social Worker (Tiong Bahru)
description:
Summary: Provide and lead social work intervention and project/event management to support the
work in the Day Activity Centre/Sheltered Workshop for persons with
disabilities. Casework Management Administer financial assistance and/or seek deviation for clients to allow them to participate maximally in the DAC  programme as needed. Network with other agencies to tap on community resources to provide intervention support to clients. For each client, explore and document care planning intentions with caregivers. Conduct closure of cases upon achieving sustainable service outcomes for clients. Community Work Identify the changing trends/needs of the landscape pertaining to the PWDs and coordinate community resources to enable maximally independent community living. Network with relevant referral agencies and stakeholders serving PWDs and be part of the outreach efforts. Group Work Support social workers to plan, develop, implement and review curriculum, psycho-educational training and group work for PWDs and/or their caregivers as necessary. Supervise facilitation of group work interventions and evaluate progress and effectiveness of group work interventions across the organization Project management Support social workers to plan events such as SPD open house or caregivers’ appreciation day or other projects as assigned. Support and lead social workers to plan, develop, implement and review curriculum for Enabling Skills & Living Programme Professional Development Lead and support Lead Social Worker in developmental programs and supervision for personal and professional growth & development. Maintain minimum requirements for renewal of membership with professional association Admin Duties Coverage as needed for programme head / supervisor with programme accountability reporting duties. Other duties as required/assigned by the supervisor or Head of Division. JOB HOLDER REQUIREMENT Hold a degree in Social Work from recognized institutions Have excellent communication and interpersonal skills with demonstrated ability to build relationships. Preferably have minimum 7 years’ experience in social work with some experience in disability sector. Have minimum of 3 years’ experience in supervision.

title: IAM Specialist - Systems Integrator

The team works closely with organisations to strengthen access controls, governance, and compliance across complex technology environments. This role suits an experienced Identity & Access Management professional who enjoys hands-on delivery and working across the full project lifecycle. Responsibilities Deliver end-to-end Digital Identity & Access Management initiatives across enterprise environments Define identity strategies, operating models, and governance frameworks aligned with title: Senior Counsellor
description: Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre (EMCC) established in 1997, is one of the region’s foremost organisations that pioneered family mediation and training, and one of the few agencies with expertise to provide integrative services in mediation and psychotherapy. EMCC has continued to evolve yet always endeavouring to be true to our vision of Bringing Hope and Restoration – to lost and hurting individuals, and broken relationships through our counselling and mediation services.  On the upstream, EMCC actively develops prevention and early detection of psycho-emotional and relational problems. This includes training, public talks and workshops, in-company training, community outreach programmes, resource development, among others. These are aimed at empowering individuals through imparting and equipping of knowledge and self-help skills to effectively manage their interpersonal relationship challenges.  We advocate for destigmatisation of mental health and normalise seeking help through public campaigning. EMCC is a non-profit with Institute of Public Character status. Job Description We are seeking an experienced counsellor to provide counselling services, consultations, assessments and interventions for individuals, couples and families experiencing socio-emotional and mental health challenges of moderate to high complexity and risk. The incumbent will provide clinical supervision of lesser experienced counsellors, develop, deliver and oversee the training curriculum, conduct training for other professionals and corporate clients, and lead community outreach initiatives.  As the Senior Counsellor, you must have knowledge of case management and the ability to engage and network with external stakeholders to promote the organisational objectives of EMCC. The successful candidate should demonstrate high professionalism, leadership, and management skills. Responsibilities Provide early-risk marriage support e.g. minor and trans-national couples, separation support and family and marital counselling. Lead group counselling and other therapeutic interventions to individuals and families facing challenges; and to work with relevant partners to promote the welfare of the clients. Proficient in various counselling approaches (e.g. systemic etc) and tools to help with clients' concerns effectively. Possess the ability to manage challenging cases, including crisis interventions and referrals to other agencies. Deliver clinical supervision for staffs’ professional development. Provide oversight of high-risk cases and consultation to less experienced counsellors handling them. Supervise and manage counselling interns, when necessary. Identify the training needs of stakeholders and work with partners to plan, develop and implement training/programmes for the different client groups. Identify gaps in clients service delivery and improve work processes. Comply with workflow processes and improve protocols where required. Support the Lead Counsellor in the development and implementation of the counselling and training services plan to meet organisational KPIs. Ensure funded projects comply with the funding terms and conditions. Ensure budgets are adhered to and financial systems maintained. Provide timely reports on statutory, grants/funders for internal purposes and requirements. Perform any other duties assigned by the Executive Director. Educational Qualifications Master’s Degree in Counselling or Counselling Psychology by accredited institutions under the Singapore Association for Counselling (SAC). SAC registered counsellor and preferably SAC registered Clinical Supervisor. Training in different modalities. Experiences and Competencies More than five years of experience in counselling individuals, couples, and families with at least two years of supervisory experience. Experience in counselling all age groups (children and youths, adults, and seniors). Experience in facilitating psycho-education and therapy groups. Proven track record of helping clients to achieve their psychological well-being goals. Experience working with different nationalities and cultures. Passionate about advocating the importance of mental health. A people-oriented team player that contributes to a positive work and team culture. Ability to adapt and be flexible with changes. Excellent written (e.g. report writing) and presentation skills. Committed, able to multi-task and possess a can-do attitude.

Description for Internal Candidates Headquartered in Singapore, Jurong Port is a leading international multipurpose port operator. Established in 1965, the port initially served Singapore’s Jurong Industrial Estate. Jurong Port has since expanded to serve as Singapore’s main general and bulk cargo gateway. title: IT Assistant Manager / Manager
description: Job Scope The Assistant Manager / Manager will be responsible for end-to-end IT governance, cybersecurity, and operational excellence across ASEAN subsidiaries. This includes designing, implementing, and maintaining secure, scalable, and compliant IT environments while driving automation and digital transformation initiatives. The role requires a strong technical foundation combined with the ability to manage regional rollouts and vendor relationships effectively. Key Responsibilities 1. Infrastructure & Network Management Administer and maintain Windows Server environments (Active Directory, DNS, DHCP,GPO). Manage network infrastructure including firewalls, VPNs, and wireless systems. Ensure high availability and performance of core IT services across multiple sites. 2. Identity & Endpoint Security Implement and enforce identity and access controls using modern enterprise tools. Manage device compliance, patching, and security baselines for endpoints. Deploy and optimize endpoint protection and vulnerability management solutions. 3. Microsoft 365 & Cloud Services Configure and secure Microsoft 365 services including Exchange, SharePoint, Teams. Govern collaboration and data protection policies across regional entities. Drive adoption of productivity and security features within the Microsoft ecosystem. 4. Cybersecurity & Risk Management Monitor and respond to security incidents using advanced threat protection tools. Maintain IT risk registers and implement mitigation strategies. Ensure compliance with applicable regulatory requirements and internal control standards. 5. Automation & Digital Transformation Design and implement automation workflows using Power Automate and scripting. Develop dashboards and reporting tools for operational and security metrics. Identify opportunities for process optimization and efficiency improvements. 6. Governance & Audit Readiness Prepare and maintain documentation for internal controls and audits. Coordinate evidence collection and testing for IT governance requirements. Ensure adherence to company policies and regional regulatory obligations. 7. Regional Rollouts & Vendor Management Lead IT deployments and standardization across ASEAN subsidiaries. Manage vendor relationships for hardware, software, and managed services. Monitor license compliance and renewal schedules for enterprise solutions. 8. Team Mentoring & Development Provide guidance and technical mentorship to junior IT staff. Share best practices and promote continuous learning within the team. Support skill development aligned with evolving technologies and compliance needs. Requirements ·        Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related discipline. ·        Minimum 6 years of hands-on experience in IT operations, cybersecurity, and governance, including 2+ years in a managerial role. ·        Strong technical proficiency in: Windows Server administration (Active Directory, DNS, DHCP, GPO). Microsoft 365 ecosystem (Exchange Online, SharePoint, Teams, etc) andsecurity controls. Identity and endpoint management (Entra ID, Conditional Access, Intune,Autopilot, etc). Network and firewall technologies (FortiGate, VLAN, VPN). Endpoint protection and vulnerability management (Defender forEndpoint). ·        Practical experience with automation tools (Power Automate, PowerShell scripting) and reporting (Power BI). ·        Familiarity with backup and disaster recovery solutions . ·        Knowledge of IT governance and compliance principles aligned with applicable regulatory requirements and internal control standards. ·        Strong analytical, problem-solving, and documentation skills. ·        Experience in multi-country IT rollouts and regional standardization. ·        Excellent communication skills in English and the ability to collaborate effectively with stakeholders from diverse cultural and country backgrounds across APAC. ·        Ability to travel within Asia Pacific as required for regional projects and subsidiary support.

title: Ecommerce Operations Assistant
description: Retykle is Asia’s first and largest online consignment store for buying and selling designer baby, children's & maternity fashion, gear and toys. The platform started in Hong Kong in 2016 and launched into our first overseas market in Singapore in 2021. We are looking for talented people who are hungry to learn and contribute to our team. Enthusiasm for creating efficient processes and solving problems. Have the ability to work independently and be self-motivated while still working within a team environment. Strong attention to detail, hands-on with organisation skills Fluent in English

description: Job Profile Summary: As Lead in the Compliance & Risk Organisation (CRO) Office, you will play a pivotal role in shaping and delivering strategic initiatives that strengthen compliance and enterprise risk management across the organisation. Reporting to the Head of CRO Office, you will collaborate with colleagues across the CRO division, as well as with various departments and Group functions, to drive transformation, automation, and robust risk oversight. title: HIP HOP TEACHER & PROGRAMME EXECUTIVE
description: JOB SUMMARY The Hip Hop Teacher& Programme Executive is a dual-role position combining high-quality hip hop teaching with programme coordination and execution responsibilities. The role supports the delivery, development, and growth of Crestar’s hip hop programmes by teaching structured classes, preparing students for performances and examinations, and working closely with HQ and centres on curriculum, enrolment, events, and departmental initiatives. This position is suited for passionate hip hop educators who are organised, collaborative, and keen to develop programme management capabilities. JOB ROLE Hip Hop Programme Plan and deliver structured hip hop lessons aligned with Crestar’s curriculum and teaching standards. Conduct classes and learning activities in accordance with HQ guidelines and approved lesson plans. Prepare and coach students for performances, examinations, and competitions. Engage parents through showcases, parent-teacher meetings, and regular communication to support student progress and retention. Guide and support centre Operations personnel on dance-related matters and class execution. Monitor student attendance, retention, and conversion trends, and provide input to support enrolment growth. Programme Planning & Execution Support Support the design and implementation of dance course plans in collaboration with dance professionals and the Dance Manager. Assist in market and competitor research (e.g. area demand, curriculum trends, competitor offerings) and consolidate findings for review. Track and analyse dance enrolment data, attendance, and dropout trends; prepare reports and recommendations to improve intake and retention. Coordinate and support dance department meetings, including preparation of materials, minutes, and follow-up actions. Assist in budget tracking and expenditure monitoring for the dance department. Programme Management & Quality Support Support the planning and execution of dance programme initiatives in collaboration with HQ and centre teams. Assist in the development, review, and enhancement of dance curriculum, syllabi, and training manuals. Contribute to the professional development of local and overseas instructors through mentoring, training support, and knowledge sharing. Support the implementation of departmental systems, policies, and SOPs to ensure consistent programme delivery across centres. Collaborate with internal departments (Operations, Marketing, Academic) on cross-functional initiatives. Events & Projects Work with HQ to organise and execute concerts, recitals, examinations, and competitions. Choreograph and prepare students for performances, including coordination of rehearsals, costumes, music, and props. Lead or support special projects such as exchange programmes, workshops, and holiday dance camps. Provide on-site support during major events to ensure smooth execution and positive participant experience. Human Resource & Assets Coordination Support HQ in the recruitment, onboarding, and retention of quality hip hop teachers. Support teacher and staff manpower planning through scheduling, allocation, and administrative coordination. Assist in monitoring teacher performance indicators and support appraisal and feedback processes. Coordinate training, documentation, and staff development activities as guided by the Dance Manager. Maintain and track course-related assets and resources to ensure proper usage and availability. Branding & Marketing Support Support marketing initiatives by providing programme information, schedules, and content inputs. Assist in coordination of marketing campaigns, events, and outreach activities to drive enquiries and enrolment. Collect feedback from centres and customers to support ongoing brand and programme improvement. Job Requirements: Experience & Qualifications Proven experience in teaching hip hop to children, teens, as well as adults at beginner to advanced levels. Strong background in hip-hop or street dance styles. Experience in dance education, enrichment programmes, arts administration, or programme coordination is an advantage. A track of record winning recognised competitions is an advantage. Diploma in Dance is an advantage. Skills & Competencies Strong knowledge of hip hop/street dance techniques, terminology, and performance practices. Ability to choreograph and prepare students for examinations, performances, and competitions. Excellent communication skills and ability to engage and motivate students. Strong project management capability, with organisational, coordination skills and attention to detail. Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, including PowerPoint (presentation preparation), Excel (data tracking and basic analysis), and Word (documentation and reports). Good written and verbal communication skills; able to engage parents, teachers, and internal teams professionally. Ability to manage multiple tasks, timelines, and stakeholders in a structured environment. Basic data tracking and reporting skills (e.g. enrolment, attendance, retention). Personal Attributes Passionate about dance education and committed to nurturing students’ artistic and personal growth. Energetic, patient, and engaging teaching approach. Ability to work collaboratively with other teachers, operations, admin and management staff. Responsible, proactive, and willing to learn. Physically fit to demonstrate dance movements and correct students’ techniques safely. Adaptable and able to support both operational and programme-related tasks. Willingness to work evenings, weekends, and during school holiday programmes as required.

title: Account Manager/Senior Account Manager
description: Account Manager / Senior Account Manager (Singapore) Location: Singapore Function: Client Servicing and Account Management Reports to: Head of Account Servicing About Click2View Click2View is a content marketing and production agency partnering with global brands across APAC and CEMEA. The role We are hiring an Account Manager or Senior Account Manager in Singapore. You will manage day-to-day client relationships, lead briefs on calls, coordinate creative and production, and drive account plans, forecasts, and revenue growth with Account Directors and Creative Leads. You will own and lead a portfolio of 2 to 5 accounts. Your mandate is to be the account champion who understands the client’s business, people, and priorities deeply. Key responsibilities Account ownership and growth Own and lead a portfolio of 2 to 5 accounts with accountability for relationship health, delivery quality, and commercial outcomes. Become the internal and external champion for each account, knowing stakeholders, objectives, brand guardrails, and ways of working. Identify horizontal growth opportunities within existing accounts and support onboarding of new accounts. Convert qualified opportunities into proposals and statements of work. Account planning, forecasting, and QBRs Build quarterly account plans with content roadmaps, milestones, and risks. Set annual targets by account and maintain rolling forecasts updated monthly. Track performance against targets and course correct with clear actions and owners. Lead QBRs for your accounts, presenting performance, learnings, next quarter priorities, and budget outlook. Client relationship management Serve as primary contact for assigned accounts. Run agendas, WIPs, notes, and action trackers with clear next steps. Map stakeholders, align expectations, escalate risks early, and keep delivery updates separate from scope or budget discussions. Business development support Respond to briefs and RFPs with scopes, timelines, and estimates, coordinating inputs from Creative and Production. Contribute to case studies and credentials with outcomes, metrics, and references. Project oversight and creative quality Plan and manage schedules, dependencies, and resourcing with Producers. For simpler work, run AM-as-Producer where scoped. Partner with Creative Leads to uphold intent and quality. Consolidate feedback logs, ensure version control, and confirm brand compliance before release. Keep project management tools and the resource schedule updated with milestones, bookings, and status so teams have full visibility. Commercial hygiene Align scopes to budget, track hours and burn, manage change requests, prepare PO and invoicing details with Finance, and close projects with clean documentation. Reporting and reviews Lead clear weekly updates and end-of-project reviews. Support KPI and outcome reporting for QBRs and leadership reviews. What you will bring Experience AM: 2 to 4 years in an agency or content and production environment. SAM: 5 to 7 years with senior client handling, multi-workstream coordination, and demonstrated growth on existing accounts. Capabilities Proven account ownership across multiple concurrent accounts with strong written and verbal communication. Quarterly planning, annual target setting, forecasting, and sound commercial judgment. Project discipline across schedules, risks, budgets, and documentation. Creative quality control to review scripts, edits, and designs and give actionable feedback that protects brand voice and standards. Collaboration with Creative Leads, Producers, Editors, and Designers with clear roles and handovers. Tools Confident with Google Workspace and strong file hygiene. Familiarity with common AI assistants and review platforms is a plus. Other Category fluency in B2B or complex brands – for example tech, finance, banking or insurance clients – is an advantage. Success in the first 90-120 days • Established as the account champion for 2 to 3 accounts, with stakeholder maps and quarterly account plans in place for at least the 2 key accounts. • Annual targets set per account with a rolling forecast and a simple tracking rhythm. • Reliable WIP cadence with documented decisions and next steps. • Clean budget tracking with PO and invoicing details submitted on time. • Positive feedback from Creative and Production on clarity and quality at each milestone. How to apply Apply on MyCareersFuture or email your CV to hello@click2view.asia with the subject “AM or SAM Singapore – Your Name”. —----

title: Marketing Partnership Strategist - Singapore
description: Netflix is one of the world's leading entertainment services, with over 300 million paid memberships in over 190 countries enjoying TV series, films and games across a wide variety of genres and languages. Members can play, pause and resume watching as much as they want, anytime, anywhere, and can change their plans at any time. The role: We are seeking a Creative Strategist (10+ years) who is a master at developing creative strategies deeply rooted in fan behaviour and cultural truths. Someone who can put an interesting twist on a common problem, who can see the unexpected and natural intersection between Netflix IP and a brand, or can interrogate a problem to understand the truth that lay behind it. This person will be working across both Brand Partnerships and Ads Marketing strategy within the Marketing Partnerships Team. With regards to Ads Marketing, this individual will be responsible for working with our Ads Marketing team and our local markets to think through the right GTM strategy and positioning for the market, developing narratives or working with global ones and tailoring them to the realities and insights of our local markets. They will need to understand the unique problems and gaps that sit within each of the markets when it comes to advertisers and brands, and develop thought leadership and narratives that address them. From a brand partnerships perspective, they will be partnering with the local market creatives to develop the creative strategy at the beginning of a brand partnership as a sparring partner. This requires delving into fan insights, cultural conversations and bigger human truths to drive us to interesting and unexpected creative directions. While they may not be authoring the work, they need to be deep collaborators, creating the frameworks that local markets can use to drive new insights and ways in. If you are passionate about culture, creative and entertainment, with demonstrated experience in crafting and executing impactful creative campaigns/programmes, we’d love to hear from you. Key responsibilities: Introducing new brands, advertisers and agencies to the Netflix Ads Suite through highly strategic creative campaigns and executions. Elevating the strategic bar. Constantly asking the bigger questions around why we’re doing something, understanding the problems we’re solving for or the opportunities we’re capturing. Working with local market creatives to elevate strategic creative thinking on brand partnerships and find new, interesting, curious, unexpected ways to bring a partnership to life. Developing strategic frameworks, guidance, inspiration for the local markets to help drive unexpected strategic thinking. Leading creative workshops, presentations, briefings and owning the feedback loop and output with partners and agencies. Qualifications/Experience: THE BASICS: 10+ years of track record in brand or consumer marketing at advertising agency, or in-house Creative team. Experience in Entertainment is a plus. STRATEGIC PLANNING: Connector of dots, able to scan through data, behaviours, comms plans, consumer research to find partner insights and content insights and bridge them in the right sweet spot to create the most fertile territory for great ideas to flourish. CREATIVE THINKER: A media-agnostic thinker who can ideate for any space and impact the right audience. Knows how to get to great work and brings a fresh perspective to the way people connect with culture, content and brands. CREATIVE LEADER: A selfless creative leader & strong storyteller who can shape and pitch creative concepts. Lead the thought process with the partner and agencies in all aspects of the program - from initial briefing, creative strategy, big idea and production. PROJECT MANAGEMENT: A creative shepherd and problem solver who is able to drive multiple projects from start to finish and turn cool ideas into reality. Knowledge of production with the ability to execute flawlessly. Doesn’t hesitate to get into the details, and does not lose sight of the broader strategic intent. CLEAR & CONCISE COMMUNICATOR: We are looking for a fluent English speaker with excellent communication skills, who is comfortable collaborating with multiple internal cross-functional teams and confident in influencing external senior stakeholders. Knowledge of other Asian languages is a plus. Personality Attributes: Curious and creatively-minded in every way. Entrepreneurial and selfless collaborator who is selfless and goes beyond roles and titles to get great work done. Able to give and receive feedback positively, and see the value of different perspectives. Able to think and act with agility. Capable of being impactful without the support of a large team of direct reports. Demonstrates strong judgment in the face of ambiguity. Flexible and adaptive to evolve the role based on our business needs. A natural and deep affection for movies and television and the role of entertainment in today’s media landscape. A desire to thrive in our unique culture . Inclusion is a Netflix value and we strive to host a meaningful interview experience for all candidates. If you want an accommodation/adjustment for a disability or any other reason during the hiring process, please send a request to your recruiting partner. We are an equal-opportunity employer and celebrate diversity, recognizing that diversity builds stronger teams. We approach diversity and inclusion seriously and thoughtfully. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, caste, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic makeup, marital status, or military service.
