title: Associate Project Manager (Regional)
description: A Note to MOWIN 's Future Partner: We are not looking for a typical employee. We are looking for a partner in the trenches. We are transforming MOWIN across the region (China, Indonesia, Vietnam). To support this, I need a Associate Project Manager (Regional) —someone to manage IP, cultural rights, and marketing projects across the region. But before you apply, I want to be blunt. The Reality (Read this first): This is not a 9-5 job. Transformation is messy. It requires grit. We work fast, and we work hard. If you prioritise "work-life balance" over "mission success," this isn't for you. We believe in work-life integration —where we enjoy the hustle and celebrate the wins together. No "Corporate Fluff." I don’t believe in political correctness or walking on eggshells. We operate on old-school values: Loyalty, Honour, and Results. We are a family-style unit—we protect each other, but we also hold each other to high standards. All Ages & Backgrounds Welcome. I don’t care if you are 20 or 60. I don’t care if you have a PhD or no degree. I care about your hunger. If you are willing to learn, I am willing to train you from scratch. What You Will Do: Shadow Me: You will report directly to me, acting as my right hand in executing regional projects. Orchestrate: You will ensure our projects in China and SE Asia stay on track, coordinating between teams and handling the dynamics of cross-border business. Grow: You will learn the ins and outs of Intellectual Property and Cultural Rights—a rare skillset in today's market. The Remuneration: I believe that if the project wins, you win. Stable Base: A salary that covers your needs comfortably and provides security. High Upside: This is the exciting part. You will receive incentives directly from the project profits. The more successful we are together, the more you earn. There is no glass ceiling here. Who You Are: Driven: You don’t wait for instructions. Resilient: You can handle the pressure of a fast-moving regional business. Honest: You speak your mind and you keep your word. Open: Whether you are a digital native or a veteran looking to reinvent yourself, you are humble enough to learn and bold enough to lead. How to Apply: Ignore the standard cover letter. Send me a message or drop your CV with a note telling me why you have the grit to join MOWIN.

title: Market Analytics Manager
description: We are seeking a skilled Market Analytics Manager, Semiconductor to lead market intelligence, addressable market insights, and strategic analytics across the semiconductor ecosystem , with coverage of the semiconductor market alongside products, applications, and technologies. This role will steward data integrity, build robust reporting and dashboard solutions, and partner with cross-regional business stakeholders (internal & external) to deliver insights that influence strategic business decisions across product, technology, and commercial teams. Semiconductor Market & Addressable Market Analysis Define and maintain comprehensive Total Addressable Market (TAM) models covering different geographical semiconductor markets , including materials, devices, applications and technologies. Analyze market size, growth rates, competitive dynamics, customer demand, and material-specific trends (e.g., wafer fabrication and packaging materials, specialty gases, photoresists, etc.) that drive the semiconductor materials industry. Segment markets by products, industries, applications and technology nodes to support strategic planning and prioritization across regions. Data Integrity, Governance & Reporting Frameworks Ensure integrity of market data through monitoring, governance frameworks, and process controls, while streamlining the end-to-end data-reporting lifecycle for global and regional datasets. Develop and deliver data and reporting outputs through continuously gather inputs from cross-functional and cross-regional teams to define and refine data elements, enhancing market data relevancy and accuracy . Establish scalable processes for data curation, validation, maintenance, and quality assurance. Dashboards, Visualization & Analytics Build and maintain interactive dashboards (e.g., Tableau ) to visualize market trends, materials segmentation, forecasts, KPIs, and competitive landscapes for business leaders across different geographical region. Steer management toward data-driven decision-making by revamping dashboards, automating reporting, and generating analytical insights that influence strategic outcomes. Partner with data engineering and analytics teams to standardize BI tools and reporting workflows for both regional and global use. Partnering with Business Stakeholders (Global & APAC) Collaborate with product management, materials teams, technical application, commercial, finance, R&D, and operations across APAC and global functions to align data solutions with business needs. Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver data solutions that provide insights, projections, measurable metrics, and performance tracking — including usage trends for semiconductor materials and related product portfolios. Act as a trusted adviser on market data, trends, materials dynamics, and strategic insights to influence senior leadership decisions. Qualifications Master’s degree / PhD in Engineering, Materials Science, Economics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related field. 10+ years’ experience in market analytics, strategic insights, or market research —within semiconductors or electronics high-tech sectors with global and APAC remit . Strong expertise in addressable market modeling, materials segmentation, forecasting, data visualization, and reporting across products and materials markets. Experience with semiconductor industry data sources (equipment, materials, fabrication, packaging) and research vendors covering Global & APAC markets . Proven ability to manage large, complex datasets and ensure high standards of data integrity . Proficiency with Tableau and dashboard development; experience with other BI/analytics platforms is a plus. Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence stakeholders and translate complex data into actionable business insights.

title: Medical Social Worker
description: You will be posted to Vanguard Care Home @ Senja / Woodlands. This Medical Social Work (MSW) will provide psycho-social assessment, social work intervention and support to residents and their families to resolve personal, social, emotional, and financial issues. The role involves facilitating residents to benefit from comprehensive health and social services that help to reduce the burden on them and their families. Job Responsibilities MSW works with the Nurse Manager, Rehab Manager, and Ops Team to facilitate resident admissions and to ensure the appropriate support is provided to our residents. Attend multi-disciplinary meetings with medical staff, nurses, and other healthcare professionals to discuss, propose, and coordinate care plans for residents. Design and develop relevant programmes on psycho-socio care that promote quality care that look at well-being of residents. Counsel resident and their family members on psycho-socio care plans Liaise with organisations that provide services to meet residents' and their families needs and refer them to community support organisations if necessary. Focus on family-centred care by encouraging participation among residents' family members. Active participation in visiting residents at wards and join organization/residents' related events. Identify gaps and streamline work processes within organization and service deliveries related to care and needs of residents and families. Advocate for changes with relevant stakeholders to enhance the care and well-being of residents. To keep up to date with the latest government guidelines on healthcare and social service programs, funding schemes available within the community/sector. Develop the ability to work with different levels of internal colleagues to foster close liaison on issues arising and/or feedback related to residents. Participate as part of the Multi-disciplinary Case Assessment Team (MCAT) assessors to conduct residents' pre-admission assessment. Establish and confirm source of social facts shown in the social background of all new applicants. Manage Medifund administration and applications in collaborations with Finance Dept. Administer renewals of residents' funding schemes when needed, e.g. Household means testing, etc. on a timely basis. Develop and monitor the social wellness care plan of residents. Develop discharge care plan for residents if their health condition is fit for discharge. Any other duties as assigned by Reporting Manager/Supervisor. Requirements: Candidate must possess a Degree in Social Work. At least 1 - 3 years of relevant healthcare industry experience. Pleasant and mature disposition with empathy and patience dealing with residents, next-of-kins, and staff alike. Demonstrate inititative to identify, plan, develop, and implement resourcefulness to find solutions to missing gaps in work processes and mutually benefits stakeholders. Required Skill(s): Good communication and people engagement skills. Good organisation ability. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Social Worker
description: At Rainbow Centre we envision a world where persons with disabilities are empowered and thriving in inclusive communities. Our team of social workers uncovers and enables the strengths of persons with disabilities and their families through person centred planning and supports them in pursuit of their Good Life - where they are safe, healthy, engaged, connected, independent, included and heard. We are looking for a Social Worker for our Special Education Schools. In considering if you should send us your CV for consideration, we like you to know that in addition to a degree in Social Work, it is far more important that you enjoy knowing and connecting with people of diverse abilities, uncovering possibilities and harnessing the power of communities. The social worker will conduct assessments of client needs, provide counselling, support and crisis intervention and participate in programme and policy development. The social worker works closely with families, social and community agencies and volunteers to strengthen the circles of care around our clients with disabilities. description: At Wise, we're on a mission to build the best way to move and manage money internationally. We're making financial services more fair, accessible, and affordable for people and businesses worldwide. As we continue to grow, we're looking for a Growth Marketing Manager with a focus on Mainland China & Hong Kong markets . This role is pivotal in driving our growth strategy, managing multi-channel campaigns, and fostering partnerships to increase our transaction volume in these key markets. Your Mission As a Growth Marketing Manager for Greater China, you will: Drive growth initiatives and country level marketing strategies, ensuring market penetration and user acquisition. Organize and execute multi-channel marketing campaigns, coordinating closely with internal teams and external partners to maximize reach and impact. Lead product launches in the region, tailoring strategies to meet local needs and preferences. Collaborate closely with paid channels and media agencies to identify new channels to test and scale successful tactics, optimizing for cost-effective customer acquisition. Collaborate with local partners, including but not limited to expat groups in China, Chinese diaspora communities in popular destination countries, education and immigration agencies to explore innovative avenues for transaction volume growth. Work closely with the Organic Social and Influencer marketing teams to drive a strong online presence on dedicated platforms, and increase our brand presence in the market. Work closely with the analytics team to monitor campaign performance, generate insights, and produce comprehensive reports to guide future strategies. Qualifications You'll be a great fit for this role if you have: Experience & Expertise: A minimum of 5 years of marketing experience, with at least 2 years specifically focused on the China market or Chinese - speaking audiences Proficiency in key Chinese channels, with experience launching and managing campaigns on platforms such as WeChat, RedNote, and Douyin Strong preference for experience within the fintech or related industries Deep understanding of local nuances, skilled in tailoring strategies for diverse cultural contexts and demonstrating strong localization expertise Core Competencies: Data-Driven Approach: Root your strategies in analytics, leveraging data effectively to inform decisions and drive measurable growth Creativity & Innovation: Bring a creative approach to your work, consistently seeking ways to engage audiences and achieve business objectives Teamwork & Leadership: A proactive team player with excellent project planning skills, capable of efficiently leading and managing cross-functional initiatives Communication: Effective communicator, able to clearly articulate ideas and collaborate efficiently with both internal teams and external stakeholders Language Skills: Strong proficiency in Mandarin Cantonese proficiency is a plus Nice to Haves: Experience with Paid Marketing strategies/ campaigns/ channels experience Working experience in fintech/ financial services/ tech/ app-based product companies What’s in It for You? Opportunity to significantly impact Wise's growth in the Greater China region, contributing to our mission of building money without borders Empowerment in your role, with the support of a talented and passionate team Competitive compensation package, including RSUs Hybrid working model, allowing for a blend of home and office work Generous self-development budget and comprehensive benefits package The chance to work in a diverse, inclusive, and innovative environment
